title: Learning High-Order Relationships with Hypergraph Attention-based Spatio-Temporal Aggregation for Brain Disease Analysis_supp1-3690795.pdf
authors: Aijing Lin
publish: N/A
abstract: Functional connectivity derived from functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) primarily captures pairwise interactions between brain regions, which limits its ability to characterize complex high-order relationships. Hypergraph-based methods provide a natural way to model such interactions, yet most existing approaches rely on predefined hypergraph structures and neglect temporal dynamics, resulting in limited expressiveness and interpretability. To address these challenges, we propose a novel framework that jointly learns informative and sparse high-order brain structures along with their temporal dynamics. Inspired by the information bottleneck principle, we introduce an objective that maximizes information and minimizes redundancy, aiming to retain disease-relevant high-order features while suppressing irrelevant information. The proposed framework consists of three key components: (1) a multi-hyperedge binary mask module for hypergraph structure learning, (2) a hypergraph self-attention aggregation module that captures spatial features through adaptive attention across nodes and hyperedges, and (3) a spatio-temporal low-dimensional network for extracting discriminative spatio-temporal representations for disease classification. Experiments on benchmark fMRI datasets demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance compared to the state-of-the-art approaches and effectively captures meaningful high-order brain interactions. These findings provide new insights into brain network modeling, showing potential for analyzing neuropsychiatric disorders.

title: Modeling and Simulation Based Development of a Teleoperated Slit Lamp
authors: Sasisekhar Govind, Gabriel Wainer, Lindsey Marvel, Kasturirangan Iyengar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: A significant proportion of blindness and visual impairment is preventable through early diagnosis, yet access to critical diagnostic tools remain limited in under served areas. Traditional medical equipment, which is often expensive and requires operation by trained ophthalmologists, poses a barrier to effective care in remote areas. To help serve these regions better, several not-for-profit organizations typically establish eye clinics, but the need for medical professionals to visit these areas for diagnoses beyond preliminary assessments remains a significant challenge. The widespread adoption of the internet offers an opportunity to bridge this gap through telemedical devices (internet connected medical equipment) that can be operated remotely by doctors, enabling them to serve multiple, distant areas and marginalized communities. However the hurdles of development of telemedical devices still persist. Hence, this work proposes the development of a teleoperated medical device (specifically a device called the Slit Lamp), harnessing modeling and simulation based engineering to aid with the development of a robust telemedical device. We explore the use of the Discrete Event Systems Specification (DEVS), to create a discrete event model of the entire telemedical system (predominantly the slit lamp). DEVS Simulation tools, such as Cadmium, allow users to test and refine the model before implementation, ensuring the design meets performance standards. Once the system is simulated and optimized, it can be implemented as-is in the real world (which is an advantage of using DEVS), significantly reducing development time and improving reliability. In the future, this also opens the door to the implementation of a digital twin of the slit lamp, which would provide real-time feedback to medical practitioners by reflecting their inputs as movements in the system. More importantly, digital twins would allow for running simulations in-situ, helping prevent potential accidents.

title: Quantum Technology Readiness Adoption Model: A TOE-Aligned Framework for Addressing Quantum Technology Risk Exposure
authors: Shadya Maldonado Rosado, Trevor Bowman, Andrew Smallwood, Anna Shebanow
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Quantum computing, quantum communication and networking, quantum sensing, and post-quantum cryptography (PQC) each require distinct organizational readiness assessment. Existing maturity models address these domains in isolation, conflate PQC with quantum technologies, omit quantum networking, exhibit regional standards bias, or mandate specific vendor relationships. The Quantum Technology Readiness Adoption Model (QTRAM), grounded in the Technology-Organization-Environment (TOE) framework, structures assessment through five independent Technology sub-dimensions within shared Organization and Environment contexts, using six maturity levels (05) aligned with international standards from IEEE, NIST, ETSI, ITU, TNO, and regional bodies. QTRAM remains theoretical and requires empirical validation.

title: High Granularity, Moving Target Address Space Layout Randomization Through Dynamic Fragmentation
authors: Patrick W. Hong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Code-reuse attacks are a common cyberattack technique that reuses existing code segments in the code base. Attackers create functional “gadgets” out of the existing code base and execute chains of gadgets to achieve their malicious goals.This paper identifies a system and method for address space layout randomization (ASLR) called Dynamic Fragment ASLR (DFASLR), US Patent 11,809,871. DFASLR separates position independent instructions from the rest of the code base and subdivides the relocatable instructions into groupings called fragments. Software hardening is applied to the fragments which are then randomly placed and reconnected at load time. During runtime, continuous re-randomization of fragment location occurs. DFASLR offers randomization with configurable granularity up to disjointing and randomizing individual instruction locations. DFASLR also allows for true runtime ASLR. To date, no practical and generally applicable runtime ASLR has been demonstrated. Along with load and runtime ASLR, DFASLR offers side-channel blinding and is resistant to the state of the art (SoTA) ASLR attacks such as side-channel analysis and memory disclosure attacks. The DFASLR process also provides covert technique insertion, gadget smashing capabilities, Return-Oriented Programming (ROP)/JumpOriented Programming (JOP) traps, and other benefits due to the nature of the technique.

title: CV-QKD Security Improvement Through SNMP-Based Quantum-Network Management
authors: Alberto Sebastia'n-Lombraña, Laura Ortiz, Sebastian Etcheverry, Arturo Villegas, Josefina Cresta, Marco Cofano, Vicente Mart¹n
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Quantum key distribution (QKD) offers information-theoretic security based on quantum mechanics, but practical implementations must address real-world threats to achieve certification. Standards such as ETSI GS QKD 016 define security objectives and requirements to mitigate risks including physical tampering, side-channel exploitation, and protocol-level attacks. This work explores how SNMP-based quantum-network management, following FCAPS principles, can enhance the practical security of continuous-variable QKD (CV-QKD) systems integrated into classical networks.

title: Resolving Bottlenecks in Quantum Machine Learning: Encoding Theory and Data Augmentation
authors: Mariia Baidachna, Isabel Pedraza, Sergei Gleyzer
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Quantum machine learning (QML) models operating on classical data require an encoding step to map information into the Hilbert space. However, this imposes a computational bottleneck absent in classical frameworks, necessitating rigorous theoretical complexity analysis and algorithmic mitigations. We provide theoretical foundations of different encoding methods that prepare quantum states for both simulators and hardware, analyzing normal and sparse high energy physics data. In addition, we propose a quantum data augmentation method based on randomly sampled circuits to improve sample efficiency and mitigate the data scarcity bottleneck. We demonstrate the effectiveness of this method on a generative quantum diffusionlike model to produce synthetic samples of classical images. Our results demonstrate that choosing domain optimal encoding and applying the novel quantum data augmentation leads to faster convergence and improved model performance, offering a highly sample-efficient and scalable approach to encoding and augmenting sparse classical data for QML.

title: A Machine Learning–Driven Framework for Enhancing Cognitive Function Using tDCS and Brain Gym Interventions
authors: Wasee Ahsan, Md Labib, Fahima Haque, Md Minhajul Islam, Md Iqbal Hossain, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Rifatuzzaman Khan Afridi, Md Tareq Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Structured cognitive training produced the strongest effects within the short-term, doing better than the other two interventions, Brain Gym + tDCS and Brain Gym-only methods. Cognitive training intervention participants exhibited significant within-group improvement between pre and post testing in all of the targeted cognitive domains (global cognition, processing speed, and executive function), with results large enough to be considered practically significant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<.05$</tex>) and the group differences were large enough to be considered statistically significant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p$</tex> <. 001) ANOVA. The main strengths of this research are the integration of an interpretable machine learning pipeline along with a non-pharmacological cognitive enhancement protocol that used a multimodal approach. To synthesise change scores across the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), Trail Making Test A (TMT-A), and Trail Making Test B (TMT-B), a new composite scale was created the Cognitive Improvement Index. Thirty patients were assigned randomly to three time pre-intervention) arms: structured cognitive training, Brain Gym + tDCS and Brain Gym only. To overcome the burden of a small sample size, oversampling was done on the training data to make the development of the model more robust. Gradient Boosting with 5fold cross-validation achieved the lowest prediction error (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.5542$</tex>). These results indicate that the derived features and the composite outcome metric capture a meaningful shift in cognitive performance. These results highlight the potential of cognitive training as a core component of transient boosts of cognitive performance and confirm the value of transparent, machine learning-supported assessment procedures in complex neurocognitive interventions. However, sample size and synthetic augmentation issues point to the necessity of future research conducted with larger randomised groups and in modified approaches in response to the intervention.

title: Power Cycling Lifetime Modeling and Validation for Flip-Chip PQFN GaN Devices
authors: Siddhesh Gajare, Duanhui Li, Angel Espinoza, Ricardo Garcia, Peter Tieu, Shengke Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: The power cycling reliability of flip-chip power quad flat no leads GaN transistors was investigated over a wide range of power-on durations. A lifetime model was developed to characterize the Weibull power cycling lifetimes as a function of power-on durations, by incorporating the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t_{0 n}$</tex> term into a modified Norris-Landzberg equation, yielding a frequency exponent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\gamma)$</tex> of −2.09. Failure analysis revealed that gate solder joint cracking was the dominant wear-out failure mechanism. Finite element analysis simulations using COMSOL validated the failure analysis results by predicting the shortest lifetime at the same gate bump location. After implementing the Anand viscoplasticity and Darveaux fatigue models, further simulations showed good agreement with experimental results, validating the applicability of the developed power cycling lifetime model.

title: Integrated Design and Experimental Study of GIS Current Sensing Based on Fiber-Optic Integrated Basin Type Insulator
authors: Kun Zhang, Shuai Yang, Zhongyu Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: To meet the integration requirements of primary and secondary systems in smart grids, a novel integrated solution of fiber optic current transformer (FOCT) and gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) based on the basin type insulator is proposed. By integrating the optical sensing unit inside the GIS basin type insulator, high-precision current signal acquisition and coordinated monitoring of equipment status are achieved. The structural design of the integrated basin type insulator is completed, and the distribution of electric and magnetic fields is simulated and verified using finite element analysis software. The simulation results show that the electric field distortion rate of the basin type insulator under the integrated solution is small, and the magnetic field uniformity meets the sensing requirements. This paper further discusses the long-term reliability of the fiber-resin bonding structure under complex working conditions, the impact of casting process defects on insulation performance, and the costbenefit and manufacturability of the proposed scheme compared with traditional solutions, which further improves the theoretical and engineering application value of the scheme. This research provides a feasible technical path for the primary and secondary integration upgrade of GIS equipment and has significant engineering application value.

title: National Quantum Strategies: a Data-Driven Approach to Understanding the Quantum Ecosystem
authors: Simon Richard Goorney, Emre Aslan, Aleksandrs Baskakovs, Borja Munoz, Jacob Sherson
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: As quantum technologies (QT) move from foundational research toward industrial and societal deployment, national strategies have become critical instruments for shaping the future of this emerging field. In this study, we conduct the first large-scale, data-driven analysis of 62 national quantum strategic documents (QSDs) from 20 countries. Using AI-based natural language processing (topic modeling), we extract 12,786 paragraphs and identify 12 topics present in the text, ranging from technical development areas to transversal aspects such as workforce development and governance. Temporal analysis reveals a distinct shift in policy discourse toward applications of QT and commercialisation, and relatively away from basic science. Our findings highlight the increasing diversification of the QT field, and contribute to the growing area of quantum policy studies. We advocate for more AI and data-driven analyses of the quantum ecosystem, to work toward a scalable framework for understanding the technological and societal challenges of the second quantum revolution.

title: PhoenixNet: A Deep Learning-Based Low-Light Image Enhancement Method for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Afsana Nadia, Md Nuruzzaman, Khan Md Shibli Nomani, Md Masum Billal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: In low-light environments, autonomous vehicles face difficulty to detect the object. In order to overcome these shortcomings, PhoenixNet, a deep learning architecture, has been proposed for enhancing low-light images in autonomous driving scenarios. The objective of this paper is to enhance low-light images in a way that maintains both structural details and perceptual quality and also improves object detection performance with higher confidence scores. We evaluate PhoenixNet against four state-of-the-art methods-Zero-DCE, RQ-LLIE, ExCNet, and PairLIE using some computational metrics, PSNR, SSIM, MS-SSIM, LPIPS, MSE, MAE, and loss. Our experimental results show that PhoenixNet consistently achieves superior performance with its lowest error rates and the highest perceptual quality scores. The model has the highest validation PSNR (36.40), the highest SSIM (0.9764), low perceptual loss LPIPS (0.0656), and low loss values (0.0115). These outcomes ensure the enhanced object detection in low-light conditions. The PhoenixNet model could be a possible solution for enhancing low-light images in real-world autonomous driving applications.

title: Reliability and Anti-Interference Analysis of Emergency Diesel Generator Sets for Geological Drilling Rigs
authors: Chao Li, Tong Lu, Wenwu Liu, Kun Guo, Yuwen Huang, Qing Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: The continuous and stable operation of geological drilling rigs is crucial for the safety and efficiency of geological drilling projects, necessitating an emergency power supply to ensure stable operation and maintain power quality. As the primary emergency power source, the operational reliability and stability of emergency diesel generator sets are self-evident for geological drilling rigs. This paper analyzes the abnormal operation of the emergency diesel generator set and its control system for electric drilling rigs in the Huainan Coal Mine Flood Control Project. Through a detailed study of the harmonic distortion propagation mechanism, it elucidates the mutual influence between the nonlinear load in the electric drilling rig system and the emergency diesel generator set. Based on these findings, we propose systematic improvement measures for the configuration of emergency diesel generator sets for electric drilling rigs. These optimization strategies aim to enhance the operational stability of the emergency power supply, thereby ensuring continuous and safe construction of geological drilling projects.

title: SAE: Subgraph-Aware Edge Classification Framework for Monitoring Suspicious Financial Transactions in Anti Money Laundering Environments
authors: Rejuana Sushmi, Maisha Alam Mim, Sadia Islam, Raqeebir Rab, Abderrahmane Leshob, Ashikur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Money laundering continues to be one of the toughest challenges faced by financial institutions. It involves systematically transferring illicit funds through complex, indirect paths of transactions designed to obscure their origin. Traditional AML systems rely heavily on predefined rules and transactionlevel thresholds, leading to massive numbers of false positives while often missing multi-step laundering behaviors. Recently, graph neural network-based approaches have shown promise by modeling transaction networks; however, most prevailing methods primarily focus on identifying suspicious accounts rather than specific transactions that allow for illicit financial movement. This paper proposes the Subgraph-Aware Edge (SAE) Classification Framework for Monitoring Suspicious Financial Transactions in Anti-Money Laundering Environments. This approach first represents transactional data as a directed graph and then applies an ISMAGS-based subgraph matching for the detection of laundering-specific cyclic motifs. The transactions belonging to these motifs are labeled using domain-driven temporal and monetary rules and utilized for training edge-level GNN models. Four models, GraphSAGE, GAT, GCN, and GATConv, are evaluated, while feedback-based model refinement with a consensus voting strategy is employed to enhance prediction reliability. Experimental results obtained on a synthetic transaction dataset demonstrate that the proposed method effectively identifies laundering patterns; GAT and GATConv yielded superior performance in terms of $\mathbf{F 1}$ score of $95 \%$ and $89.12 \%$, respectively. While the feedback refinement model decreased false positives rate by $14.40 \%$ in the GAT model and $53.23 \%$ in GTAConv. The framework provides interpretable, transaction-level insights, which are well-suited for practical AML investigations.

title: File-Size-Aware Heterogeneous Coded TeraSort
authors: Haoyun Deng, Yong Deng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: This paper considers the design of coded Terasort with heterogeneities in both input data popularities and sizes, which are commonly seen in practice. While no existing work has studied coded TeraSort with heterogeneous file sizes, we propose a file-size-aware low-complexity key-value (KV) pair placement strategy to maximize the efficiency of coded multicasting. We develop a heterogeneous coded TeraSort (HCT) algorithm that efficiently handles files with heterogeneous sizes, while considering the KVs to be sorted are of heterogeneous popularity. Experimental results show that the proposed HCT outperforms existing uncoded and coded TeraSort algorithms.

title: Study on Fault Location Method of Power Cable Using Wavelet Transform Modulus Maxima
authors: Xingchao Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: In the process of locating a power cable fault, finding the singular point of fault traveling wave is a challenge and the uncertainty of traveling wave velocity affects the accuracy of fault location. In this paper, a cable fault location algorithm to remove the impact of wave velocity was derived based on the wavelet transform modulus maximum theory and the reflection law of cable fault traveling wave. The simulation model of the cable fault location system was built by Matlab/Simulink software, the db5 wavelet 3-layer decomposition of fault waveform was realized, and the modulus maxima sequence of high-frequency coefficients was extracted for analysis and calculation. The simulation ranging results indicate that the relative errors of different fault distances under different fault types are less than 1.83 %, which indicates that the power cable fault location approach based on wavelet transform modulus maxima theory can easily detect the singular points of fault signals, remove the interference caused by traveling wave velocity effectively, and improve the accuracy of cable fault location.

title: A Multi-Star Astronomical Positioning Method Based on BFGS with Backtracking Line Search
authors: Yulin Wu, Tao He, Xianyi Li, Chao Zhuo, Lei Ren, Xiaoxuan Lin
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Astronomical positioning is a critical technology for autonomous navigation. To address the inherent limitations of geometric ambiguity and initialization sensitivity in traditional multi-star positioning, we propose a robust multi-star positioning framework based on a star vector model. We formulate the positioning problem as a constrained least-squares optimization problem within the Cartesian coordinate system and investigate two numerical solving methods, specifically Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and the Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (BFGS) Quasi-Newton method equipped with backtracking line search. While PSO offers global search capabilities without the need for initial position information, the proposed BFGS algorithm effectively solves the non-linear multi-star equations by approximating the inverse Hessian matrix to utilize second-order curvature information and incorporating backtracking line search to ensure global convergence. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to the PSO method, the BFGS approach achieves significantly higher positioning accuracy and computational efficiency, providing a robust solution for high-precision autonomous astronomical navigation.

title: Cross Talk in Multiplexed Continuous-Variable Quantum Key Distribution
authors: Olena Kovalenko, Vladyslav Usenko
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We address the role of linear inter-mode cross talk in continuous-variable quantum key distribution in the most typical prepare-and-measure scenario. Considering coherent and well as squeezed signal states and either homodyne or heterodyne detection in the receiver station, we show how the cross talk deteriorates the secret key rate and robustness of the protocols to the channel noise. We show how optimization of the signal modulation can improve the performance of the protocols in the presence of cross talk and point out possibilities for further cross talk compensation.

title: Machine Learning-Based Typhoid Prediction Using Clinical Data
authors: Amit Kumar Ghosh, Shahriar Marjan, Rejowan Arifin Nayeem, S.M. Abdullah Al Muhib, Md. Hasan Imam Bijoy, M. Humayet Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: The paper investigates the issue of early diagnosis of typhoid fever with the help of a machine learning procedure based on the clinical and demographic data. Such preprocessing of the data was based on which a number of models were trained and assessed. Gradient Boosting turned out to be the most efficient model due to the given 100 per cent accuracy and the totally accurate multiple classes. The results indicate that machine learning and, in particular, the ensemble methods could act as an effective and efficient diagnostic instrument in a low-resource environment. Such method can be an alternative and cheaper to use as the traditional diagnostics. They still need to be externally validated using other datasets, though, to be sure of generalizability.

title: Applying Mission Engineering to Counter-Unmanned Aircraft Systems Missions
authors: Shannon Dubicki, Risa Gorospe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) are changing modern warfare due to their rapidly evolving tactics. Small UAS are inexpensive and increasingly capable, which when paired with Counter-UAS (C-UAS) statutory authorities, create a complex mission space in United States homeland defense and theater defense. Both technical and non-technical solutions are required to defend against UAS. Due to the complexity of the C-UAS mission space, mission engineering is needed to adequately explore the problem space and to recommend solution sets to stakeholders that consider their individual constraints and needs. This paper captures observations from applying mission engineering to $\mathbf{C}$ UAS missions. The engineers followed the United States Department of War’s Mission Engineering Guide (MEG) Version 2.0 and noted any deviations. The deviations span various aspects of mission engineering such as mission stakeholder adjudication, characterization of the adversary, and modularity of the solution architectures. From this C-UAS mission engineering effort and the noted deviations from the MEG, seven recommendations to update the MEG are provided.

title: Extending Repeater Spacing in All-Photonic Quantum Repeaters
authors: Ryosuke Shiina, Kenneth Goodenough, Filip Rozpędek
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Quantum communication between two distant parties will serve as a cornerstone of the future quantum internet. However, many existing quantum communication schemes face a critical bottleneck: the need to generate a sufficient number of entangled Bell pairs over long distances. Although photons are ideal carriers of quantum information, overcoming photon loss and the exponential attenuation of signals remains a major challenge. We propose an all-photonic quantum repeater architecture that enables quantum communication over a distance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,000 \text{km}$</tex> with equidistant repeater spacing extended to 9 km -approximately 2 to 6 times longer than in previously proposed all-photonic schemes. This extended repeater spacing arises from the combined use of elementary entangled Bell pairs protected by both continuous-variable and discrete-variable quantum error correction codes-namely, the bosonic Gottesman-Kitaev-Preskill (GKP) code and the [[7], [1], [3]] Steane code-whose combination yields a synergistic effect that surpasses a simple additive improvement, enhancing robustness against photon loss. This architecture incorporates a new ranking criterion and a multi-reflection mirror-based optical cavity as a free-space photonic memory module, which we model in terms of its length and mirror-reflection efficiency. To more accurately capture realistic physical conditions during photonic resource preparation, we model the presence of switching-induced imperfections by introducing an optical switch into the simulation, in addition to commonly considered imperfections such as finite GKP squeezing, finite detection efficiency, and finite fiber coupling efficiency. In the presence of these imperfections, our realization requires fewer than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 8 0 0}$</tex> GKP qubits per repeater station per protocol run-a value significantly smaller than in prior all-photonic proposals.

title: Evaluating Machine Learning Methods for DDoS Detection in IDS
authors: Latha R, Caleb S, A.Marimuthu, Sivagami S, Senthilvadivu S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In recent years, the number of cyber threats experienced and their complexity especially DDoS attacks have been on the rise. Usually, traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) based on rule-based or signature-based approach cannot detect new or changing patterns of DDoS attacks. In dealing with this shortcoming, the paper considers some of the machine learning (ML) methods to achieve a better detection rate, flexibility and real-time performance within IDS. We perform a comparative analysis of five supervised learning models: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN). The models are evaluated using three benchmark datasets. There is normalization, class balancing, and Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) which is used to reduce dimensionality and feature selection within the data preprocessing pipeline. The evaluation of model performance is based on the standard evaluation measure, real-time measure of detection latency and false positive rate. It is clear that XGBoost is the best performing model with a detection rate of 98.1%, F1-score of 0.95 and the lowest latency of 25 milliseconds executed in a simulated real-time environment. Moreover, feature selection method was used, which reduced train time by up to 42 percent at a cost affordable to the performance, making it effective in resource-limited environments. All in all, the investigation enriches insight into ML-based IDS and proves the feats of XGBoost as a highly effective and practical model to use in detection of DDoS attacks in the design of intelligent real time deterrence of threats.

title: A Hybrid Edge-Cloud IoT Framework for Real-Time Plant Disease Detection using Multimodal Data Fusion
authors: K. Rama Gangi Reddy, K.S Thirunavakkarasu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The rising rate of plant diseases poses a significant risk to global agriculture, leading to reduced yield and financial loss. The current approaches of disease detection are through manual visual inspection, or processing using cloud images, with all methods struggling with latency, scalability and power consumption limitations; especially in remote rural areas. This work presents a new, light-weight, and effective IoT-based system called SmartSense+, which is aimed for real-time detection of plant diseases using hybrid edge-cloud infrastructure and multimodal data fusion. The system incorporates NodeMCU and ESP32-CAM modules for image acquisition at the edge level and for monitoring environmental parameters such as temperature, humidity, and soil moisture. By using adaptive thresholding and light-weight feature extraction processes on the edge device, only the necessary data is sent through MQTT protocol to a cloud server. The cloud layer uses a Decision Tree classifier with decision-level data fusion augmented features, which are combined with sensor data to enhance the prediction capability. Experimental results validate that the devised system provides a detection accuracy of 92.3% with considerably smaller data transmission size and latency in comparison with traditional cloud-only and TinyML techniques. In addition, the system facilitates real-time alerts and includes an easy-to-use mobile and web dashboard for farm surveillance. The contribution of this paper is a scalable, energy-efficient, and cost-effective solution optimized for small and medium-scale farms. The suggested hybrid IoT platform is highly promising for solving actual agricultural problems by facilitating early and precise detection of plant diseases to protect plant health and develop more sustainable agriculture.

title: Organizational Efficiency: The Role of SHRP and Technology Integration in Driving Performance
authors: Belal Shneikat, Khaled M. Alhyasat, Hamza Elrehail, Sharon Mendoza-Dreisbach, Abdallah Mohammad Ta’amneh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study focuses on SHRP and technology integration as the source of organizational efficiency and performance in the scenario of rapidly changing organizational environment, especially in digitally driven sectors. This research uses a quantitative research design aimed at the Information Technology (IT) industry in Abu Dhabi, UAE, where organizations are actively undergoing digital transformation and complex workforce situations. A structured online questionnaire was used to collect data, which were analyzed using SmartPLS on 290 valid responses to investigate direct, indirect and mediating relationships. Results showed that SHRP, technology integration and organizational efficiency had positive and statistically significant relationships. Additionally, SHRP was found to moderate the relationship with efficiency through technology integration, and the synergy is obvious. The strong implications of these findings for HR leaders and organizational strategists are that HR investing in digital HR systems and collaboration between the HR and IT functions and the adoption of integrated strategic planning practices are necessary for higher performance outcomes. What the study points out is that organizational efficiency is not any more determined by individual practices but requires a coordinated strategy based on the combined use of human capital and technology as interdependent assets.

title: Decentralized Deepfake Detection: Feature-Aligned Federated Learning with Multi-Backbone Fusion
authors: R. Geetha, Jayanthi. V, G.R.Priyasri, P. Geetha, Kavitha. T
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Digital security besides media forensics now face a noteworthy difficulty in detecting fabricated faces due to the spread of deepfake technologies and facial forgery techniques. In order to identify face forgeries while protecting user privacy, this research presents a new-fangled framework named FFDL, which stands for Feature Fusion- Deep Learning utilising Federated Learning. This approach creates a privacy-aware, decentralised detection scheme by combining federated learning (FL) multi-source feature fusion. The model extracts strong visual features on client side by utilising the Inception-v3 and Xception architectures. Prior to being fed into a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifier, these characteristics are fused using Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA) to boost their correlation besides discriminative capacities. By keeping all raw data inside the client environment during model training with the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) protocol, can guarantee that your data will remain private. In validate the experiments using FaceForensics++ and WildDeepfake for cross-dataset generalisation, Deepforensics-1.0 dataset was used. Outperforming both traditional centralised and non-fused models, the suggested FFDL model attained a peak accuracy of 98.99%, precision of 98.80%, recall of 98.85%, besides F1-score of 98.82%. Results from ablation studies validated the importance of each component, besides model's consistency in performance across new datasets proved its durability. In addition, the training and validation AUC-ROC scores showed that the classification capability was almost flawless. The efficacy of federated, fused-feature deep learning in attaining accuracy, scalability, and privacy is demonstrated in this paper. -supporting the identification of fake faces, a crucial step in the development of trustworthy methods of authenticating media and verifying identities in practical settings.

title: Smart Cradle: An IoT-Based System for Intelligent Infant Monitoring
authors: Adithiyaa D, Lakshhmi Narayanan T, Gokulakkrishna Saravanan, Parvathy A K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an IoT-based smart monitoring system that is fitted onto a baby's cradle and performs predefined, automated actions to help keep the baby safe and to help soothe the baby without the physical intervention of caregivers. An ESP32 microcontroller is used as the main controller to track key environmental conditions. The BME680 sensor monitors air quality and temperature and soil moisture sensor is used to detect diaper wetness. Baby crying is identified using a sound sensor. The system is triggered automatically whenever any sensors crosses its set threshold limit and as a response it performs one of the following actions, which includes switching on the fan, swinging the cradle using a servo motor, sending alerts to the caregiver, and playing lullabies to calm the baby. Also, to help caregivers monitor the baby remotely at any time, an ESP32 CAM module is used to stream live video of the cradle. The system is designed so that it can be operated through manual remote control in the Blynk platform, and even a web dashboard is provided for real-time data monitoring. Overall, this work presents a complete and practical childcare solution by combining embedded hardware with cloud-based IoT technology.

title: AI-Generated Fake News Detection Using a Multimodal Approach on the MiRAGeNews Dataset
authors: Tamil Selvan V P, Vignesh S, Siranjeevi Rajamanickam, Balasubramanian Palani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: As social platforms evolve and AI-generated visuals become very common, the risk of fake news spreading increases, impacting public opinion and sentiments. Most of the current fake news detection methods relying solely on text overlook important visual cues, which can also be manipulated using AI. In this work, a multimodal fake news detection approach using the MiRAGeNews dataset is introduced that integrates both textual and visual features through advanced deep learning techniques. Social media now spreads stories faster than ever, and AI can create lifelike pictures and convincing captions that fool people in seconds. To fight this, an AI-generated fake news detector is built that checks words and images together. The model is trained on the MiRAGeNews set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2, 0 0 0}$</tex> posts made from real news and AI-made look-alike photos from Midjourney and captions written by GPT-4. The model tries three ways to mix what it reads and sees: a simple merge, Concatenation layer on RoBERTa + ViT acheives 0.985 accuracy that lets text and picture features interact, and a deeper bilinear blend. In every test, using both text and images worked better than using only one, helping to catch fake news more often. This tool aims to help people spot AIgenerated fake news and to keep public news and conversations honest.

title: Open-Set Domain Generalization for Hyperspectral Image Classification via Evidential Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Amirreza Khoshbakht, Erchan Aptoula
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Open-set domain generalization for hyperspectral image classification tackles challenges from both unknown classes and cross-domain generalization without target adaptation. We propose a framework that extracts domain-agnostic features through attention-weighted frequency analysis, while capturing complementary spectral-spatial information via parallel pathways. It provides reliable open-set classification through uncertainty-aware pathway weighting. Experiments on Pavia University and Centre indicate performance levels comparable to state-of-the-art domain adaptation methods despite not requiring target domain access during training.

title: House Price Prediction Algorithms and Model based on Machine Learning
authors: B. Naveen Kumar, Kunta Mallik Raj, Podishetti Uday Kumar, Chanda Gopi Reddy, Mohammed Zia Azeem
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This project presents a machine learning-based solution for predicting house prices with high accuracy. It utilizes advanced algorithms such as Linear Regression, Decision Trees, and Random Forest to build a robust and intelligent system capable of estimating property values based on a range of input features. These features include key elements such as the property's location, total area, number of bedrooms and bathrooms, and amenities like parking space, gardens, proximity to schools, and hospitals. The model is trained using historical real estate data, which helps it learn trends in the housing market and understand how different variables affect property prices. By analysing this data, the machine learning algorithms can uncover complex patterns and relationships that might be missed using conventional methods. Each algorithm adds value in its own way: Linear Regression provides a straightforward and interpretable model, while Decision Trees and Random Forests are effective in modelling non-linear relationships and feature interactions. This predictive system proves beneficial to a wide range of users in the real estate industry. Potential homebuyers can assess whether a property's asking price is fair, and sellers can identify optimal pricing strategies. Real estate agents and professionals also gain from this data-driven approach, which supports accurate valuations and enhances client services. In essence, this system brings more transparency and efficiency to property transactions, allowing stakeholders to make informed decisions backed by data analytics.

title: PWM-Based Multi-Mode Converter for DC-AC and Isolated Renewable Energy and EV Applications
authors: Sowmyashree M S, K. Bindu Madhavi, G. Meenakshi Sundaram, A.Sagai Francis Britto, Deepa Mathew K, Natrayan L
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: A pulse-width - modulated (PWM) multi - mode converter has been developed. It makes the system capable to work in DC-AC mode or isolated mode and also it supports renewable and electric vehicle (EV) applications. This converter has three modes: separate mode, inverter mode and chopper mode. By changing between each mode, the reverse flow of current between the DC and AC terminals is controlled and the energy transfer is maximised. Due to the easy changeability of the operation mode, the voltage gain and efficiency are stable. The converter supplies 2000 W of power with an efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 9 {\%}}$</tex> and a maximum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 4 {\%}}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 3 {\%}}$</tex> total harmonic distortion (THD). It has two DC inputs, which are 100 V and 200 V. These inputs produce a constant AC output of 300 V Tests in grid connected and off grid environment confirms its effectiveness. Simulation and experimental data demonstrate the performance slightly better than conventional converters whose THD varies from 6.8% to 18.3% and its efficiency equals 95.7%. The PWM-based control allows a better thermal stability and electromagnetic compatibility, since less switching losses and also the uniform current flow are achieved. The design consists of a wide operating range, reduced number of passive components, and a one stage architecture. Chuck favors the ground source renewable hybrid system and EV charging station, which needs to be compact, bidirectional, and isolated.

title: HS-ANET: Star Spectral Type Enhanced Astrometric Calibration for Hyper Spectral Space Imaging
authors: William Mitchell, Kevin Phan, David Chaparro, Enrique De Alba, J. Zachary Gazak
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Traditional lost-in-space algorithms, such as those implemented in astrometry.net, solve for spacecraft orientation by matching observed star fields to celestial catalogs using geometric asterisms alone. In this work, we propose a novel extension to astrometry.net that incorporates stellar spectral type, which is derived from hyperspectral imagery, into the matching process. By adding this spectral dimension to each star detection, we constrain the search space and improve match specificity, enabling successful astrometric solutions with significantly fewer stars. Our modified pipeline demonstrates improved fit rates and reduced failure cases in cluttered or ambiguous star fields, which is especially critical for autonomous space situational awareness and traffic management. Our results suggest that modest spectral resolution, when incorporated into existing geometric frameworks, can dramatically improve robustness and efficiency in onboard star identification systems.

title: Spectral Fidelity Aware Hyperspectral Image Denoising via Unsupervised Triple Attention with Endmember Preservation
authors: Mohith Ram Sai Karthik Phalgun Kothamasu, Satya Sai Ramesh P, Arun P V
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) denoising is essential for improving data quality in remote sensing applications. While recent deep learning approaches achieve strong noise suppression, they often distort spectral fidelity, which is critical for tasks such as material identification and unmixing. In this work, we propose an unsupervised deep learning framework that introduces a novel Endmember Preservation Loss (EPL) to explicitly safeguard pure spectral signatures during denoising. The framework combines spatial-spectral feature modeling with both loss-based and architectural mechanisms for spectral consistency. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that incorporating EPL significantly improves spectral angle metrics while maintaining competitive PSNR and SSIM, enabling effective denoising without compromising spectral integrity.

title: Video-to-Task Learning via Motion-Guided Attention for Few-Shot Action Recognition
authors: Hanyu Guo, Suzhou Que, Wanchuan Yu, Kaiwen Du, Yan Yan, Hanzi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: In recent years, few-shot action recognition has achieved remarkable performance through spatio-temporal relation modeling. Although many spatial and temporal alignment modules have been proposed, they primarily address spatial or temporal misalignments at the video level, while the spatio-temporal relationships across different videos at the task level remain underexplored. Recent studies use class prototypes to learn task-specific features but overlook these cross-video relationships— especially in the spatial dimension, which offers rich information. In this paper, we propose a novel Dual Motion-Guided Attention Learning method (called DMGAL) for fewshot action recognition, aiming to capture the spatio-temporal relationships from the video-specific level to the task-specific level. To achieve this, we propose a carefully designed Motion-Guided Attention (MGA) method to identify and correlate motion-related region features across levels. Specifically, the Self Motion-Guided Attention module (S-MGA) achieves spatio-temporal relation modeling at the video level by identifying and correlating motion-related region features between different frames within a video. The Cross Motion-Guided Attention module (C-MGA) identifies and correlates motion-related region features between frames of different videos within a specific task to achieve spatio-temporal relationships at the task level. Such a way enables the model to construct class prototypes that fully incorporate spatio-temporal relationships from the video-specific level to the task-specific level. We validate the effectiveness of our DMGAL method by employing both fully fine-tuning and adapter-tuning paradigms. The models developed using these paradigms are yielding DMGAL-FT and DMGAL-Adapter, respectively. Extensive experiments demonstrate that both our DMGAL-FT and DMGAL-Adapter models achieve favorable results against other state-of-the-art methods on five few-shot action recognition benchmarks. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/ReverseSuzhou/DMGAL.

title: Vision Transformer Integrated with Shift Operation for Brain Tumor Detection and Classification
authors: Gnanaprakash V, Manu Raju, Sarasu R, Saranya N, Jeen Retna Kumar R, K. Harsha Sai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In order to diagnose neurological problems, organize treatments, and keep track of patients, it is essential to identify and separate brain malignancies from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) data. Brain tumors are responsible for one out of every four cancer-related deaths, making cancer the second most frequent cause of death globally. Prompt therapy can result from an accurate and fast diagnosis. The quick development of image categorization in recent days has improved Computer-assisted diagnostics. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are the most widely used neural network models for image classification. However, it has trouble in accurately identifying the lesion's focal location, its efficacy is limited. This paper develops a unique brain tumor classification model by combining shift operation with visual transformer to address the aforementioned challenges. Highly accurate brain tumor diagnoses are being made using deep learning techniques. Computed tomography, ultrasound, and X-ray imaging are less successful than MRI, which is the gold standard for machine vision-based brain tumor diagnosis. In medical image processing, deep learning models like CNN as well as sophisticated architectures like Vision Transformers (ViT) and hybrid models have shown impressive results. According to the result, deep learning models can greatly increase the performance and speed of diagnosing brain tumors, which will lessen the workload for radiologists. But in order to train the model, a lot of parameters are required. In order to address this, the vision transformer's attention mechanism is replaced with a shift operation. Its sole function is the exchange of a little percentage of channels across adjacent features; it lacks parameters and arithmetic calculations. Building on this straightforward process, ShiftViT model is suggested as a base network that substitutes shift operations for the attention layers seen in ViT.

title: Optimal Pre and Post-Silicon Validation Strategy for Vision-Centric ASIC Platforms
authors: Kranti kumar Thota
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The emergence of vision-oriented ASIC platforms requires stringent validation solutions to achieve functional accuracy and performance stability to the various real world workloads. Pre- and post-silicon validation stages are both important but limited due to the costly nature of computing them, and the time-intensive nature of their process. Conventional exhaustive testing methods find it difficult to effectively model corner-case behavior in complex vision workloads causing more design cycles and slow time-to-market. This work presents best-fit validation system to combine a gated dual-branch attention (GDBAtt) mechanism with the improved groundwater flow optimization (EGFO) algorithm to focus more on the critical test scenarios and fasten coverage-based verification. The modeling workload behavior, finding instruction and data patterns, and optimizing the process of pre-silicon simulation and post-silicon test vectors selection is modeled using a deep starlike neural network (DSNN). Performance on benchmark vision workloads has shown 8x (up to) improvement in simulation and test time, and 95.8 % coverage of edge-case behaviors. The findings affirm the subset sustains operational variety and identifies functional aberration, halving verification effort, cutting design periods and enhancing ASIC reliability.

title: TriCA-Net: A Tri-Context Adaptive MoE Framework for Brain Metastases Segmentation Using Multimodal MRIs
authors: Ameer Hamza, Robertas Damaševičius, Muhammad Attique Khan, John Abbas, Wardah Ibrar
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Metastases are the most frequent form of intracranial tumor impacting adults and are the greatest challenge to clinical practice due to their multifocal characteristics and heterogeneous appearance in MRIs. Accurate, reproducible segmentation of metastatic lesions is critical for facilitating diagnosis, treatment planning, and post-therapy assessment. However, segmenting brain metastases remains a major challenge owing to the extreme variability in size, shape, boundary uncertainty, and spatial dispersion across patients. To address these challenges, we proposed TriCA-Net, a Tri-Context Adaptive Expert Network based on a heterogeneous MoE framework for multi label segmentation. For the experiments, BratsMet2023 and BratsMet2025 are used, and the proposed method achieved the highest dice scores of 90.66% and 72.14%, respectively. Moreover, the proposed framework has an inference time of 106.5 ms and outperforms several state-of-the-art architectures in both performance and computational efficiency.

title: QuCumber: Wavefunction Reconstruction with Neural Networks
authors: Aditya Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: As the field of quantum technology continues to evolve, accurate quantum state preparation is becoming increasingly important for various applications in materials, matter, and devices. Computational methods allow the reconstruction of quantum states from experimental data, but the increasing complexity of quantum systems demands continuous algorithmic innovations. In this paper, we introduce QuCumber, an open-source software package designed to reconstruct quantum states using machine learning techniques. QuCumber leverages restricted Boltzmann machines (RBMs) to represent quantum wavefunctions based on projective measurements. By utilizing RBMs, QuCumber efficiently generates new measurements that facilitate the calculation of physical observables, some of which may not be directly accessible through experimental data alone. This paper demonstrates QuCumber’s capability to handle both real and complex wavefunctions, providing valuable tools for advancing quantum state analysis in line with the growing scale of quantum systems.

title: Real-Time GR-Based Shoulder Dislocation Risk and Strength Monitoring System Using Arduino for Rehabilitation and Preventive Care
authors: Neeththi Aadithiya B, Nivasan K, Praveen Kumar K, Nithishkumar M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: One of the most recurrent musculoskeletal injuries is shoulder dislocation, which, if not identified quickly, can result in pain, instability, and long-term issues. Expensive imaging procedures like MRIs and X-rays are a major part of current diagnostic procedures, but they are not always practicable for ongoing monitoring or preventive care. In the presented research a non-invasive, portable shoulder dislocation monitoring device that tracks abnormal muscle activity around the shoulder joint using surface Electromyography (sEMG) sensors is thus proposed. The presented system identifies the dislocation, weariness, and strain by using RMS-based threshold detection. The analysis of the real-time recorded signals is processed via a Raspberry Pi, which is actually amplified and digitalized using an ADC. The proposed method can be readily utilized by athletes and can be utilized in physiotherapy clinics and rehabilitation centers as the system is user friendly. Overall the Proposed system will be an alternate solution for the expensive scans and will promote the earlier detection.

title: Distribution Network Disaster Prediction Based on Adaptive Random Forest Algorithm under Extreme Weather
authors: Jinfeng Ye, Bin Wu, Xuan Lin, Chaoyang Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: To address the insufficient accuracy of distribution network disaster prediction under frequent extreme weather events, this paper proposes a prediction method based on Adaptive Random Forest (ARF). First, historical distribution network operation data under extreme weather events are cleaned and standardized, and key input variables are selected through correlation analysis. Then, an ARF model with dynamic adjustment capabilities is constructed. Its core lies in online optimization of the number and depth of decision trees based on changes in the distribution of input data, thereby improving the model's adaptability to non-stationary and high-noise environments. The model training employs a time-series cross-validation strategy to balance generalization ability and stability. Experiments on distribution network data show that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 92.3 %, a recall of 89.7 %, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score of 90.8 %, significantly outperforming mainstream models such as XGBoost and LightGBM. Furthermore, it demonstrates excellent temporal stability and robustness under extreme events of varying intensities, including typhoons and rainstorms. This research confirms that the adaptive mechanism of ARF can accurately capture dynamic fault modes, providing reliable technical support for improving the resilience and emergency response capabilities of distribution networks.

title: Low-Area Fused Floating-Point Four-Term Adder
authors: Thiruvenkadam Krishnan, Parthibaraj Anguraj, Barkunan SR, Saravanan Subramanian, Vivek Karthick Perumal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Floating-point (FP) logic is used in the computation field. Among different operations, FP addition has been studied more in recent years. This paper deals with an area-efficient architecture for Fused FP Four-Term (FFPF-T) addition. In the proposed design, a modified exponent processing unit and a new Significand Alignment (SA) block are used. A simple adder is applied for Mantissa Addition (MA) to perform the computation. The existing method is extended to support FFPF-T addition. The efficiency of the designs is evaluated using the Altera Quartus II simulation tool. The implementation outcome indicates that the proposed design requires less hardware area compared to the existing schemes.

title: SecureEndoNet: An Encryption-Enabled, Transfer Learning–Based Approach for Endoscopic Image Classification
authors: Ahmmad Musha, Ahsanul Islam, Tonmoy Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: The use of deep learning models on endoscopic images for detecting gastrointestinal diseases presents a major privacy challenge, as it requires processing sensitive patient data and risks the unauthorized disclosure of personal health information. To address this issue, we propose SecureEndoNet, a transfer learning–based framework compatible with homomorphic encryption for privacy-preserving endoscopic image classification. SecureEndoNet employs the CKKS scheme to encrypt client data during inference and utilizes a pre-trained VGG-19 model for feature extraction. Experiments conducted on the publicly available KVASIR v2 dataset, which contains eight classes, demonstrate that the framework achieves identical classification accuracy on both plaintext and ciphertext inputs (90.125%), with an inference time of 1.25 seconds per encrypted sample. Moreover, VGG-19 provides the fastest inference speed while achieving over 90% accuracy, outperforming other pretrained models including DenseNet-201, MobileNetV2, ResNet50, and EfficientNetV2B0.

title: Simulation-Based Dynamic Spectrum Management Using Deep Q-Learning for Cognitive Radios
authors: Aryan Routray, Aishik Tokdar, Abhijay Dhar, Nirmala Paramanandham
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The ever-increasing demands of wireless communications and the rapid proliferation of wireless devices have made the efficient and dependable use of the radio spectrum more important than ever. Unlike traditional Cognitive Radio (CR) systems, which often do not have adaptive decision-making for dynamic, real-time management of the spectrum under conditions of unpredictable interference and uncertain channel occupancy, this paper addresses the problem with an autonomous and self-adjusting Cognitive Radio framework which adapts spectrum access based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) through simulated environments. For this purpose, the designed system incorporates a Deep Q-Network (DQN) model, which derives optimal policies associated with channel access based on the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), the channel occupancy history, and estimated interference probabilities. The system models realistic conditions of primary user (PU) activity, channel fading, and interference in a dynamically simulated environment, enabling the DQN agent to learn by doing. The proposed model outperforms nonadaptive and rule-based approaches in terms of spectrum use, latency, and overall service quality (QoS). The simulation-based framework provides a solid convergence point for the anticipated hardware integration in next generation wireless networks.

title: GN2T: A Hybrid Graph Neural and Tree-Structured Architecture for ECG-Based Arrhythmia Recognition
authors: Ashraful Goni, Rubiat Jahan Momtahina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) arrhythmia classification remains challenging due to heterogeneous signal morphology and overlooked inter-feature dependencies in conventional models. This study introduces GN2T, a hybrid framework that combines correlation-driven graph convolution with tree-structured hierarchical learning. Graph-based adjacency construction captures physiological feature relationships, while CNN-RF hybrid nodes refine temporal patterns through gating mechanisms. Multi-head attention integrates multi-scale representations, enhancing the detection of subtle arrhythmia variations. Tested on four PhysioNet databases—MIT-BIH Arrhythmia, MIT-BIH Supraventricular, INCART 12-lead, and Sudden Cardiac Death Holter—GN2T achieved 98.48%–98.99% accuracy with precision above 98.70%. Comparative results show improvements of up to +2.38% over Random Forest and +9.19% over CNN, while maintaining cross-dataset stability under 1.48% variance. GN2T demonstrates strong potential for robust, clinically reliable arrhythmia detection across diverse populations.

title: UniRelease: A Unified Framework for Addressing Cross-Platform Mobile Application Build and Release Challenges
authors: Satyanarayana Gudimetla, Suresh Gangula, Sudhamadhuri Buhyavarapu
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Cross-platform mobile frameworks (Flutter, React Native, Kotlin Multiplatform) enable shared-codebase development, yet build and release engineering remains fragmented and poorly understood. This paper reports a mixed-methods study drawing on 4,827 CI/CD-related issues mined from 12 open-source projects and three framework meta-repositories, survey responses from 156 practitioners, and semi-structured interviews with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3}$</tex> practitioners. We identified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 7}$</tex> recurring challenges organized into six categories. Motivated by these findings, we developed UniRelease, a framework addressing the highest-impact categories through three mechanisms: UniConfig (declarative build configuration), UniTest (differential behavioral testing), and UniCoord (release coordination). In evaluation on eight end-user applications-four from industry partners and four open-source-spanning all three frameworks, UniConfig reduced configuration drift by 68.5 % (95% CI: [62.1%, 74.9%]), UniTest detected 45.3% of platform-divergent bugs in retrospective analysis (95 % CI: [37.3%, 53.3%]) with prospective deployment confirming 83.9 % precision across 42 release cycles, and UniCoord reduced coordination overhead by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1 \times(\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>, Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}=2.72)$</tex> across 52 release cycles at four partner organizations. These results provide evidence that empirically-grounded tooling can reduce the engineering burden of cross-platform releases.

title: Intelligent Robotic Waste Segregation using YOLOv11 for Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Doha Masrour, Yassine Salih-Alj
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: With the rapid increase in global waste generation, efficient segregation remains a critical challenge due to limited automation and reliance on manual labor. This paper presents the design and development of an intelligent waste segregation system that integrates a six-degree-of-freedom (6DOF) robotic manipulator with the YOLOv11 Nano object detection algorithm for real-time classification and sorting. The YOLOv11 Nano network is trained to identify seven waste categories: paper, plastic, metal, glass, cardboard, and textile, with optimized inference speed and detection accuracy. The robotic arm, modeled using Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) parameters, executes inverse kinematic control for precise pick-and-place operations. By integrating the state-of-the-art YOLOv11 Nano vision-based detection model with a fully articulated 6DOF robotic manipulator, the proposed design enhances the automation, accuracy, and safety of waste segregation operations.

title: Optimal Mass Transport Based Room Geometry Estimation with Acoustic Consistency Constraints
authors: R Yazhini, S Muthu Vasanth, Sudheesh P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Room geometry estimation from acoustic signals is a fundamental problem in spatial audio processing, indoor localization and room acoustics. In existing literature, most solutions are dependent on peak picking, approximate models of acoustic signal propagation and supervised learning; however, the performance of these models is influenced by model assumptions and data dependencies. Therefore, it is necessary to develop methods that minimize data-dependent assumptions in order to create algorithms that can generalize effectively. In this paper, we propose a novel solution that uses Optimal Mass Transport (OMT) applied to Room Impulse Responses (RIRs). The RIR energy is normalized, which, when subjected to pairwise OMT-based temporal shift modelling, identifies the subtle acoustic energy diffusion differences that exist in room geometries. Expected delay differences are predicted using an image source-based geometric model, accounting for both direct and reflected propagation paths. An intelligent objective function matches OMT-derived delays with geometry-based predictions while adaptively weighting microphone pairs according to their sensitivity to room length, width, and height. Room geometry is estimated using a two-frame network that combines Differential Evolution (DE) with a Basin Hopping (BH) search technique, optimizing the non-convex problem arising from geometric room size identification. The proposed solution is non-linear, training-free, and does not rely on explicit peak picking, making it robust to noise and multipath effects. Experimental simulations on different room configurations demonstrate that the proposed method estimates the room length, width, and height with percentage errors of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.77 \%, 8.33 {\%}$</tex>, and 11.68%, respectively.

title: Conversational AI in Medicine: Exploring the Role of Chatbots in Modern Healthcare
authors: Suprabhat Adhikari, Sarthak Patra, Rishab Shaw, Shweta Maurya, Tathagata Chatterjee, Jayita Pal
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The rapid development of artificial intelligence has had a significant effect on the healthcare sector and on the transformation of conventional healthcare practices. Among the numerous advances in the field of artificial intelligence, chatbots represent a feasible substitute to encourage patient participation and support professionals in healthcare. These sagacious systems are used for tasks such as rapid patient selection, personalized treatment recommendations and suggestions, initial diagnosis, and ongoing monitoring of the treatment pathway. In recent years, chatbots have played a significant role within the healthcare industry. This paper analyzes the state of the art in this field and considers some prospects in terms of many practical features. Moreover, this paper outlines several major drawbacks and difficulties restricting its widespread application. This article examines these factors, highlighting the challenges of integrating these approaches with traditional healthcare systems, concerns related to data privacy, limitations in natural language understanding, and the necessary integration mechanisms. In order to provide an honest picture, this paper provides a sample chatbot architecture for a healthcare application and demonstrates how it works using representative input-output interactions. The aim of this comprehensive overview is to inform researchers and practitioners about the present and future aspects of chatbot technology in healthcare services.

title: Plant Health Monitoring System of Potato Images: Improved Alexnet Approach
authors: Anchal Kumawat, Biswaranjan Acharya, N. Venkatesh, Pardhu Thottempudi, Rabinarayan Satpathy
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The agricultural industry encounters substantial obstacles in preserving crop well-being and productivity owing to plant diseases and unfavorable climatic conditions. To address these issues, we employ Deep Learning (DL) techniques to detect unhealthy plant leaves and diagnose diseases. Our approach leverages pre-trained CNN model includes the basic AlexNet model to identify disease. Our study explores the application of DL techniques to the Plant Village dataset, employing an 80:20 training-testing split and implementing data augmentation and preprocessing strategies to optimize model performance. The results demonstrate the superiority of the IAlexNet model over other AlexNet architectures, highlighting the potential of transfer learning in plant disease diagnosis. By automating disease detection, this approach can help reduce manual labor and improve crop yields, contributing to more efficient agricultural practices.

title: Multimodal Agentic RAG: A Hybrid Framework for Intelligent Extraction and Adaptive Retrieval on Unstructured Data Streams
authors: D. Karthika Renuka, L. Ashok Kumar, Ms. N. Deepika, Ms. K. S. Thaarugeshwari, Ms. Sriya Santhanam, Ms. K. Tejasri, Mr. N. Harisindhu, Ms. P. S. Preethi, Ms. A. K. Sangamithra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: In the context of enterprise environments, the proliferation of complex, unstructured data such as multi-column PDFs, as well as videos, is an impediment to proper information systems, especially since RAG systems have difficulty retrieving such data due to semantic flattening. In light of such challenges, the Multimodal Agentic RAG framework has been developed, moving beyond passive information retrieval towards active information orchestration. In the architecture, we have introduced three innovations: The Intelligent Extractor, which relies on Large Multimodal Models to leverage structural parsing and keyframe captioning; the Hybrid Chunking strategy, which upholds the integrity of visual objects; and the Agentic Orchestrator, which uses the Plan-Execute-Reflect workflow to self-correct the information retrieval in real-time. In the test conducted on the M2RAG and the enterprise data, the framework yielded high scores: 82 for context precision and 89 for multimodal faithfulness, far superior to the static baseline, validating the importance of agentic reasoning in information systems.

title: Machine Learning in Electric Vehicle Consumption Modelling
authors: Kritika Paliwal, Pradeep Jha, Suman Kumari, Vibhor Vaish, Nikhil Vishwakarma, Agamya Bansal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The accuracy of electric vehicles (EV's) comes down to how accurate your simulation models are, that's the bottom line. Conventional models based on mathematics often fail when faced with nonlinear or chaotic problems and unfortunately there are many such problems experienced in real life on the road. In this paper we take another tack. We combine Machine Learning (ML) algorithms with conventional simulation models in MATLAB/Simulink so as to achieve much greater accuracy in predictions and refinements. Using some actual driving data we used Random Forest Regression (RFR) and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) to estimate key parameters, namely state of charge of battery, energy consumption, efficiency of motor, etc. The result of this ML-Simulink combination was that prediction accuracy was increased by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-12 {\%}$</tex> on the successful simulation models compared with basic physical models. In other words by infusing ML into the procedure we produced simulation models which were considerably more flexible than conventional methods when used in a realistic situations, besides producing a saving in computation.

title: Smart Irrigation Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Alaa Yahya Aref Ahmad, Mohammad K. S. Ma’aitah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Effective water management in agriculture is imperative for the sustainable and judicious utilization of resources, particularly in areas grappling with the scarcity of water. Conventional irrigation techniques that are inclined towards over-irrigating or under-irrigating can terribly degrade crop productivity and proper resource-saving. This paper investigates a new AI outlook to produce a smart irrigation forecasting program that optimizes water consumption based on real-time environmental data. In this project, the program is put to the test using combinations of weather conditions such as soil moisture zone, temperature, and humidity, the model predicts the best irrigation plan for various crop types. The prediction accuracy is improved by using a combination of supervised learning methods, and the model is trained on open datasets so that it can be scaled and customized to different crops and farming techniques. The results of the evaluation demonstrate the efficiency of the model, where water usage could be cut down by as much as 30% when compared to normal irrigation methods. This study provides the foundation for the deployment of smart irrigation systems that can be incorporated within farming practices, enhance responsible water management, and improve agricultural output.

title: Feature Aggregation and Multi-Class Detection (FAMCD) Framework for Skin Cancer Classification
authors: Ifjaz Ahmed M, Sahithy Ayyagari, Uma Maheswari M, Premaladha Jayaraman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: An unwelcome threat to life, melanoma, occurs if it is inconspicuously missed in an early detection stage. This research article presents a smart detection system called FAMCD (Feature Aggregation and Multi-Class Detection-to better detect and classify melanomas).
