Steps involved in the smart detection system include pre-cleaning such as hair removal from skin images, manual annotation of the lesion (area of suspicion), and then feature extraction of both common features (shape, color, and margin of the lesion) and deep learning features based on classical rule sets such as the ABCD rule (which considers parameters such as shape and color). Additionally, special techniques are incorporated that claim a measurement of uncertainty from the model in its predictions: frequency dropout and Monte Carlo sampling. To better understand the extent of model performance, three new metrics are proposed for evaluation: TUI (Total Uncertainty Index) for quantifying uncertainty level, BPS (Boundary Preservation Score) for verifying how well the lesion edge is preserved, and SCE (Structure Consistency Error) for verifying how well the structure of the lesion is preserved. This experimental evaluation demonstrated that the approach yielded a TUI value of 1.0847, along with a BPS score of 0.4000 (30% high uncertainty) and an SCE of 0.4875 (80% retained data). The system was tested on the popular skin cancer image dataset HAM10000. In terms of classification performance, the method yielded an overall accuracy of 0.8616 and a macro-AUC-ROC score of 0.8151, highlighting strong predictive ability. However, the balanced accuracy (0.3562), macro recall (0.3562), and macro F1-score (0.3519) indicate limitations in handling class imbalance. And the Local Interpretable Model (LIME) generates intuitive visual explanations that enhance physicians understanding of the model's reasoning, thereby fostering greater trust in its decisions.

title: Scalable Artificial Intelligence Models for Big Data Processing and Analysis
authors: Surya Veera Brahmaji Rao Sunnam
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Scalable artificial intelligence (AI) models have emerged as a pivotal solution for processing and analyzing large-scale datasets in a variety of domains, including healthcare, finance, and e-commerce. With the advent of big data, the need for intelligent systems capable of handling vast amounts of unstructured and structured data has increased exponentially. This paper explores the integration of scalable AI models with big data frameworks to address challenges such as data volume, velocity, and variety. Key methods for improving scalability in AI models, such as distributed learning, parallel processing, and cloud-based systems, are discussed. Furthermore, the paper highlights the role of advanced algorithms like deep learning, reinforcement learning, and ensemble methods in managing big data. A focus is placed on how these AI techniques can optimize data processing, enhance predictive analytics, and provide valuable insights for decision-making. Additionally, the paper reviews case studies demonstrating the application of scalable AI models in real-world big data environments and discusses future directions in the evolution of AI models for big data analytics.

title: GLREM-Net: Groundnut Leaf Disease Detection using Deep Learning based ResMobileNet
authors: Suganya.J, Gethzi Jeba Leelipushpam Paulraj, Immanuel Johnraja Jebadurai, Jebaveerasingh Jebadurai
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Groundnuts are an important crop on a global scale and are suffering from leaf diseases that are reducing yields considerably. To ensure continuous agricultural productivity, it is crucial to identify these diseases as soon as possible. Manual detection of plant diseases is a challenging and time-consuming task that requires expert knowledge and continuous field monitoring. Early detection is crucial to prevent the spread of diseases and to minimize crop loss. However, most existing systems are not capable of identifying diseases at their early stages, which limits their effectiveness in practical agricultural scenarios. To address these challenges, a novel model named GLREM-NET, based on the Residual Mobile Network (ResMobileNet) architecture, is proposed for accurate and efficient early-stage detection of groundnut leaf diseases. Initially, the input groundnut leaf disease (GLD) images are preprocessed using the Trilateral Filter (TF) to improve the quality of the images. The pre-processed images are fed into the Residual Mobile Network (ResMobileNet) for extracting the fine features. Finally, the Deep Neural Network (DNN) is used to classify the ground leaf disease into normal or abnormal. The proposed DNN model is evaluated based on its F1 score (F1S), specificity (SP), precision (PS), recall (RC), and accuracy (AC). The classification accuracy of 99.87% for the proposed GLREM-NET is highly reliable for the ground leaf disease dataset. The proposed GLREM-NET for groundnut leaf disease detection model achieves an overall accuracy of 0.18%, 1.9%, 1.43%, and 3.4% compared to existing methods such as Inception-V4, ResNet50, DenseNet169, and CNN, respectively.

title: Design and Fabrication of CSRR based Multi-Port Antenna System for 5G Smart Phone Devices
authors: Alamelu Alias Rajasree S, Kalaimathi B, Adline Jancy J, Shri Lakshmi S, Swathiga A, Vinodha N
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The performance of future wireless communication systems largely relies on the behavior of technologies like Massive MIMO in 5G and future networks. The antenna is designed on a 75 × 150 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> thick 1.5 mm FR4 substrate. An 8×8 MIMO antenna system is optimized within the frequency range of 3.1 GHz to 3.6 GHz with a resonant frequency at 3.4 GHz. Simulation and analysis is carried out employing Ansys HFSS, analyzing the most critical performance parameters including S-parameters, gain, efficiency, directivity, VSWR, Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC) below 0.2, Diversity Gain (DG) of 10dB. Simulation results reveal the maximum gain to be 4.57 dB, directivity of 5.23 dB, and return loss of -25 dB at resonant frequency of 3.4 GHz. The antenna has a high radiation efficiency of 87.38%, thus ensuring efficient transmission of power with low losses. The proposed design has better radiation properties, reduced mutual coupling, and superior isolation between antenna elements. These properties together render the antenna a good candidate to be integrated into future wireless communication systems.

title: Enhanced Liver Tumor Segmentation via Optimized Preprocessing and Attention-Based U-Net Model
authors: Carine Shqeir, Mohamad Diab, Racha Soubra
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Liver cancer is a leading cause of cancer-related deaths worldwide. Accurate liver tumor segmentation from Computed Tomography (CT) images is a critical step for diagnosis, treatment planning, and disease monitoring. However, it is challenging due to heterogeneous tumor appearance, low contrast boundaries, and large variability in lesion size and shape. In this work, an Attention U-Net model integrated with Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) and Convolutional Block Attention Modules (CBAM) is adopted, with a focus on optimizing preprocessing techniques to enhance liver tumor segmentation performance. The preprocessing pipeline includes liver-focused cropping, normalization, gamma correction, and contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization to improve tumor visibility and boundary definition. The model is trained and evaluated on liver-focused CT slices derived from the LiTS dataset. Segmentation performance is assessed using Dice Similarity Coefficient and Intersection over Union metrics. Experimental results achieve a tumor Dice score of 0.9166 and an IoU of 0.8586, demonstrating a substantial improvement over baseline approaches and highlighting the critical role of preprocessing combined with attention-enhanced architectures. The proposed framework provides a robust and accurate approach for automated liver tumor segmentation while maintaining computational efficiency, supporting its potential for automated clinical image analysis.

title: Hallucination-Aware, Injection-Resistant LLMs for Scientific Text Integrity
authors: Nimit Bhardwaj, Anvit Bhardwaj, Lavanya Garg
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Large language models are increasingly used in scientific writing and enterprise applications, yet remain vulnerable to two critical issues: hallucination and prompt injection. Hallucinations introduce fabricated or inaccurate information, undermining trust and reproducibility in generated text. Prompt injections manipulate model behavior by exploiting instruction-following tendencies, posing security risks in dynamic input environments. This paper proposes SciGuard, a unified framework designed to detect hallucinated content and defend against prompt-based adversarial manipulation. The architecture integrates token-level classification, contradiction-aware logic validation, and semantic prompt rewriting to handle both threats in real time. Experiments conducted on scientific and enterprise benchmarks show that SciGuard outperforms existing models across precision, recall, and F1 metrics while maintaining low latency. The results highlight the effectiveness of integrating factual verification and input control into a single system, offering a robust foundation for secure and reliable generative AI deployments.

title: Li Ion Life Estimation System using Machine Learning
authors: Sarathkumar R, Fathima Shirin M, Haririthanyaa S, Jayaprakash S, Premnath S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent Battery Management System (BMS) designed to enhance the performance and lifespan of lithium-ion batteries through the integration of machine learning techniques. The proposed system leverages real-time sensor data to predict key battery parameters such as State of Health (SOH) and State of Charge (SOC), enabling proactive fault detection and optimized energy management. Utilizing microcontrollers and analog-to-digital converters, sensor data including temperature, voltage, and current is continuously monitored and processed. A machine learning model trained on historical battery data identifies anomalies and adjusts charging and discharging operations dynamically. Additionally, the system incorporates self-healing mechanisms that respond to early-stage faults, ensuring safety and system stability. Experimental validation demonstrates the model's accuracy and the system’s effectiveness under varied load conditions. The proposed solution supports predictive maintenance strategies, reduces operational risks, and aligns with the growing demand for efficient and sustainable battery technologies. This work contributes to the development of reliable, AI-driven BMS solutions for applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage systems.

title: Leveraging VGG16 and ResNet50 for Clinically Aligned Assessment in Breast Cancer Histopathology
authors: Yara Nassar, Mohanad Alayedi
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Recently, deep learning (DL) models have achieved high accuracies on the BreakHis dataset for breast cancer histopathological classification; these results are often inflated by data leakage from non-patient-level splits and idealized conditions. This study conducts a realistic comparative evaluation of VGG16 and ResNet50 transfer learning architectures for binary (e.g., benign against malignant) classification. In this paper, we introduce three clinically motivated protocols: first, strict patientlevel splitting to prevent leakage; second, cross-magnification testing (training on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40 \times$</tex> only, testing on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \times / 200 \times / 400 \times$</tex>); and third, robustness assessment under strong Gaussian noise and blur degradations. Metrics are reported at both the image and patient-level (via probability aggregation). According to simulation results, ResNet50 consistently outperforms VGG16, showing up to 15 % less accuracy degradation in cross-magnification and degradation scenarios due to its residual connections. These experiments reveal a significant performance gap relative to literature benchmarks, advocating for stricter evaluation standards. The proposed framework and splits are released for reproducible research.

title: Perception-Aware Intelligent Lighting Systems: Functional Safety and Control Architectures for Intelligent Vehicles
authors: Abdul Salam Abdul Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper analyzes how perception-aware intelligent lighting is evolving into a safety-critical subsystem in intelligent vehicles. Using standards, peer-reviewed literature, and OEM/semiconductor technical reports, this paper synthesizes system-level control architectures of matrix LED and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex>-pixel headlamps, their interfaces with ADAS perception stacks, and the associated functional-safety implications under ISO 26262. Reported diagnostic strategies for LED driver faults, supervision and watchdog mechanisms, and the influence of signal, power, and thermal integrity on long-term reliability are consolidated into a unified architectural view. We map representative lighting-related hazards to detection and mitigation patterns and derive a design checklist covering timing constraints, EMI robustness, current-control accuracy, derating behavior, and safe-state transitions. Remaining gaps in perception-linked lighting validation—including synchronization, explainability, and environmental robustness-are also highlighted. The results position intelligent lighting as an assurance-driven, tightly integrated element within next-generation autonomous and electrified vehicle architectures. Accordingly, the focus of this work is on system-level architectural and functional-safety considerations, rather than algorithmic development.

title: Design of a Low-Cost Smart Embedded System for Real-Time Alcohol and Accident Detection in Vehicles
authors: Dhananjay Satanure, Swapnil Biradar, Pranav Ghabade, Shenila Ansari, Aditya Chougule, Shailaja Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Road accidents caused by drunken driving continue to present a major global safety concern, resulting significant annual vehicle fatalities. Despite advances in intelligent transportation systems, most existing driver assistance and accident alert mechanisms remain functionally isolated, cloud-dependent, or lack seamless integration between alcohol detection, vehicle control, and emergency communication. To address these limitations, this paper presents the design and implementation of a low-cost smart embedded system for real-time alcohol detection and accident monitoring within an integrated platform. The proposed architecture combines an alcohol sensor, accelerometer and gyroscope, Global Positioning System (GPS), and Global System for Mobile Communications (GSM) modules, all connected to an Arduino Mega. When the detected alcohol concentration exceeds a predefined threshold, the system automatically disables the vehicle's ignition and transmits an alert message containing GPS coordinates to the emergency contacts. In the event of an accident, collision impact sensed by the Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) triggers an immediate location-based SMS (Short Message Service) notification. The system was rigorously tested under simulated conditions to ensure reliability and responsiveness. Experimental results indicate that the proposed system provides timely alert generation and costefficient operation without reliance on internet connectivity. These findings demonstrate its suitability for scalable deployment in low-cost intelligent transportation, road safety, and emergency response applications.

title: AI-Powered Skin Lesion Detection and Classification using Emerging Techniques
authors: D. Ferlin Deva Shahila, D. Lilly Evangelin, Valantina Stephen, Babitha Lincy R, Berakhah.F.Stanley, Yeswanth Hanumanthu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Deep learning-based skin lesion categorization has become a viable method for automated dermatological diagnosis and prognosis. Through the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and other deep learning facsimile, researchers have made significant progress in correctly identifying different kinds of skin lesions from digital photos. An overview of current advancements and approaches in deep learning-based skin lesion categorization is provided in this research. We talk about the difficulties in acquiring datasets, annotating them, and optimizing models. We also go over how to integrate multimodal data sources to improve classification accuracy. The usefulness and drawbacks of deep learning-based methods for classifying skin lesions are demonstrated through an analysis of a number of case studies and research techniques. Additionally, covered are ethical issues including algorithmic bias and patient privacy. This work attempts to give insights into the present state-of-the-art in automated skin lesion categorization using deep learning and identify future research paths in the subject by doing a exhaustive assessment gauge of the literature. Obtaining and annotating high-quality information is one of the main obstacles in the categorization of skin lesions. In order to guarantee the resilience and generalizability of deep learning methods, researchers have investigated a number of datasets gathering and annotation methodologies. Achieving the best classification performance also heavily relies on model optimization strategies, such as architecture design and hyperparameter tweaking. The use of multimodal data sources, including histopathological findings, clinical history, and meatoscopic pictures, has demonstrated encouraging outcomes in improving classification accuracy. Deep learning models can offer more thorough and precise diagnostic insights by integrating complimentary data from many modalities. Dermatologists may be able to increase the precision and effectiveness of skin lesion diagnosis by utilizing these methods, which might result in prompt treatment and improved patient outcomes. Deep learning-based methods present a viable way to increase dermatologists' capacity to identify skin cancer, which will finally lead to good patient care and healthcare delivery.

title: AI-Driven Forest Fire Prediction for Disaster Prevention
authors: Mohammad Shkoukani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The increasing frequency and intensity of forest fires, worsened by climate change, present a serious risk to ecosystems, human beings, and properties. The early detection of these fires helps eliminate the impacts, using timely measures to check the situation and allocate resources. This research investigates the potential of artificial intelligence (AI) for creating an advanced predictive model for the occurrence of forest fires, which makes use of machine learning algorithms, and environmental data collected from various data sources including satellite imagery, meteorological observations, and sensor networks. This study seeks to construct a model capable of real-time precise predictions, with a special focus on identifying areas vulnerable to fire through the use of a combination of historical fire data and climatic, physical, and ecological datasets using a statistical analysis approach. The current model evolved from the above-mentioned features through systematic deep learning and advanced feature selection techniques by considering data sparsity and non-linearity in the forest fire datasets. Moreover, it aims at utilizing federated learning to enable collaboration over geographical limits without compromising the services of the participating institutions through the data that they choose to share. The preliminary results show significant improvements in the performance of the model as compared to the standard methods being used, thus, underlining the possibility of implementing an AI-driven initiative for disaster prevention and environmental management. This study adds to the existing literature on forest fires prevention, providing a clearer understanding of AI's role in predictive analytics for ecological conservation and public safety.

title: Real-Time Speech Enhancement for Dysarthria Individuals Using NLP and Deep Learning
authors: Sambasivarao Chindam, Gobika R, Vijeyasri T, C. Oswald
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Individuals with dysarthria often have trouble speaking clearly, which makes it difficult for others to understand them, especially in real-time conversations. This research aims to make their speech easier to understand using new methods from natural language processing(NLP) and deep learning.We fine-tune the CrisperWhisper (CRISPR-VSPR) speech recognition model on dysarthric speech datasets (e.g., TORGO), so that it generates transcripts from unclear speech. After that, these transcripts are processed into clear and natural-sounding speech using text-to-speech (TTS) models. This system creates a real-time process which converts speech to text and then back to clear speech which helps people with dysarthria communicate more easily.

title: Investigating the Impact of Dimensionality Reduction on Spectral Unmixing Accuracy Using NIST-Traceable Colour Calibration Standard
authors: Ondřej Vaculík, Mojmír Šerý, Lukáš Šilhan, Denisa Šilhanová, Pavel Zemánek
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: This study explores the impact of dimensionality reduction (DR) on spectral unmixing accuracy using a National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) traceable colour transmission calibration slide. Hyperspectral images were acquired and analysed alongside high-accuracy NIST reference spectral transmission data. The used analysis pipeline integrates DR techniques (principal component analysis, non-negative matrix factorisation, and autoencoder neural networks) with spectral unmixing algorithms (non-negative least squares and multivariate curve resolution). Reconstruction accuracy was evaluated using root mean squared error and spectral angle distance. The findings indicate that low-dimensional representations effectively yield low spectral reconstruction error. In particular, the combination of non-negative matrix factorisation with three components and multivariate curve resolution achieved the lowest reconstruction error. This work demonstrates that integrating DR with spectral unmixing enables reliable colour patch composition estimation, balancing data compression, and analytical accuracy.

title: Edge-AI and Multimodal Sensor Fusion–Based Smart Stick for Visually Impaired Individuals with Real-Time Obstacle Detection
authors: Dhivya P, C. N. Vanitha, Suryavisanth R S, Sujith S, Joel Seba Clitues J, Sureshkumar M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Independent mobility for visually impaired individuals is often limited by the inability of conventional assistive tools to provide early and reliable awareness of surrounding obstacles. While recent smart navigation aids incorporate sensing and artificial intelligence, many existing solutions depend on cloud computation or single-modality sensing, resulting in latency, privacy concerns, and reduced robustness in real-world environments. This work proposes a fully on-device smart stick based on Edge Artificial Intelligence and multimodal sensor fusion for real-time obstacle detection. A lightweight TinyML-based convolutional neural network processes visual input on a XIAO ESP32S3 microcontroller, while ultrasonic and infrared sensors supply complementary distance and proximity information. These modalities are combined using a decision-level fusion strategy to enhance detection reliability under varying environmental conditions. Experimental validation conducted in indoor and semi-outdoor settings demonstrates an average detection accuracy of 0.89, with precision and recall values of 0.88 and 0.86, respectively. The system achieves inference latency below 125 ms and operates with low power consumption suitable for prolonged use. The results confirm the feasibility of deploying multimodal Edge-AI perception on resource-constrained assistive devices.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Using Meteorological Data
authors: Adhin Thomas, Mohamad Nassereddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power generation is inherently dependent on meteorological conditions, making accurate day-ahead forecasting critical for scheduling, reserve management, and grid stability. However, many existing machine learning (ML) approaches rely on limited meteorological integration or evaluate such inputs in an ad-hoc manner, resulting in sub-optimal performance for longer forecasting horizons. This paper investigates a hybrid deep learning approach for day-ahead PV forecasting that integrates multiple meteorological variables within a single production-feasible architecture. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model cooperatively capture structured relationships between meteorological inputs and temporal PV dynamics. The key contribution is the systematic evaluation of individual meteorological variables irradiance, air pressure, wind speed, temperature, humidity, and cloud cover within a unified hybrid ML framework, addressing the gap where such variables are often included without explicit contribution analysis. Experimental results show that meteorological augmentation improves forecasting accuracy compared to persistence, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LSTM-only baselines, while sensitivity analysis provides actionable insight into the relative operational value of each variable, supporting informed sensor deployment in practical PV applications.

title: A Fully Integrated D-Band I/Q Homodyne Transceiver Enabling a Multi-Gigabit Wired Link
authors: Vasileios Manouras, Yannis Papananos, Franz Dielacher
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a fully integrated direct-conversion I/Q transmitter and receiver fabricated in a 130-nm SiGe BiCMOS process and experimentally validated through a short-range on-board link. At a carrier frequency of 120 GHz, the prototype has demonstrated data transmission up to 50 Gbps over a 50-mm microstrip trace on a standard multilayer printed circuit board. This is accomplished using 16-QAM modulation with a measured EVM of 14.7%. Although the PCB interconnect was intentionally implemented as a low-cost, non-optimized evaluation platform, the achieved link energy efficiency of 19.2 pJ/bit underscores the robustness of the proposed D-band transceiver and validates its suitability for high-speed intra-package and module-level communication at sub-THz frequencies.

title: AI-Enabled IOT Health Monitoring with Multi-Vital Sensing and Location-Triggered Emergency Alert Mechanism
authors: Aarav Singhal, Prasenjit Sarkar, Saumy Singh, S. Latha, P. Muthu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In today's scenario, due to the increasing burden of chronic diseases, the aging population, and the limitations of periodic health checkups, the demand for health monitoring has increased drastically. Health monitoring in conventional healthcare systems is done through observation, which is not effective in detecting changes in the body. Hence, the development of intelligent health monitoring systems is highly required. This is the challenge addressed in the proposed Health Monitoring System, which combines wearable sensor technology for real-time health monitoring with machine learning analytics. These include data on essential physiological parameters (heart rate, body temperature, oxygen saturation, motion) which are acquired by embedded sensors, which are processed via an Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) model. Unlike other machine learning models, the EBM model offers high predictive accuracy and interpretability that allow easy interpretation of how each physiological parameter affects health risk prediction. It classifies health conditions into predefined risk categories and presents explanations and predictions together which augments trust and clinical usability. Integration of IoT allows for real-time data transmission and remote monitoring through a connected interface as a preventive measure when abnormal conditions are detected. Coupled with sensor-based data acquisition, explainable machine learning and remote access, the proposed system can be applied for continuous health monitoring with reliable, explainable and scalable systems. This not only advances early detection and decision-making but also is a major step forward for patient-centric intelligent health systems.

title: Adversarial Robustness in Multilingual and Code-Mixed NLP: Insights from Indian Language Settings
authors: Arunkumar G, Deepa M
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Despite the widespread use of NLP technologies have become widely used in multilingual and code-mixed settings, adversarial robustness efforts have predominantly concentrated on monolingual English, resulting in the inadequacy of the existing literature to identify adversarially vulnerable aspects within linguistically varied systems, especially Indian languages. This paper fills this gap via an extensive review of adversarial robustness in multilingual and code-mixed NLP systems with a particular emphasis on Indian language applications as high-variation deployment contexts. By systematically selecting studies, 108 papers spanning from 2019-2025 were selected among over 400 candidates across different NLP and machine learning conferences. The survey consolidates findings on six aspects: (i) linguistic characteristics of multilingual and code-mixed texts; (ii) taxonomy of adversarial attacks considering text granularity and linguistic nature; (iii) defense approaches including preprocessing, adversarial training, representation level, prompt-level, and detection-based schemes; (iv) evaluation criteria and benchmark datasets; (v) cross-lingual transfer dynamics under adversarial and distribution shifts; and (vi) ethical concerns. The study reveals three major insights. First, fragility of tokenizers and code-switching make a compound vulnerability that is currently not addressed by defenses. Secondly, the adversarial weaknesses of language models can be transferred across languages via cross-lingual transfer, thus affecting Indian low-resource languages more than other languages. Third, the present evaluation approaches that use monolingual metrics specific to each task are likely to under-estimate the problem of robustness in multilingual settings wherein adversarial-specific metrics provide more insights. Moreover, there are no benchmark tests developed specifically for assessing robustness for code-mixed language pairs in India. In terms of numbers, previous literature highlights attack success rates ranging from 36.8% to 41.1% in Indian language models against word-level attacks, adversarial confirmation bias failure rates of 14%, and 5-15 point safety alignment gaps between instruction-tuned models in English and Hindi. Thus, addressing these problems is critical for the robust development of NLP applications. Contributions include a taxonomy for attack approaches in two dimensions with Indian language examples, cross-mapping of attack approaches to defensive techniques, and vulnerability analysis of adversarial attacks against ten Indian NLP resources.

title: Pragmatic Lossless Compression: Fundamental Limits and Universality
authors: Andreas Theocharous, Lampros Gavalakis, Ioannis Kontoyiannis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: The problem of variable-rate lossless data compression is considered, for codes with and without prefix constraints. Sharp bounds are derived for the best achievable compression rate of memoryless sources, when the excess-rate probability is required to be exponentially small in the blocklength. Accurate nonasymptotic expansions with explicit constants are obtained for the optimal rate, using tools from large deviations and Gaussian approximation. When the source distribution is unknown, a universal achievability result is obtained with an explicit “price for universality” term. This is based on a fine combinatorial estimate on the number of sequences with small empirical entropy, which might be of independent interest. Examples are shown indicating that, in the small excess-rate-probability regime, the approximation to the fundamental limit of the compression rate suggested by these bounds is significantly more accurate than the approximations provided by either normal approximation or error exponents. The new bounds reinforce the crucial operational conclusion that, in applications where the blocklength is relatively short and where stringent guarantees are required on the excess-rate probability, the best achievable rate is no longer close to the entropy. Rather, it is an appropriate, more pragmatic rate, determined via the inverse error exponent function and the blocklength.

title: Conference Calendar
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Microwave Magazine

title: Acoustic Sensing and GAN-Based Augmentation for Non-Autonomous Driving Environment Recognition Towards Visually Impaired Inclusion
authors: Cihun-Siyong Gong, Yu-Hua Chen
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This study proposes an acoustic sensing–based framework for driving environment recognition in electric vehicles (EVs), designed for intelligent transportation systems (ITS) in non-autonomous driving scenarios and edge-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) environments. The proposed approach analyzes short-duration acoustic signals and employs multiple feature representations, including MFCC, LFCC, and wavelet-based features. To address data scarcity and class imbalance, a feature-level generative adversarial network (GAN) is introduced for data augmentation. Experimental results demonstrate that MFCC-based features consistently achieve stable and superior recognition performance across different configurations. Moreover, GAN-based augmentation significantly improves recognition stability and accuracy for minority classes, such as tunnel and rainy conditions. In addition, under a unified experimental framework, this study systematically investigates the interactions among acoustic features, data augmentation strategies, and model architectures. The findings indicate that passive acoustic sensing offers advantages in terms of low cost and real-time computational capability, making it well suited for deployment in edge IoT systems. The proposed approach is applicable to intelligent transportation scenarios, including driver assistance and environmental perception for visually impaired passengers. Overall, the proposed approach offers advantages in terms of low cost, minimal privacy concerns, and real-time computational capability. It is well suited for deployment in edge computing environments and ITS applications, providing a practical solution for acoustic-based environmental perception in electric vehicles.

title: Boosting the Performance of Decentralized Federated Learning via Catalyst Acceleration_supp1-3688672.pdf
authors: Qinglun Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Decentralized Federated Learning has emerged as an alternative to centralized architectures due to its faster training, privacy preservation, and reduced communication overhead. In decentralized communication, the server aggregation phase in Centralized Federated Learning shifts to the client side, which means that clients connect with each other in a peer-to-peer manner. However, compared to the centralized mode, data heterogeneity in Decentralized Federated Learning will cause larger variances between aggregated models, which leads to slow convergence in training and poor generalization performance in tests. To address these issues, we introduce Catalyst Acceleration and propose an acceleration Decentralized Federated Learning algorithm called DFedCata. It consists of two main components: the Moreau envelope function, which primarily addresses parameter inconsistencies among clients caused by data heterogeneity, and Nesterov's extrapolation step, which accelerates the aggregation phase. Theoretically, we prove the optimization error bound and generalization error bound of the algorithm, providing a further understanding of the nature of the algorithm and the theoretical perspectives on the hyperparameter choice. Empirically, we demonstrate the advantages of the proposed algorithm in both convergence speed, computational cost, and generalization performance on CIFAR10/100 and Tiny-ImageNet with various non-iid data distributions. Moreover, extensive experiments are conducted to validate the theoretical properties of DFedCata, showing strong consistency between theory and empirical observations. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/zzylyxx/DFedCata</uri>.

title: Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network-Based Traffic Sensing Approach for Satellite Communication Networks
authors: Shuotong Wei, Tao Dong, Zhihui Liu, Hang Di, Qiwei Zhang, Haijun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Satellite communication networks, characterized by the advantages of seamless global coverage and ubiquitous access, have emerged as a main focus of current research. However, satellite network topology changes dynamically and burst traffic is spatio-temporal coupling, causing the satellite network traffic sensing problem with high overhead and low accuracy. Therefore, this paper proposes a spatio-temporal graph neural network-based satellite network traffic sensing approach (STSA) to collect data efficiently and predict traffic precisely. The study designs in-band network telemetry (INT)-based Hamilton cycle to reduce the number of repeated visits to satellite nodes, reducing the redundancy of data collection, and construct spatio-temporal graph of satellite network topology and traffic. STSA takes full advantage of the spatio-temporal dependence of topology and traffic to realize satellite network traffic sensing. The satellite network state view is generated by mapping the spatio-temporal characteristics of historical traffic to the next period. Simulation shows that STSA effectively reduces sensing overhead and improves accuracy compared to the traditional traffic sensing algorithm based on the Euler cycle and long short-term memory (LSTM). The proposed STSA provides traffic data for satellite network routing, enhancing the efficiency of satellite network management and control.

title: Joint Source and Sensor Localization by Hybrid TDOA–AOA: Analysis and Solution
authors: Yongde Ye, Gang Wang, K. C. Ho
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper investigates the joint source and sensor localization (JSSL) problem using hybrid time-difference-of-arrival (TDOA) and angle-of-arrival (AOA) measurements in a multi-source-multi-sensor scenario. Without the knowledge of sensor positions, this problem jointly estimates the relative positions of the sources and sensors together. We first conduct the localizability analysis by examining the rank of the Jacobian matrix, from which we obtain the minimum number of sources and sensors required for TDOA-AOA JSSL and the special configurations where the JSSL problem is not solvable. Next, we construct a novel parametric model by integrating the TDOA and AOA measurements, and use it to formulate a non-convex constrained weighted least squares (CWLS) problem. JSSL is then accomplished by solving the CWLS problem through semidefinite relaxation and semidefinite programming. Finally, theoretical analyses are conducted. It shows that the proposed CWLS solution is able to achieve the Cramér-Rao lower bound performance under small Gaussian noise. Moreover, it reveals that increasing the number of sources or sensors can improve the overall JSSL performance. Simulation results validate the theory and also demonstrate the capability of the proposed method for TDOA-AOA JSSL.

title: Technical Program Committee
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)

title: Optimized Multiplexer-Based Carry Select Adder Implementation
authors: Parthibaraj Anguraj, Thiruvenkadam Krishnan, Mani P, Vivek Karthick Perumal, Saravanan Subramanian
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this work, a MUX-based Carry Select Adder (CSA) is designed to improve throughput and reduce LUT usage. The design combination of multiplexers with logic gates. The multiplexer selects the carry value as either 0 or 1. This method avoids using extra adder blocks. As a result, the hardware area is reduced without affecting the accuracy of the adder. The delay is also reduced, which improves performance. The design is tested for sizes from 8 bits to 128 bits. It is implemented on a Virtex4 FPGA. The results show that the design works well and is efficient.

title: Resource-Efficient Plastic Classification System Using Low-Cost NIR Spectroscopy and Edge AI: A Case Study on Sustainable Toy Recycling
authors: Tzu-Ling Kuo, Hsiao-Wei Hu, Hung-Kai Huang, Tzu-Yang Lin, Chong-Wei Wang, Chun-Wei Yu, Bo-Wei Chen
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate identification of heterogeneous toy plastics is a key bottleneck in decentralized recycling, yet conventional spectroscopic sorting often relies on expensive instrumentation or centralized computation. Addressing the challenge of AI-augmented sensor interfaces, this paper presents a resource-efficient, edge-AI plastic classification system that demonstrates how lightweight machine learning can compensate for the inherent limitations of consumer-grade spectral hardware by coupling a low-cost multi-spectral NIR sensing interface with an embedded-deployable learning model through a system-level co-design approach. The sensing front-end utilizes an AS7265x-basedmultispectral module providing 18 discrete bands from 410 nm to 940 nm. To ensure measurement consistency and embody circular economy principles, the sensor is integrated into a modular, light-shielding enclosure constructed from repurposed ABS-based blocks (LEGO elements), which maintains a fixed measurement geometry and stabilizes the optical environment. To compensate for the limited spectral resolution of cost-effective hardware, a radial basis function (RBF) kernel Support Vector Machine (SVM) is optimized for constrained deployment. The system was evaluated on a dataset of 6,650 Raman-verified, independent physical fragments (PP, PE, ABS, and “Others”). Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed SVM achieves a theoretical laboratory accuracy of 99.17% (0.9933 macro F1-score), while maintaining a practical field-test benchmark of 75.00% when deployed on uncleaned, aged post-consumer plastics. On an Arduino Uno R4 Minima platform, the system delivers a measured model inference latency of 118.6 ms. Combined with the 140 ms sensor integration time, the total end-to-end latency is approximately 258.6 ms. Based on analytical estimation from hardware datasheets, the energy consumption per classification cycle is approximately 0.0513 J, enabling real-time, offline sorting without cloud connectivity. This work demonstrates how AI-fostered, resource-efficient sensor–AI co-design can extend low-cost multispectral sensing toward practical, decentralized waste management.

title: Three-Point Calibration Method for Conductive Liquid Characterization Using High-Dynamic-Range Microwave Sensor
authors: Mehri Ziaee Bideskan, Nima Karbaschi, Zahra Abbasi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurately quantifying ionic compound levels in aqueous media remains challenging due to the strong dielectric losses introduced by dissolved ions. This work presents a resonator-based microwave sensing system designed for non-invasive characterization of such lossy media, demonstrated using ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) as a representative analyte. The system employs an electric-LC resonator tag coupled to two different reader configurations, a gap-coupled transmission line (GCTL) and a standard transmission line (TL). Changes in ionic concentration modify the effective permittivity of the surrounding medium, which in turn perturbs the resonator’s electromagnetic loading. These perturbations manifest as measurable variations in the resonant response, allowing the reader to sense the medium entirely through wireless coupling without requiring physical contact. Based on wideband dielectric assessments of the material samples, 1000 MHz was selected as the optimal operating frequency balancing sensitivity and signal attenuation. Experimental and simulation studies show that the resonance amplitude varies monotonically with ionic content, exhibiting exponential behavior for the GCTL and a logarithmic dependence for the TL reader with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.99. Leveraging these predictable behaviors, we introduce a three-point calibration methodology that parameterizes the full response curve from only three measurements. This approach drastically reduces computational overhead while maintaining high accuracy (error <2.5%) across the whole concentration range from 0 to 6000 ppm. The primary contribution of this work is the generalizable three-point calibration strategy that drastically reduces the number of required measurements while remaining valid for a wide range of ionic solutions, enabling practical deployment of resonator-based sensing in precision agriculture and environmental monitoring.

title: Time-based 8TD SRAM CIM Macro with Low PVT Sensitivity for Edge Devices
authors: Andrea Fasolino, Pio Esposito, Rosalba Liguori, Luigi Di Benedetto, Alfredo Rubino, Gian Domenico Licciardo
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: Computing-in-Memory (CIM) promises to overcome the von Neumann bottleneck; however, analog CIM designs face challenges such as read disturbance, nonlinearity, and process-voltage-temperature (PVT) variations. Therefore, this paper presents an 8-transistor SRAM CIM macro with built-in PVT robustness to tackle these challenges. The proposed dynamic cascode SRAM bitcell utilizes stacked high- and low-Vth NMOS access devices and multilevel current-mode word-line driving to enhance read stability and achieve up to 54-fold lower bitcell current variability without requiring separate read/write ports. A subthreshold current-starved ring oscillator and a transmission-gate counter perform time-based analog-to-digital conversion of MAC results, achieving an integral nonlinearity of <0.5 LSB across PVT corners without calibration circuits. Post-layout results in TSMC 65 nm CMOS LP technology show a storage density of 241 kb/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and an area efficiency of 31.32 TOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> for 2-bit inputs and 1-bit weights. The 64×128 macro maintains functionality from 0.65 V to 1.2 V and yields <0.8% MNIST accuracy loss, demonstrating robust, energy-efficient CIM for edge AI.

title: IMCET 2026 Index Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)

title: Global Sensitivity Analysis of Whole-Body Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model Parameters in [Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&#x0026;T Radioligand Therapy: Key Determinants of Individual Kidney and Tumor Absorbed Dose Uncertainty and Implications for Digital Twin Development_supp1-3690503.pdf
authors: Deni Hardiansyah
publish: N/A
abstract: Understanding how uncertainties in physiological parameters propagate into the uncertainty of estimated absorbed doses (eADs) is desirable for optimizing individualized radiopharmaceutical therapy (RPT). In this study, we applied global sensitivity analysis (GSA) to a published whole-body physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) model, developed using biokinetic data from 13 patients treated with [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">177</sup>Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&T, to identify influential parameters on the individual eAD uncertainty. Model inputs included patient-specific parameters such as receptor densities (R₀), blood flow densities (f), and release rates (λ) across relevant organs, with their uncertainties and correlations incorporated. A GSA method extended for dependent parameters (GSADPK) was employed to compute main effects (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub>) and total effects (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ti</sub>), with kidney and tumor eADs as the outputs of interest. Overall, kidney eADs showed lower uncertainty than tumors. For kidneys, receptor density emerged as the dominant contributor (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub>, median [range] = 0.40 [0.10–0.58]), followed by blood flow density (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub> = 0.27 [0.12–0.48]). In tumors, receptor density was also found as the primary determinant (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub> = 0.42 [0.02–0.80]), with tumor release rate as a secondary contributor (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub> = 0.04 [0.00–0.67]). These findings highlight that accurate estimation of receptor density and blood flow density could substantially reduce eAD uncertainty. By identifying the physiological parameters most critical for dose prediction, this work provides a foundation for digital twin–based frameworks.

title: Sentiment Analysis in Stock Price Prediction Using ML
authors: Rasha Abousamra, Thair Kaddumi, Shatha Mustafa Hussain, Mohammed A. Afifi, Gamal Khalifa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the realm of financial markets, predicting the future price of stocks is a very complex task that traders, investors, and policymakers cannot ignore. Relying on purely past price data is the approach that has been taken traditionally when stock movements were considered, but the market sentiment has been neglected. The integration of sentiment analysis with machine learning (ML) techniques in this study will be employed to increase the precision of stock price prediction. Text data from news articles, financial reports, and social media sources will be utilized to generate sentiment scores that will measure the overall mood of the market via sentiment scores generated by keyword analysis. The historical price data of the companies will be integrated into various ML models such as Random Forest, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Gradient Boosting with the sentiment scores thus formed. Experimental results show that models with sentiments analysis outperform models that rely purely on numerical data. Furthermore, this research has illustrated how fruitful it would be if financial decision-makers made use of the knowledge of stock price drivers that may be influenced by sentiment-informed ML models, thereby outlining the intricate nature of stock price fluctuations.

title: Oncoverse: A Multi-Agent Conversational System for Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Sharanya Acharya, Yami Sunil, Poshika Reddy, Padmavathy T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of breast cancer is essential, along with clinical understanding to facilitate prompt decision-making. This research introduces a multi-agent dialogue diagnostic system that combines deep learning, knowledge graphs, and reasoning generated by large language models (LLMs) via an adaptive confidence fusion approach. The suggested system includes a Coordinator Agent that manages workflow, an Imaging Agent utilizing a DenseNet CNN for classifying breast ultrasound images, and a Reasoning Agent that enhances predictions with knowledge-graph evidence and LLM-driven dialogue for diagnostic reasoning. In contrast to traditional single-model pipelines, our method adaptively modifies decision weights through an Adaptive Fusion Module that merges imaging confidence, reasoning dependability, and semantic medical connections to produce a well-calibrated diagnosis with justification and a BI-RADS-compliant risk score. Experimental results demonstrate that the adaptive fusion framework achieves superior performance compared to imaging-only and fixed-rule methods, reaching an overall accuracy of 89.84 % with high sensitivity and precision across benign, malignant, and normal classes, thereby validating its effectiveness as a robust and interpretable diagnostic assistant.

title: Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy using Intelligent Gradient Boosted Decision Trees
authors: M. Sharmila, K G.Sathiya, G.Uma, R Hemalatha, P.Pavithra, R.Suresh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), a progressive eye disease brought on by long-term diabetes, can lead to blindness or visual impairment if left undiagnosed. Due to minor retinal changes, image variability, as well as limited access to experts, timely and accurate detection is essential but challenging. Efficient screening necessitates the adoption of effective, automated, as well as easily comprehensible diagnostic techniques. Hence, this research is developed to perform the DR detection using intelligent machine learning methodology. The dataset is first gathered from the online sources called Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography (OCTA) India Dataset. This gathered data undergoes the preprocessing by the Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) + Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) approaches. The extraction of the features from these pre-processed data is done by the Visual Geometry Group (VGG19) approach. The detection of DR is done finally by the intelligent machine learning model referred as Gradient Boosted Decision Trees (GBDT). The proposed GBDT-DR model in terms of accuracy and recall is 5.51% and 6.90% better than the considered existing methods for the DR detection model respectively.

title: Machine Learning Classifier Comparative Performance Analysis of Smart Mobility: Multiclass Traffic Congestion Prediction
authors: Mutaz Rasmi Abu Sara, Murad Zeer, Jawad Hasan Alkhateeb, Khaled Musa
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: City congestion has become one of the most urgent problems of the smart city ecosystem, which has to be addressed with effective and data-driven strategies to provide real-time classifications and remedies. It gives a detailed analysis of three machine learning classifiers, Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) to predict traffic congestion (Low, Medium, High) based on a rich, multidimensional dataset that encompasses cyber-physical telemetry and social sentiment metrics. To create temporal features (Hour, Day, Month) that reflected cyclic patterns in the traffic, timestamps were used. Careful grid search optimization of models was performed followed by comparison by macro averaged (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score) metrics on stratified train-test splits. According to the results, Random Forest classifier has the highest test accuracy (96.07) and macro F1-score (95.15) compared to the Decision Tree (95.67) and SVM (91.47). Multiclass ROC curves and confusion matrices indicate a good discriminative ability as well as good generalization, in particular of important states in in terms of congestion, a good category such as the excellent category. The efficacy of ensemble techniques used in operational traffic categorization systems confirmed in this paper is useful in proactive mobility management and sustainable urban planning.

title: BERT-Electra Synergistic Model with Cross-Attention for Enhanced Fake News Detection
authors: Lakshitha Shree R M, Vishnu Kumar Kaliappan, Dharani Jaganathan, Pavithra P, Kaviya Sree S, Sarmili M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The quick dissemination of digital misinformation has turned fake news detection into a vital challenge that alters the perception of information and the public's opinion. The paper introduces a Dual-Stream Transformer architecture that brings together BERT and ELECTRA in order to seize not only the high-level semantic context but also the fine-grained token-level irregularities that are often found in the texts. BERT captures the global contextual coherence while ELECTRA identifies the lexical anomalies and they both work together through a Cross-Attention Fusion Layer that combines their representations to classify news articles effectively in binary terms. The conducted experiments using benchmark datasets highlight that the suggested model is always better than the traditional machine learning and hybrid BERT-based methods considering the metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Its modular and scalable architecture allows for real-time deployment and even future extensions, that include but are not limited to, multimodal, multilingual, and explainable AI enhancements.

title: Reconfiguration Technique for Enhancing PV Module Performance Under Mismatching Conditions
authors: Tamer I. A. Abudabbousa, Vanjari Venkata Ramana, Sudarshan Kumar Babu Valluru
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The PV module output power depends on the environmental conditions under which it works. Mismatching conditions, which occur when the environmental conditions are distributed unevenly on the module's surface, significantly affect the module's behavior and can lead to power losses. Several methods are presented to maximize power generation from a PV module under mismatching conditions. Module-level reconfiguration, the process of modifying the submodules interconnection, has proven effective in enhancing power extraction. This paper presents an easy-to-implement modification to a commercial PV module that enables dynamic reconfiguration of its submodules, along with a technique for determining the optimal submodule configuration. A buck converter is proposed for efficient power conversion. An evaluation of the presented system was carried out by comparing its module output power and load power with those of a conventional system under various environmental conditions. Under the proposed mismatching conditions, the module power improvement of the presented system ranges between 9.26% and 46.51%. Furthermore, the load power of the presented system is increased by 8.19% to 48.15%.

title: Model-Less Forecast-Proactive Intelligent Control for Inverter in Islanded Microgrid
authors: Mustafa A. Kamoona, Juan Manuel Mauricio
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Islanded inverter-dominated microgrids with high penetration of renewable energy sources face significant voltage regulation challenges under unbalanced and time-varying load conditions. Conventional voltage controllers, such as proportional-integral-derivative (PID) schemes, rely on accurate system modeling and careful parameter tuning, which limits their adaptability under uncertain operating conditions. This paper proposes a model-less forecast-proactive Reinforcement Learning (RL) voltage control framework for islanded AC microgrids supplied through a single DC source and grid-forming inverter. The proposed controller directly adjusts the inverter voltage reference using only local measurements, without requiring an explicit network model. A simple non-learning forecast feature based on the next-hour load schedule is incorporated to improve anticipatory control actions during peak demand periods. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated on a fourbus islanded AC microgrid with unbalanced and dynamic loads. Comparative studies against a model-based PID controller and an LSTM-based baseline demonstrate that the proposed RL controller achieves voltage regulation performance comparable to optimally tuned PID control while maintaining strict voltage limits of 0.98-1.02 pu. The results confirm that the proposed model-less framework provides a practical and robust alternative for intelligent voltage control in islanded microgrids.

title: Privacy-Preserving Bipartite Consensus in Aperiodic Sampled-Data Multiagent Systems: A Discrete Differential Privacy Scheme
authors: Lingyu Wang, Te Yang, Jianwei Xia, Zhichuang Wang, Guoliang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article investigates the bipartite consensus control problem for aperiodic sampled-data multiagent systems (MASs) with ε-differential privacy. First, a discrete-time time-varying state space model is constructed for heterogeneous MASs, in which the sampling interval is time-varying within a certain known range, which can better describe the segmented continuum features caused by aperiodic sampled-data. Second, a differential privacy average output bipartite consensus algorithm for sampled MASs is given. This dynamic distributed algorithm safeguards the confidentiality of its initial configuration while minimizing the need for constant controller adjustments and real-time data exchanges. A time-varying control gain is given to ensure that the convergence point’s expectation precisely matches the average of the initial states, with the convergence accuracy bounded by an explicit expression. Furthermore, a carefully designed noise mechanism is introduced in the privacy analysis, enabling agents to flexibly adjust their privacy levels. To validate the proposed approach, a multiagent collaborative control experimental platform is applied, incorporating both uncrewed ground vehicles (UGVs) and quadcopter uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs). The developed methodology effectively computes the required control gain, with experimental results conclusively demonstrating the successful implementation of our algorithm.

title: Degradation Aware Adaptive Charging Strategies for Ultra-Fast Electric Vehicle Charging
authors: K Lakshmi Prasad Reddy, M. Vijaya Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Ultra-fast charging (UFC) is essential for improving the usability of electric vehicles (EVs); however, aggressive charging currents significantly accelerate battery degradation and electro-thermal stress, adversely impacting safety and lifetime. This paper presents a degradation-aware adaptive charging strategy for ultra-fast EV charging that systematically translates battery electro-thermal degradation insights into practical charging control. A physics-informed battery model incorporating state-of-charge (SoC) dynamics, electro-thermal behavior, and state-of-health <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SoH})$</tex> degradation is employed. The proposed strategy dynamically regulates the charging current based on SoC and battery temperature, without relying on learning-based optimization. The charging framework is evaluated for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 0\ V}, \mathbf{1 8 0\ A h\ E V}$</tex> battery pack under ultra-fast charging conditions with charging currents up to 350 A over a 3600 s charging horizon. Performance is compared against conventional constant-current-constant-voltage (CC-CV) and multistage charging strategies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed degradationaware strategy significantly reduces electro-thermal stress and battery degradation. Specifically, the peak normalized battery temperature is reduced from 2691.8 (CC-CV) and 1975.6 (multistage) to 880.6, corresponding to a 67.3 % reduction relative to CC-CV charging. Moreover, cumulative degradation is reduced by more than 36 orders of magnitude, from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.9 \times 10^{58}$</tex> under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C C - C V}$</tex> charging to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 9} \times \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{2 2}}$</tex> using the proposed strategy. Although the proposed method increases charging time to 3600 s, compared to 1975 s for CC-CV charging, it establishes an effective trade-off between charging speed and battery health preservation. The results confirm that embedding degradation awareness into charging control substantially improves battery longevity and thermal safety, and provides a systematic transition toward learning-based optimal charging strategies for ultra-fast EV applications.

title: Overvoltage Stress Distribution on Transformer Bushings Under the Effect of Electromagnetic Transient Response
authors: Md Nazmus Shakib Khan, Md Abdul Ahad Juel, Abu Anas Nasim, Md. Zahidul Islam, Badhan Baishnab, Md Shahadat Jaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper investigates the overvoltage stress distribution in transformer bushings subjected to high-frequency electromagnetic transients, such as lightning impulses. A comparative simulation approach was implemented using COMSOL Multiphysics to analyze both real and perfect conductor configurations of the bushing. The study applies a lightning impulse waveform to evaluate electric field behavior along the bushing structure. Results show that conductor geometry and internal foil arrangement significantly influence field intensity, with localized stress concentrations emerging before the onset of dominant skin effect. Additionally, the output pulse exhibits a damping effect, indicating energy dissipation over time due to bushing impedance characteristics. These findings provide insights for improving insulation coordination, optimizing bushing design, and enhancing surge protection strategies in high-voltage power systems.

title: Detection and Classification of Parkinson’s Disease using Machine Learning
authors: Mohsina Mehrin, Md. Rokonuzzaman Mim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a progressive neurological disorder that affects motor and speech functions. Early detection of PD is very important for proper treatment and disease management. In recent years, machine learning techniques have shown promising performance in disease prediction using biomedical data. In this study, several machine learning algorithms including Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Naïve Bayes, XGBoost, AdaBoost, and Gradient Boost were used to classify Parkinson’s disease using a dataset from the UCI Machine Learning Repository. Initially, the models were trained using the original dataset, but the performance was not satisfactory. Therefore, two feature selection methods, correlation matrix with heatmap and information gain, were applied to improve classification performance. The models were evaluated using accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity. After feature selection, the performance of the models improved significantly. Among all models, XGBoost achieved the highest accuracy while Random Forest showed the highest sensitivity. The results indicate that feature selection plays an important role in improving Parkinson’s disease detection accuracy

title: The IEEE Vancouver Joint Transportation Chapter [ITS Chapters]
authors: David G. Michelson
publish: IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: A Compact mmWave Inset-Fed Rectangular Microstrip Patch Antenna for High-Efficiency Breast Tumor Detection
authors: Shamiul Ashraf Shoishob, Monowar Mahmud, Atikur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: This paper proposes a compact inset-fed rectangular microstrip patch antenna <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.7 \times 4.7 \times 0.508 \text{mm}^{3})$</tex> for non-invasive breast tumor detection in the mmWave Q-band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(30-40 \text{GHz})$</tex>. Designed in CST Studio Suite 2019, it achieves high radiation efficiency (91.65%), wide bandwidth (14.1 GHz), and a low SAR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.445 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg})$</tex>. Simulated within a realistic three-layer breast phantom, the antenna effectively detects tumors as small as 1.5 mm by analyzing return loss, transmission coefficient, and signal attenuation. Its compact size, high efficiency, and diagnostic precision make it well-suited for integration into mmWave biomedical imaging systems.

title: High-Order Epistasis Detection Using Factorization Machine with Quadratic-Optimization Annealing and MDR-Based Evaluation
authors: Shuta Kikuchi, Shu Tanaka
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Detecting high-order epistasis is a fundamental challenge in genetic association studies due to the combinatorial explosion of candidate locus combinations. Although multifactor dimensionality reduction (MDR) is a widely used method for evaluating epistasis, exhaustive MDR-based searches become computationally infeasible as the number of loci or the interaction order increases. In this paper, we define the epistasis detection problem as a black-box optimization problem and solve it with a factorization machine with quadratic-optimization annealing (FMQA). We propose an efficient epistasis detection method based on FMQA, in which the classification error rate (CER) computed by MDR is used as a black-box objective function. Experimental evaluations were conducted using simulated casecontrol datasets with predefined high-order epistasis. The results demonstrate that the proposed method successfully identified ground-truth epistasis across various interaction orders and the numbers of genetic loci within a limited number of iterations. These results indicate that the proposed method is effective and computationally efficient for high-order epistasis detection.

title: Dissolved Hydrogen Prediction in Oil-Immersed Transformers Using FEM-Based Fault-Scenario Data Augmentation and TCN-Informer
authors: Jinrui Kang, Hanbo Zheng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Dissolved gas analysis (DGA) is important for transformer condition assessment, but real fault samples are scarce and fault-state scenarios are difficult to cover, which limits purely data-driven prediction methods. To support prediction-oriented functions in transformer digital twins (DTs), this paper develops a finite element method (FEM)-based electro-thermal-fluid multiphysics model to generate dissolved-hydrogen evolution data under representative fault durations and ambient temperatures. Based on the resulting simulation-generated fault-state dataset, a hybrid predictor combining a temporal convolutional network (TCN) and Informer is constructed for one-step-ahead forecasting of hydrogen concentration at the oil sampling port. The TCN extracts local temporal features and short-term dependencies, while Informer captures longer-range sequence correlations, enabling joint modeling of local fluctuations and global evolution trends. Experimental results under rolling sequential evaluation show that the proposed method achieves the best performance among the compared models, with a root mean square error (RMSE) of 2.713347 and a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.312037. Compared with the second-best model, TCN-LSTM, RMSE and MAE are reduced by 6.54% and 12.56%, respectively. The results demonstrate that physics-based fault-scenario data generation combined with a hybrid sequence predictor is a feasible technical path for DT-oriented dissolved-hydrogen prediction.

title: BIE: Bipedal Robots Using Implicit-Explicit Learning Framework
authors: Cheng Xing, Xiaopeng Chen, Yuhan Qiu, Qi Deng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Bipedal robots possess the unique capability to adapt human-centric environments, yet achieving agile and robust locomotion on complex terrains remains a formidable challenge due to their inherent instability and the noise associated with visual perception. Current approaches often struggle to effectively integrate high-dimensional visual data with precise motor control, leading to conservative behaviors or failure in harsh environments. In this paper, we propose a novel learning-based framework that synergizes a Teacher-Student architecture with a dual-level Implicit-Explicit learning mechanism to address these limitations. Our method leverages a teacher policy trained with privileged information to guide a student policy that operates solely on proprioceptive and noisy visual inputs. Crucially, we incorporate an implicit-explicit estimation module within the student network: the explicit component reconstructs terrain features to provide a direct understanding of the environment, while the implicit component captures latent dynamic parameters to compensate for actuation and sensing uncertainties. This hybrid approach enhances the student's ability to generalize across diverse terrains by aligning both behavioral policies and state representations. We validate our framework through extensive simulation and real-world experiments on a bipedal robot. Results demonstrate that our method achieves great performance in traversing challenging terrains with high agility and robustness, successfully bridging the sim-to-real gap without requiring complex pre-mapping.

title: Building a Balanced Deepfake Dataset: Aligned Faces for Robust Model Training and Evaluation
authors: Md Raisul Islam, Mohammad Monirul Islam, MD. Aminul Islam Rakib, Md. Shihab Mahmud Anik, Amit Kumar Kundu, Md. Golam Morshed Tuhin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Deepfake media—highly realistic AI-generated face swaps—pose a growing threat to the authenticity of digital content. As a contribution towards research into detecting such forgeries, we introduce a deepfake video dataset that is large-scale and well-annotated. It comprises 480 videos (110,694 frames) which are equally split between genuine and forged content. Deepfake videos were generated with two modern face-swap workflows (open-source Roop Face-Swapper and commercial Akool AI tool), worked on the videos of 30 volunteers (15 males, 15 females). Frames were downsampled at 5 fps, and a face detector (MTCNN) was used for cropping and aligning a main face to extract <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3, 7 4 6}$</tex> real-face images and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 6, 9 4 8}$</tex> fake-face images. It is demographically and generation-method balanced which can serve as a diverse reference for deepfake detection. Ethical clearance was gained from Daffodil International University. The whole dataset, including images, labels and metadata, is accessible to the research community. Our dataset is unique from previous works such as FaceForensics++ and Celeb-DF due to the demographic diversity, multi-pipeline generation, and transparent creation pipeline. To illustrate its effectiveness, a custom CNN classifier trained on the dataset achieved an accuracy of 97.8% at differentiating between real and fake faces. For the sake of transparency and reproducibility, we present full information of the recording, generation, and preprocessing pipeline.

title: Multi-Shot Quantum Sensing for RF Signal Detection with MIMO Rydberg-Atom Receivers
authors: Saman Atapattu, Harini Hapuarachchi, Nathan Ross
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Rydberg-atom quantum receivers (RAQRs) enable electric-field sensing with quantum-noise-limited performance, yet their optical readout provides only magnitude measurements whose fluctuations follow Rician statistics governed by atomic projection noise, optical shot noise, reference-field injection, and short coherence times. These non-Gaussian, phase-blind measurements invalidate classical single-shot RF detectors and necessitate multi-shot quantum sensing strategies. This work develops a physically consistent multi-shot statistical model for RAQRs and derives both the optimal genie-aided likelihood-ratio test (LRT) and a practical phase-averaged LRT that removes dependence on the unknown RF-field phase.
