Closed-form test statistics and thresholds are obtained for both detectors, and the limits imposed by finite quantum shots-due to atomic dephasing and measurement backaction-are explicitly quantified. A fully non-coherent energy detector is also analysed, with exact detection probability derived using noncentral chi-square models. Monte Carlo results show that only 5-10 quantum shots yield major gains: the phase-averaged LRT closely approaches the genie bound and RAQR detection markedly outperforms classical RF energy detection under comparable received power. The proposed framework provides the first unified statistical basis for multi-shot Rydberg-based weak-field detection and underscores the potential of RAQRs for quantum-enhanced signal detection.

title: FCS-MPC Based Dual-Mode Control Strategy for Stability Enhancement of Multi-Converter System
authors: Lingyu Wu, Li Zhen, Chengbo Zhang, Jiawei Liu, Yingqi Wang, Zhenbin Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy, power systems frequently exhibit weak grid characteristics. Although dual-mode control enables adaptive operation, line impedance mismatches in multi-converter clusters often induce oscillatory instability after the system transitions to the gridforming mode. This paper identifies that these mismatches generate circulating currents, creating a destabilizing positive feedback loop with high grid impedance. To address this, a composite control framework is proposed. It features an improved gridforming strategy integrating reactive power sharing and voltage recovery loops to decouple control objectives, alongside Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control for fast inner-loop dynamics. Simulation results confirm that the proposed strategy achieves precise power sharing and smooth mode switching, ensuring robust stability even under extremely weak grid conditions.

title: Intelligence-Assisted Site Selection for Military Installation Planning
authors: Connie N. Summers, Mary Margaret Mitchell, Hyeyon Y. Bastian, Patrick R. Ables, Randy K. Buchanan, Susan Wolters
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The inherently complex, time-intensive, and manual nature of military planning often results in inflexible master plans ill-suited to rapidly evolving mission requirements. This disconnect hinders effective adaptation and can compromise mission success. Recent advances in automated data flows and artificial intelligence (AI) offer a promising solution. An AI-enabled tool that incorporates mission requirements, restrictions on developmental land, and planning and facility design standards would empower installation planners to rapidly identify optimal locations for asset development, sustainment, restoration, and modernization on military installations, significantly reducing planning time while enabling a more agile and responsive investment decision support, ultimately enhancing mission effectiveness at the speed of relevance.

title: A Time/Utility Function for Scheduling Quantum Applications
authors: Luca Marchese, Bethany Davies, Stephanie Wehner
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We introduce the concept of quantum time/utility functions (TUF) to enable effective scheduling decisions when executing quantum applications. The quantum TUF captures the relationship between the projected execution quality of an application and its execution time, enabling real-time, noise-aware scheduling. We consider different strategies for computing the TUF while addressing practical constraints, and introduce the Haar TUF, a simple, application-agnostic formula for random circuits. We implement the Haar TUF for a delegated quantum computing application, where we showcase both its ability to capture meaningful behaviour and its limitations in describing application-specific instances.

title: WASTEWISE: Towards an Eco-Friendly Sustainable Waste Management System for Decomposable and Long-Life Waste
authors: Sirajum Munira, Ohidujjaman, Mahmudul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Waste management is a growing environmental concern worldwide today. Every day, Bangladesh generates increasing amounts of waste. But ineffective waste segregation directly undermines environmental sustainability and public health outcomes. Addressing this issue, this study represents a deep learning approach to automatically classify waste according to its decomposition nature. We evaluated CNN, VGG19, and InceptionV3 models with a custom, real-world dataset collected from households, streets, and the local environment of Bangladesh. Among these, InceptionV3 showed the best overall accuracy and generalisation, at 98.76 %. The findings of this study highlight how effectively deep learning can help to develop low-cost automated waste-sorting systems that can improve smart waste handling, recycling efficiency and sustainable environment.

title: Socio-Economic-Technical Synthesis: A Novel Triple Bridge Framework for Quantifying the Impact of Organizational Knowledge Gaps on Technical System Failures
authors: Raymond K. Jonkers
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The primary challenge in managing systemic risk is the policy-to-physics gap; the inability to quantitatively link high-level organizational decisions to low-level technical system outcomes. This paper addresses this gap by proposing the SD-DoE-ML Triple Bridge that shows how project failures are symptoms of a deeper systemic failure: the inability of traditional methodologies to effectively integrate and manage socio-economic-technical factors and risks. Furthermore, engineering disasters, from the Challenger space shuttle to the Titan submersible, confirm that fragmented information and organizational blind spots are the ultimate cause of failures. Preventing project failures and engineering disasters requires a fundamental paradigm shift in methodology. This paper proposes the SD-DoE-ML Triple Bridge, a novel approach to enable robust design optimization (RDO), improve enterprise communication, and establish systemic resilience. This Triple Bridge fuses system dynamics (SD), design of experiments (DoE), and AI machine learning (ML) to create a high-fidelity predictive digital twin of a coupled management flight simulator (MFS) and model-based systems engineering (MBSE) ecosystem. This expanded ecosystem is uniquely capable of achieving causal discovery by translating non-linear socio-economic-technical factors into quantitative integrated DoE and ML features. These features play into standard project management (PM) and systems engineering (SE) management curves. The proposed framework is validated through non-linear response surface methodology (RSM) that maps the interaction between management-side knowledge investment and system-level probability of failure. Results indicate that strategic gaming serves as a critical policy lever, where earlystage resource allocation yields a significant reduction in systemic risk. By quantifying the diminishing returns of these interventions, the model provides a decision-support system (DSS) for balancing organizational policy with technical resilience requirements. This creates a bidirectional digital thread that provides prescriptive policy levers to ensure systemic robustness and resilience against escalating project stressors

title: TCAD-Based Variability Analysis of Cylindrical NVDRAM and VNAND Architectures with 3D Multi-Granular Polycrystalline Ferroelectric–Dielectric Phases
authors: Bellala Sai Sampath, Sarita Yadav, Sourav De, Govind Bajpai, Anand Bulusu, Kai Ni, Nitanshu Chauhan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This work investigates the influence of random fluctuations and the interaction between randomly shaped dielectric (DE) and ferroelectric (FE) phases, modelled using Voronoi grains within the ferroelectric layer of cylindrical Metal-Ferroelectric-Metal (MFM) capacitors, Non-Volatile DRAM (NVDRAM), and vertical NAND FeFET structures. The study reveals: 1.) The inclusion of a DE phase introduces non-uniformity and leads to a reduction in the overall polarization of the FE layer. 2.) Larger Voronoi FE grain sizes show decreased variability (low <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma / \mu$</tex>) in MFM capacitor performance. 3.) The presence of a DE phase induces additional variability in NVDRAM, causing a decrease in the bitline read margin for bit '0' and bit '1'. 4.) Despite DE phase introduction, NVDRAM retains a superior bitline read margin compared to conventional DRAM, even at 50% DE phase. 5.) The effect of the DE phase on the memory window characteristics (low and high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{th}}$</tex>) of vertical NAND FeFET is also analyzed.

title: Horizontal Thruster-Based VTOL Imposition for Universal Flight Control System of Existing Vehicle
authors: Meer Shadman Shafkat Tanjim, Ashrafun Nushra Oishi, M Nafees Emtiaz Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Swamped metropolitan area always faces the daily rush and high vehicle usage. Emergency transportation, road blockage, armed security become an issue in that circumstance. Roads or highways' planning and public transportation sector never remain hassle-free from the problematic issues along with the traffic jam. Overpassing few significant vehicles with the proposed system of this research will subdue the problems and increase the height of comfort in current vehicle's flight system concept. Bernoulli's principle and duct theory are the major inspiration for this research. By this universal Flight Control System (FCS), a balanced, portable, comfortable and instant flight facilitation have been introduced. With a prototype, the overall system has been demonstrated. Data have been analyzed for the final implementation on any mid-weighted vehicle. With a preliminary feature of instant flight and forward-backward movement along with left or right turns, the system can be installed on any existing vehicle. The driver can move that vehicle on the air, like a comfortable jerk-less vehicle on the road and seamlessly land wherever it is needed. A major solution for national emergency, military activity, and luxury sector can be facilitated with this horizontal thruster-based Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) imposition in some existing vehicle on demand along with hybrid driving license generation from corresponding authority.

title: Quantum Circuit for Quantum Fourier Transform for Arbitrary Qubit Connectivity Graphs Based on 1-Cover Path
authors: Kamil Khadiev, Aliya Khadieva, Vadim Sagitov, Kamil Khasanov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: In the paper, we consider quantum circuits for the Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT) algorithm. We present a generic method for constructing quantum circuits for QFT on quantum devices with restrictions. Many quantum devices (for example, based on superconductors) have restrictions on applying two-qubit gates presented by a qubit connectivity graph. Typically, researchers consider only the linear nearest neighbor (LNN) architecture, but current devices have more complex graphs. We present a method for arbitrary connected graphs that minimizes the number of CNOT gates in the circuit for implementing on such architecture.

title: Bio-Inspired Divergent Stereo SLAM with Enhanced Rotational Robustness_supp1-3690993.mp4
authors: Bin Han
publish: N/A
abstract: Robust visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (vSLAM) is essential for autonomous navigation, yet a limited field of view (FoV) leads to feature loss during fast rotations, degrading tracking and localization performance. Traditional parallel stereo vSLAM offers limited FoV due to high camera overlap, resembling monocular systems. Existing FoV extension methods, based on wide-angle lenses or multi-camera setups, suffer from distortion or high system complexity. Inspired by navigation-oriented species, which use divergent eye placements to expand their FoV, we propose a divergent stereo vSLAM system that uses two outward-facing cameras with partial overlap, thereby extending the overall FoV. Traditional stereo systems use the left camera for primary vision and the right for depth estimation, limiting the use of the divergent configuration. To overcome this, we propose divergent stereo modeling, treating the cameras as virtual cameras to improve depth estimation and wide-angle perception. Although multi-camera systems can adopt a divergent configuration, existing methods overlook inter-camera correlation. We address this with a cross-camera map point association and a dual-map bundle adjustment (BA) with fixed extrinsics to maintain consistency and reduce redundancy. Experimental results on simulated and real-world datasets show that our framework improves localization accuracy (ATE) by 65.8% over parallel stereo and achieves superior rotational robustness. All code will be open source.

title: Hybrid AI-FACTS Optimization for Nova Scotia Grid: A Physics-Informed Deep Learning Approach for Real-Time Voltage Stability Enhancement
authors: Mahmoud Kiasari, Hamed Aly
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The rapid decarbonization of modern power systems coupled with the necessity to integrate large amounts of intermittent renewable energy sources (IRES) (Like wind power) into transmission grids under a reduction of synchronous inertia. This transition causes severe problems in power quality, such as voltage instability, reactive power shortage, harmonics, and short-circuit strength. This paper proposes an extensive hybrid AI -- FACTS control framework specifically tuned to the Nova Scotia (NS) Power Grid, which is a representative weak grid system that experiences voltage deviation at major load centers because of variable wind production from geographically spread sites. The proposed architecture involves coupling of physics-informed neural network (PINN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) recurrent units to provide robust 15 min Ahead Voltage Magnitude Forecasting and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) controller for coordinated real time dispatching of Static Var Compensators (SVC) and Static Synchronous Compensators (STATCOM). Unlike traditional black box models for machine learning, PINN incorporate the fundamental laws of AC power flow (i.e., Kirchhoff’s Current Law and nodal power balance) directly into the training goal, which means that the predictions inherently meet the physical constraints and safety bounds. Extensive simulations on a detailed 6-Bus reduced-order model of Nova Scotia transmission backbone, validated with actual historical data (load profiles collected from PJM Interconnection and wind speed time series collected from NREL Wind Integration Database) demonstrate that the hybrid AI-FACTS framework reduces average voltage deviation by $67.7 \%$, decreases transmission power losses by $7.7 \%$, and maintains the total harmonic distortion (THD) within the compliance limits of the international standards, standard 519 (IEEE-519-2014) significantly more than the original decentralized PI-based FACTS control. The system achieves a computational efficiency appropriate for edge deployment with an inference latency of less than 50 ms per control cycle on commodity hardware.

title: A Distributed System to Analyze the Impacts of Aircraft Predictive Maintenance
authors: Samridhi Girdhar, Peash Ranjan Saha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Predictive maintenance for an aircraft enforces unscheduled repairs or replacement of any mechanical components. It complements routine maintenance for an aircraft. Predictive maintenance is crucial, as it utilizes real-time data while an aircraft is in operation, unlike scheduled maintenance. However, it may have a significant impact on passengers’ experiences, such as an unexpected flight delay. It also connects airports’ external services, such as personal support for passengers. It is not certain that every airline always performs predictive maintenance, as it may have long-term consequences for their operation. Some airlines may prioritize short-term or immediate fixes instead. An analysis to unfold the passengers’ experiences due to predictive maintenance is paramount. In this research, a distributed system is designed to analyze real-time maintenance and operational data for airports. The system constructs a database for each region with one or multiple airports. It deploys a dedicated server for an airport in a distributed fashion and performs partitioning of the central data for the region. Airport-specific external factors are also merged in the impact analysis. Some analytics is performed to explore the patterns of passengers’ experience for a region. We implemented the system in the Hadoop distributed database framework (HDFS) integrated with MongoDB, and experimented with synthetic instances. The results show some insights into passenger impact for different aircraft and flight types. A complete system would be a useful guide for airlines to make informed decisions on improving passengers’ experiences while performing predictive maintenance.

title: Monte Carlo-Based EM Lifetime Modeling of Power Delivery Network with Redundant BGA Pins
authors: Jae-Gyung Ahn, Omkar Gupte, Jim O'Donnell, Jonathan Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: High-power Integrated Circuits (ICs) with redundant Ball-Grid Arrays (BGAs) demand accurate Electro-Migration (EM) lifetime estimation. This work presents a Monte Carlo methodology that incorporates lognormal variability, the physics of parallel resistance networks, and dynamic current redistribution to realistically model EM behavior in Power Delivery Networks (PDNs). Applied to 20- and 38-pin PDNs, the approach captures the impact of redundancy on stress currents, IR drop, and temperature increase due to Joule heating (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{T}_{\text{JHE}}$</tex>), demonstrating that leveraging redundancy can increase the PDN EM lifetime by up to a factor of four compared with the conventional series-system models.

title: Teleportation of Coherent-State Qubits with Asymmetric and Lossy Entanglement Distribution
authors: Davide Andromari, Andrea Giani, Moe Z. Win, Andrea Conti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Quantum teleportation enables transferring quantum states by leveraging entanglement. The distribution of entangled states is challenged by decoherence, which degrades the teleportation performance. Continuous-variable teleportation with coherent-state qubits has gained particular interest because it can provide better performance than discrete-variable teleportation with polarization-encoded qubits and is more resilient to decoherence. However, most existing schemes employ a specific configuration of coherent-state qubits, namely Schrödinger cat states, and assume symmetric photon-loss channels. This work explores the teleportation of qubits based on coherent states through asymmetric quantum channels impaired by photon loss. First, we characterize the teleportation protocol for arbitrary coherent-state qubit configurations considering asymmetric and photon-loss channels. Then, we present case studies under both ideal entanglement distribution and lossy conditions. Results reveal a trade-off between state distinguishability and teleportation fidelity, providing insight into the optimal design of coherent-state qubits in practical teleportation protocols.

title: Multi-Label Thoracic Disease Detection and Weakly Supervised Localization in Chest X-Rays Using DenseNet121 and Probabilistic CAM Pooling
authors: Ahmad Fahmid, Abdul Ahad, Sanjida Rahman, Shaharia Ferdausi, Aashish K C, S M Sabbir Hossain Pranto, Kazi Tanvir Akter, Dewan Aminul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Chest X-ray (CXR) interpretation is essential for diagnosing multiple thoracic diseases, yet it requires specialized expertise and is susceptible to human error. This paper presents a deep learning system for multi-label disease classification and weakly supervised disease-related region localization in CXRs, utilizing DenseNet121 with Probabilistic Class Activation Mapping (PCAM) pooling. Trained on the CheXpert dataset, the model predicts five key conditions—cardiomegaly, edema, consolidation, atelectasis, and pleural effusion—with high performance (AUC scores ranging from 0.8703 to 0.9436), while localizing disease-relevant regions without pixel-level annotations. Deployed via a Flask web application, the system demonstrates both diagnostic accuracy and practical usability in clinical work-flows. We evaluate its classification and localization performance, visualize interpretability results, and discuss its clinical relevance and future potential.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Class Medical Disease Classification Using DenseNet121
authors: Al Arian Ahmad, Salehin Ahmad, Durjoy Kumar Dutta, Md. Abdul Wadud, Nymur Rahaman Antor, Nakib Aman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Accurate identification of rare and clinically significant disease categories is a critical challenge in modern medical diagnostics. In this study, a deep learning-based framework is presented for automated detection and classification of 25 disease categories, encompassing diverse syndromes and pathological conditions. We evaluated two state-of-the-art convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, DenseNet121 and MobileNetV2, in combination with Explainable AI (XAI) techniques to ensure both high predictive performance and model interpretability. Experimental results reveal that DenseNet121 achieves superior performance with an overall classification accuracy of 99.82%, outperforming MobileNetV2 in diagnostic precision. The integration of XAI provides meaningful visual explanations of model decisions, enhancing transparency and clinical trustworthiness. These findings demonstrate the potential of deep learning coupled with explainability to provide robust and interpretable diagnostic support tools, paving the way for improved early detection and better clinical decision-making across multiple disease categories.

title: HQCPA: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Parallel Architecture for Quantum Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xinliang Wei, Kejiang Ye, Cheng-Zhong Xu, Yu Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel hybrid quantumclassical parallel architecture (HQCPA) designed specifically for quantum reinforcement learning. The architecture effectively combines the strengths of both quantum and classical computing paradigms, aiming to develop a more efficient hybrid quantum reinforcement learning model. The concept of this architecture is founded on the hybrid sequential architecture, where the classical neural network serves as either a pre-processing or postprocessing module. By partitioning the intermediate sequential quantum layer into multiple parallel layers, the proposed model enables more precise control over the model's architecture. This not only enhances the flexibility in model design but also mitigates the computational errors that typically arise from an increase in the number of qubits and circuit depth. In addition, a flexible data re-uploading structure for HQCPA, denoted as HQCPADRU, is introduced to enhance the inference performance. Furthermore, a hybrid quantum-classical reinforcement learning (HQCRL) framework is implemented. This framework integrates the proposed HQCPA and HQCPA-DRU models, along with other classical RL algorithms. Comprehensive simulation results validate the efficacy of the proposed HQCPA and HQCPA-DRU. In terms of average training and inference performance, these models outperform other benchmark models by up to 18.7 %.

title: Multi-Class MRI-Based Brain Tumor Classification Using EfficientNet-B0 with Enhanced Interpretability and Clinical Applicability
authors: Md. Abu Johab, Ahmad Al Arian, Nakib Aman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: One of the most serious neurological disorders is brain tumors, and the patients with these disorders must be identified appropriately and with precision as quickly as possible. The paper compares 15 -class brain tumor classification based on the MRI image using four deep learning architectures, namely, EfficientNet-B0, ResNet50, Vision Transformer and DenseNet121. The dataset, consisting of 4,456 MRI images across 15 tumor classes, was preprocessed and augmented to address class imbalance before model training. Of the models assessed, EfficientNet-B0 provided the most accurate performance with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.35 \%, 0.9853$</tex> precision, 0.9580 recall, and 0.9686 of F1-score and provided the best performance in both the accuracy and performance levels. ROC, precision-recall charts, and Grad-CAM visualizations further demonstrate its robustness and reliability, and thus have a great potential of being used in clinical decision support when diagnosing brain tumors in a multi-class approach. Future work is to incorporate multi-modal and 3D MRI data, use more sophisticated domain adaptation methods and to do a lot of real world clinical validation. Furthermore, the integration of the emerging XAI brings an additional view of clarity and exposes the healthcare professional to its trustworthiness and widespread adoption in the various clinical setups.

title: Research on Digital Twin Modeling and Multi-Domain Co-simulation Technology for Mechatronics Automated Production Lines in Smart Manufacturing
authors: Xiangheng Kong
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Addressing the pain points of traditional production lines, such as low design and debugging efficiency, high operation and maintenance costs, and insufficient accuracy of multi-domain coupled simulation, in the context of smart manufacturing and the need for digital transformation of mechatronics automated production lines, this paper proposes a full-element digital twin modeling and multi-domain co-simulation technology for production lines. The core objective is to construct a high-precision, dynamically calibrated digital twin model for engineering implementation, achieving seamless integration and co-simulation optimization of multi-domain models based on the systematic integration of well-established methodological frameworks. Key technologies include hierarchical digital twin architecture design, multi-physical attribute modeling, data-driven model calibration, and multi-domain time synchronization and joint solution optimization. Experimental verification was conducted using an automotive parts mechatronics production line as an example. This research provides technical support for the digital upgrade of automated production lines and has significant engineering application value.

title: SafeRide: A Real-Time IoT-Based Smart Helmet with Road-Condition Intelligence
authors: Mohammed Sakib Hasan, Md Abdur Rakib, Jihadul Islam, Md Mohidul Alam, Md Abdul Jobayer, Md Mahbubur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Motorcycle riders are among the most vulnerable road users, often facing a high risk of accidents and fatalities. These risks arise from unsafe riding habits, poor road conditions, and delays in emergency response. Many existing smart helmet systems focus on single features such as alcohol detection or accident alerts, but lack a complete and scalable solution. To address these issues, this paper proposes a low-cost, realtime IoT-enabled smart helmet system that combines multiple safety and awareness features into one framework. The system integrates helmet-wear detection, alcohol monitoring, drowsiness detection, rear-vehicle proximity alerts, accident and emergency notifications, and weather-based warnings. A new road-condition prediction model using machine learning is introduced, which classifies upcoming roads as excellent, good, poor, or very poor based on GPS data. The helmet and motorcycle units are synchronized to control ignition and share data in real-time, while hazard information is uploaded to Firebase for community alerts. A smartphone application provides riders with live sensor data, navigation, and voice-based notifications to reduce distractions. Additionally, the SafeRide dataset of road conditions has been developed to support large-scale road-quality prediction across Bangladesh. Experimental results show that the proposed system is effective in improving rider safety and enabling intelligent transportation solutions.

title: W-State Leader Election for PBFT Based Quantum Leader Election
authors: Juan Pablo Robledo Meza, Jelena Mišić, Vojislav B. Mišić
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We propose a quantum leader election protocol for high-traffic permissioned blockchains, leveraging multipartite <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$W$</tex>-states combined with a CSMA/CA-style INTENT/ACK handshake. Our design distributes a full W-state locally to each validator, enabling collision detection through single-qubit measurement and ensuring fairness without requiring global entanglement distribution. We formalize three approaches to improve performance, Exponential Backoff, Fixed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$W$</tex>-state, and Closing Window, respectively, and analyze their expected termination times, showing that the Closing Window variant provides the best convergence behavior. Using Qiskit simulations, we evaluate correctness and demonstrate that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$W$</tex>-state-based election integrates naturally with PBFT-style consensus layers.

title: Aging Aware Body Biasing Technique for NBTI-Mitigation in Digital LDO Regulator
authors: Kartikay Mani Tripathi, Madhav Pathak, Sanjeev Manhas, Anand Bulusu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: Negative bias temperature instability (NBTI) is a significant reliability issue with the PMOS devices, increasing their threshold voltage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{V}_{\text{TH}}\right)$</tex> over time. Digital Low-Dropout Voltage Regulators (DLDOs), favored for their compatibility with low supply voltages and ease of integration, are vulnerable to aging-induced degradation. This paper analyzes the degradation of load transient performance and load current capacity of DLDOs due to NBTI-induced aging. We also propose an Aging-Aware Body Biasing (A-ABB) technique that utilizes Forward Body Bias (FBB) to reduce the body potential and compensate for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{TH}}$</tex> degradation in the PMOS array. The A-ABB technique is implemented in a commercial 22nm FDSOI CMOS process and is validated through circuit-level simulations for a 5-bit DLDO under pre- and post-aging stress equivalent to five years of NBTI degradation. Results show that without A-ABB, the DLDO experiences a 10% reduction in maximum load current capacity and a 9.6% increase in voltage droop during transients. However, these metrics are restored to near pre-aging conditions with the proposed A-ABB at a minimal area/power overhead.

title: Three-Class Logistic Regression for Mental Health Risk Detection on Social Media
authors: Farzana Afrin Hafsa, Sunjida Khaton, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: People often share their deepest emotions on social media, including loneliness, depression, and even thoughts of self-harm. Many of these posts remain unnoticed, leaving individuals without the support they may urgently need. This study presents a simple but effective three-class model for identifying mental health risks in social media posts. Using two real Reddit datasets, we classify posts into Safe, Warning, and Harmful categories to capture both early distress and severe risk. After preprocessing the text and applying TF-IDF features with a multinomial Logistic Regression classifier, the model achieved 98% accuracy and strong F1-scores across all classes. Introducing the Warning class allows the system to detect early signs of emotional struggle that traditional two-class methods often miss. The proposed approach is lightweight, interpretable, and suitable for large-scale monitoring. Our findings highlight the importance of early-detection tools in creating safer online spaces and supporting timely mental-health interventions.

title: Malicious Command Line Detection
authors: Emilie Coote Atlas, Taylor Perkins, Brian Lachine, Chandra Majumdar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Given the significant amount of encrypted traffic used in enterprise environments, host-based monitoring is a necessity in any intrusion detection strategy. Important hostbased artifacts are the command lines executed on host systems, whether they are used for regular system operation and administration or for malicious purposes. Researchers have investigated various machine learning approaches to improve malicious command-line detection. This work sought to achieve detection capabilities comparable with contemporary research that could also be realistically used in a production SOC environment that continuously processes significant amounts of host data. The aim of this research was to develop a light weight malicious command line detector with effective detection performance that could be leveraged in a production SOC environment. This research used malicious command line samples from two open-source datasets and host-based data from two different corporate production networks. The developed solution did not require any de-obfuscation and first conducted data preprocessing using Bloom filters and fuzzy matching for efficient whitelisting, then used RoBERTa for text representation followed by k-NN clustering to identify malicious command lines. Four different experiments were conducted, including separate client and malicious combinations between training and testing to validate and demonstrate the generalization of the proposed solution. The implemented detector achieved results comparable to contemporary research, achieving F1 scores ranging from 97.9% to 99.3% when tested on enterprise production network data.

title: Autonomous Exploration Planning using Directed Single-Source Shortest Paths
authors: Fabrício C. S. Xavier, Sérgio R. Barros Dos Santos, Sidney N. Givigi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Autonomous exploration requires mobile robots to continuously decide where to navigate next and plan how to get there. Traditionally, these decision-making and path-planning tasks are handled separately to save computational power, relying on straight-line distance estimates to choose the best destination. However, in obstacle-dense environments, these simple estimates often fail, trapping the robot in inefficient paths. Relying on exact topological distances instead would solve this, but standard exact methods hit a mathematical bottleneck-scaling as $\mathcal{O}(m+n \log n)$, where n is the number of grid vertices and m is the number of edges. This complexity makes them too slow for high-resolution mapping. This paper presents a Unified Exploration Planner that overcomes this limitation by integrating a directed single-source shortest path algorithm with a sublogarithmic complexity of $\mathcal{O}\left(m \log ^{2 / 3} n\right)$. By computing the exact travel cost for the entire mapped area in a single pass, our architecture eliminates the need for rough distance estimates and merges destination selection with optimal trajectory generation. Simulations in CoppeliaSim demonstrate that this approach successfully navigates complex layouts by generating trajectories that adhere to the true topological cost. Furthermore, we demonstrate an inverse stabilization effect: as the robot maps more of the environment, the computational effort per node actually decreases, ensuring scalable and efficient long-term operations.

title: Laser-Guided Control for Multicopter UAVs: Preliminary Design and Testing
authors: Ruaa Nakkar, Hassan Abid, Syed Saad Azhar Ali, Mohamed Ismail
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: This paper presents a preliminary study of a laserguided, vision-based framework for evaluating and tuning multicopter trajectory tracking. A floor line and a UAV-projected laser dot are captured by a downward-facing onboard camera, and an offline image-processing pipeline computes the signed lateral deviation between them for proportional-integral-derivative (PID) tuning. We compare the vision-derived error directly against OptiTrack on straight X/Y trajectories at fixed altitude. Experiments show that the vision metric gives more repeatable and less variable lateral-error signals than OptiTrack, with mid-range proportional and derivative gains yielding the most stable tracking, while excessive integral action degrades performance.Overall, the method delivers a practical, vision-based means for controller tuning and lays the groundwork for future real-time visual control of UAVs.

title: Predictive Modeling of Wildfire Risk in California, North America, using Machine Learning Ensembles
authors: Walid Hakimzada, Colton Carvalho, Craig Beaubien, Thanh Le, Bonaventure Chidube Molokwu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Wildfires in California are increasing in frequency and severity due to climate change and prolonged drought, highlighting the need for accurate daily ignition prediction. This study presents a weather-only machine learning framework using a 41-year (1984-2025) statewide daily meteorological dataset to predict whether any wildfire ignition occurs on a given day. The approach addresses seasonal cyclicity, lagged weather effects, and moderate class imbalance through temporal feature engineering, cyclic encoding, normalization, SMOTE balancing, and stratified 5-fold cross-validation. Among evaluated models, a tuned Random Forest performed best, achieving Precision $=0.6032$, Recall $=0.7646, \mathrm{~F} 1=0.6744, \mathrm{MCC}=0.4913$, and ROC-AUC $=0.8211$ on the test set, with minimum temperature and seasonal signals as key predictors. Results show that combining temporal features with imbalance-aware ensemble methods substantially improves ignition forecasting, offering a scalable foundation for daily wildfire risk awareness and potential integration into operational workflows.

title: Federated Learning for Rural Healthcare Diagnosis
authors: Martala Abiram Reddy, Shaik Mohammed Humza, Usama Abdur Rahman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Federated learning has become a potential paradigm of privacy-preserving machine learning in a healthcare setting where sensitive patient information could not be centrally distributed. This paper introduces a patient monitoring system that is federated learning-based and applied with a distributed edge-cloud platform with the use of classical machine learning algorithms, i.e., the Support Vector Machine (SVM) and the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The suggested system embraces organized electronic health documents consisting of demographic, clinical, administrative, billing, and time characteristics of patient admission and discharge. Categorical encoding, feature normalization, temporal feature extraction, and missing value manipulation are all data preprocessing methods that are executed locally at an edge node. The training of the models takes place in isolation at the edges, so that the raw patient information does not exit the source and this maintains the privacy of the data and guarantees compliance with regulations. The cloud server only receives model parameters and then does federated aggregation to create a global model that is later sent back to edge nodes to undergo continuous refining steps. SVM can be used to deal with high-dimensional structured healthcare data effectively, and KNN is a light weight adaptive solution that fits well in edge cases. Experimental findings show that the given framework is capable of scalable, privacy-conscious, and efficient patient monitoring, which is why it can be applied to real-time healthcare analytics in distributed healthcare institutions.

title: Reducing PM-to-AM Noise Conversion in Atomic Vapors
authors: Kaitlin Harpenau, Nate Ristoff, James Camparo
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Ultrasonics, Ferroelectrics, and Frequency Control
abstract: Laser phase noise (PM) to transmitted intensity noise (AM) conversion is a fundamental aspect of the field/matter interaction, and a major noise process that limits the frequency stability of next-generation atomic clocks. It arises from the absorption cross section’s dependence on laser phase and is especially pernicious for vertical-cavity surface-emitting lasers (VCSELs) given their relatively broad singlemode linewidths (e.g., 40 MHz). This is unfortunate, since VCSELs (with their very low operating power and low probability for mode hops) are otherwise ideal for quantum devices. Here, we demonstrate that atomic spectroscopy with N VCSELs reduces the total PM-to-AM noise by a factor of ~N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1/2</sup>. Consequently, atomic clocks with (for example) five VCSELs would have a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>) approximately 120% higher than the same device operating with one VCSEL (SNR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub>) at the same total power: SNR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>/SNR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> ≌ 2.2.

title: A Study of Data-Driven Insights into the Growth and Development of India’s Contemporary Equity Market
authors: Aishwarya Tewari, Nisha Bhandari, Mukund Ramniklal Jakhiya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study employs data-driven analysis to examine the evolution of India's stock market since the implementation of the 1991 liberalization policy. At the same time, exploring the role of how foreign direct investment (FDI) and institutional investment (FII) have changed India's equity landscape, how regulatory bodies such as SEBI have been affected through the evolution and adoption of digital technologies and technological innovation, specifically looking into key trends during the COVID-19 pandemic and its impact on the online and market landscape. This will be assessed through data analysis to illustrate the technical development of the Indian equity market, highlighting the sector's progress and future prospects while providing researchers, investors, and policymakers with insights into emerging patterns and business intelligence.

title: Multiscale Deformable Attention Enhanced YOLO Framework for Cervical Cancer Cell Classification
authors: Jheelam Mondal, Rajdeep Chatterjee, Mahendra Kumar Gourisaria
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Cervical cancer is the fourth most common cancer globally and one of the most common cancers in women. Pap smear tests make it possible to find and screen cervical cancer early. The smear analysis which is done by experts, is laborious, takes a long time and is likely to make mistakes. The lack of resources and highly qualified professionals causes a low output of cervical cancer detection procedures in mainly developing and under-developed countries. Researchers are inspired to use deep learning (DL), which has recently gained popularity to diagnose cervical cancer. This research employs a hybrid convolutional neural network (CNN) and Transformer framework, wherein convolutional elements (YOLOv5 encoder and Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast layer) facilitate fast local feature extraction, while a multiscale deformable attention neck incorporates transformerstyle global contextual modeling. This integration improves representation learning by merging local spatial accuracy with adaptable long-range dependency modeling. Our proposed model achieves a test accuracy value of 96.77% using the SIPAKMED benchmark data set.

title: Efficient Stock Market Forecasting Through Hybrid Neural Architecture: Integrating Parameter Transfer Learning with Multi-Modal Feature Synthesis
authors: Sayantan Mazumder, Adyan Ali, Akil Ahmed Choudhury, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Chiranjib Parida
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Financial markets are very volatile and financial price prediction is affected in two ways: Deep models such as LSTM and Transformer require a lot of hyper-parameter tuning and market data is not uniform and consists of different datasets such as market data, technical data and macro data, as well as sentiments. To address this challenge, we propose a hybrid approach that optimizes parameters from lightweight ANNs to larger models, thereby reducing both training and cost, and combines several feature extraction streams: CNN, which is good for detecting local patterns, LSTM, which is good for modeling sequential data, and Transformers, which are good for longterm dependencies. In the course of the research, LSTM, CNN, GRU, variants of CNN-LSTM, CNN-BiLSTM, Transformer, and several tunings were considered. The simplified Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) provides the best performance and the lowest RMSE at 3.2669 and is low by 80%, which shows the potential of theoretically accurate and computationally efficient models for practical market forecasting.

title: Lung Cancer Data Feature Selection using Improved Grey Wolf Optimization with Learning Rate Adaptation (IGWO-LRA) in Data Mining
authors: N. Malathi, A. Prakash
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The increasing global incidence of lung cancer necessitates improved early diagnosis and predictive modeling. While high-dimensional biomedical datasets offer valuable insights, they often contain noisy, redundant, or irrelevant features that compromise model performance and interpretability. This paper presents an Improved Grey Wolf Optimization with Learning Rate Adaptation (IGWO-LRA) for feature selection in lung cancer data mining. Unlike dimensionality reduction techniques like Principal Component Analysis (PCA), our method preserves original feature interpretability while effectively selecting the most discriminative biomarkers. The proposed IGWO-LRA enhances conventional Grey Wolf Optimization through adaptive learning rate mechanisms and opposition-based learning, addressing critical challenges of premature convergence and local optima stagnation. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance in selecting optimal feature subsets that significantly improve prediction accuracy while reducing computational complexity. The framework proves particularly effective for structured, non-image lung cancer datasets, offering medical professionals transparent and actionable insights. Comparative evaluations show substantial improvements in classification metrics, underscoring IGWO-LRA's potential to advance precision medicine in oncology.

title: Explainable Malaria Detection with Efficientnet and XAI: a Human-Centric Diagnostic Approach
authors: Prince Nath, Ritesh Kumar Muduli, Gourab Dandapat, Anuja Kumar Acharya
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: This work presents an Explainable Malaria Detection system that integrates an EfficientNet-based classifier with interactive visual explainability and agentic diagnostic reporting for transparent, clinician-oriented malaria screening. The deployed Streamlit application allows users to compute and visualize pixel-level saliency and SmoothGrad maps on demand, adjust explanation parameters, and download overlay images, offering a fully interactive exploration of model reasoning. In parallel, an embedded large language model (LLM) generates concise, clinician-style diagnostic summaries with a local fallback template for offline use. This combination of XAI visualization, LLM-driven narrative reporting, and a polished, user-friendly interface bridges the gap between deep learning accuracy and clinical interpretability, advancing malaria AI systems toward human-in-the-loop, trustworthy diagnostics rather than opaque automation.

title: FuzionNet: Fuzzy-Enhanced VGG16 for Weed and Crop Classification
authors: M. Prasannakumar, P Anusha Devi, Muthupandi M, Surendran S, Vanitha L, M. Revathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Sustainable farming depends on the classification of weeds and crops. Although CNNs are very effective in image classification, their performance is usually constrained by the availability of datasets. This is tackled by FuzionNet which integrates deep learning, fuzzy logic and superior data augmentation methods. It provides data consistency based on real-life datasets and extensive preprocessing, whereas LeafGAN increases the adaptability of new situations. FuzionNet is able to utilize uncertainty and noise in agricultural images because of the combination of expertise through fuzzy rules, which results in a strong classification. The Fuzzy-Enhanced VGG16 architecture combines both fuzzy logic and deep learning, and it performs exceptionally well with 98.45% accuracy, better than state-of-the-art and pre-trained models. The proposed approach supports sustainable weed management, enhances crop productivity, and promotes environmentally friendly agricultural practices.

title: Anomaly Detection in BETH Dataset Using Bi-LSTM for Cybersecurity
authors: Iman Akour, Said Salloum, Amaal Al-Amawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Cybersecurity threats are constantly evolving, requiring strong anomaly detection methods. Highly imbalanced datasets in cybersecurity make it hard for typical machine learning models to detect rare anomalys, resulting in low recall. We evaluate the effectiveness of a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) model, trained on data from normal and anomalous cases, on the BETH dataset. For class imbalance, we use SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) and class weighting. It reaches a final accuracy of 90.49%, proving that it learns in a good way normal data; but it fails the detection of anomaly data, with a 15% recall. Demonstrated that additional optimization (e.g., attention mechanism, customized hybrid models, and tuning of detection threshold) is required to enhance model recognition capability of cyber threat detection. These contributions inform on potential opportunities for improving anomaly detection in real world cybersecurity settings.

title: Locomotive Scheduling Optimization Based on Bio-Inspired Algorithms
authors: Adarsh Ramachandran, Swapnesh S., Rekha K. S.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization technique for locomotive scheduling using the bio-inspired Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The locomotive scheduling problem is an important problem in railway operations management. As the cost of locomotives is high, reducing the fleet size significantly saves capital and operational costs for railways. Manual locomotive scheduling is a challenging task due to the scale of operations and the constraints involved. We have done the scheduling using both GA and PSO, and a comparative analysis is also presented. Robustness of the algorithm used for optimization is checked using Monte-Carlo analysis.

title: Entropy-Driven Multi-Scale Convolutional Neural Networks for Image-based Malware Classification
authors: K Malathi, Md Afnan Faheem K, Manoj Kumar M, Hamsaananth A N
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study presents a novel and robust framework for malware classification by integrating grayscale image transformation with an optimized Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), addressing critical gaps in real-time threat detection. Unlike conventional methods, our approach introduces a unique hierarchical feature extraction mechanism that captures intricate malware patterns with unparalleled precision. The proposed system leverages a large and diverse malware image dataset, meticulously preprocessed using advanced augmentation and normalization techniques to enhance model generalizability.A key innovation is our custom-designed CNN architecture, which combines multi-scale convolutional layers with adaptive pooling to efficiently extract discriminative features while minimizing computational overhead. Additionally, we incorporate a dynamic class-weight balancing technique to mitigate data imbalance, significantly improving detection accuracy for underrepresented malware classes. The model’s performance is rigorously evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix analysis, achieving 93% classification accuracy—a substantial improvement over existing methods.Our framework not only demonstrates superior generalization across diverse malware variants but also sets a new benchmark for real-time processing, making it a scalable solution for practical cybersecurity applications. This work represents a significant advancement in deep learning-based malware detection, offering a faster, more accurate, and adaptable system capable of combating evolving cyber threats.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning–Driven, Explainable Framework for Competition-Aware and Risk-Sensitive Athlete Nutrition
authors: Sasikanth Yangalasetty, Sai Vamsi Chennupati
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The paper introduces a Hybrid AI Systems-based, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) framework to competition conscious and risk conscious athlete nutrition as a part of a safety-critical Decision Support Systems architecture. The system combines the multimodal food knowledge, the modespecific context-sensitive reasoning and the hierarchical decision modeling to produce high-quality individualized dietary proposals based on competition dynamic constraints. RiskSensitive Modeling is added to implement the medical safety limits with underperforming readiness. The multimodal athlete nutrition datasets obtained through experimental assessment showed better predictive reliability with a 0.95 accuracy, F1-score of 0.93 and a lower Risk Violation rate at 3%. The framework has a low Expected Calibration Error of 0.021, or can be sure of probabilistic confidence in the recommendations. The explainability consistency stands at 0.92, which makes valid the consistent and transparent attribution of decisions. Feasibility in real-time is established and end-to-end latency is 52 ms on average. The findings support the efficiency of the integration of Hybrid AI Systems, Context-Aware Decision Systems, and Personalized Health Informatics to provide interpretable, risk-conscious, and competition-wise nutrition intelligence to high-performance athletics settings.

title: Enhancing Gold Price Forecast Accuracy through Feature Engineering and Financial News Sentiment Integration
authors: Rosalinda Abou Mrad, Khalil Chtaiwy, Yaacoub Khalil, Michel Owayjan, Ali Abdallah
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This research explores an enhanced approach to predicting gold prices by combining machine learning algorithms with real-world news sentiment. The project focuses on generating daily Buy, Sell, or Hold signals and predicting short-term gold price trends using models like LSTM, Prophet, and Linear Regression. To evaluate their performance, the models were trained on gold price data from January 2023 to March 2025 and tested on April 2025. Alongside price-based features, additional indicators such as moving averages, momentum, volatility, and trend signals were used. One of the key contributions of this work is the use of news sentiment— headlines and summaries were analyzed using GPT-4o to assess whether they had a likely positive, negative, or neutral impact on gold. These daily sentiment scores and event flags (e.g., mentions of inflation or geopolitical conflict) were merged with the price data to improve accuracy. Results show that adding sentiment features significantly reduced prediction error. The best-performing model, a linear regression with sentiment input, achieved a very low root mean squared error of $2.35 when forecasting April 2025 prices, showing strong potential for practical use in financial forecasting.

title: A Production Framework for Adaptive GPU Resource Management in Deep Learning Inference
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Rohit Nimmala, Milan Parikh
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Deploying AI models in production requires balancing throughput, latency, and infrastructure cost. Existing inference systems often rely on static GPU allocation and fixed batching strategies, leading to underutilized resources during low traffic and degraded performance during demand spikes. This paper presents a practical framework for adaptive AI inference that dynamically adjusts batching and GPU allocation based on real-time workload patterns. We implement the system using commodity GPU instances and evaluate it with vision and language models under varying traffic conditions. Results from experiments on NVIDIA A4000 and A5000 GPUs show throughput improvements of 1.4× to 2.1× compared to baseline configurations, with latency reductions of 18–32% during steady-state operation and 25–38% better GPU utilization. An ablation study confirms that adaptive batching and elastic GPU scheduling are complementary: neither mechanism alone captures the full benefit of their joint operation. The framework is designed for straightforward deployment in existing cloud environments without requiring specialized hardware or extensive reconfiguration.

title: Social Media Bot Detection with Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Varun HG, Vishnu Venkatesh Vasista, Ashwin PM, Yamanuri Shrishail Nad, Swati Pratap Jagdale
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Social bots are a critical threat to the integrity of digital information, user privacy, and public safety. These automated accounts commit deceptive practices, manipulate public opinion, and pave the way for fraud. Most of the conventional detection methods are based on either account metadata or simple content analysis; nuanced, multi-faceted understanding in identifying sophisticated bots mimicking human behavior is missing. This paper presents an advanced bot detection framework that relies on multimodal sentiment analysis to overcome such limitations. The system implemented here processes the textual and visual content of social media postings in a unique way. It uses a cutting-edge, multilingual XLM-RoBERTa language model for the task of text sentiment classification, while fine-tuned ResNet-50 performs the extraction of visual sentiment. A novel mechanism fuses such signals in an intelligent way into comprehensive emotional profiles, which are analyzed together with behavioral metadata for the detection of inconsistencies typical of automated accounts. This has achieved accuracies as high as 75 percent for text and 75 percent for image sentiment analysis. Our framework facilitates the integration and correlation of emotional cues from multiple modalities, which can be more resilient and accurate against evolving bot strategies-a meaningful step beyond the conventional single-modality detection systems.

title: Design and Analysis of a Low-Cost Smart Parcel Receiving System with Dual-Layer Authentication
authors: S.N. Kumbar, Rahul J. Teradal, Radhika Kiran Lohar, Muskan Abdul Latif Dhalayat, Soumya Gadag, Ganesh H Gadiwaddar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: E-commerce's rapid expansion has significantly raised the need for safe and dependable methods of parcel delivery, especially in the case of unattended deliveries that security breaches, losses, and denial of delivery still constitute the major risks. To mitigate these issues, a design and analysis solution are given in this document for a budget-friendly intelligent parcel receiving system with dual-layer authentication that can/have the ability to facilitate delivery safety and energize the handling of operations in residential environments. The main controller of the proposed system is an Arduino Uno that will use sensor data to decide, perform authentication, control the lock, and manage communication. The system is turned on by an IR sensor detecting the delivery person, while a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 3$</tex> keypad is used for OTP-based authentication to allow a safe parcel placement. A servo motor is used for automatic locking and unlocking, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 2$</tex> LCD is there to give the system's real-time feedback to the user. The owner's authentication is done with an RC522 RFID module, and the delivery and access notifications are sent using an ESP8266 Wi-Fi module. A regulated 5 V power supply is used to keep the system operating smoothly. Experiment findings show an authentication accuracy of 98.46 %, delivery success rate of 96 %, security efficiency of 97.91 %, and average system response time of 5.45 seconds. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is confirmed by these results. The system provides a safe, economical, and scalable method for household and office parcel delivery. The next developments comprise sophisticated sensors, encrypted communication, and smart authentication methods.

title: Digital Meme Forensics: A Multimodal Transformer Framework for Offensive Content Detection on MultiOFF
authors: Vignesh S, Tamil Selvan V P, Srinivasan R, Nikkath Bushra S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: With the rise of memes on social media, it has become harder to moderate content, especially when the combination of text and images makes something offensive. This paper presents a multimodal approach for identifying offensive memes utilizing the MultiOFF dataset. It uses DistilBERT to understand the text and DeiT (Data-efficient Image Transformer) to understand the images. It combines the two by using methods like concatenation, bilinear fusion, attention, gated and crossmodal attention. Experiments demonstrate that the integration of text and image features significantly outperforms their individual use, as evidenced by the DistilBERT+DeiT attention fusion model, which attained an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 6. 5 1 \%}$</tex> and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 9 5 \%}$</tex>. And with the rise of meme culture in social media, it is important to regulate those activities as this may sometimes cause negative effects among the audience also. These results show that it's important to know how words and pictures are related in order to find offensive content in memes.

title: DMLO-6G: A Distributed Machine Learning Orchestration Framework for Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency QoS Prediction Across Heterogeneous Network Slices
authors: Vinod Kumar R, Velmurugan P, Sree Dharma A V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The fast development of 6G networks requires ultra-reliable and low-latency Quality-of-Service (QoS) forecasting and various network slices. The work presents a design of a Distributed Machine Learning Orchestrator (DMLO) that optimizes learning on the edge, fog machine, and cloud layer and delays the provision of timely and accurate QoS information. DMLO integrates four novel algorithms, namely Adaptive Slice-Aware Federated Optimizer (ASFO) to dynamically aggregate models, Latency-Constrained Gradient Pruning (LCGP) to minimize communication overhead, Cross-Tier QoS Fusion Network (CT-QFN) to fuse features among multiple layers and Real-Time Slice Drift Detector (RTSDD) to observe sudden fluctuations in services. Assignments to learning tasks are made based on slice sensitivity, mobility patterns and predicted congestion to be able to update models resource-efficiently and responsively. Inter slices simulations of eMBB, URLLC and mMTC show better accuracy of QoS prediction, lower inference latency, and resource usage. The findings confirm the feasibility of distributed intelligent orchestration as a feasible remedy to improving slice-level performance and reliability on 6G networks in the future.

title: GRU-based Voltage Control for Islanded DC Microgrids
authors: Dudekula Ismail, V Chandra Sekhar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The rapid penetration of renewable energy resources, such as photovoltaic (PV) and fuel cell systems, has significantly transformed modern power networks, leading to the development of decentralized and flexible micro-grids. Among these, DC micro-grids are gaining prominence due to their compatibility with DC-based renewable energy sources and efficient energy management. However, maintaining voltage stability in an islanded DC micro-grid poses significant challenges, particularly when the micro-grid becomes disconnected from the main grid. Islanding can occur intentionally for maintenance, fault isolation, or unintentional events such as grid faults and disturbances. During islanding, the micro-grid loses the voltage reference from the main grid, leading to potential voltage fluctuations and instability, especially under varying load and generation conditions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based voltage controller specifically designed for islanded DC micro-grids. The ANN controller continuously learns from system dynamics and adapts its control strategy in real-time, effectively managing voltage variations despite unpredictable changes in power generation and load demands. The proposed controller enhances voltage stability by rapidly compensating for disturbances and maintaining a steady voltage profile. Comprehensive stability analysis, including Bode, Nyquist, and root locus plots, demonstrates the superior performance of the ANN-based voltage controller under various operational scenarios. Simulation results show that the ANN-based controller significantly improves voltage regulation, mitigates transient oscillations, and ensures grid stability during islanded operation. This adaptive and intelligent control strategy provides a reliable solution for maintaining voltage stability in islanded DC micro-grids, contributing to the resilient and sustainable integration of renewable energy resources.

title: Virtualization in Cloud Network Security Architecture: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Joud Alhodaithy, Nouf Mohamed, Jaidaa Hafez, Mahnoor Iftikhar, Abul Bashar
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Virtualization has become the foundation of modern cloud computing, allowing for scalable, efficient, and flexible infrastructure. However, it also brings in challenges in network security and stability. This study investigates the importance of virtualization in cloud network security architectures, giving an overview of the virtualization ecosystem and its crucial role in resource isolation and communication. This paper articulates the security implications of virtualized settings, with an emphasis on network stability issues and hypervisor vulnerabilities. To assess the practical impact of these topologies on network performance, a quantitative analysis was conducted using three major cloud service providers: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). For analyzing network resilience in virtualized environments, a methodology based on 500 ping tests was used, which measured critical performance metrics such as latency, jitter, and packet loss. The research draws on these findings to identify significant performance differentiators and explores the trade-offs between virtualization benefits and security threats. The paper concludes by summarizing the comparative findings and suggesting future possibilities for hardening virtualized network systems against rising threats.

title: Smart Home Device Anomaly Detection Using AI
authors: Marina Ahmad Shyyab, Salma Hameedi Abdullah, Ayman AbuBaker
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: As the integration of Internet of Things (IoT) devices into smart homes grows, securing and ensuring the reliability of these devices is ever more critical. Changes in the behavior of a device can signal possible malfunctions, cyber threats, or misconfigurations, which can thus impair the performance of a system and the safety of users. This paper presents a new robust AI-enhanced framework designed for the anomaly detection in smart home environments. The proposed method incorporates machine learning and deep learning algorithms in the monitoring of real-time device activities to determine when they deviate from normal operational patterns. By utilizing datasets composed of smart home device logs and sensor data, the framework is trained to differentiate between benign anomalies (e.g., temporary network disconnections) and malicious occurrences (e.g., unauthorized access or malware attacks). The proposed system highlights scalability, flexibility in the usage of different device types, and energy efficiency that would be compatible with the constraints of smart home applications. Experiments show that the system can detect anomalies with a high degree of accuracy, deliver low rates of false positives, and significantly enhance the detection of threats at an early stage. This research emphasizes the importance of AI-driven smart home security, while also preparing the path for the invention of more advanced intelligent networking security solutions.

title: Smart Fabric Design and Defect Inspection System
authors: Dineshkumar Thangaraju, Asokan R, Balamurugan R, Abishiek R, Adithya P
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In the textile industry, ensuring fabric quality is crucial to maintaining product standards and reducing waste. This project aims to develop an automated system for fabric design verification and defect detection using image processing with interactive web application. High- resolution images of cloth fabrics are captured and analyzed to ensure consistency of design and identify defects such as tears, holes, stains, mis- weaves, etc., and their count. Techniques like template matching, edge detection and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are employed to detect and classify damage, while Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) is used for pattern comparison. The system provides a real- time detection interface using OpenCV and Stream lit, making it suitable for textile manufacturing industries. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves accuracy, consistency and efficiency in fabric inspection. The system enhances the quality control in textile manufacturing, reduces manual inspection errors and minimizes fabric wastage, offering an efficient solution for fabric inspection across various industries.

title: Implementing Secure API Gateways: A Case Study of AWS API Gateway and Spring Cloud Gateway
authors: Saket Chaudhari, Rajkumar Modake, Sharath Reddy Venna, Pratik Badri, Anusha Nerella, Piyush Ranjan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: APIs are the lifeblood of modern software systems, allowing service interaction. Securing user interaction with an API is vital, however, as its function in manipulating sensitive data and providing critical functionalities are at risk. API gateways forms a layer on which a centralized security element can be created with the following basic features: authentication, access control, request rate limiting, and request monitoring. In this context, the research involves a case study that focuses on implementing secure API gateway case, namely, AWS API Gateway and Spring Cloud Gateway. The security mechanisms are studied and compared on the basis of their capability in authenticating through OAuth, JWT, and API keys, as well as their capabilities in traffic management, threat detection, and auditing. Deployment limitations and scaling aspects, plus integrations in the cloud models, are also part of the study. From the findings, key differences emerged between AWS API Gateway-as-a-service, a fully managed cloud-native solution, and Spring Cloud Gateway, which is an open-source alternative designed for microservices. On fine-grained security access control and seamless integration with AWS services, AWS API Gateway seems to be built-in security focused. In contrast, Spring Cloud Gateway includes flexibility, customizability, and substantial control over the API security policies. Performance trade-offs, cost factors, and best practices are also discussed: develop and choose a gateway on the basis of security and business justification. This considers security in APIs not just a technical concern but a strategic business decision that demands proper planning and execution. By contrasting two of the most frequently used API gateways, we show how to increase the API security, scalability, and flexibility against evolving threats in modern cloud environments.

title: VM Integrator Using Single VDCC
authors: Devanshu Saxena, Meghana Shrivastava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper illustrates a efficient minimal component design of voltage-mode Integrator using the active building block, namely Voltage Differencing Current Conveyor (VDCC). The proposed circuit avails only one VDCC element and a single grounded capacitor. The circuit is designed to operate with a supply voltage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.9 \mathrm{V}$</tex>, a bias current of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex>, and an electronically controllable amplitude. The proposed integrator circuit have been verified through PSPICE simulations using CMOS VDCC implemented in 0.18m TSMC technology and also validated using MATLAB evaluations.

title: Deterministic Embedded Machine Learning for Adaptive Fighter Aircraft HUD Stabilization
authors: Ihsen Ben Mbarek Brahmi, Sandric Bretecher, Line Hoffmeyer-Kuntz, Clément Chanvalon
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Head-Up Displays (HUDs) are mission-critical cockpit interfaces in fighter aircraft, requiring high visual stability under extreme flight dynamics including rapid rotations, structural vibrations, and aggressive maneuvering. Conventional inertial stabilization approaches rely on fixed model-based filtering techniques that lack adaptability to rapidly varying motion conditions, often resulting in residual visual jitter and increased pilot cognitive workload. This paper proposes a deterministic embedded machine learning approach for adaptive fighter aircraft HUD stabilization. The core contribution lies in integrating lightweight on-device intelligence capable of real-time inertial motion classification under strict memory and latency constraints compatible with safety-critical avionics systems. Using tri-axial gyroscope data, the system classifies four fundamental aircraft motion statesyaw, roll, pitch, and level flight-through temporal signal windows of 1536 samples (512 per axis). A comparative benchmarking study evaluates Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), SEFR, and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) models trained on 150 motion sequences per class.
