Experimental results demonstrate that the MLP-based implementation achieves the optimal trade-off between classification accuracy and embedded resource usage, reaching a balanced accuracy of 97.50% and a quality index of 93.5, while requiring only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.8 \text{KB} \text{RAM}, 24 \text{KB}$</tex> Flash memory, and 3.3 ms Worst Case Execution Time (WCET) on a microcontroller-class platform. The deterministic execution behavior ensures bounded latency suitable for realtime avionics applications. Compared to conventional inertial stabilization methods, the proposed adaptive strategy enables context-aware HUD rendering with improved visual robustness and operational reliability under dynamic flight conditions.

title: Movable Antenna-Enabled Reliable Multi-User Uplink Transmission Under Jamming Attacks
authors: Zhihui Shang, Zhiqiang Xiao, Zhi Lin, Xiaoqiang Qiao, Hao Wu, Tao Zhang, Guan Gui
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Movable antenna (MA) technology has emerged as a promising solution to enhance wireless reliability against malicious jamming. It achieves this by exploiting spatial degrees of freedom through dynamic antenna repositioning within a confined area. This paper investigates the max-min fairness problem in an MA-enabled reliable multi-user uplink system under jamming attacks. The objective is to maximize the worst-case signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) at the base station (BS) by jointly optimizing the positions of the MAs and the receive beamforming (RB) vector, while satisfying the SINR requirement for each user equipment. The formulated optimization problem is non-convex and thus challenging to solve directly. To address it, we first consider a special case where the antenna positions of the BS are fixed, and derive a closed-form optimal RB based on the minimum mean square error (MMSE) criterion. Building on this insight, we develop an alternating optimization framework coupled with successive convex approximation (SCA) to iteratively update the MA positions and RB design in the general scenario. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms conventional fixed-antenna systems in terms of both communication efficiency and anti-jamming robustness, highlighting the practical potential of MAs for wireless communications in adversarial environments.

title: Quantum Circuit-Based Adaptation for Credit Risk Analysis
authors: Halima Giovanna Ahmad, Alessandro Sarno, Mehdi El Bakraoui, Carlo Cosenza, Clément Bésoin, Francesca Cibrario, Valeria Zaffaroni, Giacomo Ranieri, Roberto Bertilone, Viviana Stasino, Pasquale Mastrovito, Francesco Tafuri, Davide Massarotti, Leonardo Chabbra, Davide Corbelletto
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: Noisy and Intermediate-Scale Quantum, or NISQ, processors are sensitive to noise, prone to quantum decoherence, and are not yet capable of continuous quantum error correction for fault-tolerant quantum computation. Hence, quantum algorithms designed in the pre-fault-tolerant era cannot neglect the noisy nature of the hardware, and investigating the relationship between quantum hardware performance and the output of quantum algorithms is essential. In this work, we experimentally study how hardware-aware variational quantum circuits on a superconducting quantum processing unit can model distributions relevant to specific use-case applications for Credit Risk Analysis, e.g., standard Gaussian distributions for latent factor loading in the Gaussian Conditional-Independence model. We use a transpilation technique tailored to the specific quantum hardware topology, which minimizes gate depth and connectivity violations, and we calibrate the gate rotations of the circuit to achieve an optimized output from quantum algorithms. Our results demonstrate the viability of quantum adaptation on a small-scale, proof-of-concept model inspired by financial applications and offer a good starting point for understanding the practical use of NISQ devices.

title: IMS2026 Registration Announcement
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Microwave Magazine

title: HAF-Net: Hybrid CNN–Transformer Model with Multi-Head Attention for Accurate and Explainable Pneumonia Diagnosis from Chest X-Rays
authors: Progga Parmita Roy, Shionty Ghosh, Amit Chakraborty, Md Shakib Ahamed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Pneumonia is a widespread and serious respiratory infection which requires immediate and accurate treatment. In this study, a hybrid deep learning architecture is proposed on a base of Multi-Head Attention Pooling (MHAP) with the use of pretrained CNNs (DenseNet121, ResNet152) and a novel multitask transformer model architecture called Dual Intent and Entity Transformer (DeiT). The proposed HAF-Net model showed good diagnostic results with the test accuracy of 93.68% and the Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC) of 0.99. Attention mechanisms were used to locate clinically relevant regions to improve interpretability, and a report generator based on LSTM generated initial radiology-style summaries that can assist with the making of clinical decisions faster and more uniformly. The structured radiology-style reports enhance interpretability and enhance the possibilities of efficient clinical decision-making.

title: Multimodal Integration of Sensors with Computer Vision to Detect and Alert Wild Animal Intrusion
authors: Jefferson David Kingston, V. Adithya, V. Srivatsav, C.V. Santhosh, P. Mercy Rajaselvi Beaulah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel AI-powered wildlife detection and alert system that integrates YOLOv8 for Object Detection, DeepSORT for real time tracking, and sensor based proximity detection. The proposed approach enables accurate detection and tracking of wild animals in natural environments, particularly in human wildlife conflict zones. The Detection and Tracking of these wild animals are crucial in order to maintain a safe ecosystem and ensure that the animals do not harm the villagers living close by and vice versa. Unlike Traditional methods that rely solely on camera-based detection and motion sensors, this hybrid approach reduces false positives, improves detection in areas of low visibility and estimates the distance precisely. The system provides an alert in the form of SMS to forest officials and small buzzers placed near the villages to warn the villagers in case any animal has been spotted. The outcomes reveal its possibility of real time surveillance, ecological research, and mitigation of human-wildlife conflict which provides a scalable and robust solution of wildlife monitoring.

title: Corruption-Resilient Combinatorial Bandit Learning for Heterogeneous Network Systems
authors: Junkai Wang, Xutong Liu, Jinhang Zuo, Yuedong Xu, John C.S. Lui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: We study online decision-making problems in network applications using the framework of contextual combinatorial multi-armed bandits (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MAB). Although bandit methods provide a natural solution, two key challenges arise in practice: corruption in feedback and heterogeneity across clients. Corruption may stem from adversarial behaviors or system anomalies, leading to biased reward estimations, while heterogeneity reflects differences in clients’ environments such as computation capabilities or network conditions. To address these challenges, we formulate the C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MAB under corruption (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MAB-C) problem and extend it to the setting with heterogeneous environmental parameters. We then propose two novel algorithms, namely CW-C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>UCB, which leverages confidence-weighted estimations to mitigate corruption effects, and CW-C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>CLUB, which further integrates an online clustering mechanism to adaptively group tasks based on shared structure. We derive tight theoretical regret bounds for both algorithms under various corruption models showing that our upper bounds match the established lower bounds up to logarithmic factors. Empirical results on three real-world applications, content delivery networks, client selection in federated learning, and VR video streaming, demonstrate that our proposed algorithms significantly outperform existing baselines, achieving lower regret and stronger resilience in adversarial environments.

title: TalkWell: Natural Language Processing-Assisted Deep Learning Model for Real-Time Mental Health Monitoring
authors: Krishna Goswami, Krisha Raval, Arpan Gadhiya, Rajat Chaudhary, Prajakta Sadaria
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper introduces a truly novel way of determining mental health problems like anxiety, feelings of sadness, and stress in chat and audio communication, on the fly in online chats. The proposed model involves natural language processing (NLP) and voice analysis to understand the content as well as emotions expressed in text, in contrast to ordinary chatbots which rely only on keyword spotting. In this research, the system model operates in two phases; initially, the model interprets textual data for psychological signs using powerful language models, including but not limited to BERT models, and machine learning algorithms such as K-Nearest Neighbours, Convolutional Neural Networks, Support Vector Machines, as well as Long Short-Term Memory networks. Secondly, Deep Learning algorithms are adopted to extract the emotional component from the speech clips. This integrated proposed model performs a real-time mental health risk score, which can alert caregivers or identify appropriate support providers. Lastly, the performance analysis of the model is added as a browser plugin or linked into current chat platforms to allow for early identification of mental health challenges, raise awareness, and provide timely support to prevent catastrophic mental health emergencies.

title: A Smart Vision-Guided Autonomous System for Safe and Efficient Coconut Scraping
authors: Rohini Dasgupta, Areen Paresh Churi, Advay Shukla, R. Jansi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper discusses a robot which autonomously scrapes coconuts. The coconut scraping robot has an integrated vision system and force feedback control system to allow it to determine the size and location of the coconut and to receive feedback from the scraping process. Once the robot knows that it has scraped enough from a coconut, an adaptive spiral trajectory is ready to be programmed into the robot before starting the scraping process. The robot will use PID control through a load cell to perform real-time force regulation and maintain a constant penetration depth during scraping to prevent damage to the coconut material. The robot records vibration through an accelerometer and captures the features of the vibrations using features extracted from both the time and frequency domains. These features are analyzed using an Isolation Forest model for anomaly detection to ensure that the robot can operate safely throughout the entire coconut scraping process. Overall, the coconut scraping robot has a hierarchical structure, whereby the high-level perception and anomaly analysis is performed on a Raspberry Pi/laptop, while the low-level motion and force control is done by an Arduino microcontroller. Performance logistics demonstrate that the coconut scraping robot accurately estimated the geometric shape of the coconut, and due to the implementation of a multiple-sensor approach to determining if there was any anomaly detected during the coconut scrapping process, the coconut scraping robot is performing effective, reliable, and robust methods to monitor the coconut robot's safety.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning-Based Edge Computing System with Multi-Vital Sensing for Health Monitoring
authors: Agniva Mukherjee, Sayantam Giri, Ronald Julian, S. Kolangiammal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The convergence of Internet of Things (IoT) and Deep Learning (DL) is transforming the contemporary healthcare system by providing a real-time health monitoring and early disease prediction. This paper introduces the design of a smart hybrid disease prediction system that integrates physiological measurements collected from IoT-based sensors and user symptom inputs for effective disease identification. The proposed system is designed and developed using a Raspberry Pi microcontroller that is interfaced with a MAX30102 SpO2 sensor for the measurement of vital physiological parameters such as heart rate of the person and the person's blood oxygen saturation levels. The model is trained using symptom datasets for disease prediction on Deep Neural Network (DNN) model, and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is used to examine the waveform data from sensors for the evaluation of the patient's physiological status. The hybrid system improves the accuracy of disease prediction by integrating symptom-driven classification with real-time vital analysis. The experimental results show an average prediction accuracy of about 90%, thus proving the efficacy of the system for remote health monitoring and preliminary diagnosis. The system gives disease information and preventive guidelines, thus acting as a useful and intelligent tool for next-generation smart healthcare systems.

title: YOLOv8-Based Intelligent Traffic Monitoring for Multi-Class Vehicle Detection, Counting, and Speed Estimation in Smart Cities
authors: Gautami G. Shingan, Priyanka N. Jadhav, Amol M. Jagtap, Priyanka Gejage, Pradnya Mane, Aishwarya Waghmode
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Efficient real-time traffic monitoring is essential for smart cities and road safety. This paper proposes a YOLOv8-based Intelligent Traffic Monitoring System that detects, counts, and estimates the speed of various vehicle types using the GRAM-RTM M-30 dataset. The framework tackles challenges such as heavy traffic, occlusions, and poor visibility, providing timely and accurate monitoring. Experimental results show F1-scores ranging from 92% to 96% for detection. Counting accuracy exceeds 96%, and speed estimation errors are under 4%. This system outperforms YOLOv5 in both accuracy and processing speed. With an average processing speed of 35 FPS on standard GPU hardware, it is well-suited for live traffic monitoring and works well under different traffic densities, lighting conditions, and vehicle types. Future improvements will focus on better traffic analysis, improved handling of occlusions, and broader city deployment. The proposed YOLOv8-based system offers an effective and scalable solution for intelligent traffic monitoring, supporting smart city projects and improving road safety.

title: AI for Drug Repositioning in Rare Diseases
authors: Safa Daoud
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Drug repositioning, the process of identifying new therapeutic uses for already existing drugs, is a cost-effective and time-efficient alternative when compared to drug discovery through traditional means. This approach does not only serve the rare disease but also is the last resort to the drug company when either the patent of a new drug is about to expire and it can no longer be sold at a luxurious price or when it creates a competitive disadvantage in the market because of its high price. Artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as one of the most important tools of this field. AI, through its power of data analysis, pattern recognition, and predictive modeling, provides the means for searching out a potential drug-disease relationship. The scope of this paper lies in the implementation of AI-driven methodologies via machine learning, natural language processing, and graph-based algorithms to be used in the field of drug repositioning primarily for rare diseases. We investigate a number of data sources including genomic datasets, electronic health records, and biomedical literature, in order to be able to discover hidden connections between already existing drugs and the rare diseases they target. Other problems such as data scarcity, model interpretability, and validation in rare disease scenarios are also considered. Through the combined use of AI and domain expertise, this study aims to shorten the time taken to point out promising drug candidates and ultimately contribute to the metabolic syndrome treatment pipeline by the development of innovative medications that can enhance patient outcomes in rare disease management.

title: A Review of Data-Driven Approach to Congenital Fetal Heart Disease Detection Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models
authors: Seemakurthi Jaswanth, K. Kavitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Congenital fetal heart disease (CFHD) is one of the most prevalent congenital anomalies and a leading contributor to neonatal morbidity and mortality. Early identification during pregnancy plays a crucial role in improving perinatal outcomes and enabling timely clinical intervention. The new developments in machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) applications for detecting fetal and congenital heart diseases (CHD) enable 2D echocardiography to undergo full analysis automatically, and cardiac chambers to be segmented by experts, or ventricular volumes and ejection fraction to be accurately determined. Automated pipelines incorporating view classification as well as multi-vessel segmentation and feature extraction have played a crucial role in the detection and identification of congenital defects, including ASD, VSD, and complex heart defects, in fetal ultrasound. This research review provides a comprehensive analysis of datadriven approaches employed in prenatal screening and diagnosis of CFHD, emphasizing imaging-based assessment, cardiac structure segmentation, multi-view analysis, and physiological signal interpretation. In addition, the review discusses the impact of image quality control and standardized performance evaluation metrics on screening accuracy. Emerging trends in multi-view fetal echocardiography, video-based analysis, and integrated diagnostic pipelines show promise in reducing dependence on operator expertise and expanding access to prenatal cardiac screening, particularly in resource-limited settings. The review concludes by outlining current limitations, ethical considerations, and future directions required for the effective clinical translation of scalable and reliable prenatal heart disease detection systems.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Compact Multi-Port Metasurface Antenna for Sub-Terahertz Applications
authors: Parnapalli Sanjay Kumar, Parnapalli Sanjay Kumar, Jakki Vasanthasena, Talari Sivakumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: In this study, a compact multi-port Meta Surface Antenna for sub-terahertz communication and sensing is proposed. In this design, the periodic pentagonal array of the Meta Surface Cell is introduced for the improvement of gain, directivity, and impedance bandwidth for the frequency range of 40-220 GHz. A 4×4 Multi-Port Feed Network is implemented to feed the Meta Surface for efficient radiation. The simulation of the proposed design is performed by full wave EM analysis. The proposed design illustrates a maximum realized gain of 11.82 dB at 62.73 GHz frequency and stable radiation patterns for the higher sub-terahertz frequencies such as 157.11 GHz. In S-parameter analysis, the minimum S11 = −19.3 dB at 90.6 GHz frequency having a return loss of less than −40 dB at 157 GHz. Radiation patterns demonstrate directive broadside beams with Meta surface-controlled field distribution. The proposed antenna provides a promising low- profile solution for nextgeneration sub-THz systems.

title: A LoRa Based IoT System for Precision Monitoring and Smart Irrigation in Rose Cultivation
authors: Pavan Kumar P, Koushic N, Manoj Krishna R, Diana Emerald Aasha S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: A LoRa-based Smart Rose Plant Monitoring System with automatic irrigation for precision agriculture is presented in this study. Sensors check the soil's moisture, temperature, humidity, light intensity, and pH. These things can all affect how roses grow and bloom. LoRa technology sends data over 1 to 5 km with very little power, which is great for farmers in the country. A water pump turns on when the soil moisture level drops below a certain point, which saves water. A cloud platform lets you check sensor data in real time using web or mobile apps. Smart agriculture moves forward with real-time monitoring and automated control in a scalable, cost-effective way that works for both tiny gardens and big commercial farms.

title: Design and Analysis of a Reduced-Switch Dual-Input DC-DC Converter for PV-Powered Homes
authors: Lavanya A, Divya Navamani J, Kaushal Chakraborty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this paper, a dual input DC-DC converter is proposed, focusing on rooftop solar-powered domestic applications. It has the capability of combining two input sources, as well as achieving an improved voltage gain when compared to the traditional DC-DC boost converter. RSDI was proposed as the dual input DC-DC converter by incorporating auxilary switch with a source cell into the traditional Luo converter configuration. The operational analysis of the proposed RSDI DC-DC converter is also explained. Unlike the traditional DC-DC converters, the proposed dual input structure has only one path of the source network, thus eliminating the chances of brownout occurring due to source switchover. To examine the performance of the proposed DC-DC converter, the efficiency analysis of the DC-DC converter is conducted. The validity of the proposed 100 W RSDI converter prototype is proved through simulation and experimental results

title: The Silicon Safety Barrier: How Numerical Formats Define Reliability
authors: Larisa Goffman-Vinopal
publish: IEEE Reliability Magazine
abstract: As AI moves from the cloud to the edge, the choice between FP8 formats is no longer just about precision—it is about preventing catastrophic failure in the autonomous systems that steer our world.

title: A Robust Deep Learning Approach for Automated Cardiovascular Disease Diagnosis Using ECG Image Analysis
authors: M D. Allama Shiam Shanto, Md. Ferdous Jaman, Sadia Jannat Mitu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) continues to be one of the leading causes of death worldwide, with approximately 20 million deaths taking place in 2015. Studies show that deaths from CVD will amount to 23.6 million annually by 2030. Early recognition is vital to improve patient survival rates, particularly in the health system where there are limited resources and insufficiency of experienced professionals. The objective of this study was to develop an automated machine learning technique which would classify paper electrograph (ECG) recordings into four electrocardiographically different classes, namely acute myocardial infarction (MI), previous MI, irregular cardiac rhythm, and normal cardiac function. Using the broad ECG Images DataSet on Kaggle with cardiac patient data (i.e., 11,148 images processed and augmented) three different convolutional neural network (CNN) models were tested: VGG16, MobileNetV2 and InceptionV3. Of those VGG16 gave the best result overall by balanced performance measures, achieving 98.71% classification accuracy and F1 score 0.9853 in respect of results. This was a marked improvement when comparing results obtained with the InceptionV3 and MobileNetV2 models especially for classes of cases less well represented within these data, such as previous myocardial infarction cases. This is encouraging for the use of systems based on CNNs allowing rapid and effective interpretation of ECGs thereby guiding rapid diagnosis and reducing the risk of fatal outcome.

title: Drone-based GPR with YOLOv10 for Intelligent Mapping and Detection of Subsurface Pile Foundations
authors: Dhruvkumar Jayantilal Patel
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: A study was done to develop an intelligent framework for detecting and mapping the underground pile foundations by employing a drone-mounted Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) system combined with the YOLOv10 deep learning model. This innovative method takes advantage of the drone's mobility and aerial coverage for efficiently surveying huge construction or infrastructure sites; thus, it captures high-resolution subsurface data. The GPR system gathers the real-time radar signals reflected from the objects, which are then processed and converted into visual records. The records become the input for the YOLOv10 model, which has been trained to identify and categorize the features of pile foundations with a high level of confidence. The use of YOLOv10 means that fast object detection and localization in complex radar images are possible, thus the automation and the reliability of pile foundation mapping are improved. Besides that, the proposed system allows users to avoid ground surveys, and therefore, it is less time-consuming, less expensive, and less labor-intensive. This method is a breakthrough in geotechnical monitoring, and it is also very helpful in the implementation of smart construction practices as it connects advanced sensing and deep learning technologies.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Classifying Myocardial Infarction Complications Through ECG Images with XAI
authors: Progga Parmita Roy, Aishwarya Saha, Mahafujer Rahman, Khadiza Akter
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Every year, cardiovascular diseases cost the lives of around 18 million people; this number is sometimes increased by inaccurate or delayed diagnoses. Standard Electrocardiogram (ECG) interpretation is important, but it often faces challenges due to unclear procedures and human bias. In response, this work presents a daring vision: an ECG picture categorization framework that combines clinical rigor with Deep Learning (DL) and its revolutionary ability, surpassing the limitations of convention. Its core is a redesigned DenseNet201 that has been transformed via dual dropout regularization, transfer learning, and judicious augmentation. The model achieves an accuracy of (99.46%) while maintaining equilibrium across precision, recall, and F1-score after being trained on a carefully selected dataset of 928 ECG pictures from four important diagnostic classes. To prevent the overlapping between training and validating images, a 5-fold cross-validation is applied. Finally, the model is validated with an external independent dataset to prove its robustness. The use of Grad-CAM as a form of Explainable AI (XAI) method enhances interpretability in addition to numerical superiority, making the decision-making process transparent and clinically significant for practitioners.

title: Nano-Stream: Quantized Online 1D-CNN for Adaptive Traffic Classification and Dynamic QoS in SDN
authors: Varun Kumaran V, Sivarjun Aravind, R Mohandas, Kandavel N, Lydia Shammi W, Karthikeyan P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The rapid development of end-to-end encryption protocols, of which TLS 1.3 is a prime representative, has dramatically reduced the efficacy of traditional traffic classification methodologies based on Deep Packet Inspection. Recent works have shown that Deep Learning (DL) models can accurately classify such encrypted traffic. However, state-of-the-art solutions rely on computationally intensive architectures-such as ResNet or LSTM, which are ill-suited for resource-constrained edge routers, these static models are liable to concept drift due to dynamic network patterns, thus leading to rapid performance degradation. This paper presents Nano-Stream, a lightweight, self-adaptive traffic classification framework for Software-Defined Networking (SDN) environments. A quantized 1D-Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) is deployed, which was trained from scratch using 32-bit floating-point representations and then quantized in an 8-bit integer format, reducing inference latency by 41.5% while retaining competitive accuracy. More importantly, our framework leverages a drift-aware replay buffer that enables online finetuning, allowing it to adapt at runtime without experiencing catastrophic forgetting. Empirical evaluations show that Nano-Stream achieves an accuracy of 89.4%, with an inference latency of only 1.17 ms per flow, thereby allowing the direct enforcement of dynamic QoS rules directly on the switch.

title: Trans-SPAnet: A Unified Transformer-CNN Architecture with Enhanced Boundary Optimization for Polyp Segmentation
authors: Tasneea Zahra, Kalyan Kumar Halder
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of anatomical structures in medical images, such as polyps, remains a critical, yet challenging task due to the variability in appearance, low contrast, and irregular boundaries. While U-Net-based architectures demonstrate strong local feature extraction, they often lack the capability of global context modeling. On the other hand, transformer-based models capture long-range dependencies but struggle with local detail preservation, which requires a huge amount of data. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel hybrid CNN-transformer U-Net architecture that integrates spatial pyramid convolution blocks (SPCB) and spatial pyramid pooling blocks (SPPB) for multi-scale local feature extraction, with a transformer bottleneck for global context understanding. Furthermore, we introduce a boundary-sensitive loss function to enhance performance at object boundaries, a known problem in conventional approaches. Our decoder employs refined transformer fusion modules to reconcile semantic gaps across different scales. We evaluate our model on Kvasir-SEG dataset for polyp segmentation, which performs 2-3% better than existing baseline segmentation models. The results demonstrate that our architecture performs better than existing CNN-only or transformer-only models in medical image segmentation.

title: Edge-Guided U-Net with Frequency Domain Refinement for Efficient Skin Lesion Segmentation
authors: Aryan Kumar Singh, Md Rakibul Islam Midul, Md Shohan Mia, Priyanshu Kumar Gupta, Md Kayser Ahmed Hridoy, Chowdhury Hasibur Rahaman, Rabul Hussain Laskar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Precise segmentation of skin lesions is crucial to support the diagnosis of skin cancer within computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems. Although deep learning-based models like U-Net along with its variants have demonstrated strong segmentation performance, they often require substantial computational and energy resources. In this study, a lightweight and computationally efficient framework for skin lesion segmentation is proposed, leveraging information across the spatial and frequency domains. The proposed model, Edge-Guided U-Net (EGU-Net), integrates edge information directly into the input and incorporates a frequency-domain refinement step based on the discrete cosine transform (DCT). Through this integration, EGU-Net enables improved delineation of lesion boundaries while suppressing high-frequency noise in the predicted masks. The model underwent training and evaluation on the ISIC 2018 dataset, resulting in a Dice score of 87.85%, recall of 90.62%, precision of 89.02%, an intersection over union (IoU) of 80.35% and an accuracy of 95.47%. These results demonstrate that EGU-Net can approximate the performance of more complex architectures while maintaining significantly lower computational complexity. Consequently, EGU-Net establishes a strong baseline for interpretable and efficient pixel-wise skin lesion segmentation under constrained computational settings.

title: Hybrid CEO–LMO based Combined Economic–Emission Dispatch with Renewable Energy Integration
authors: K. Deepak, G. Pandu Ranga Reddy, Y. Hazarathaiah, Shaik Sameena, Devanuru Rishika, Mangali Meghana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Combined economic-emission dispatch becomes difficult when renewable generation pushes thermal units toward their operating limits. Photovoltaic and wind power reduce net thermal demand, shrinking the feasible dispatch region and making constraint handling dominant. This work addresses that problem directly. Economic-emission dispatch is solved as a capped-simplex optimization in which power balance and generator limits are enforced by projection, not penalties. A hybrid metaheuristic combining Cosmic Evolution Optimization and Logarithmic Mean Optimization is used. Exploration and refinement operate in parallel within each iteration, and objective normalization is applied to reduce sensitivity to weight scaling. Renewable generation is treated as a fixed injection that lowers thermal demand. When high renewable output violates minimum generation feasibility, curtailment is applied only to restore feasibility. Curtailment is not optimized. The method is tested on the IEEE 30-bus system with six thermal generators. All candidate solutions remain feasible at every iteration. Compared to thermalonly dispatch, renewable integration leads to lower thermal cost and emissions and faster convergence, without forcing generators to boundary limits. The results show that explicit feasibility enforcement and normalization produce stable CEED solutions in operating regions where conventional weighted-sum methods become fragile.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Disease Prediction and Healthcare Assistance System Using Feed-Forward Neural Network
authors: Debanjan Pathak, Hitesh Mohapatra, Soumya Ranjan Mishra, Subhadip Pramanik
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: In recent years, with the introduction of advanced machine learning-based diagnostic approaches in the healthcare industry, intelligent healthcare systems have become increasingly important for early disease detection and providing initial diagnostic support. This work presents a machine learning powered web application with the functionality of predicting probable diseases a person may be suffering from according to the description provided by them and further helping them connect with doctors, nearby hospitals and pharmacies. A FeedForward Neural Network (MLPClassifier) was trained using backpropagation on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 9 6 1}$</tex> patient records containing 132 symptom features and 41 disease classes. After pre-processing which included Boolean normalization, one-hot encoding, median imputation, feature scaling and duplicate record removal, the dataset was reduced to 304 unique samples. The proposed model achieved 98.36% test accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.9707, with cross-validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9967 \pm 0.0066$</tex>. In addition to disease prediction the platform integrates geolocation services for locating nearby hospital and pharmacy, emergency contacts, and doctor recommendations. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the model in predicting diseases based on symptoms, making it a valuable step toward hybrid machine learning-assisted healthcare and early diagnosis support.

title: TransVort: A Temporally-Coherent Physics-Guided Neural Network for Super-Resolving and Denoising 4D Flow MRI of Cerebrospinal Fluid_supp2-3690957.docx
authors: Vitaly Rayz
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: To enhance the diagnostic utility of 4D flow MRI in assessing cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dynamics by super-resolving and denoising measured velocities using temporally coherent, physics-guided neural networks (PGNN). Methods: Synthetic 4D flow MRI was generated from 40 computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations across 10 ventricular geometries. These simulations were used to generate paired synthetic 4D flow MRI and high-resolution velocity fields used for supervised training. Here, we compare a previously developed temporally independent network (div-mDCSRN-Flow) using divergence-based regularization with two novel temporal PGNNs (tempo-mDCSRN-Flow using divergence-regularization and TransVort additionally constrained by the vorticity transport equation). Results: In application to synthetic 4D flow MRI of a double gyre flow showed the temporal PGNNs improve vorticity estimation. Similarly, both temporal methods improved estimation of vorticity and time-averaged wall shear stress (TAWSS) of synthetic 4D flow MRI in the 3rd and 4th ventricle. While using temporal PGNNs improves velocity and vorticity quantification across temporal resolutions, TransVort demonstrated additional improvement at fine temporal resolutions. Application of TransVort to in vivo 4D flow MRI of CSF flow captured vortex formation and dissipation in the 4th ventricle over the cardiac cycle. Conclusion: Leveraging the temporal information of 4D flow MRI improves reconstruction of high-resolution velocity fields. This leads to better estimation of gradient-based flow metrics such as vorticity and TAWSS, which are associated with neurodegenerative and neurovascular diseases. Significance: Augmenting the accuracy of 4D flow MRI increases its potential for adoption as a clinical tool for diagnosing and monitoring disorders of neurofluid dynamics.

title: Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding for Large Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference remains computationally expensive due to the autoregressive nature of token generation. Speculative decoding has emerged as a promising technique to accelerate inference by using a small draft model to predict multiple tokens in parallel, which are then verified by the target model. However, existing speculative decoding approaches require loading both models simultaneously, often exceeding the memory capacity of commodity GPUs. We present a memory-efficient speculative decoding framework that enables practical deployment on resource-constrained hardware through model quantization and optimized memory management. Our approach achieves a 2.31 × speedup in token generation throughput on an NVIDIA RTX A4000 (16 GB) while maintaining output quality, using only 12.0 GB of GPU memory. We demonstrate that speculative decoding can be made practical for edge and on-premise deployment scenarios where hardware resources are limited. Experimental results on LLaMA-7B show an average acceptance rate of 76.3% and consistent latency reductions of 56.8% across diverse prompts.

title: TriTree-VoteNet: An Interpretable Soft Voting Ensemble Framework for Liver Disease Diagnosis
authors: Mehazabin Hossain Lamiha, Md. Ehsanul Haque, Jeba Maliha, Md Naimul Islam, Sabrina Khan Mim, Md Asif Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Liver disease is a severe health issue that in most cases grows without any signs thus, early diagnosis is important in treatment. Machine learning has demonstrated the promise in aiding clinical decision-making, including the detection of complex trends among patient data. Nevertheless, most current methods have problems with imbalance in classes, poor interpretability, and the absence of computational efficiency assessment. This paper presents the TriTree VoteNet, a softvoting ensemble framework, which is optimized with grid search to predict liver disease. The framework uses a combination of dual-stage feature selection based on One-Way ANOVA and Genetic Algorithms with a hybrid data balancing approach that incorporates GAN and SMOTE Tomek. Random Forest, Extra Trees, and XGBoost are the three best-performing individual baseline models, which are combined into the ensemble to enhance predictive performance. Cross-validation, 95% confidence intervals, Matthews Correlation Coefficient, and an inference time analysis are used to evaluate the model on a publicly available liver disease dataset, yielding a test accuracy of 99.74%, crossvalidation accuracy of 99.78%, Matthews Correlation Coefficient of 0.9935, precision of 0.9982, recall of 0.9953, and a total test inference time of 0.3279 seconds. These findings indicate that systematic feature selection, hybrid data balancing, and explainable ensemble learning can be used to improve liver disease prediction with moderate complexity and realistic feasibility.

title: Probing Deeper Zone of Lung using Four Electrode Techniques: An FEM Approach
authors: Mahjabin Mobarak, Habiba Begum, Md. Moniruzzaman, Abdullah Al Mahfazur Rahman, Md. Ruhul Amin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Electrical Impedance Techniques are being explored as a low-cost, non-invasive method for detecting lung disorders by measuring bioimpedance differences between healthy and diseased tissue. Air-filled lungs (inhalation) show higher impedance, while fluid-filled or collapsed lungs (exhalation) show lower impedance. Methods like Tetrapolar Impedance Measurement (TPIM) and Focused Impedance Method (FIM) enable localized lung assessment. In this study, Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations using a simplified lung model evaluated the effectiveness of various electrode configurations-implementing electrodes on one side and both sides. Simulation results revealed that the contribution of the lung to the total measured impedance (CLTI) varied significantly depending on the electrode configuration and the respiratory phase. For the both-sided electrode placement, CLTI ranged from 13–24% during inhalation to 23–45% during exhalation, indicating a strong influence of the lung’s condition on the overall impedance. In contrast, the one-sided electrode configuration yielded lower CLTI values, ranging from 7–12% during inhalation to 14–20% during exhalation, suggesting reduced sensitivity to lung-specific impedance changes when current and voltage electrodes are confined to a single side of the thorax. The transfer impedance changes of 28–37% and 10-16% for both sided and one-sided configuration respectively. These findings indicate that the both-sided setup allows deeper current penetration through the thoracic region and greater responsiveness to changes in lung aeration. The results suggest that simplified Electrical Impedance Methods with both sided electrode placement can potentially detect localized lung abnormalities.

title: Toward Robust AI Agents: A Closed-Loop Task Planning–Execution–Feedback Framework for Open Scenarios
authors: Ning Lyu
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Background: In open environments, AI agents often suffer from insufficient robustness and verifiability, primarily due to target drift, tool uncertainty, and data distribution variations. Consequently, their reliability and verifiability remain insufficiently realized. Methods: A closed-loop mechanism of “Planning-Execution-Feedback” (PEF) was constructed. The model was constructed as a risk-sensitive POMDP model, combining the task graph with the safety threshold. Counterfactual gain allocation (IPS/DR) and off-policy evaluation (BCa CI, Holm - Bonferroni) were used to update the policy. Temperature and Dirichlet calibration strategies were used to reduce the ECE evaluation value. Results: In the specific set of NYC-311 (2024) subsets, the PEF test results for the NYC-311 (2024) subset were ideal, with a success rate of 83.3 %, a latency of 903 milliseconds, a hallucination rate of 4.0 %, a violation rate of 7.1 %, and an ECE index of 0.047. This system outperformed the Planner+Executor by 7.0 percentage points, while maintaining the same latency performance. The Holm-corrected p-value was less than 0.0001. Even in the face of high disturbances, the success rate remained at 78 %, and the cost level remained unchanged. Conclusion: The application of closed-loop technology effectively improves the robustness of the system and achieves Pareto optimality in efficiency. Even in the face of growth in scale and tool complexity, it can maintain stable expansion. This system design is suitable for implementation in open scenarios.

title: Cybersecurity in Medical AI: Parkinson Voice Biomarkers Security: Anti-Adversarial Protection Against Attacks
authors: Md Muhasin Ali, Ashraful Hasan Chad, Shovan Samanta Turzo, Mohammad Soad Khan, Md. Jahidul Islam Mozumdar, Nasim Parvez
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Parkinson Disease (PD) is a progressive nervous system disease that interferes with motor control and speech, and therefore, it is necessary to detect it early but challenging because there are no conclusive lab tests. Clinical assessment is commonly used in the traditional diagnosis and this might be subjective and time consuming. The proposed research is intended to develop a reliable and correct artificial intelligence system of PD detection based on voice biomarkers. The publicly available speech features Kaggle dataset was used. In the preprocessing stage, such techniques as feature scaling and dimensionality reduction were used, six machine learning models were run: LightGBM, CatBoost, Extra Trees, Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), Gaussian Process Classifier (GPC), and Multi-layer perceptron (MLP). The findings show that GPC and MLP performed better with 95% accuracy and CatBoost was second with 94%. Adversarial attacks (FGSM and PGD) were also proposed to evaluate robustness, and a substantial decrease in performance was found. This was followed by the use of adversarial training that somewhat regained accuracy and enhanced resistance to attacks. The novelty of this work consists in collaboration between the cybersecurity perspectives and medical AI in the detection of PD so that models are not only precise but also robust. This system provides a secure, nonintrusive, deployable telemedicine application. A quick comparison to the state-of-the-art (CNN, LSTM, ensemble models) shows our system similar or better accuracy along with more adversarial robustness.

title: Design of a Real-Time High-Gain Microstrip Patch Antenna from 2 GHz to 4 GHz for RF Transceiver Applications
authors: Naman Gupta, Durga Prasad Mishra, Harish Chandra Kumawat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a rectangular probe fed microstrip patch antenna operating at 2.4 Ghz within the S-band freqency range of 2-4 GHz frequency band. Its main applications is in weather radar, satellite communication links, and IoT backhaul systems. Traditional waveguide and horn antennas have many limitations to overcome them, this planar structure uses a rectangular patch on an FR-4(epoxy) substrate to achieve a balance between performance and stability. Antenna design and optimization were performed using ANSYS HFSS through parametric Analysis. Important performance parameters including return loss (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex>), gain, and radiation patterns were analyzed. The results demonstrate excellent impedance matching across the operating band and stable radiation pattern, reliability and suitability of this low-profile antenna for modern wireless communication platforms.

title: Microfluidic Simulation Framework for Point-of-Care Diagnostics: Modeling Advection-Diffusion-Reaction Transport in Lab-on-Chip Systems
authors: Tashdid Ahmed Shitab, Anika Ibnat Oni, Md. Emam Hossain Emon, Razin Rayan Rahat, Anjuman Ara Mim, Prima Fariya Auishe
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Point-of-care diagnostics (POCD) rely heavily on microfluidic lab-on-chip systems, yet their design process is often hindered by trial-and-error prototyping and inconsistent reproducibility. Efficient diagnostic performance requires a clear understanding of how advection, diffusion, and biochemical reactions jointly govern analyte transport at the microscale. This work presents a lightweight numerical framework for modeling solute behavior in microfluidic channels using the advection–diffusion–reaction equation. A finite-difference solver was applied to a 5 mm × 100 μm channel under physiologically relevant flow conditions, comparing three regimes: diffusion-only, advection + diffusion, and advection + diffusion + first-order reaction. Results show that high Péclet numbers (Pe ≈ 1000) drive plug-like transport with minimal lateral mixing, while Damköhler numbers near unity (Da ≈ 1) produce significant axial decay due to reaction kinetics. Visualizations of concentration fields and breakthrough curves demonstrate how Pe and Da directly influence sensor placement, analyte preservation, and diagnostic sensitivity. The framework provides a transparent and computationally efficient tool for early-stage device design, offering practical guidance for optimizing flow control, channel geometry, and capture-surface behavior in lab-on-chip diagnostics.

title: Switched-Capacitor Based Bidirectional DC–DC Converter with Enhanced Efficiency for EV and Renewable Systems
authors: G. Melviah, R. Ramaprabha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this work, a MBC using the S-C technology for an electric vehicle system has been provided. The proposed topology is a three-port system with the ability to support buck and boost operations. This makes it possible to handle power transfer among multiple sources as well as the load. The proposed topology attains high voltage gain with lower switch voltage stress with fewer components compared to the traditional topology. The proposed system is validated for its effectiveness as a multi-source energy manager.

title: Automatic Red Teaming LLM-based Agents with Model Context Protocol Tools_supp1-3691201.pdf
authors: Shouling Ji
publish: N/A
abstract: The remarkable capability of large language models (LLMs) has led to the wide application of LLM-based agents in various domains. To standardize interactions between LLM-based agents and external resources, model context protocol (MCP) tools have become the de facto standard and are now widely integrated into these agents. However, the incorporation of MCP tools introduces the risk of tool poisoning attacks, in which malicious MCP tools can steer the behavior of LLM-based agents toward unintended outcomes. Although previous studies have identified such vulnerabilities, their red teaming approaches have largely remained at the proof-of-concept stage, leaving the automatic red teaming of LLM-based agents under the MCP tool poisoning paradigm an open question. To bridge this gap, we propose AutoMalTool, an automated red teaming framework for LLM-based agents by generating malicious MCP tools. Our extensive evaluation shows that AutoMalTool effectively generates malicious MCP tools capable of manipulating the behavior of mainstream LLM-based agents while evading current detection mechanisms, thereby revealing new security risks in these agents.

title: Consumer Data Privacy-Aware Federated Orchestration for Communication, Computing, and Control in 6G Consumer Services
authors: Syed Danial Ali Shah, Ali Kashif Bashir, Daehan Kwak, Amina Salhi, Farman Ali
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Emerging Consumer Electronics (CE) systems, such as connected vehicles, immersive AR/VR environments, cloud gaming platforms, and intelligent home robotics, are increasingly characterized by their dependence on distributed intelligence, seamless connectivity, and autonomous control. These systems often span multiple Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) and serElectronicsders, requiring coordinated management of communication, computing, and control resources at the network edge. When AI and RAN workloads share edge infrastructure, this coexistence naturally achieves integration: RAN workloads provide communication and control while AI workloads supply computing intelligence. However, as MNOs increasingly share infrastructure under the Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) paradigm, efficient resource allocation across heterogeneous workloads and independent Service Level Agreements (SLAs) becomes critical. At the same time, preserving consumer data privacy across administrative boundaries remains a major concern. This paper proposes the Converged AI and O-RAN Architectural (CAORA) framework, a federated and SLA aware orchestration solution that integrates AI and RAN workloads with communication, computing, and control. CAORA employs local spike-aware workload forecasting using attention-based LSTMs and aggregates predictions through federated learning to avoid raw data sharing between operators. A centralized Soft Actor Critic (SAC) reinforcement learning agent then allocates edge resources fairly and efficiently while meeting SLA constraints. Experiments on real 5G traffic traces from Barcelona show that CAORA achieves about 90% task completion, reduces SLA violations to below 10%, and reaches nearly 100% resource utilization under peak load.

title: Multitree Genetic Programming with Behavioral Semantics for Large-Scale Dynamic Air Traffic Flow Management
authors: Tong Guo, Yi Mei, Mengjie Zhang, Hui Ding, Wenbo Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Dynamic Air Traffic Flow Management (DATFM) is vital to modern aviation industries, requiring real-time routing and sequencing aircraft within constrained airspace to ensure safety and efficiency, particularly during unforeseen disruptions. Multitree Genetic Programming (MTGP) provides an automatic design framework to simultaneously learn routing and sequencing policies. However, the coupling interdependence between the routing and sequencing decisions in MTGP tends to produce structurally valid but semantically ineffective offspring, thereby limiting MTGP’s search ability within the heuristic space. This paper presents a novel Multitree Genetic Programming with Behavioral Semantics (MrGPBS) approach that automatically evolves effective reactive scheduling heuristics for DATFM. First, we develop a specialized multitree representation incorporating informative terminals that enable concurrent evolution of routing and sequencing decisions, coupled with a problem-tailored heuristic template that decodes these representations into executable solutions in an online manner. Second, we propose a behavioral semantics-guided evolutionary mechanism that addresses the coupling interdependence issue of existing MTGP methods. Our method preserves well-evolved tree structures by promoting recombination between behaviorally compatible individuals, thereby enabling incremental improvement within the heuristic search space. We evaluate MrGPBS on comprehensive benchmark instances derived from real-world air traffic data, encompassing diverse problem scales and dynamic operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

title: D-PolTransGAN: Scattering Guided Hybrid Transformer-GAN for SAR-Optical Image Translation using Dual Polarization SAR
authors: Jagrati Talreja, Tewodros Syum Gebre, Leila Hashemi-Beni
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides reliable imaging in all weather and lighting conditions, but its speckled appearance makes visual interpretation difficult and limits its integration with optical-based analyses. To address this challenge, we propose a new SAR-to-optical image translation framework tailored for flood assessment. The proposed model processes SAR VV and VH polarizations through separate feature extraction streams, enabling each stem to learn its unique scattering characteristics. These features are then fused using cross-attention to capture complementary information from both polarizations. The architecture combines convolutional networks for local detail extraction with transformer modules to model global context, all within a generative adversarial learning framework. We train and evaluate the model using the DEEPFLOOD, SEN1FLOODS11 and SEN12MS datasets, which provides well-aligned Sentinel-1 (VV, VH) and Sentinel-2 image pairs. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual quality (LPIPS), while preserving fine spatial details critical for flood analysis. Qualitative results on real flood events further demonstrate that the generated optical-like images recover visual structures and contextual information that are difficult to interpret in raw SAR imagery. <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/D-PolTransGAN</uri>

title: AgenticVM: Agentic AI for Adaptive Software Vulnerability Management
authors: Asrul Arifin, Hussain Ahmad, Yiyao Zhang, Diksha Goel
publish: IEEE Software
abstract: As software systems grow in complexity, vulnerability management faces increasing challenges due to high alert volumes, fragmented tools, and manual triage processes. This paper presents AgenticVM, an engineering-focused multi-agent system that integrates large language models with security tools to automate vulnerability aggregation, enrichment, prioritisation, and reporting. Across four evaluation scenarios, AgenticVM reduces raw scanner outputs into compact actionable queues, achieving up to 98% alert reduction (e.g., from 3,983 findings to 82 high-priority items), while predicting missing CVSS attributes with 89.3% accuracy. These results demonstrate improved prioritisation and reduced analyst workload without compromising risk coverage. We further distil key engineering lessons on agent decomposition, tool–LLM boundaries, failure containment, observability, and human-in-the-loop governance. Overall, AgenticVM shows that agentic architectures can enable scalable, coordinated, and practical vulnerability management while providing actionable design guidance for AI-enabled cybersecurity systems.

title: Toward Mitigating Surface Degradation of TOPCon Passivation: Understanding the Effects of Fast-Firing Temperature and Poly-Si Doping
authors: Benjamin Hammann, Wolfram Kwapil, Jana-Isabelle Polzin, Jörg Horzel, Anna Damm, Florian Schindler, Martin C. Schubert
publish: IEEE Journal of Photovoltaics
abstract: We investigate the degradation of n- and p-type tunnel oxide passivating contact (TOPCon) under illumination and elevated temperatures to elucidate the degradation mechanism and identify mitigation strategies. Because the understanding regarding surface-related degradation (SRD) differs between n- and p-type TOPCon, we address different aspects for each layer type. Regarding p-type TOPCon, the focus is on the impact of the fast-firing peak temperature, whose correlation has already been investigated for n-type TOPCon but not for p-type TOPCon. It is found that the degradation extent of p-type TOPCon is inversely correlated with the peak temperature of the fast-firing step. This is consistent with previous reports on n-type TOPCon, making a common degradation mechanism for n- and p-type TOPCon likely. For n-type TOPCon, samples processed by plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition and low-pressure chemical vapor deposition (LPCVD) exhibit markedly different degradation extents. We attribute the lower degradation extent observed for LPCVD layers to its higher phosphorus concentration. We propose a unified mechanism for the degradation of both, n- and p-type TOPCon, that explains the various experimental data: hydrogen diffuses from a hydrogen containing dielectric layer (e.g., silicon nitride, SiNx) through the highly doped poly-Si layer toward the Si/SiO2 interface, where interface defects are formed. Based on this pathway, the degradation can be mitigated either by reducing the hydrogen content within the dielectric layers or by hindering the diffusion of hydrogen to the Si/SiOx interface—for example, by increasing the poly-Si dopant concentration.

title: A Conceptual Study for Prediction of the Floating behavior of Metallic Particle
authors: Seungmin Bang, Chanyeol Ryu, Bang-Wook Lee
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Metallic particle was generated inside the Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) due to incorrect assembly process or mechanical operation. During normal operation, the influence of the electric filed causes the metallic particle to float over the conductor, resulting in partial discharge and dielectric breakdown. It is very important to prevent insulation accidents by predicting the floating height of metallic particle. However, the shape and size of metallic particle very diverse, and the floating height varies depending on the type of voltage. Previous studies had analyzed the movement or partial discharge characteristics of metallic particle through experiments. Also, the movement of the metallic particle was performed only through simulation, and were not consistent with the experimental results. Therefore, in this paper, a conceptual study for prediction of the floating behavior of metallic particle. The floating height and lift-off voltage of the metallic particle were investigated according to the shape, size and voltage type. The floating height of metallic particle were simulated by Boundary Element Method (BEM), and the results of the BEM and the experiment were consistent. It is found that lift-off voltage and floating height of the metallic particle could be predicted without conducting experiments.

title: A Novel Approach to Detect Speech Impairment Severity Using Multi-Channel Spectrograms
authors: Manisa Manoswini, Biswajit Sahoo, Aleena Swetapadma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid model using self supervised learning and deep convolutional neural networks for the assessment of the severity level of speech impairment. Wav2vec2 embedding technique are used for the generation of pseudo-severity labels using the cosine distance between embeddings for healthy and impaired speeches. Speech signals are converted into three-channel spectrograms, which include log-mel, delta and delta-delta spectral features. The custom convolutional neural network designed for classifying the severity level in binary, three, and four-class classification problems. The experimental results demonstrate over 99 % classification accuracy.

title: Unlocking Flexibility Potential through DVFS-controlled Data Center Clusters in Unit Commitment
authors: Yiping Yuan, Yufei Teng, Liqian Zhu, Yihang Cao, Zhenyuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Data center clusters (DCCs) have become recognized as significant energy consumers in electrical power system oper-ations. Workload reallocation processes for multiple DCCs, im-plemented by dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) control schemes, can provide flexibility potential capabilities and contribute to maintaining the security of power systems opera-tions through Unit Commitment (UC). To achieve this, The flexi-bility potential of workload reallocation hosted by multiple DCCs is analyzed. Building on this analysis, an interval-based work-load reallocation strategy is extended to depict flexibility poten-tial from workload response while avoiding service-time latency. Then, an innovation set of physical security constraints repre-senting DVFS-controlled DCCs is developed, and related special-ordered sets-of-type-1 (SOS1) linearized constraints are derived through the McCormick Envelopes method. Furthermore, these intractable constraints are incorporated into UC, and an en-hanced UC model, which considers the flexibility potential offered by DVFS-controlled DCCs, is developed. Several experiments are conducted on the IEEE 6 buses test systems to assess the validity and scalability of the proposed models.

title:
D
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    FT-Net: Frequency-Spatial Dual Domain Fine-Tuning of Vision Foundation Models for Remote Sensing Domain Generalization Semantic Segmentation
authors: Zimeng Shen, Jue Wang, Tianyu Wei, Wenchao Liu, Xiaoyan Lu, Liang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In recent years, remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation has attracted increasing attention due to significant domain shifts caused by variations in sensors, imaging conditions, and geographic regions. Vision foundation models (VFMs) possess strong general-purpose feature extraction capabilities and can be effectively transferred across diverse data, showing great potential for remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation. However, existing VFM-based spatial domain parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods struggle to handle pronounced cross-domain intra-class variations in remote sensing. To address this issue, the Adaptive Frequency Aware Adapter (AFA-Adapter) is proposed, which adaptively selects frequency components to improve cross-domain intra-class feature consistency. Building upon this, the Spatial Multi-Prototype Adapter (SMP-Adapter) is proposed, which clusters multiple prototypes for features of each land-cover category to model complex intra-class diversity. Land-cover features are then weighted by their nearest intra-class prototype, thereby enhancing the discriminability of easily confused features at feature cluster boundaries. By integrating these two modules, we propose a Frequency–Spatial Dual Domain Fine-Tuning Network (D<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>FT-Net), which effectively alleviates cross-domain intra-class variations and improves the generalization capability of VFMs for remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation. Extensive experiments under four cross-domain settings demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed D<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>FT-Net, which achieves an average mIoU improvement of 1.09% over state-of-the-art methods, with the best gain reaching 1.64%. The source code will be released at https://github.com/ssshen0315/D2FT-Net.

title: Multimodal Analysis of Family Interactions and Children's Psychology
authors: Yingtong Aia, Libin Yang, Liwen Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: Family interaction is a crucial environmental factor influencing children's psychological development. Traditional assessment methods have drawbacks such as strong subjectivity and insufficient dynamic capture. To achieve an objective and precise correlation between family interaction and children's psychological states, this paper proposes a family interaction analysis scheme based on multimodal data. Firstly, a multimodal data collection system covering vision (facial expressions, body movements), audio (voice emotions, dialogue interaction), and text (dialogue transcription) is constructed to obtain natural interaction data from 120 families and complete preprocessing and annotation; Secondly, a Transformer-based multimodal fusion model with attention mechanism is designed to efficiently extract family interaction features; Finally, a correlation model integrating the fused features with children's psychological states (such as happiness, anxiety, concentration, etc.) is established. Experimental results show that the accuracy of children's psychological state recognition by the proposed multimodal fusion model reaches 89.7%, which is 6.2% to 8.5% higher than the optimal single-modal model (83.5% for visual modality and 81.2% for audio modality), and through SHAP value analysis, five core influencing features such as the frequency of parent-child eye contact and the proportion of positive language are clarified. This research provides objective quantitative technical support for children's mental health assessment, and can assist in family parenting guidance and early intervention.

title: Towards Robust Alignment for Video Dehazing with Temporal Lookup Table
authors: Haoyou Deng, Zhiqiang Li, Feng Zhang, Bin Xu, Qingbo Lu, Changxin Gao, Nong Sang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Video dehazing aims to restore clean scenarios from a sequence of hazy frames, where frame alignment is a critical stage for leveraging temporal information. However, haze degrades contrast and obscures details, making alignment challenging. Existing methods ignore the impairment of haze on alignment and thus struggle to align frames accurately. To address this challenge, we propose an alignment network with the temporal lookup table (temporal-LUT), which effectively enhances the haze-degraded frames and provides vivid cues for precise alignment. Specifically, to tackle the color degradation of haze, we employ a learnable lookup table (LUT) to enhance hazy color. The color mapping nature of LUT favorably preserves the naturalness of enhanced outcomes. Besides, we introduce a temporal weight prediction strategy to strengthen inter-frame interaction, which ensures temporal consistency across enhanced results and thereby benefits alignment. Extensive experimental results on two widely used benchmarks and real-world scenes demonstrate the superiority of our method.

title: Semi-Supervised Model-Free Bayesian State Estimation from Compressed Measurements
authors: Anubhab Ghosh, Yonina C. Eldar, Saikat Chatterjee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: We consider data-driven Bayesian state estimation from compressed measurements (BSCM) of a model-free process. The dimension of the temporal measurement vector is lower than that of the temporal state vector to be estimated, leading to an under-determined inverse problem. The underlying dynamical model of the state’s evolution is unknown for a ‘model-free process.’ Hence, it is difficult to use traditional model-driven methods, for example, Kalman and particle filters. Instead, we consider data-driven methods. We experimentally show that two existing unsupervised learning-based data-driven methods fail to address the BSCM problem in a model-free process. The methods are – data-driven nonlinear state estimation (DANSE) and deep Markov model (DMM). While DANSE provides good predictive/ forecasting performance to model the temporal measurement data as a time series, its unsupervised learning lacks suitable regularization for tackling the BSCM task. We then propose a semi-supervised learning approach and develop a semi-supervised learning-based DANSE method, referred to as SemiDANSE. In SemiDANSE, we use a large amount of unlabelled data along with a limited amount of labelled data, i.e., pairwise measurement-and-state data, which provides the desired regularization. Using benchmark chaotic dynamical systems, we empirically show that the data-driven SemiDANSE provides competitive state estimation performance for BSCM using a handful of different measurement systems, against a hybrid method called KalmanNet and two model-driven methods (extended Kalman filter and unscented Kalman filter) that know the dynamical models exactly.

title: Smart ID Card Detection and Authentication Using Deep Learning and Computer Vision
authors: D. Vinoparkavi, V. Mythili, Dharun Raj R, Deepika Y, Nithwin V M
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: ID card verification has become operational bottleneck in banking and education sectors etc. Manual verification of ID for example, takes 2-5 minutes for each person and because of this the queues during peak hours can get very long. In these conditions the percentage of error could get as high as 15-20%. The following idea is for an automated system for the mentioned above problem using a multi-stage framework which uses YOLOv5 for detecting ID cards in real-time Tesseract OCR with a template-based method for text extraction and face recognition for identity matching of persons. This system will operate on generic computing hardware and does not require any special device. Experimental results show that the system detects with an accuracy of 96.8% extracts text with precision of 94.2% and has a speed of 25-30 frames per second. Security features developed in this system have been able to detect fraud from the printed or digital reproductions with an efficacy of 85.8%. This solution in the paper reduces the spell of verification from minutes to just a few seconds and with high accuracy without being sensitive for the type of cards and light conditions.

title: Topography-Aware Adaptive Fusion for Multi-Scale Super Resolution of Multi-Source DEMs
authors: Haiyan Pan, Jianqiao Ji, Zhonghua Hong, Kuifeng Luan, Jiang Tao, Chenchen Jiang, Han Zhang, Yanling Han, Yun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Super resolution (SR) of digital elevation models (DEMs) is crucial for numerous geospatial applications, yet existing methods still face severe challenges when dealing with multi-scale reconstruction and multi-source data. In particular, mainstream DEM SR approaches primarily rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), whose inherent local receptive fields limit their ability to model the global structural consistency of large geomorphic units such as mountain ranges. As a result, when handling terrains with spatial heterogeneity, it is difficult to simultaneously ensure the fidelity of local details. To address this feature representation bottleneck, this paper proposes a unified framework named TAAF-SRNet. The framework integrates collaborative learning with meta-learning-based upsampling to enable flexible multi-scale reconstruction. At its core are the enhanced feature extraction module (EFEM), which adopts a parallel CNN-Transformer architecture, and a novel terrain aware adaptive fusion mechanism. This mechanism can dynamically assess local terrain complexity and adaptively adjust the weighting between the local textures captured by CNNs and the global context modeled by Transformers. The terrain aware strategy enables a unified model to adapt to diverse landforms, ranging from vast abyssal plains to rugged terrestrial mountain ranges. Extensive experiments on different DEM datasets covering three representative heterogeneous geomorphologies, namely land, land-sea transition, and seabed, demonstrate that TAAFSRNet not only outperforms current state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods on image quality metrics such as PSNR and LPIPS, but also better preserves terrain fidelity, as reflected by lower MAE and RMSE in elevation, slope, and aspect estimation. The code will be available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/bbqboy666/TAAF-SRNet</uri>.
