Further tests using Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) simulations affirm the durability and practicality of the MPC approach. The paper also discusses the design needs for high-performing boost converters and inverters, highlighting the importance of digital signal processing in improving system efficiency. We used Texas Instruments’ TMS320F28379D digital signal processor, which features dual CPUs and a 16-bit resolution ADC, to develop and test specific MPC controllers and electronic circuits. This setup significantly enhances the performance of high-efficiency charger boards and offers a cost-effective solution for smart homes, optimizing energy utilization through the smart grid.

title: Graph Neural Network Framework for Interpretable EEG-Based Emotion Recognition Using Frequency-Band and Connectivity Analysis
authors: Ch Anwar Ul Hassan, Azhar Imran, Khursheed Aurangzeb, Hashim Elshafie
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The healthcare field, human-computer interaction, and affective computing development require accurate human emotional recognition through electroencephalography (EEG) signals. EEG signals present intricate spatial temporal and spectral dependencies that both traditional machine learning and deep learning methods fail to capture resulting in restricted cross-subject generalizability and limited understanding. We introduce MER-TFC, a new EEG-based emotion recognition system which uses GraphNeuralNetworks (GNNs) with attention mechanisms to create inter-channel connectivity and temporal models from EEG data while connecting theta, alpha, beta, and gamma frequency bands. The framework is evaluated using leave-one-subject-out cross-validation (LOSOCV) on DEAP and AMIGOS.benchmark datasets. The experimental results show that MER-TFC achieves 88.2 % average accuracy with 87.7 % F1-score and 0.93 ROC-AUC and 87.9 % balanced accuracy which outperforms Transformer-CNN, Multilayer Graph Transformer, Frequency-Adaptive Dynamic Graph Transformer (FreqDGT) and Adaptive Progressive Attention GNN (APAGNN). Attention analysis reveals brain regions that play critical roles and shows how emotional states correspond to functional brain network connections. The proposed framework also maintains high performance when EEG signals experience background noise, which demonstrates its ability to function well on different subjects.

authors: N/A

title: A Cross-Modal Semantic Alignment Method and Computer-Aided Translation System for English Translation
authors: Jia He
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: To address the surge in cross-modal demands for English translation in a globalized context, existing cross-modal semantic alignment algorithms suffer from low accuracy and poor adaptability, and computer-aided translation (CAT) systems struggle to effectively integrate image, audio and text multimodal information. This paper proposes a translation-oriented modality adaptive attention semantic alignment (TMAASA) algorithm and constructs a CAT system based on this algorithm. Multiple original constraints and computational models are designed, and the algorithm is encapsulated and integrated into the system. Simulation experiments verify its performance. Experiments show that the TMAASA algorithm achieves an alignment accuracy of 92.78%, an average alignment error of 0.036, a BLEU-4 value of 58.96, and a single-sentence response time of 0.32s, all outperforming mainstream comparison algorithms. The constructed system is functionally complete and stable, effectively improving the quality and efficiency of English translation. This research provides a new technical path for the application of crossmodal technology in English translation, possessing significant engineering application value and theoretical reference significance.

title: Fault-Tolerant Consensus Control for T-S Fuzzy Multiagent Systems Under Aperiodic Time-Constrained Sampling Communication and Stochastic Actuator Faults
authors: Pratap Anbalagan, Yibin Tian, Zhiguang Feng, Tingwen Huang, Yukang Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: The main objective of this study is to develop a fault-tolerant fuzzy time-constrained memory-sampled-data control (TCMSDC) mechanism for analyzing the consensus performance of nonlinear multiagent systems (MASs) affected by stochastic actuator faults and external disturbances. To achieve this, the nonlinear MASs are first converted into quasi-linear subsystems using the Takagi–Sugeno (T-S) fuzzy model. In contrast to existing memory-sampled-data consensus methods, the constructed TCMSDC signals vary over time within each sampling period, thereby enhancing consensus performance. Thereafter, a Markov variable process is applied to model multiple stochastic actuator faults in the considered MASs. Furthermore, an aperiodic-sampling-dependent asymmetric dual-looped functional (ASADLF) is constructed, which incorporates different sets of matrices designed for each sampling instance. Subsequently, a new nonorthogonal polynomial integral inequality (NOPII) is introduced to approximate the integral quadratic terms. By leveraging this ASADLF along with the proposed NOPII technique, less conservative consensus criteria are derived in the form of linear matrix inequalities (LMIs), and the TCMSDC gain parameters are obtained to guarantee mean-square asymptotic consensus with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> performance for the considered MASs. Finally, the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed TCMSDC approach in enhancing fault tolerance and achieving reliable consensus under stochastic actuator faults are validated through numerical simulations on multi-ship steering autonomous surface vehicles (SSASVs) and mass–spring systems.

title: Variable Selection-Based Recursive Method for Adaptive Quality-Related Fault Detection
authors: Yanyu Chen, Hao Ma, Yan Wang, Feng Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Fault detection (process monitoring) for dynamic and nonlinear industrial processes, which can be viewed as complex systems, has become increasingly important in recent years. Under complex and dynamic operating conditions, traditional multivariate statistical process monitoring (MSPM) methods struggle to effectively capture the evolving behaviors of quality indicators at the system level. In this article, a novel variable selection-based adaptive MSPM method is proposed for quality-related process monitoring. First, an improved part mutual information (PMI) method is proposed for variable selection, categorizing process variables into quality-related and quality-unrelated groups, addressing the shortcomings of the original PMI being able to measure the correlation between only two variables. Second, a novel recursive kernel principal component regression (RKPCR) incorporating the hierarchical model order-reduction strategy is proposed to effectively track quality indicators. This method screens high-quality data for model updates based on distance-based classification and linear approximation, which mitigates model degradation caused by faulty data. In addition, a recursive kernel principal component analysis (RKPCA) method similar to RKPCR is proposed to monitor quality-unrelated faults. Finally, a numerical example and two industrial processes are used to demonstrate the performance of the proposed method.

title: Malware Detection and Encryption: A Meta-Survey with a Unified Analytical Framework
authors: Aleksandra Pawlicka, Marek Pawlicki, Paweł Rajba, Rafał Kozik, Wojciech Mazurczyk, Luca Caviglione, Michał Choraś
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a meta-survey of the current state of the art in malware detection and the use of cryptographic evasion and obfuscation techniques in malicious software. Based on a corpus of over 70 selected studies, it synthesises heterogeneous survey findings via a unified analytical framework. Then, as the topic of crypto-malware is largely absent from the academic survey corpus, a narrative review of those mechanisms is provided, drawing on primary technical literature and industry threat intelligence. The paper identifies four principal findings: malware detection is increasingly framed as a representation-aware learning pipeline; robustness must be treated as a first-order qualifier of any state-of-the-art claim; the cryptographic and steganographic dimension of malware is underrepresented in the survey literature relative to its operational relevance; and persistent white spaces remain in the literature, particularly for IoT graph-based detection, Android image-based detection, and crypto/stego-aware detection pipelines.

title: Defense Energy Allocation for Networked Control Systems Under False Data Injection Attacks
authors: Xiao-Hui Liu, Guang-Hong Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article studies the defense energy allocation problem for networked control systems based on a Stackelberg game framework. Different from the existing works, an active false data injection (FDI) attack defense scheme is introduced, which can reduce the impact of undetectable FDI attacks on the estimator. The optimal energy allocation strategy in the active defense scheme is derived from the framework of the dynamic Stackelberg game. When the attacker's reward function is unknown, a sufficient condition for the existence of the optimal defense energy allocation strategy is derived. Under this condition, the original problem is transformed into a linear programming problem without loss of optimality. Finally, a numerical example illustrates the validity of the proposed results.

title: Corrections to “45°/90° Broadband Phase Shifter MMICs Using Switchable Electric–Magnetic Coupling Path Architecture”
authors: Na Ji, Guangxu Shen, Dingyuan Zeng, Wenjie Feng, Wenquan Che
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In the above article [1], there were errors on pages 2 and 8. They are explained below, and the corrections are provided.

title: Design of a Tunable Microstrip Combline Bandpass Filter Using Varactor Diodes for UWB Applications
authors: Raneem A. Al-Mashni, Lujain F. Al-Hunity, Yanal S. Faouri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, tunable microstrip combline bandpass filter (BPF) designed for ultra-wideband (UWB) applications. By loading the resonators with varactors, continuous center frequency tuning was achieved from 3.38 to 7.105 GHz, which corresponds to a 2.1:1 ratio. According to the simulation results, the design maintained an insertion loss below 3 dB and a return loss better than -10 dB. Stability in the fractional bandwidth is observed between 310 and 450 MHz across the entire range, whereas group delay fluctuations are maintained within 1.0–2.2 ns. These performance metrics make the filter a practical solution for reconfigurable RF front-end and cognitive radio systems.

title: Field-Oriented Control of Induction Motor Drive Using Indian Drive Cycle
authors: Soha Yadav, Meera Murali, Sushil Ramdasi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive simulation and performance analysis of a Field-Oriented Control (FOC) based induction motor drive intended for electric vehicle applications. The Indian Drive Cycle (IDC) is employed as the reference speed profile to emulate urban driving behavior. The induction motor drive (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.5 \text{kW}, 1440 \text{rpm}, 400 \mathrm{V}, 50 \text{Hz}$</tex>) is simulated in Simulink. The drive is tested under a near-rated constant torque load of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$49 \mathbf{N} \cdot \mathbf{m}$</tex> to emulate demanding EV traction conditions such as vehicle launch and gradeability. MTPA and field weakening algorithm is employed to regulate proper functioning of speed and torque according to the IDC parameters as per the torque load. Simulation results demonstrate effective decoupled control of torque, accurate speed tracking, reduced torque ripple, improved efficiency and robustness under specific load.

title: Design and Implementation of a Unified Financial Decision-Support System Using Predictive and Anomaly-Based Analytics
authors: Joshua S, Abdul Azim S, Vengadesan S, B. Monica Jenefer
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Accelerated expansion of digital payment infrastructures, algorithmic trading environments, and continuous market ecosystems has amplified demand for intelligent, cohesive, and adaptive financial decision-support research platforms. Conventional financial applications function as fragmented utilities, providing restricted predictive insight, limited anomaly awareness, and negligible user-level customization. A stock trend forecasting component applies statistical learning techniques over historical equity data to estimate price movement across a 90-day interval, achieving a predictive accuracy of \textbf{92.4%} during experimental evaluation, with automated notification delivery. anomaly-oriented classification models capable of recognizing irregular behavioral patterns in real time, yielding \textbf{94.1% detection accuracy} during prototype validation. A live equity observation interface incorporates publicly available financial data streams for continuous price tracking and performance ranking. Personal finance supervision includes income-expense logging, expenditure visualization, budget deviation alerts, and rule-driven allocation guidance supporting investment-oriented planning. Experimental outcomes confirm improved financial awareness, strengthened risk mitigation, and enhanced personalization under real-time operational conditions. The originality of the presented financial intelligence framework can be highlighted. It clearly describes the system design, analysis process, and design architecture of integrated modules. The most important evaluation indicators and the results of experiments are briefly stated in order to mark quantitative performance. Any type of generic and literature-style descriptions have been eliminated to enhance technical specificity. The updated abstract is dedicated to the originality and practice applicability of the suggested work. These changes bring about greater compliance with the standards of publication at conferences and originality.

title: Robo-taxi Fleet Coordination at Scale via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Luigi Tresca, Carolin Schmidt, James Harrison, Filipe Rodrigues, Gioele Zardini, Daniele Gammelli, Marco Pavone
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Fleets of robo-taxis offering on-demand transportation services, commonly known as Autonomous Mobility-on-Demand (AMoD) systems, hold significant promise for societal benefits, such as reducing pollution, energy consumption, and urban congestion. However, orchestrating these systems at scale remains a critical challenge, with existing coordination algorithms often failing to exploit the systems' full potential. This work introduces a novel decision-making framework that unites mathematical modeling with data-driven techniques. In particular, we present the AMoD coordination problem through the lens of reinforcement learning and propose a graph network-based framework that exploits the main strengths of graph representation learning, reinforcement learning, and classical operations research tools. Extensive evaluations across diverse simulation fidelities and scenarios demonstrate the flexibility of our approach, achieving superior system performance, computational efficiency, and generalizability compared to prior methods. Finally, motivated by the need to democratize research efforts in this area, we release publicly available benchmarks, datasets, and simulators for network-level coordination alongside an open-source codebase designed to provide accessible simulation platforms and establish a standardized validation process for comparing methodologies.

title: A New Approach to the Data-Driven Output-Based LQR Problem of Continuous-Time Linear Systems
authors: Liquan Lin, Haoyan Lin, Jie Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: A promising method for constructing a data-driven output-feedback control law involves the construction of a model-free observer. The Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) optimal control policy can then be obtained by both policy-iteration (PI) and value-iteration (VI) algorithms. However, this method requires some unknown parameterization matrix to be of full row rank and needs to solve a sequence of high dimensional linear equations for either PI or VI algorithm. In this paper, we first show that this matrix is of full row rank under the standard controllability assumption of the plant, thus removing a hurdle for applying this method. Then we further modify the existing method by defining an auxiliary system whose LQR solution will lead to a data-driven output-feedback control law. By this new method, the rank condition of the unknown parameterization matrix is not needed any more. Moreover, we derive a new sequence of linear equations for either PI or VI algorithm whose number of unknown variables is significantly less than that of the existing PI or VI algorithm, thus not only improving the computational efficiency but also relaxing the solvability conditions of the existing PI or VI algorithm. Further, since the existing PI or VI algorithm only applies to the case where some Riccati equation admits a unique positive definite solution, we prove that both the PI algorithm and the VI algorithm in the literature can be generalized to the case where the Riccati equation only admits a unique positive semi-definite solution.

title: A Bit-Level Loosely Coupled Spiking Neural Network Accelerator With Fast Inference and Hybrid Early Termination
authors: Junchuan Gu, Yiwen Gu, Xinran Li, Haibin Shen, Kejie Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Deep spiking neural networks (SNNs) tend to suffer from long inference latency. While existing encoding schemes are insufficient, this work applies a bit-level loosely coupled (BLC) SNN in which output bits are generated sequentially from the current input bit. Consequently, the time steps can be further reduced with minor accuracy loss. The BLC SNN is optimized for hardware implementation and configured based on quantized artificial neural networks (ANNs). A hybrid early termination (ET) scheme is employed to skip redundant computation cycles without accuracy degradation. A pipelined digital accelerator architecture is designed to implement the BLC SNN, in which line buffers are utilized to maximize data reuse. Simulation results show that the proposed hybrid ET scheme reduces computation cycles by 28.31% on LeNet-5. Simulated in SMIC 28 nm technology, the accelerator consumes <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.10~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>J/img at 500 MHz with 30.49 TSOPS/W and is adaptable to 8/4 time steps of inference.

title: Enhancing Multi-Step Solar Irradiance Forecasting through TVF-EMD-AWNN: a Time-Varying Hybrid Framework for PV Power Plant
authors: Sakshi Shukla, Sumanta Pasari, Kshitij Gupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Computing
abstract: Accurate solar irradiance prediction is inevitable for estimating photovoltaic energy generation and meeting utility demands. This paper presents a novel TVF-EMD-AWNN hybrid framework that integrates time-varying filter empirical mode decomposition (TVF-EMD) with an adaptive wavelet neural network (AWNN) for enhanced prediction accuracy at an hourly timescale. The proposed framework encompasses a sophisticated three-fold practically relevant integration architecture. First, the adaptive filtering mechanism of TVF enables dynamic frequency time optimization while maintaining non-stationary signal characteristics through its time-varying filtering process. Then, the EMD decomposes these enhanced signals into intrinsic mode functions, exhibiting unique temporal-spectral signatures and facilitating comprehensive multi-resolution analysis. Finally, the adaptive wavelet parameters in AWNN synergistically represent these decomposed components through dynamic scale-translation optimization in the wavelet space. Additionally, the proposed TVF-EMD-AWNN model is also validated through the expanding window cross-validation approach. The theory is illustrated on a long hourly solar irradiance data (2015–2021) at four diverse climatic regions of India. Results demonstrate noteworthy accuracy improvement in terms of relative RMSE reduction in comparison to established standalone and hybrid models, such as MLP (83%), LSTM (85%), Bi-LSTM (82%), EDBi-LSTM (80%), AWNN (78%), CEEMDAN-LSTM (83%), CEEMDAN EDBi-LSTM (80%), TVF-EMD-LSTM (65%), and TVF-EMD BiLSTM (54%). Besides, the performance of TVF-EMD-AWNN remains consistent even at multi-step hourly predictions. Therefore, the proposed hybrid framework with additional benchmarking, ablation studies, and time series validation offers robustness towards practical power system operation.

title:
Ferroelectric-Modulated Spin to Charge Conversion and THz Emission in Bilayer WTe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Heterostructures
authors: C Kishandev, Joel Isaac Joy, Jeevan Thomas Korah, Eldho Markose, Nikhil Kumar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Bilayer WTe2 is investigated as an efficient spin–charge conversion interface for ultrathin spintronic terahertz (THz) emitters. First-principles calculations reveal that the out-of-plane ferroelectric polarization non-volatily dictates the symmetry of the Rashbasplit bands, enabling a binary reversal of the Edelstein tensor sign and leading to polarization-dependent spin accumulation near the Fermi level. While spintronic THz emitters have emerged as a novel route toward compact THz sources, the next challenge remains the active, low-power modulation of emission without relying on external magnetic fields. We address this by exploiting the intrinsic structural symmetries of bilayer transition metal dichalcogenides; specifically, the distorted 1T′ crystalline phase and the emergence of sliding ferroelectricity in WTe2. In this work, we present a theoretical analysis of an Al/CoFeB/WTe2/LiNbO3 heterostructure where the ferroelectric polarization acts as a low-energy gate to modulate the interfacial inverse Rashba–Edelstein effect (IREE). By implementing a propagation model based on the Transfer Matrix Method (TMM), we show that the ferroelectric polarization direction induces a sizable modulation of the THz emission amplitude along with a clear phase reversal upon polarization switching. Unlike traditional spintronic emitters that require energy-intensive magnetic field bias for modulation, our results demonstrate that sliding ferroelectric architectures enable non-volatile, reconfigurable regulation of THz emission.

title: IPsec Based on Quantum Key Distribution: Adapting Non-3GPP Access to 5G Networks to the Quantum Era
authors: Asier Atutxa, Ane Sanz, Eire Salegi, Gaizka González, Jasone Astorga, Eduardo Jacob
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: The advent of quantum computing threatens publickey cryptography mechanisms currently used in 5G networks, including IPsec-based tunnel establishment relying on Diffie-Hellman (DH) key exchange. This issue is especially relevant for non-3GPP access scenarios, where User Equipment (UE) connects to the 5G core through the Non-3GPP Interworking Function (N3IWF) using IPsec. This paper presents a Quantum Key Distribution (QKD)-based mechanism for non-3GPP access in 5G networks, replacing the classical DH exchange with QKD keys during IPsec tunnel establishment. The proposed system is experimentally validated in a testbed combining commercial QKD equipment and an open-source 5G core implementation.

title: Effect of ECRAM Temperature-Dependent Conductance on Analog In-Memory Computing Inference Accuracy
authors: M.A. Islam, A. Dozier, H.M.R. Farque, R.C. Dempsey, T.Q. Nguyen, M.J. Voegtle, M.N. Kozicki, M.J. Marinella
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: Analog in-memory computing (AIMC) based on electrochemical random-access memory (ECRAM) offers a promising route to energy-efficient and high-accuracy AI computing. However, the temperature-dependent conductance introduces error that degrades AIMC accuracy. This work characterizes device conductance over the temperature range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2}^{\circ} \mathbf{C}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}^{\circ} \mathbf{C}$</tex> and develops a transport model based on nearestneighbor hopping. The temperature dependence degrades CIFAR-10 and ImageNet accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a temperature-aware scaling method that compensates for conductance variation. Applying this correction technique maintains CIFAR-10 accuracy within 2% of ideal through the entire temperature range, demonstrating a path to accurate AIMC inference across commercially relevant environments.

title: Research on Fault Section Location in Distribution Network Based on Improved Spider Wasp Optimization Algorithm
authors: Jiadong Su, Zhijian Liang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: In recent years, the topology of modern distribution networks has undergone continuous evolution and restructuring due to the large-scale integration of distributed energy resources. As a result, conventional intelligent optimization algorithms are increasingly unable to meet the requirements for rapid fault location in these networks. In response to the aforementioned challenges, this paper innovatively applies the Spider Wasp Optimization Algorithm (ISWO) to fault location in distribution networks. By leveraging its strong exploration and exploitation capabilities, global search performance, high search efficiency, and solution accuracy, the algorithm is further enhanced through three improvements: optimal point set initialization, sine-based search guidance, and adaptive mutation and crossover probabilities. These modifications significantly improve its effectiveness in addressing the optimization objectives of fault location. Extensive comparative experiments and data analysis demonstrate that the improved ISWO achieves superior performance in terms of fault location speed, accuracy, and fault tolerance.

title: An Explainable Stacked Ensemble Framework for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Protyush Das Gupta, Sourav Adhikary
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Credit card fraud continues to evolve with increasingly complex attack patterns which is creating serious challenges for financial institutions and consumers. This paper presents an explainable stacked ensemble framework that integrates Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) and a Deep Neural Network (DNN) as base learners with a Logistic Regression meta-classifier. Unlike prior works that focus solely on accuracy, our approach jointly addresses predictive performance and interpretability by incorporating SHAP for global feature importance and LIME for local decision explanations. Experiments on a large-scale Kaggle dataset demonstrate that the proposed ensemble achieves superior precision, recall, and F1scores compared to individual models and recent IoT-focused methods. Moreover, we introduce two SHAP configurations—fast and slow that allow trade-offs between computational cost and interpretability, making the framework adaptable for both research and real-time deployment. These results highlight the potential of explainable ensemble learning as a practical solution for secure and transparent fraud detection systems.

title: Design and Integration of an Intelligent Battery Management System for Low Voltage Redox Flow Battery with Solar Interface
authors: S Hemavathi, P. Sanjeevikumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Redox flow batteries (RFBs) are receiving increasing attention as reliable solutions for long-duration energy storage, particularly integrated with renewable energy sources. Unlike conventional battery systems, low-voltage RFB stacks operate over narrow voltage ranges and exhibit strong electrothermal hydraulic coupling, which poses unique challenges for battery management system (BMS) design and accurate state estimation. The paper presents the design and experimental validation of an intelligent BMS specifically developed for lowvoltage RFB stacks operating over an extended voltage range of $8-28 \mathrm{~V}$, supporting both 12 V and 24 V configurations. The proposed system integrates a bidirectional buck-boost DC power converter with a solar photovoltaic (PV) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) unit, a multi-domain sensing architecture, and a 32-bit embedded controller capable of executing hybrid state-of-charge (SOC) and state-of-health (SOH) estimation algorithms. SOC for validation was established using controlled current integration with voltage correction, enabling robust monitoring under dynamic operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate consistent MPPT convergence, stable charge-discharge behavior, SOC estimation errors within $\pm \mathbf{5} \boldsymbol{\%}$, and round-trip energy efficiency exceeding 90%. These results indicate that the proposed BMS provides a compact, scalable, and effective solution for solar-coupled low-voltage RFB systems.

title: Research on a Comprehensive Evaluation Method for Power Outage Losses of Users in the Power Grid
authors: Wei Liu, Shenglong Zhu, Jian Ge, Chao Zhen, Zhengkai Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: In order to enhance the resilience of the power grid under natural disasters and accurately quantify power outage losses, this study establishes a system for classifying outageaffected users and evaluating losses based on multidimensional data analysis. First, by collecting and integrating historical outage data caused by natural disasters such as storms, floods, and ice storms, a multidimensional feature set is constructed, and clustering analysis is applied to achieve refined user portrait classification within the power supply grid. Second, combining statistical and machine learning techniques, differentiated models for evaluating economic losses and social impacts of power outages are developed for different user categories (residential, commercial, industrial). Finally, by integrating regional grid structures, disaster scenarios, and user distribution differences, a natural disaster outage risk identification model is developed to identify weak points in the grid and high-risk user groups and areas, thereby providing scientific basis and decision support for optimizing pre-disaster prevention and post-disaster rapid restoration strategies.

title:
Retention Characteristics in HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Based FeRAM: Degradation Mechanisms and Lifetime Prediction
authors: Wanmei Zhang, Wenhao Liu, Xingcheng Jin, Chongyong Guo, Hongbo Li, Yutai Man, Fei Yu, Peiyuan Du, Jiayi Zhao, Jiabao Ye, Junru Qu, Keyang Zhu, Hongrui Zhang, Huan Liu, Wei Mao, Jiuren Zhou, Ran Cheng, Yan Liu, Bing Chen, Xiao Yu, Genquan Han
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This study conducts a systematic investigation of the retention characteristics of HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based ferroelectric random-access memory (FeRAM) in both planar and 3D-trench structures. The retention characteristics are evaluated under various voltages and temperatures in different states, including initial wake-up and fatigue. Two dominant degradation mechanisms are identified: reversible degradation caused by thermally assisted polarization switching and long-term degradation due to charge injection. In addition, a retention lifetime prediction is conducted. For the first time, it is confirmed that Arrhenius-based modeling is applicable to FeRAM retention prediction at the wafer scale.

title: A Unified Model-Based Framework For Whole Warship Engineering
authors: Pierluigi Gravili, Andrea Gualco, Gianluigi Mattia, Manuela Nardini, Elisa Nitoglia, Francesco Inglima, Francesco Nudo, Giuliano Pompeo, Antonella Persechino, Pasquale Polisi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The increasing technological complexity of modern military ships calls for innovative methodologies capable of managing Systems-of-Systems with high integration and interdependence. This paper introduces the Whole Warship Unified Model-Based Framework, a novel Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) approach developed by Orizzonte Sistemi Navali in collaboration with Capgemini Engineering. The framework treats the warship as a single integrated system, consolidating functional, physical, logistic, and human-related aspects within a coherent digital model spanning the entire lifecycle. Acting as a model-centric backbone, it connects requirements, behaviors, and constraints from conceptual design to operation and maintenance, ensuring traceability and validation continuity. The Whole Warship model embeds systems engineering and integrated logistics support into a common digital environment, enabling consistent analysis of operability, crew workload, and human-machine interactions. Due to the incorporation of logistic and human factors from the outset, the framework supports lifecycle trade-offs in cost, availability, and maintainability. This unified approach represents a major step forward in warship design, providing a single authoritative source of truth that improves efficiency, transparency, and decision-making throughout lifecycle. In addition, its architecture establishes digital continuity across domains and facilitates the integration of AIassisted analyzes for improved design iteration and operational evaluation.

title: Leveraging SATCOM Ka-Band Signals for PNTaaS
authors: Maor Dov, Alison Brown, Oleg Yakimenko, Giovanni Minelli, Noah Weitz
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) technology has existed for decades and has become so pervasive in modern life that reliance on it now extends far beyond what was originally envisioned, particularly in military applications. As GNSS usage expanded, the threats and vulnerabilities associated with it also grew, especially on the battlefield where GNSS signals are frequently targeted by adversary jamming. GNSS suffers from several inherent disadvantages, foremost among them its narrow and well-known frequency bands, which make it comparatively easy to disrupt. In response to these limitations, a new field of research has emerged in recent years, aiming to develop alternative Position, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) solutions that provide far greater resilience than GNSS. This field, known as Signals of Opportunity (SoOP), leverages opportunistic, non-GNSS signals to construct robust PNT solutions while avoiding many of the fundamental weaknesses inherent in GNSS technology. NAVSYS has recently advanced this concept by developing an innovative cloud-based technology that offers PNT as a Service (PNTaaS). This system aggregates signals from multiple satellite providers across diverse frequency bands to generate an alternative and more resilient PNT solution. To strengthen PNT robustness, incorporating signals from as many providers and orbital infrastructures as possible is essential. Building on NAVSYS’s efforts, this paper investigates the potential use of orbital systems that emit signals in the Ka band-specifically the O3b mPOWER constellation, a high-throughput medium Earth orbit (MEO) system. The discussion includes the envisioned system architecture, along with opportunities and challenges associated with tracking Ka-band signals from both MEO and geostationary (GEO) satellites. These signals may enhance positional dilution of precision, thus improving the performance of SoOP-based PNT solutions. Additionally, this paper presents preliminary results related to system modeling, signal integration approaches, and anticipated performance assessments for incorporating Ka-band satellite emissions into future resilient PNT architectures.

title: Graph Neural Network-based Detection of Man-in-the-Middle Attacks
authors: Awatef Khoury, Avi Mendelson, Ori Shacham-Barr
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks occur when an adversary intercepts and manipulates communication between legitimate entities, posing a serious threat to network confidentiality and integrity. These attacks remain challenging to detect and mitigate, particularly in scenarios involving multiple MitM nodes. This paper introduces a detection and localization method that leverages the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) and a Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based model to accurately identify MitM nodes. The proposed method represents network communication as a graph comprising IP-MAC address relationships and intermediate connection nodes, enabling the GNN to learn both traffic patterns and structural anomalies indicative of MitM activity. Experimental evaluations on real and simulated datasets demonstrate that the system achieves an F1 score exceeding 99 percent, outperforming existing methods in detection accuracy and introducing a previously unexplored capability-precise attacker attribution.

title: Meta-Learning for Quantum Optimization via Quantum Sequence Model
authors: Yu-Cheng Lin, Yu-Chao Hsu, Samuel Yen-Chi Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: The Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) is a leading approach for solving combinatorial optimization problems on near-term quantum processors. However, finding good variational parameters remains a significant challenge due to the non-convex energy landscape, often resulting in slow convergence and poor solution quality. In this work, we propose a quantum meta-learning framework that trains advanced quantum sequence models to generate effective parameter initialization policies. We investigate four classical or quantum sequence models, including the Quantum Kernel-based Long Short-Term Memory (QK-LSTM), as learned optimizers in a “learning to learn” paradigm. Our numerical experiments on the Max-Cut problem demonstrate that the QK-LSTM optimizer achieves superior performance, obtaining the highest approximation ratios and exhibiting the fastest convergence rate across all tested problem sizes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex> = 10 to 13). Crucially, the QKLSTM model achieves highly robust parameter transferability by synthesizing a single, fixed set of near-optimal parameters, leading to a remarkable sustained acceleration of convergence even when generalizing to larger problems. This capability, enabled by the compact and expressive power of the quantum kernel architecture, underscores its effectiveness. The QK-LSTM, with only 43 trainable parameters, substantially outperforms the classical LSTM (56 parameters) and other quantum sequence models, establishing a robust pathway toward highly efficient parameter initialization for variational quantum algorithms in the NISQ era.

title: Joint Scheduling of Use-Based and Calendar Inspections in Military Aircraft Fleet Planning
authors: Matteo Vescovi, Gianluca Massara, Valentina Breschi, Ten. Col. Roberto Valdambrini, Col. Andrea Mercurio, Mara Tanelli
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The scheduling of flight operations and maintenance activities is commonly referred in military aviation as Flight and Maintenance Planning; and it is essential to guarantee military fleet availability and mission readiness for both routine and high-intensity operations. Although maintenance programs may vary significantly depending on configuration, usage patterns, or specific system requirements; in a general form, aircrafts follow a strict, predefined inspection schedule, including both use-based (in term of flown hours) and calendar-based inspection, that may differ from one aircraft to another within the same fleet. Moreover, it is common practice to merge inspections of different types when they are scheduled to occur in close succession, thereby reducing downtime of shared maintenance operations. Existing studies rarely address such mixed-type inspections and often rely on excessively simplified, unrealistic maintenance schemes. This work proposes an ad hoc Mixed-Integer Programming formulation that extends established models by integrating inspection data from lookup tables directly into the optimisation constraints, allowing the joint management of complex flight-hour-based and calendar-based inspection plans within a unified framework. Tests on a simple yet realistic fleet confirm the model’s ability to plan mixed inspections effectively while preserving operational feasibility and resource balance. The approach offers a flexible foundation for real-life preventive maintenance planning, even outside the aviation or military context.

title: Development of Correlation for Assessment of BIPV Potential Under Shading Constraints
authors: Priyanka Rai, Raj Gupta, Jyotirmay Mathur, Amartya Chowdhury
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Building Integrated Photovoltaic (BIPV) systems are increasingly considered for solar deployment in dense urban areas where rooftop availability is limited. However, adjacent buildings often obstruct solar access and cause substantial irradiation losses, leading to inaccurate energy yield estimates at early design stages. This study develops a geometry-based correlation to quantify shading-induced irradiation losses for building-integrated photovoltaic façades under urban obstruction conditions. A large parametric simulation framework was established using DesignBuilder with EnergyPlus as the simulation engine and Python-based automation. Obstruction height, width, and inter-building distance were systematically varied along with façade orientation and geographical location. More than 5,500 annual simulation cases were generated across multiple Indian cities covering a wide latitudinal range. Irradiation losses were evaluated relative to unobstructed reference cases and analysed using regression and feature-importance techniques. The results show that obstruction geometry dominates shading losses, while latitude and altitude have a secondary influence. East and west façades exhibit the highest sensitivity to shading, whereas north facades show comparatively lower losses. Orientationspecific polynomial correlations are proposed for rapid estimation of shading losses during early design. The developed correlations provide a practical and interpretable tool for realistic performance assessment of façade-integrated photovoltaic systems in compact urban contexts.

title: Design of a Hall Signal Simulator for Three-Phase DC Brushless Motors
authors: Hengtian Yang, Fuyuan Deng, Lulu Chen, Liangmei Zhang, Kangjun Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: In the design and development of three-phase brushless DC motor drivers, using a physical motor as the load introduces issues like large mechanical inertia, high energy consumption, and safety risks; thus, high-power resistors are often used for static debugging to replace motor windings, but driver commutation logic relies heavily on Hall signals for rotor position feedback. To address this, this paper proposes a pure hardware Hall signal simulator based on CD4060 and CH250: it constructs a high-precision clock source with CD4060 (using a parameter-matched external crystal oscillator for stable output) and leverages the CH250's three-phase single six-step mode to generate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 0 ~ H z}$</tex> three-phase analog Hall signals with 120° mutual phase difference-these signals can directly replace a motor's built-in Hall outputs, meeting drive circuit debugging needs. Analysis shows the simulator accurately emulates physical motor position feedback, providing a reliable hardware solution for driver closedloop logic verification.

title: Structured Reasoning Frameworks for LLM-Based Venture Capital Evaluation: Integrating Systems Engineering and Business Analysis
authors: Dan Velarde, Stephen Gillespie
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Venture capital decision-making for early-stage startups involves high uncertainty and relies heavily on qualitative reasoning from pitch decks. This study investigates whether large language models (LLMs) equipped with structured knowledge frameworks can improve predictive accuracy in startup evaluation. We designed four evaluation personas using Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview: a baseline model, a venture capital-informed model, a systems engineering-guided model using established standards from IEEE, INCOSE, NASA, and MITRE, and a combined model integrating both frameworks. Testing on 14 historical pitch decks from companies including LinkedIn, Theranos, and WeWork, the combined persona achieved $85.7 \%$ accuracy in predicting startup outcomes, outperforming specialized and baseline configurations. The systems engineering rubric provided quantitative risk assessment while the venture capital knowledge stack enhanced market opportunity identification. Results demonstrate that LLMs can function as structured reasoning engines when constrained by domain-specific frameworks, offering transparent and auditable decision support for high-uncertainty investment environments.

title: Towards Energy-Efficient Collaborative Inference and Fine-tuning: Matching Model Compression and Offloading with Resource Availability_supp1-3689748.pdf
authors: Lianbo Ma
publish: N/A
abstract: We consider the collaborative inference acceleration task via cloud-edge-end collaboration, which involves a series of tightly coupled decision-making steps, including which DNN model to be selected, how much to compress model, how to partition model, and where to offload partitioned submodels. In practical deployments, these decisions jointly affect both fine-tuning and inference performance, and must jointly account for such aspects as the model being used, the computational resources and local datasets available at each device, as well as network latencies, which significantly increases the complexity of optimizing the problem. Yet, no existing studies focus on such joint optimization problem for these tightly coupled decisions. In this paper, we model this problem as a multi-dimensional optimization problem, jointly optimizing collaborative inference and fine-tuning by selecting the DNN model, compression level, partition strategy, and computational resource allocation, with the objective of minimizing the overall energy consumption of the learning-inference process, subject to accuracy and latency constraints. To this end, we propose an algorithmic framework called JQODI combining a time-energy tree diagram to represent the learning process, a dynamic programming solution strategy, and a data-driven theoretical approach to predict the expected total number of training epochs that meet the accuracy requirements. We prove that JQODI approximates the optimal solution with polynomial complexity. Numerical results demonstrate that JQODI surpasses state-of-the-art methods in both energy efficiency and latency.

title: LELN: A Large Language Model-Dynamically Enhanced Learning Network for Patient Similarity Calculation
authors: Zhichao Zhu, Bo Bai, Jianqiang Li, Han Wang, Rui Li, Lan Lan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The rapid expansion of Electronic Medical Record (EMR) data has advanced AI-driven patient similarity computation, a key technology for intelligent healthcare. However, the handling of heterogeneous EMR formats and the integration of domain knowledge constrain existing methods. While graph-based approaches show promise, they still struggle with these issues. To address this, we propose a Large Language Model-Dynamically Enhanced Learning Network (LELN), leveraging LLMs' commonsense knowledge and reasoning to dynamically structure EMR data and enhance medical knowledge integration. LELN in tegrates two LLM-basedmodules:DS-EE(DeepSeek-Event Extraction) extracts medical events to construct structured EMR event graphs, and DS-KB (DeepSeek-Knowledge Base) infers disease-relevant knowledge to augment feature representations. The model employs a dual-stage spatial-temporal feature aggregation strategy: a Graph Attention Network captures intra- and inter-event dependencies, followed by a Bidirectional Long-Short Term Memory (BiLSTM) with attention to model temporal disease progression. Additionally, a clinical prior-guided attention mechanism emphasizes discriminative diagnostic features, improving clinical relevance. Extensive experiments on heterogeneous datasets—a real-world Chinese dataset and public MIMIC-III—show LELN outperforms baselines, achieving F1 scores of 87.66% and 85.95%, demonstrating robustness and accuracy.

title: Device-Sharing-Based Bidirectional DC-DC Converter with Wide Voltage Regulation and Enhanced Fault Tolerance
authors: Yiliang Li, Yijie Wang, Yueshi Guan, Dianguo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: In response to the evolving requirements for wide voltage range and high reliability in electric vehicle (EV) high-voltage traction bus architectures, this paper proposes an isolated bidirectional DC-DC converter based on a devicere-use methodology. The topology can achieve wide voltage regulation under a low transformer turns ratio via a power-path reuse mechanism. Furthermore, by employing switching state reconfiguration, the converter maintains stable power output even under partial power device failures, significantly enhancing system fault tolerance. This paper provides an indepth analysis of the circuit characteristics of the converter under both steady-state and three typical fault conditions. To prevent performance degradation during faults, an optimized modulation scheme aimed at extending the soft-switching range and reducing the current stress. is proposed. Finally, an experimental prototype validates the theoretical analysis and demonstrates the superior performance of the converter under both wide‑gain range conditions and fault conditions.

title: Channel Estimation and 3D Near-Field Localization for Extremely Large Antenna Arrays
authors: Rafik Guellil, Karim Abed-Meraim, Adel Belouchrani, Nguyen Linh Trung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper proposes a semi-blind approach for near-field channel estimation and 3D source localization for Extremely Large Antenna Arrays. It considers Uniform Planar Arrays and Cross Arrays as particular cases. Unlike conventional Least-Squares estimators relying solely on predefined pilots, the proposed method exploits unknown data symbols within a subspace framework. Since it does not require any prior knowledge of the propagation model, our proposal achieves improved accuracy and robustness. To reduce computational complexity, a Divide-and-Conquer strategy is adopted, enabling fast and parallelizable subspace estimation. In a multipath single-user scenario with Inter-Symbol Interference, localization parameters (angles and range) are recovered from the first channel tap via a low-complexity least-squares fitting, assuming a line-of-sight path. The extension to Cross Arrays provides a simple specialization of the Uniform Plannar Array approach while drastically reducing the computational load. Simulation results confirm that the proposed method achieves accurate channel estimation and 3D localization with substantial complexity reduction as compared to the state of the art.

title: Learning Koopman Reduced-Order Model for Feedback Control in Dexterous Manipulation
authors: Mohamed Mohamedahmed, Muhammad Fuady Emzir, Agus Ismail Hasan, Ramy Rashad
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: We propose a data-driven feedback control method for dexterous manipulation that uses a Koopman-based reduced-order model (ROM) and a simple static state-feedback law in a lifted state space. From demonstrations, we learn Koopman reference dynamics, and from rollouts with control, we identify a controlled Koopman model using extended Dynamic Mode Decomposition with control (eDMDc); both are projected onto a common reduced subspace where the feedback gain is chosen to match the reduced closed-loop dynamics to the reduced reference. To ensure stability of the learned reference, we apply spectral clipping (SC) to enforce a desired discrete-time stability margin. We also derive a tracking-error bound that separates the effects of model mismatch, projection and invariance errors, and residual disturbances. The method is evaluated on tool-use and door-opening tasks in the MuJoCo Adroit hand environment, using third-order polynomial observables to study success rate, RMS tracking error, final distance to the goal, and controllable modes as functions of the reduced order, with and without SC, and achieves high success with microsecond-level online computation time.

title: A Highly Compact 530-610 GHz Bandpass Filter Using Patch Resonators to Develop SSB Spectrometer
authors: Berhanu T Bulcha, Diego Tononi, Thomas R. Stevenson, Edward J. Wollack
publish: IEEE Journal of Microwaves
abstract: In double sideband (DSB) receiver systems, both the desired signals and unwanted image frequencies are downconverted, necessitating effective filtering to enhance system selectivity and sensitivity. This manuscript presents a novel submillimeter-wave bandpass filter developed to suppress the unwanted image frequencies in a spectrometer system that is optimized to detect key atmospheric constituents in the passband, including water vapor (H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">16</sup>O – 559.9 GHz), deuterium oxide (HDO – 539.0 GHz), and oxygen (O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> – 570.1 GHz). The filter design employs compact triangular microstrip patch resonators that achieve sharp roll-off characteristics and wide stopband performance, making it ideal for image rejection applications across submillimeter-wave and THz bands. The filter is fabricated on a 15 μm quartz substrate with 0.5 μm gold metallization, providing a 3 dB bandwidth of approximately 80 GHz with well-defined rejection bands below 530 GHz and above 610 GHz. Signal coupling is accomplished through microstrip waveguide feed probes for input and output. The design methodology offers significant flexibility, allowing straightforward scaling and adjustment of center frequency and bandwidth for implementations ranging from 1 GHz to 2 THz. The versatility of the filter architecture presented has utility in radio astronomy, defense systems, and high-frequency communication network applications.

title: A Fully Integrated Dual-Phase Switched-Capacitor Rectifier for Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting With High Power Extraction Enhancement
authors: Honglei Xu, Yuzhou Mao, Quan Sun, Yanzhao Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This brief presents a fully integrated dual-phase switched-capacitor (DPSC) rectifier for piezoelectric energy harvesting (PEH). An on-chip reconfigurable switched-capacitor (SC) converter is implemented to eliminate the need for external inductors and capacitors, significantly reducing the overall system volume. The efficiency is enhanced by reusing the inherent capacitance (Cₚ) of the transducer as a flying capacitor of the SC converter through a dual-phase charge transfer technique. Furthermore, an all-digital flipping control scheme is employed to minimize system power consumption. The proposed circuit was fabricated in a 0.18μm BCD process. The measurement results show that a 76% flipping efficiency and a 6.7× energy extraction enhancement are achieved.

title: Keeping Battery Electric Buses Moving Without Delays: Rolling Horizon Charging Scheduling for High Frequency Public Transport System
authors: Ankit Kumar, Vijay Mohan Shrimal, Amardeep Singh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Large-scale electrification of urban bus fleets imposes strict operational constraints arising from limited charging infrastructure, fixed grid capacity, and tight timetables on high-frequency routes. This paper proposes a hybrid rollinghorizon charging scheduling framework that integrates dayahead optimisation, real-time receding-horizon control, prioritybased heuristics, and grid-aware power management to ensure service reliability under uncertainty. The collaborative charging scheme has been developed using real-world data from Chandigarh's Transport Undertaking (CTU), which has basic timetable information. It is determined that the energy usage of BEBs (Battery Electric Buses) varies from 0.73-1.17 kWh/km depending on the operating conditions. The approach outlined produced a high rate of adherence to the scheduled dispatch of BEBs with no change in route schedule, headway frequency or depot infrastructure and achieved a reduction in depot peak demand and increase in charger utilisation when compared to using static and heuristic charging optimisation schedules. It came to light that the viable operation of large-scale BEBs deployment should not be constrained by the availability of infrastructure, but also determined by the intelligence with which operational charging scheduling decisions are made, making operational scheduling the critical driver for urban public transit electrification.

title: Early Burnout Prediction Using Temporal Language Drift in Chat Conversations
authors: Shashank Sekhar Pradhan, Atharv Mathur, Shristi Sinha, G. Gulothungan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Employee burnout is an increasing occupational health problem, impacting an estimated 77% of employees worldwide. Current detection methods are primarily dependent upon static sentiment classification or conducting periodic surveys; however, these methods do not account for the gradual degradation of language that occurs prior to clinical burnout. This work presents a framework for detecting temporal language drift through five complementary components: document-level contextual sentiment scoring using RoBERTa, z-score normalization of message length to capture behavioral engagement, a weighted burnout index derived from empirically determined weights (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$w s=0.7$</tex>, we <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.3$</tex>), anomaly detection using Isolation Forest on drift trajectories, and supervised classification using Gradient Boosting. The proposed approach was evaluated on the Dreaddit dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(n=500)$</tex>, using an 80/20 train-test split with 5-fold cross-validation. The model achieved an overall accuracy of 89.33%, precision of 88.12%, recall of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 8. 1 2 {\%}}, \text{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 8. 1 2 {\%}}$</tex>, and an AUC-ROC value of 0.9555. Sensitivity analysis of weight configuration validated proper weighting in the calculation. In addition, significant improvement compared to three baseline metrics was demonstrated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{p}<0.01)$</tex>. This is the first framework developed to make the use of temporal language drift a formalized indicator of workplace burnout for real-time monitoring in organisations.

title: Smart Detection of Defects in Railway Tracks Using Ultrasonic Sensor
authors: Atul Roshan Naik, Vankadara Sahasra, Lalith Karthikeyan M P, Mohammed Numan, Tarun Kanaka Manjunath, Parth Sarathi Panigrahy, Swarna M Patra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Trains are an indispensable medium of transportation for people and goods, and their effective maintenance is therefore very important. One of the main reasons for train accidents is track faults or hidden cracks, which usually lead to derailments. Detection of such faults at an early stage can greatly mitigate the chances of accidents. Traditional manual techniques of inspection are time-consuming, manpower intensive, and have limited coverage, and therefore automated methods are imperative. This paper describes a lightweight, rail-guided testbed with ultrasonic sensors and motors for self-locomotion. The ultrasonic sensor measures the variations in distance between sensor and rail surface. Surface level anomalies such as dents, gaps, and visible cracks produce abrupt changes in the measured distance, that can be visualized as spikes in the real-time distancetime plots generated using Python. The prototype provides an efficient, cost-effective, and reliable means of crack detection, thus facilitating smarter rail maintenance and enhanced safety. Experimental evidence supports its ability to detect simulated cracks with accuracy. The proposed prototype is only designed to detect surface-level anomalies and discontinuities rather than subsurface fatigue cracks within the rail material.

title: Automated Iterative Analytical Modeling of Transient Electromagnetic Disturbance Coupling to Multiconductor Transmission Lines_supp1-3686048.pdf
authors: Yanzhao Xie
publish: N/A
abstract: Building upon the distributed analytical representation and iterative technique (DARIT-field) derived from frequency-domain waveform relaxation, this article presents DARIT-auto: an efficient automated iterative technique for transient electromagnetic disturbance (TED) radiated field coupling to multiconductor transmission lines (MTLs). While the original DARIT-field method yields analytical solutions through a four-step iteration and demonstrates superior efficiency compared to the conventional chain parameter matrix method for a large number of conductors, strongly coupled scenarios may require more iteration steps to meet prescribed accuracy requirements. Furthermore, although the DARIT-field framework theoretically allows for additional iterations to enhance accuracy, manual derivation of voltage and current expressions along the lines beyond four iterations becomes impractical due to the rapid escalation of algebraic complexity. To address these limitations, the DARIT-auto method automatically derives analytical equations for arbitrary iteration steps without the need for manual derivation. This work implements a unified treatment using Taylor expansion to resolve propagation constant challenges, guaranteeing both analytical consistency and numerical stability regardless of the line configuration. A key contribution of this work is the development of an automated framework that systematically generates analytical expressions for MTLs voltages and currents across arbitrary iteration steps. Extensive validation demonstrates that the DARIT-auto method could efficiently handle TED coupling to MTLs with large conductor counts and provides the flexibility to balance computational overhead and accuracy by adjusting the number of iterations.

title: Online Lifelong Optimal Adaptive Control of Partially Uncertain Strict Feedback Discrete-Time Systems With Application to Quadrotor UAVs
authors: Maxwell Geiger, Sarangapani Jagannathan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This article considers the infinite time horizon optimal adaptive tracking control of partially uncertain strict feedback discrete-time (DT) systems with application to quadrotor uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs). First, the strict feedback DT system is transformed into an equivalent affine nonlinear DT system in terms of the tracking error dynamics. The optimal adaptive tracking control problem is solved using an augmented system approach, where a horizon of future bounded reference trajectory points is used in the augmented state, when compared to using a single point. It is assumed that the internal dynamics of the strict feedback system are unknown, but the control coefficient matrix is known. By applying approximate dynamic programming (ADP) using exclusively multilayer neural networks (MNNs), the optimal control policy is obtained without any other stabilizing controller. Lastly, regularization penalties are introduced for both the actor and critic NNs to support multitask learning. Upper bounds for the multitask learning hyperparameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lambda $</tex-math> </inline-formula> are proved for the first time in the online learning regime. Under our proposed MNN weight update laws, the tracking and weight estimation errors are proved to be uniformly ultimately bounded (UUB) using Lyapunov analysis. In particular, we consider the case of a quadrotor UAV where the system drift dynamics are unknown, but the control input matrix is known, corresponding to knowledge of the mass and principal moments of inertia of the UAV. The efficacy and reliability of the method are demonstrated through computer simulations and hardware implementation on the Quanser QDrone2 UAV, where the cost is reduced as much as 68% without the regularization penalty when compared to the initial control policy. With the regularization penalty, the cost is reduced by 2% compared to without using the penalty.

title: Acute Effects of Exergaming Session and Play Mode on Physiological and Psychosocial Responses in Sedentary Young Adults: A Quasi-Experimental Study
authors: Yunlong Xu, Runting Zhong, Xinhui Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Games
abstract: Purpose: This study investigated the acute effects of Nintendo ARMS™ exergaming on physiological and psychosocial responses in sedentary young adults. Methods: Fifty-two male college students (20.14 ± 1.34 years) participated in a 2 (exergaming session: pretest vs. posttest) × 2 (play mode: single player vs. multi-player mode) experiment. Participants were assigned to single-player or multi-player modes based on their registration preference, with multi-player participants playing in pairs. All participants completed a 30-minute exergaming session. Energy expenditure and mean heart rate were recorded during gameplay, while reaction time, positive affect, and sociability were assessed before and after the session. Semi-structured interviews were conducted to collect participants' feedback. Results: Nintendo ARMS™ exergaming achieved moderate-intensity physical activity. Reaction time and positive affect improved significantly from pretest to posttest. No significant differences between play modes were observed for energy expenditure, heart rate, reaction time, and positive affect. Sociability increased significantly only in multi-player mode, whereas no significant pre-post change was observed in single-player mode. Participants generally reported positive attitudes toward exergaming and considered it an enjoyable and convenient form of home-based exercise. Conclusion: Nintendo ARMS™ can provide moderate physical activity, and short-term physiological and psychosocial benefits, with multi-player sessions showing particular potential for enhancing sociability.

title: IEEE Journal of Microwaves Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Journal of Microwaves

title: Retraction Notice: Image-Based Time Series Forecasting: A Deep Convolutional Neural Network Approach
authors: Jnaneshwar Pai Maroor, Dillip Narayan Sahu, Ginni Nijhawan, A Karthik, A. K. Shrivastav, M. Kalyan Chakravarthi
publish: 2024 4th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: In this study, we examine the relatively new subject of image-based time series forecasting by using deep convolutional neural networks (also known as CNNs). The use of statistical methods and accurate numerical data have always been prerequisites for accurate time series forecasting. However, as a result of the accomplishments of CNNs in computer vision and the development of deep learning, a fresh approach has emerged. This work outlines a method for transforming time series data into structures that are analogous to pictures in order to facilitate the use of CNNs for the modelling of temporal patterns and the development of more accurate forecasting models. The research contributes to a greater understanding of the usefulness of deep learning in time series forecasting by providing insights into the process of picking models based on criteria such as accuracy, processing capability, and the specific dataset requirements. Keywords— Time Series Forecasting, Deep Convolutional Neural Networks, Image-based Forecasting, Deep Learning, Neural Networks, Predictive Modeling, Temporal Patterns, Model Comparison.

title: Combined Masking Strategies for the Identification of Black Plastic Samples Through Mid-Wavelength Infrared Hyperspectral Imaging
authors: Marco Papetti, Marco Diani, Marcello Colledani
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Black plastic materials pose a significant challenge in industrial sorting and recycling due to their invisibility to conventional Near-Infrared (NIR) detection systems. Recently, Mid-Wavelength Infrared Hyperspectral Imaging (MWIR HSI) has emerged as a promising technology to overcome this limitation by enabling spectral discrimination of black polymers. However, the low emission intensity in the MWIR range and the presence of surface reflections and complex geometries hinder the effective spatial identification of such materials. This study proposes a dual masking strategy that relies solely on MWIR hyperspectral data, without support from external sensors, to achieve accurate spatial localization and classification of black plastic samples. The first mask employs a strict threshold-based selection on specific spectral bands to retain only reliable pixels for classification. The second mask applies K-means clustering to Lab* and RGB reconstructions of the hyperspectral image to recover spatial information and define object boundaries. The combination of these two masks enables robust, pixel-wise classification while preserving spatial completeness, making the approach suitable for in-line applications. Results demonstrate that the method effectively identifies black plastic samples down to sizes of 1-2 cm, with inter-sample distances above 0.5 cm. Below these thresholds, higher-resolution imaging or pre-trained models may be required.

title: Explainable XGBoost Model for Reliable Tumor Classification in Extraskeletal Osteosarcoma
authors: G S Pradeep Ghantasala, Rajesh Sharma R, Akey Sungheetha, Vamsi Priya K, Thrilok Kolla, U Ananthanagu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Because of its unusual presence outside of the skeletal system, extraskeletal osteosarcoma (ESOS) is an uncommon and aggressive malignant tumor that reasons diagnostic problems. In this work, we endorse an explainable XGBoost-based model that makes use of imaging and medical data to reliably and correctly classify ESOS. A carefully selected dataset is used to train the model, that's then tuned for sensitivity, specificity, and standard classification accuracy. In order to enhance openness and confidence in the decision-making procedure, the SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) used to interpret every feature contribution to tumor classification. Medical experts can higher recognize crucial indicators related to ESOS with the usage of the explainability framework, which gives insightful data approximately the model's predictions. The recommended technique performs higher and is less complicated to recognize while as compared to standard machine learning classifiers. Through obvious and data-driven decision help systems, this paradigm opens the door for AI- assisted diagnostics in uncommon tumors, facilitating early prognosis and higher patient outcomes. First and foremost, this contribution entails creating an interpretable XGBoost-based classification framework that focuses on ESOS diagnosis utilizing integrated radiological, histological, and clinical data, with an added focus on explainability through SHAP.

title: Integrating Machine Learning into Cyber Defense: Detection and Mitigation Approaches
authors: Goutham Sunkara, Rakesh Keshava, Sridhar Krishna Korimilli, Kamal Mohammed Najeeb Shaik, R.Maruthi R. Maruthi, Bharath Balaraman
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The frequency and complexity of cyber threats is increasing, and as a result, we now require more intelligent, adaptive defenses in systems that are more advanced than their existing rule-based counterparts. This study suggests a novel Hierarchical Ensemble-Driven Resilient Architecture (HEDRA-ML) that is driven by multiple data modalities, adversarial training, and reinforcement learning for real-time detection and mitigation of cyber threats. We showcase an implementation of HEDRA-ML that is evaluated on the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset across many modern attack scenarios and we perform quantitative comparisons with existing approaches including Random Forest, DeepLog, CNN-LSTM, and Isolation Forest + SVM. The results showed that HEDRA-ML performed best with a 98.7% detection rate, 1.3% false positive rate, and high levels of adversarial robustness (90.4%) out of the existing approaches. Overall, the results conveyed that by proposing a resilient model that offered significant increases in detection accuracy, time to respond, and adaptability toward new threats, HEDRA-ML opens a promising direction for intelligent, real-time, resilient cyber solutions in today's evolving digital space.

title: From Automation to Human-Centric Manufacturing: A Systematic Literature Review on the Transition from Industry 4.0 to Industry 5.0
authors: Tahera Kalsoom, Ijeoma Onwumere, Masood Ur Rehman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The transition from I4.0 to I5.0 shifts manufacturing from automation-driven efficiency to a human-centric, sustainable, and ethical framework. While I4.0 emphasized cyber-physical systems (CPS), AI, and IoT, I5.0 integrates cognitive AI, collaborative robotics (cobots), and sustainability-driven innovations. This study conducts a systematic literature review to explore the key enablers, challenges, and implications of this transition. Findings highlight cognitive AI, human-machine collaboration, and digital twins as critical enablers, with sustainability and ethical AI governance shaping future industrial systems. However, challenges such as regulatory gaps, workforce reskilling, and SME adoption barriers must be addressed. A multidisciplinary approach integrating technology, policy, and strategic workforce development is essential. This study provides a roadmap for industries and policymakers, emphasizing the need for empirical research and policy-driven solutions to ensure an equitable transition to I5.0.

title: Cost effective MFL NDT with Hall sensors for small ferromagnetic samples: penetrating defects vs thickness detection
authors: Vlad Marsic, Petar Igic, Iain Thayne, Soroush Faramehr
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Magnetic scanning non-destructive testing (NDT) methods are a real interest for many research and industrial areas. However, the already well-established popularized instruments and techniques for this area require complex, heavy and expensive equipment supported by specialist software and expertise. Consequently, building a system to test a hypothesis or enhance a current toolset may be discouraging for any adjacent research and industry areas with interest in magnetic NDT. This work demonstrates that a cost effective NDT reduced size magnetic scanner is possible to be created and exploited with a basic knowledge of electronics, programming and signal processing. The system is using the magnetic flux leakage (MFL) technique by means of Hall effect sensors and permanent magnets, at room temperature. The approx. 50 × 50 mm steel sheet scanning exemplifies two usage cases for the magnetic system: determining the sheet’s thickness variation from 0.7 mm to 3.5 mm and, the possibility of detecting holes as defects on a separate 3 mm steel sheet (i.e., from 2 mm to 5 mm radius). The system is non-motorized for simplicity and it is interfaced with MATLAB to illustrate data acquisition, visualization and analysis, all on the same software platform. Since the magnetic field analysis methodology used for both show-cases differs significantly, the parallel for the two investigations reveals the common and unique points of each. This approach is set to unfold the work set for a straightforward magnetic field investigation and set the expectations on the right path when using off-the-shelf inexpensive components.

title: An Improved Seagull Optimization Algorithm with Opposition-Based Learning, Centroid Guidance, and Lévy Flights
authors: Padarbinda Samal
