publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents an Improved Seagull Optimization Algorithm (I-SOA) aimed at overcoming the premature convergence and stagnation issues of the standard Seagull Optimization Algorithm (SOA). The proposed I-SOA incorporates three key mechanisms: opposition-based learning to enhance population diversity, centroid guidance to accelerate convergence, and Lévy flights for adaptive exploration. The performance of I-SOA is assessed on a suite of ten benchmark test functions, covering both unimodal and multimodal cases. Experimental results, summarized in terms of mean and standard deviation, indicate that I-SOA consistently outperforms SOA, achieving lower mean fitness values and reduced variability across all functions. These enhancements allow I-SOA to maintain a well-balanced trade-off between exploration and exploitation, demonstrating superior convergence stability and overall optimization performance compared to the standard SOA.

title: Information-Preserving Framework for Gas Leak Detection and Quantification With Uncooled Snapshot Infrared Spectral Imaging
authors: Tianxiang Xiong, Pengyu Wang, Tongtong Li, Yijie Wang, Hao Yan, Shijie Liu, Liyin Yuan, Chunlai Li, Jianyu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Recent advances in infrared gas leak detection have substantially improved learning-based detection and quantification across different imaging instruments. However, achieving gas detection, classification, and quantitative analysis within a unified system remains challenging because of limited real-time capability, signal instability, and information loss caused by inter-channel inconsistency during normalization. To address these issues, this paper presents a framework with information-preserving preprocessing (IPP) in our uncooled snapshot multi-spectral infrared imaging system. A radiometric signal model is established to characterize information degradation in the raw-to-digital conversion process, and the Relative Background Radiance Contrast (RBRC) metric is introduced to preserve absorption-related spectral variations across channels after normalization. Methane and ammonia are used as representative gases to validate the quantitative performance in controlled gas cell experiments under different background temperatures. With ΔT ranging from 10 to 25 K, the proposed method achieves average relative errors of 11% for methane and 7.8% for ammonia at 3000 ppm·m, while the error increases under low-concentration conditions with weak absorption. Outdoor leakage experiments further show that the proposed preprocessing improves inter-channel consistency and enhances gas detection performance across multiple mainstream object detectors. In addition, the detector-guided post-processing enables quantitative plume segmentation and contrast-enhanced visualization for gas analysis.

title: A Novel Consequent-Pole Bearingless Permanent Magnet Motor with Active Axial Suspension
authors: Xiaowei Quan, Zhiquan Deng, Chencheng Wu, Lei Mei, Zeyuan Liu, Xin Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: This article proposes a novel consequent-pole bearingless permanent magnet (PM) motor (CP-BPMM) with reduced PM usage, featuring additional air gaps, large axial force generation, and natural decoupling among torque, radial force, and axial force. Through the regulation of the current in axial force coil, the axial air-gap flux is adjusted to produce active axial force. With this design, the proposed CP-BPMM reduces bias PM volume while boosting active axial force and torque. The topology and operation principle of CP-BPMM are introduced first, and the radial and axial force equations are deduced. Based on the 3D finite element method, the decoupled characteristics are verified, and the influence of additional air gaps and bias PM dimensions on axial force is analyzed. To demonstrate the merits of the proposed CP-BPMM, comparative performance analyses of the electromagnetic and suspension performance are performed between two CP-BPMM designs, validating the superiority of the proposed motor.

title: EV-Rec-R1: A Framework for Electric Vehicle Charging Station Recommendation with Large Language Models and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Junkang Feng, Chuanlin Zhang, Chenggang Cui, Pei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: With the intensification of climate change and the accelerating shift toward transportation electrification, electric vehicles (EVs) play a critical role in enabling transportation decarbonization and sustainable mobility. As the EV population continues to grow, the management of user charging behavior has become increasingly important. In this context, high-quality charging station recommendations not only improve users’ charging experience but also help reduce unnecessary energy consumption, thereby supporting low-carbon mobility goals. However, traditional recommendation approaches, typically based on static optimization or conventional machine learning, struggle to capture the heterogeneity of user attributes and psychological preferences, limiting the effectiveness of personalized charging recommendations. To address this challenge, this study proposes EV-Rec-R1, a framework that integrates large language models (LLMs) with reinforcement learning (RL) through group relative policy optimization (GRPO). By incorporating user characteristics, psychological preferences, and charging station information, the framework constructs a behavior-aware charging station recommendation model. A composite reward mechanism guides the model toward generating more accurate and interpretable recommendations. Experiments conducted using real-world survey data and charging behavior records demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional machine learning models and unoptimized language models. The findings provide methodological insights into EV charging behavior, improve personalized charging services, and contribute to the development of intelligent and electrified transportation systems.

title:
Miniaturized PtSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -based piezoresistive pressure sensors with enhanced sensitivity
authors: Ragini Gupta, Sebastian Lukas, Nico Rademacher, Michael Gross, Nikolas Dominik, Georg S. Duesberg, Max C. Lemme
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Device miniaturization has emerged as a key driver in the advancement of micro- and nano-electromechanical systems (MEMS/NEMS), enabling enhanced performance, reduced power consumption, and the integration of multiple functionalities into compact platforms. Platinum diselenide (PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>), a noble metal dichalcogenide (NMDC), has unique characteristics that are particularly advantageous for piezoresistive sensing. Membrane-based PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> piezoresistive pressure sensors have demonstrated excellent sensitivity in previous studies, although typically using large arrays of membranes designed to counteract low device yield. In this work, we push the boundaries of device miniaturization and demonstrate PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> pressure sensors with single cavities and circular channels. Our sensors exhibit sensitivities of up to 1 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> for a 42 μm cavity device and achieve high area-normalized sensitivity values of more than 2 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> for 20 μm cavity devices without compromising sensor performance. These results highlight the potential of PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> for highly sensitive, miniaturized pressure sensing applications.

title: A Novel Photonic Crystal Fiber Biosensor for Organic Acid Detection in the THz Regime
authors: Mohammad Abrar Nasif, Md. Jahedul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: In this work, we present a novel photonic crystal fiber (PCF) specifically developed for the detection of organic acids in the terahertz (THz) frequency range. The organic acids we are detecting: Hexanoic Acid, Octanoic Acid, Oleic Acid and Valeric Acid. The proposed study considered three configurations, featuring cores with three, five, and seven rectangular slots. Several key parameters of the proposed PCF were analyzed over a broad terahertz (THz) frequency range, from 0.4 to 3.0 THz. The PCF model was optimized by varying the core diameter, the pitch and diameter of the cladding air holes, the porosity, and the choice of background materials. At the operating frequency 1.2 THz, shows an overall best result for Hexanoic Acid at 86% porosity. Achieving a relative sensitivity of 94.365%, material losses as low as 0.029532 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> were observed, and a core power fraction of 83.485%. The mode’s effective area was calculated as 1.9173×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-07</sup> μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, with a confinement loss of 2.078×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-19</sup> dB/cm. These excellent characteristics suggest that the proposed model could serve a key role in applications such as biomedical Imaging, supercontinuum generation, and sensing.

title: Colonoscopy Classification using PVT-Xception ensemble Framework with Explainable AI
authors: Nafis Fahim, Sadman Samir Rafith, Md. Fahim Kabir Chowdhury, Saif Mostasir Rohan, Avijeet Dey Sarker, Md. Sayem Kabir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Colorectal and gastrointestinal disorders such as polyps, ulcerative colitis, and esophagitis require timely and accurate detection for effective treatment. Conventional diagnostic methods, while effective, are limited by subjectivity and dependence on expert interpretation. Deep learning has emerged as a promising solution, yet many models struggle with generalization and interpretability. This study presents a hybrid framework that integrates Pyramid Vision Transformer v2 (PVT v2-B5) and Xception backbones for complementary feature extraction, combined with a custom fully connected classifier head. Using a balanced dataset of 6,000 colonoscopy and endoscopy images across four classes, the model achieved 96.89% validation accuracy, with Cohen’s Kappa and MCC of 0.960 and a macro F1-score of 0.97, outperforming state-of-the-art CNNs, Transformers, and traditional classifiers. Ablation studies confirmed the complementary strength of both backbones, while Grad-CAM, Grad-CAM++, and LIME visualizations highlighted clinically relevant regions, reinforcing interpretability. These results position the proposed PVT–Xception framework as a reliable AI-assisted tool for gastrointestinal disease classification.

title: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) for UAV - Assisted RAN Coverage in Emergency Scenarios
authors: B. Nazeer Hussain, K. Gopi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In scenarios where conventional means of communication are unavailable, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) can be used to quickly establish Radio Access Network (RAN) coverage. This paper presents a new designed framework that employs Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) technology for user detection and service delivery in UAV-assisted RAN systems. The UAV functions as a dual-purpose node by utilizing RF sensing and communication resource allocation, thus reducing system overhead and deployment latency. A simulated environment was built over a disaster-affected region using synthetic user distribution together with a real drone-captured aerial image for system behaviour validation. The analysis focused on the UAV’s sensing accuracy, communication coverage, SNR, and overall user throughput along a circular flight path. Findings indicate that the ISAC-enabled UAV has achieved greater than 74% sensing detection and greater than 90% communication coverage with average user throughput of more than 25 Mbps. Graphical evaluations such as SNR distribution alongside other proposed metrics show the practicality of this work. It is demonstrated how ISAC enabled platforms can be rapidly deployed in intelligent, spectrum-efficient manners for emergency network needs in 6G or beyond.

title: Exploring Facial Feature Extraction and Temporal Dynamics for Deepfake Video Detection: A CNN-RNN Study on Faceforensics++
authors: Umang Jeswani, Priyadharshini S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The rise of generative artificial intelligence has led to the creation of deepfake videos-synthetically altered content where a person's appearance or voice is manipulated using deep learning. While these technologies have creative potential, their misuse poses serious risks including mis-information, identity theft, and privacy violations. Increasing access to deepfake tools makes it harder to distinguish real from manipulated content, undermining digital trust. This paper explores hybrid deep learning models that integrate spatial and temporal features for deepfake detection. CNNs are used to extract frame-wise facial features, while RNNs such as GRUs and LSTMs model temporal dynamics. Three architectures are implemented and compared: CNN + GRU, ResNeXt50 + LSTM, and XceptionNet + GRU. The models are trained on the FaceForensics++ dataset and tested on a curated set of ten videos. The ResNeXt50 + LSTM model achieved the best results, with 80 % accuracy, 0.75 F1-score, and 0.80 ROC-AUC, demonstrating its ability to capture both appearance based and sequential patterns. This work highlights the importance of temporal modeling in deepfake detection and contributes toward building scalable, robust solutions for applications in multimedia forensics and digital authentication.

title: Knowledge-Centric Deep Learning Frameworks for System-Level Intelligence in 6G Wireless Networks
authors: R. Ravi, A. Priya, Reshma P, D. Abinaya, S. Hariprabha, Kannan R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The development of the sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks requires smart system-level decision-making models that can accommodate the ultra-low latency, enormous connectivity, combined sensing and communication, and ultra-reliable network models. Whereas there is high ability in modeling data-driven deep learning methodologies, they are usually characterized by high sample complexity, low interpretability, and poor generalization in non-stationary and highly dynamic wireless settings. In this paper, a new knowledge-based deep learning (KCDL) framework is presented that incorporates domain knowledge, expert-developed policies, physically-based model, and network semantics within the deep learning model architecture, training, and inference phase of the network to support statistically robust system-level intelligence in 6G networks. The most important feature of the presented framework is the hybrid modeling method that is unified and makes prior knowledge a structural constraint and semantic guidance in learning modules, which makes undesired exploration less essential and decision stability better. Thorough statistical analysis of the proposed KCDL model using extensive simulation of representative 6G scenarios, such as intelligent radio resource management, network slicing, edge intelligence, and integrated sensing and communication, has shown that convergence in the proposed KCDL framework is 40 - 45 times faster, end-to-end latency is reduced by 20-25 percent, spectrum utilization efficiency is increased by 15 - 20 percent, and performance deterioration under unseen network conditions is less than 8 percent relative to over 25 percent with conventional data-driven baselines. These statistically significant advantages affirm the fact that knowledge-based deep learning offers scalable, power efficiency, interpretability, and reliability of the autonomous, resilient, and context-aware 6G network architectures.

title: A Multi-Disease Detection Framework Using Smartwatches Based on a Guided Quantum Generative Adversarial Network with Prism Refraction Search
authors: Jenifer S, K. Alice
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Multi-disease conditions significantly affect human health by simultaneously impacting multiple physiological systems, increasing the risk of severe complications and mortality. Early and continuous detection of multiple diseases is crucial for timely medical intervention and effective healthcare management. However, existing detection approaches are often limited by noise-prone sensor data, single-disease focus, and suboptimal feature learning, which reduce diagnostic accuracy and reliability. To address these limitations, a Multi-Disease Detection framework using Smartwatches based on a Guided Quantum Generative Adversarial Network with Prism Refraction Search (SGQGAN-PrRS) is presented. Initially, real-time physiological data are continuously collected from patients through smartwatch sensors. The raw data are fed into an Adaptive Self-Guided Loop Filter (AS-GLF)to remove noise and enhance signal quality. The refined signals are then processed by a Dual Selective Fusion Transformer Network (DSFTrN), which extracts robust multi-scale temporal and contextual features. These features are further analyzed using a Self-Guided Quantum Generative Adversarial Network (SGQGAN) to accurately learn disease-specific representations. Finally, the Prism Refraction Search (PrRS) algorithm optimizes network weight parameters to improve convergence and predictive performance. The suggested model has been proven to be effective, with the results of the experiment showing the following accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and specificity 99.85 %, 99.69 %, 99.58 %, 99.76 %, and 99.70 %, respectively, and the AUC is 0.993. These findings validate the power and appropriateness of the suggested framework in real time and non-invasive detection of multiple diseases through smartwatches.

title: Federated Learning for Privacy Preservation of IoT Device
authors: Akashdeep Dash, Sai Suraj Voleti, Adithya P, Jasmine S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Large-scale data collection for intelligent applications has been made possible by the Internet of Things (IoT) explosive growth, but centralized machine learning poses serious privacy and security issues. This problem is solved by Federated Learning (FL), which permits decentralized model training without the need to share raw data. However, device heterogeneity and straggler effects cause performance degradation in conventional synchronous FL. In order to recognize human activity using IoT sensor data, this paper suggests an asynchronous, privacy-preserving federated learning framework. The Human Activity Recognition dataset is used to train a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) model, where each client is a non-identically distributed Internet of Things device. The framework includes staleness-aware aggregation, adaptive client selection, differential privacy, energy-aware training, and model compression. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves accuracy comparable to centralized learning while outperforming synchronous FL in heterogeneous IoT environments.

title: Spectral Subset Hierarchical Bayesian Optimization for Hyperspectral Band Selection
authors: Elaveni Palanivel, Vrushali Kamalakar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Band Selection of Hyperspectral Images is a significant precursor that aids in reducing the computational complexity of any further utilization. Choosing a spectrally significant and spatially rich band helps augment stability and lends structure to further applications. Majority of the existing frameworks in this category define fixed selection rules to choose optimum bands. Some literature exists that models band selection as an optimization problem. However, this paper proposes a novel band selection routine that explicitly treats the hyperspectral band selection complexity as a budget constrained Bayesian optimization problem. The proposed framework introduces probabilistic surrogate modelling, uncertainty aware decision making and Hierarchical coarse to fine optimization for hyperspectral band selection. The proposed Spectral Subset Hierarchical Bayesian Optimization (SSH-BO) algorithm introduces 2 levels of Bayesian optimization to achieve benchmark hyperspectral band selection. In the first level (Level 1 - SSH - BO), the spectral subsets that models maximum information are identified. In the next level (Level 2-SSH-BO) the ideal bands from each of the selected SS are extracted. This explicit stratified optimization coupled with hierarchical search ensures that the proposed algorithm achieves competitive performance with significantly fewer bands. A mean, precision, recall and F1-score of 94.3%, 94.8% and 94.5% respectively is achieved. Thus, the proposed band selection framework uses only 10.8% of the huge spectral bandwidth while achieving the same overall accuracy.

title: Modeling and Feasibility Evaluation of a 5G mmWave Wake-Up Receiver for Battery-Less IoT Applications
authors: Ahmed Musfi Alasal
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in smart cities poses a critical challenge in terms of longterm power sustainability, particularly in harsh environments where battery replacement is logistically impractical [17], [11], [12]. This paper presents a system-level feasibility assessment of a zero-power wake-up receiver (WuRx) operating on 5G millimeter-wave (mmWave) signals at 28 GHz [1]. A rectenna-based receiver architecture is considered, comprising a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> microstrip patch antenna array and a multi-stage rectifier modeled with non-linear power conversion efficiency characteristics [2], [3]. Rather than relying on idealized assumptions, the study employs a realistic, simulation-driven framework incorporating practical gNodeB EIRP constraints (53 dBm) and the Close-In (CI) path loss model to capture key urban propagation effects, including blockage, shadowing, and beam alignment sensitivity [6], [14]. The results indicate that while continuous energy harvesting at mmWave frequencies is not viable over extended ranges, the proposed architecture can generate a wake-up trigger voltage exceeding 50 mV at distances of up to approximately 71 meters under favorable Line-of-Sight (LOS) conditions [4]. These findings suggest that 5G mmWave-based rectennas can support on-demand wake-up triggering for ultra-low-power IoT nodes within specific deployment regimes, providing a low-maintenance and sustainable solution for selected smart city applications such as smart metering, structural health monitoring, and environmental sensing [15].

title: Target Recommendation for Home AI Device Control Commands
authors: S D Madhumitha, S A Amutha Jeevakumari, Amit Kumar Mandal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Smart home ecosystems are expanding rapidly, yet most control interfaces struggle with natural, ambiguous, and evolving user commands. This paper presents a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) based smart home command system that grounds Large Language Model (LLM) outputs in a dynamic structured JSON knowledge base representing device locations, capabilities, parameter ranges, and live states. To improve reliability and reduce hallucinations, the architecture integrates hierarchical intent classification, uncertainty aware confidence gating with fallback activation, embedding based semantic caching, multi hop decomposition for compound commands, context aware device prioritization using location and time, and graceful degradation through fuzzy matching and clarification prompts. The system closes the loop by applying validated state updates and continuously re indexing the vector store. Evaluated on a 305 query test suite spanning six intent families, the system achieves 85.57% overall success. The full system outperforms Pure LLM, LLM with tool calling, and retrieval only baselines. A proof of concept integration with the Home Assistant platform through REST and MQTT achieves 100% dispatch success across 25 live commands. A dedicated adversarial battery across 13 attack sub types shows 93.3% graceful handling. Implemented using LangChain with FAISS based retrieval, the approach enables safer and more responsive smart home control than static rule based or ungrounded LLM systems.

title: Multi-Objective Coordination of Electric Vehicle Charging Prices and Mobile Energy Storage
authors: K. Deepak, G. Pandu Ranga Reddy, Y. Hazarathaiah, H. Md. Rizwan, Shaik Moinuddin, S. Abdul Mohimin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Electric-vehicle charging is now occurring at a scale where treating prices, storage operation, and feeder limits as separate layers begins to break down. In practice, these elements interact continuously, and isolating one from the others tends to hide important system effects. In this study, pricing signals, EV charging response, and mobile energy storage actions are handled together, with feeder conditions assessed directly through a backward-forward sweep load-flow calculation embedded within the scheduling process. A 69-bus radial feeder is used as the test system, not as a benchmark convenience, but because its downstream voltage behavior makes operational stress easier to observe. The scheduling task is approached as a three-objective problem involving operator profit, user charging cost, and voltage deviation, without privileging any single outcome in advance. NSGA-III is applied to explore this space, allowing economically aggressive and technically conservative solutions to coexist during the search. The resulting solution set indicates that profit improvements can be obtained relatively quickly once price flexibility and discharge incentives are introduced. Voltage behavior, by contrast, changes more slowly and settles within a narrow range that appears closely tied to feeder structure rather than pricing alone. User cost shows less regularity, varying with the timing and location of charging and discharging rather than with total energy exchanged. The compromise solution selected from the Pareto set suggests that coordinated EV pricing and mobile storage scheduling can deliver meaningful economic benefit while keeping voltage performance within acceptable bounds, as long as network physics remain inside the optimization loop rather than being enforced only after the fact.

title: An Integrated Data-Driven IoT Framework for Real-Time Downstream Petroleum Logistics
authors: Syed Arshad Raza, Atiq W. Siddiqui, Zeeshan Tariq, Abul Bashar
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: An integrated, real-time view of downstream petroleum logistics is essential for supporting Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 and National Transport and Logistics Strategy objectives, yet current practice largely relies on fragmented systems, manual planning and cost-only optimisation of hazardous fuel transport. This paper proposes the Downstream Petroleum Logistics Management (DPLM) system, an integrated IoT-based framework for real-time, data-driven distribution of petroleum products from suppliers to petrol stations, airports and industrial customers. DPLM combines three core components: a Tank-data Tracking and Transmission system (TTT) that continuously captures tank levels and product temperatures over secure wireless networks; a Data Management System (DMS) that consolidates inventory, customer, routing and fleet information; and a Decision Support System (DSS) that generates delivery plans using a bi-objective optimisation model. The DSS explicitly balances transportation cost and hazardous materials risk, enabling managers to explore a Pareto frontier of cost-risk trade-offs and to embed varying levels of risk aversion and operational constraints in routing and scheduling decisions. Conceptually, the framework is designed to reduce manual planning effort, improve utilisation of trucks and storage capacity, minimise emergency deliveries and support green logistics objectives through risk-aware, emissions-conscious routing choices. The paper formalises the architectural design, data integration structure and optimisation logic of DPLM, providing a systematic foundation for future implementation, mathematical modelling and empirical validation in Saudi downstream petroleum logistics networks.

title: Machine Learning-Based Anomaly Detection for ITER’s Tokamak Systems Monitor: A Gyrotron Case Study
authors: Joris Paret, Daniel Iglesias, Daniel Sabio Ruiz, Dilin Meloni, Andrea Antonione, Ruggero Bertazzoni, Giuseppe Carannante, Martino Ferrari, María Ortiz de Zúñiga, Mario Cavinato, Francisco Sánchez Arcos, Alfredo Portone
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: The Tokamak Systems Monitor (TSM) software will provide ITER operators with timely assessments of machine health, component lifetime, and early warnings of potential faults. Among its key functions, the TSM will employ data-driven anomaly detection methods to identify unexpected or abnormal behavior across a broad range of systems and diagnostics. This work presents an initial proof-of-concept for anomaly detection applied to gyrotron pulses, leveraging data from the European gyrotron prototype. The method combines dimensionality reduction and clustering techniques to identify deviations from expected operational patterns. This approach enables the detection of subtle anomalies that might otherwise go unnoticed. While this first demonstration focuses on intershot anomaly detection for gyrotrons, the methodology is designed to be adaptable to other systems and signals within the TSM, contributing to the improved maintenance strategies and reliability of the ITER tokamak.

title: Polarity Effects of Air Discharge at High Altitude Under Steep-Front Voltage Excitation
authors: Bo Zhu, Hao Zhou, Xi-Mu Han, Xiang-Jie Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: To investigate the influence of voltage polarity on air discharge characteristics of electric aircraft operating in low-temperature and subatmospheric environments under steep-front voltage excitation, this study primarily employs numerical simulation, supplemented by experiments to observe key discharge phenomena. A gas discharge model of a needle–plate electrode configuration is established on a multiphysics simulation platform, and three sets of Helmholtz equations are introduced to describe the photoionization process. To validate the simulation results, a streamer-discharge experimental platform capable of low-temperature and subatmospheric operation is constructed, and discharge images under different polarity conditions are recorded using an ICCD high-speed camera. The results show that, for both polarities, the electric-field strength exhibits a temporal evolution characterized by an initial increase, subsequent decrease, and a secondary rise, and the discharge velocity increases with altitude. Electron density decreases as altitude increases, and its spatial distribution differs significantly between the two polarities: under positive polarity, electrons concentrate near the electrode surface, whereas under negative polarity, the high-density region shifts away from the needle tip. The streamer propagation velocity generally follows a pattern in which the mid-stage develops faster than the early stage, and the late stage is slower than the mid-stage; however, at high altitudes, the late-stage velocity may exceed that of the mid-stage, indicating a distinct evolutionary trend.

title:
Wearable PPG/SpO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Based Sleep Apnea Risk Assessment Using CausNet
authors: Ayush Dam, Satyam Mishra, Pranav Premnath, N. Veni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Sleep apnea (SA) is a common sleeping disorder that is related to serious cardiovascular and metabolic risks and needs to be screened with the help of accessible and continuous screening solutions. Polysomnography (PSG) is the clinical gold standard of diagnosis but it is expensive, intrusive and requires laboratory analysis, which restricts its use for large-scale and long-term monitoring. The paper presents CausNet, a wearable sleep apnea risk-assessment system based on multimodal photoplethysmography (PPG) and blood oxygen saturation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{SpO}_{2}\right)$</tex> signals, trained with a lightweight causal temporal convolutional network. The model provides long-range cardiopulmonary and oxygen-desaturation dynamics without future context or recurrent architecture, and can be deployed in real-time on wearable devices. Using the Sleep Heart Health Study (SHHS) and Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Centre (BIDMC) PPG data, the proposed system has an accuracy of 80.53 % and an Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUROC) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 \%}$</tex> for window-level detection. The framework also facilitates subject-level severity classification of low, moderate, and high apnea risk groups as per the estimated Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) thresholds established by clinical guidelines from the American Academy of Sleep Medicine (AASM). The proposed solution offers a lightweight architecture and low-inference latency to support microcontroller-based wearables and can provide efficient non-intrusive sleep apnea screening for continuous home monitoring and early clinical referral.

title: Computational and IoT Methods for Myocardial Infarction Prediction: A Review
authors: Yuvarani P, Maragatham T, P. Balasubramanie, Nandhini S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This study bridges algorithms, datasets and deployable hardware to integrate computational-oriented methods with internet-of-things (IoT)-based MI prediction. We evaluate sequence models (1D-CNNs, temporal transformers) and baseline learners (logistic regression, gradient boosting) learned on ECG and accompanying photoplethysmography using a patient level splitting with attention to having beat segmentation prior to preprocessing out of concern about data leakage. Assessment includes utility under class imbalance, discrimination as well as decision-curve analysis and calibration. We study the edge deployment paths using single-lead patches, 12-lead recorders, and wrist wearables based on Coral TPU, NVIDIA Jetson and mobile NPUs, to illustrate typical latency, energy and memory constraints. According to the authors, on-device inference, encrypted transport, federated learning and lifecycle documentation consistent with TRIPODAI, ISO 14971 and IEC 62304 all help ensure privacy and security. The clinical system interoperability is being developed as part of the streaming gateways and HL7 FHIR. We propose a phased translation approach, including pretraining on publicly available corpora, fine-tuning using adjudicated labels for HS troponin pathways, external validation, shadow-mode trials (if appropriate), and drift-monitoring and planned recalibration. Finally, we suggest lightweight reference streams for measuring accuracy-latency-energy trade-offs and open issues to be addressed in the future including robustness across sites and devices, fairness across sex and age groups, as well as uncertainty quantification. The aim is to develop a feasible, repeatable pipeline for accurate, interpretable, and resourceefficient MI risk scoring in the home, clinic, and ambulance with open baselines and transparent reporting.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Resource Scaling Framework for Performance-Aware Edge Computing in IoT-Enabled Distributed Environments
authors: Abu Ryan Ali, Yash Raj, M.K Srilekha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Edge computing has emerged as a relevant method to support Internet of Things applications with the need of low response time and continuous monitoring. Nevertheless, most edge computing systems continue to use static and rule-based resource management, which is not very responsive to dynamic workload conditions. This tends to create underutilisation in practice or a situation of unforeseen overload. In this paper, an AI-based dynamic resource scaling in edge computing is designed which allows predictive resource control in edge computing. The system that is proposed incorporates sensor acquisition unit based on Arduino and Raspberry Pi as the edge processor. Vital signs like voltage, current, temperature, humidity, and fire detectors are taken in real-time and used and processed on-site. An example of a machine learning model that is implemented on the edge device to process such inputs and predict resource demand is a Random Forest. Connected loads are also regulated by the use of relay modules based on prediction conditions to model adaptive scaling. The system also enables local monitoring using LCD display and remote viewing using a cloud platform. The prototype designed indicates that predictive scaling is more economical on resource use and stability of operation than the traditional approaches of controlling that are statical. The framework suggested points to the feasible and realistic method of managing smart resources within the context of edge computing. Index Terms-Dynamic resource scaling, machine learning, predictive resource management, edge computing, Internet of Things.

title: Comparative Design of Common Source Low Noise Amplifier using Inductive Degeneration
authors: Amankumar Pandey, Gaurav Saini
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: A Low Noise Amplifier (LNA) is a crucial component of communication systems, particularly in the first active block radio frequency (RF) receivers. Its primary purpose is to increase incoming signals that are incredibly weak without appreciably reducing their signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Since these signals are typically very low in amplitude, such as those received by antennas in satellite, radar, mobile, or wireless communication systems, the LNA must introduce very little additional noise during amplification in order to preserve signal integrity for further processing. This paper presents LNAs with various topologies that use an inductor as the load rather than a resistor, which tends to introduce noise. Most popular LNA topology is the CS-LNA structure with inductive degeneration. This presentation then presents the inductive degeneration CS-LNA with diode connected load and the inductive degeneration CS-LNA with cascode structure, along with parameter analysis such as the Noise Figure and Sparameters. The design was simulated using a 90 nm CMOS technology in the Cadence Virtuoso environment, operating with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2} \mathbf{V}$</tex> supply voltage at a target frequency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 G H z}$</tex>.

title: MSFSNet: Multi-Source Few-Shot Adaptation Network for Cross-Subject Depression Recognition from EEG Signals
authors: Kang Wang, Yanan Zhang, Yingwei Zhang, Fa Zhang, Jian Shen, Bin Hu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Depression is a prevalent mental disorder with severe socio-economic implications, and its early identification and intervention are crucial for mitigating disease progression. However, existing machine learning and deep learning-based approaches for depression recognition exhibit limited generalization across individuals, making them less adaptable to new subjects and restricting their practical applications. To address this issue, we propose a cross-subject depression recognition method based on Multi-Source Few-Shot Adaptation (MSFSA) using electroencephalography (EEG). The proposed method integrates multi-source domain adaptation and ensemble learning strategies. Specifically, the multi-source domain adaptation module employs an alternating training mechanism combining unsupervised domain adaptation and few-shot adaptation, reducing the model's dependency on specific subjects. Meanwhile, ensemble learning improves model robustness and stability by aggregating multiple model predictions, reducing the impact of individual model biases and enhancing classification reliability. Experiments were conducted on the public MODMA EEG dataset, comprising 53 subjects (24 patients with major depressive disorder and 29 healthy controls). With a theoretical chance level of 50% for the cross-subject classification setting, the results demonstrate that, compared with traditional machine learning methods, existing EEG-based depression recognition models, and advanced domain adaptation algorithms, leveraging the Alpha and low-Gamma band features as the key contributing factors, the proposed method achieves a significant improvement in accuracy, reaching 87.12%, which outperforms the state-of-the-art HEMAsNet (80.67%) and WDANet (70.94%) on the same dataset under the 10-fold cross-subject validation protocol. These findings indicate that the proposed approach effectively reduces subject dependency in EEG-based depression recognition and provides a promising solution for improving cross-subject adaptability.

title: Improving the Performance of ECG-Based Affective Classification Systems Using Cepstral Features
authors: R Yazhini, S R K Madhavh, D V Prashannaa, S Dharun, H Haritha, C Santhosh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate classification of physiological signals is crucial for effective monitoring of stress and affective states. This study explores a deep learning framework for the classification of five affective states: transient, baseline, stress, amusement and meditation using the electrocardiogram (ECG) signals from the WESAD dataset. Initially, ECG data is segmented into 3-minute duration samples to capture consistent physiological patterns. From each segment, both time and frequency domain features are extracted to characterize signal dynamics. These features are then used for training a support vector machine (SVM) classifier, which serves as a baseline system for comparison. Subsequently, we developed a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) based classifier with raw time and frequency domain features as input. The input features have person-specific and affective state specific information. To enhance the affective state classification (ASC) accuracy, the effect of person-specific variations needs to be minimized. We then explored using cepstral features to minimize person-specific variations on the classifier. Further, we experimented with cepstrum mean subtraction (CMS) to minimize the effect of person-specific information in the input features. The proposed CMS-ASC achieved a performance improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$29.23 \%, 43.98 \%, 8.77 \%$</tex> and 0.178 in overall accuracy, sensitivity, specificity and Area Under the Curve - Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUC-ROC) score, respectively, over the baseline, giving an overall performance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.64 \%, 93.68 \%, 98.04 \%$</tex> and 0.99, respectively.

title: Interpretable Spectral Super-Resolution with Gram-Polynomial Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks
authors: Satya Sai Ramesh P, Mohith Ram Sai Karthik Phalgun Kothamasu, Arun P V
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Spectral super-resolution (SSR) enhances multispectral images by generating hyperspectral data, enabling advances in agriculture, remote sensing, and medical imaging. Traditional methods such as interpolation and sparse recovery struggle with noise and fail to capture complex spectral properties. While recent deep learning CNN models improve accuracy, they often operate as “black boxes,” limiting trust and scalability. We propose a Residual Autoencoder with Gram-KAN Convolutions(KAGN)-a novel Kolmogorov-Arnold Network variant using Gram polynomials for efficient and interpretable feature learning. This design avoids heavy 3D convolutions and opaque transformers, offering transparent spectral-spatial modeling with state-of-the-art performance. On benchmark datasets, our approach achieves superior MSSIM and PSNR with lower error rates. By uniting accuracy with interpretability, Gram-KAN(KAGN) Residual Autoencoders provide a scalable, trustworthy framework for hyperspectral imaging.

title: Enhancing FinTech Risk Management Using Support Vector Machines for Fraud Detection and Risk Classification
authors: Yojna Bansal, Ahmed Hamed, Ahmed Sebihi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The financial technology (FinTech) has emerged at a rapid rate, and as a result, cybersecurity threats and defrauders have become more widespread. Traditional rule-based security practices tend to lack in keeping with the changing nature of financial fraud. This study proposes a classification scheme based on Support Vector Machines (SVM) to identify fraudulent transactions and evaluate a financial risk. By using the publicly available sets of financial data, the suggested model separates legitimate and fraudulent transactions improving risk assessment in digital payments and online banking and digital lending circles. The efficiency of the SVM approach is compared with the commonly used machine learning classifiers, such as Random Forest, Logistic Regression, and Neural Networks confirming its higher accuracy, precision, and recall. The results point to the potential of SVM for bettering fraud detection, reducing false positive rates, and better credit risk assessment. This study highlights the need to incorporate a machine learning-based fraud detection into FinTech applications to minimize financial losses and give consumers confidence.

title: Adversarial Image Quality Assessment Against PGD Attack on ResNet-34
authors: Srutipragyan Swain, Prasant Kumar Pattnaik, Suvendra Kumar Jayasingh, Suneeta Mohanty, Motirani Nayak, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: This study aims at exploring the behavior of ResNet34 model under the adversarial perturbations generated by Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) attack. The implementation is carried out on CIFAR-10 dataset and the impact after performing the attack on image quality parameters have been assessed in terms of PSNR, SSIM, SNR, and MSE. The result shows a significant decline in model's accuracy as the strength of perturbation increases. However, the perceptual similarity retained at lower epsilon values. Additionally, the Fool AI feature integrated in the model makes visualization of incorrect classification of images resulting from adversarial perturbation. This study gives a valuable insight to the tradeoff between adversarial perturbation and perceptual quality analysis.

title: An Efficient Approach to Predict Storm Path in Indian Sub-Continent Using Machine Learning Model
authors: Satya Sundar Mallick, Monideepa Roy, Sujoy Datta
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Cyclones in India have a tremendous impact on the national economy of the country. The direct and immediate impact is basically felt through the damage to agriculture, infrastructure, and the loss of livelihood of people in the impacted areas. In the last <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4-5$</tex> years, we had seen at least one-two major and destructive cyclones making the landfall each year. In the past few years researchers explained how cyclones are occurring frequently and extremely intense <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup>. Forecasters normally use data from satellites, weather stations, and aircrafts to predict the cyclone tracks using numerical models. If we predict the storm tracks correctly, we can be able to save lives and resources. When a cyclone threatens our community, we must be prepared for the evacuation if we live within storm surge area. Correct prediction of the storm track allows enough time for us to prepare. [<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{17]}$</tex>. The Government also takes necessary actions to clear the area to reduce loss of life. In this paper we have collected 87 cyclone's track data during 2011-2024. This track data is processed using a LSTM machine learning model to predict the storm track. Predicting Storm tracks using ML models takes lesser resources than traditional forecasting models as well as the ML models being faster. These ML models can work independently or can work with traditional models for bias correction.

title: The Influence of E-Payment Usage on Customer Satisfaction and Loyalty
authors: Ignacio Nicolas Vishnu Firstyawan, Adele B L Mailangkay, Dicky Hida Syahchari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study aims to research the influence of using e-payments on customer satisfaction and loyalty in Indonesia's growing digital payment ecosystem. E-payment systems, such as GoPay, OVO, and Dana, have changed consumer behavior by providing secure, efficient, and easy alternatives compared to traditional ways of paying. The research used a quantitative approach and purposive sampling. Data was obtained from 151 valid respondents. The findings showed that perceived value, customer satisfaction, trust, and e-service quality significantly affect customer loyalty. Perceived value enhances satisfaction through convenience and cost efficiency, while satisfaction drives trust and e-service quality. All these factors, in turn, strengthen customer loyalty, encourage users to recommend and continuously use e-payment services. This study sends an important signal to improve data security, diversify services offered, and enhance infrastructure for better adoption and customer experience. By improving these areas, the providers of e-payments will be able to establish much better relationships with their customers for the sustainable development of Indonesia's digital economy.

title: Cost Effective Adaptable Hyperspectral Imaging of Manuscripts: Mapping Surface Topology Variation
authors: M. Davies, M. Stuart, E. Allen, L. Costantini, E. Leonidas, M. Hobbs, I. Scott, J.R. Willmott
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Ancient and medieval parchments were frequently reused as covers and binding material bindings for early printed texts due to their durability and the availability of manuscripts considered obsolete. This has persevered texts that may otherwise have been lost at the cost of significant wear and damage which reduces legibility. Additionally, transport of these fragments, especially those still in situ, risks damage and so photographs are all that is generally available without visiting the libraries and repositories where they are preserved. Non-contact imaging techniques are critical to recovering the text from these fragments without causing further damage. We utilize a PryOptik Chromamapper, a portable, low-cost hyperspectral imaging system for the retrieval of ancient and medieval texts due to the enhanced processing that can be done on such datasets. We also demonstrate a technique to construct 3D surfaces of the fragments utilizing hyperspectral datacubes which enables a digital version of the object to be studied as if it were physically present but without the risk of harm to the fragile samples.

title: A Hybrid Lightweight Cryptographic Algorithm for Resource-Constrained IoT Systems
authors: Divya James, Lakshmi K.S., Pallavi Ajith, Ahina George
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) though popular, poses serious security issues. Cryptography enables the protection of information from unauthorized access, but the modes for desktops and servers are conventional and unsuitable for constrained environs like sensor networks and cyber-physical infrastructures. This paper proposes a hybrid lightweight cryptographic algorithm by integrating symmetric and asymmetric encryption modes that balance performance and security, making this ideal for low-powered devices. A comprehensive performance evaluation was executed through parameters like encryptiondecryption time, memory consumption, and energy efficiency. The experimental results show this proposed algorithm significantly improves processing speed and resource efficiency while ensuring robust security. The findings validate its feasibility for secure communication in constrained IoT environments. This research strives to develop efficient, scalable, and lightweight security mechanisms for modern IoT applications.

title: Farm Time Saver: A Location-Aware Veterinary Appointment System
authors: Vijay N S, Annu Sharma
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Traditional appointment systems generally lead to long waiting times due to journey delays, emergencies, or conflicting appointments, causing users to lose valuable working time. Farm Time Saver is an android application that intends to plan veterinary visits optimally by automatically re-routing them based on the veterinarian's present location. With GPS tracking and Reordering the appointment list, the application minimizes waiting hours and enhances the efficiency of delivery of veterinary services and notifies users when the veterinarian approaches 1 km through SMS. In addition, the application also has a missed-call appointment feature for keypad mobile phone users, where they can book an appointment by simply missed calling on the veterinary phone number. Built using Flutter as the front-end and Flask as the back-end, the application uses the location tracking and SQLite for database in the back-end. This innovative solution aims to streamline the appointment procedure and improve the user experience for users.

title: GenAI-Powered Multimodal Vision Transformer for Real-Time Face Liveness Detection and Adaptive Anti-Spoofing
authors: Suraj Singuluri, Vinit Kumar Modanwal, U.Manosagar, Nawaz Khan, M. Sri Lakshmi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: To guard against these spoofing attacks, face liveness detection is necessary to guarantee the liveness of the face in biometric authentication systems. In this paper, a new liveness detection and adaptive anti spoofing powered by a text based GenAI multimodal input vision transformer architecture is presented. The proposed methodology embeds structured textual descriptions of facial movements, lighting conditions, types of spoofs, types of micro motion cues and sensor modalities as modality specific token embeddings. They are fused using a vision transformer with cross modality attention followed by a temporal modeling module that acquires dynamic fine grained behavioral dynamics. The generative AI module synthesizes adversarial spoof variants and feeds them to a self-adaptive learning loop that results in the improvement of robustness against unseen attacks. Experimented on synthetically generated dataset, GMViT attains 96.8% accuracy with 2.1% FAR and 4.3% FRR, much better than baseline models like BERT or the standard ViT architectures. These results demonstrate that modality aware fusion and adaptive training leads to an improved anti spoofing performance even without the presence of the visual modality. A new direction for multimodal biometric security by means of language driven vision modeling is proposed in this approach.

title: Machine Learning-Based Traffic Prediction for Smart Cities
authors: Shatha Habeeb Jafer Al-Khalisy, Ahmed T. Sadiq Al-Obaidi, Ahmed Alzarooni, Alaa Momani, Abdelrahman Radwan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The rapid urbanization and the advent of smart cities have intensified the demand for traffic management systems that are efficient. Accurate traffic prediction remains critical to decreasing congestion, allowing for reduced travel times, as well as resulting in better urban mobility. In this paper, the work on employing machine learning (ML) techniques in order to predict the traffic pattern is presented along with the very high capacity of ML systems to analyze complex non-linearities in the traffic data. A comparative analysis is provided in this paper between several ML models, including traditional algorithms such as Support Vector Machines (SVM) and modern approaches, for example, Deep Learning (DL) models used herein. By using real-world datasets from smart city infrastructures, the proposed methodology combines various factors such as weather conditions, road events, and historical traffic data to make correct predictions. Our findings clearly show that ML models are capable of producing accurate short-term and long-term traffic forecasts, with the performance of the deep learning methods, mainly Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, outperforming traditional methods ultimately. This research gives due regard to the promising aspect of machine learning to change urban traffic management through scalable applications, where solutions will emerge in real-time for smart cities. In the future, a key area of focus will be the integration of federated learning to increase data privacy as well as the scaling up of the capabilities of distributed traffic systems.

title: Is ISO 56002 the Missing Link? A Literature Review on Standard-Based Innovation Management
authors: Ahmed Alomari, Hani Alamri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study explores how the ISO 56002 standard contributes to systematic innovation management in organizations. Utilizing multiple theoretical lenses—including diffusion of innovation, dynamic capability, and standardization theories—we examine the impact of standard-based approaches on firm performance and innovation capacity. Theoretically, this study contributes by adding construct clarity to the relationship between standardized processes and innovation outcomes, demonstrating that ISO 56002 enhances both incremental and breakthrough innovation through improved process alignment, strategic planning, and integrated performance measurement. The findings reveal an increase in the coherence and replicability of innovation practices among firms adopting ISO 56002, while also underscoring that innovation often has a long runway before generating measurable returns. Referring to the well-recognized ISO brand further bolsters credibility and facilitates benchmarking, ultimately enabling the innovation process to be more reliably managed and assessed. Finally, providing tangible evidence of the positive effects of standardization, this study lays a robust foundation for future empirical and theoretical explorations in the field of innovation management.

title: Multi-Modal Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Resource Allocation and Interference Mitigation in 5G Ultra-Dense Networks
authors: B.Uma, M. Jamuna Rani, B.Suriya, V.Kumararaja, T.Abirami, T.Siva
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The unprecedented densification of 5G networks has amplified the challenges of efficient resource allocation and effective interference mitigation, particularly in ultra-dense urban deployments. Traditional optimization techniques often struggle to adapt to the dynamic, heterogeneous, and high-dimensional environments inherent to 5G. In this paper, we propose a novel multi-modal deep reinforcement learning (MM-DRL) framework that intelligently orchestrates resource allocation and interference management across ultra-dense 5G networks. By leveraging diverse modalities of network data—such as user mobility patterns, real-time traffic loads, spectrum occupancy, and channel state information—the MM-DRL agent learns to optimize resource scheduling and power control policies in a distributed, adaptive manner. The proposed approach incorporates advanced neural architectures, including multi-head attention mechanisms and recurrent encoders, to fuse and interpret multi-source data streams. Comprehensive simulation results demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms conventional and unimodal DRL baselines in terms of spectral efficiency, quality of service, and interference suppression, establishing a robust foundation for intelligent 5G network automation.

title: A Betti Curve-Driven Neural Network for the Detection of Signal Abnormalities in the Financial Market
authors: Jasharaj Lenka, Pragyan Paramita Das, Shivam Kumar Sahoo, Devraj Naik, Subhra Swetanisha, Dayal Kumar Behera
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The comprehensive methodology for detecting anomalies within the realm of financial temporal data is developed in conjunction with Topological Data Analysis (TDA). The procedure uses processed data along with the technique of the time delay embedding, so that any presented raw electronic signals may be converted into structured point clouds or sets of points. Topological data analysis, using persistent homology, identifies topological properties (e.g. connected components and discrete loops) and expresses them as Betti Curves, which are compacted descriptors that are not as influenced by noise as other means of describing the evolution of topological structures across filtration scales. The Betti Curves will serve as input to a custom neural network that classifies information as either normal or abnormal. To enhance the training of the neural network, the methodology proposes using a hybrid optimization strategy combining Grey Wolf Optimization with Elephant Herding Optimization strategies, and learning rates are adjusted during training based on the topological sensitivity of the neural network's learning processes. Therefore, this technique maximizes through numerous iterations the convergence speeds of the model, the models' accuracy rating and the stability of the model. The experimental results of applying the proposed technique to real world stock market data sets demonstrate that the proposed approach was superior in almost all respects to previously utilized statistical and deep learning techniques with respect to the speed of convergence (within 13 epochs), accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(94.2 {\%})$</tex> and degree of variance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.6 {\%})$</tex>. The technique utilizes topological insights instead of purely statistical metrics and therefore provides a scalable and interpretable means of detecting subtle differences in financial signals that otherwise may go undetected. Future research will investigate additional forms of topological invariants to use along with other models for the purposes of real-time application to streamed data.

title: Comparative Deep Learning Approaches for Microorganisms Classification
authors: Janardhan G, P. Nithya Sri, G. Dhanush, K. Meghana, B. Anusha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The precise identification of microorganisms is vital in fields like microbiology, medicine, and environmental science. paper presents a comparative analysis of three leading convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures: InceptionV3, EfficientNetB0, and ResNet50, aimed at classifying eight categories of microorganisms using microscopic images. The models were trained on a custom- labeled dataset and assessed with standard performance metrics. Our findings indicate that ResNet50 surpassed other models with a classification accuracy of 87%, followed closely by EfficientNetB0 and InceptionV3. Additionally, a lightweight Python-based prediction system was developed to implement the trained ResNet50 model for real-time classification tasks. This work highlights the potential of deep learning in enhancing automated microorganism identification, paving the way for scalable and reliable diagnostic tools.

title: Post Quantum Cryptography Enabled Hybrid Secure Web Browser for Future-Proof Communication
authors: Shilpa Chaudhari, Ananya A, Shree Lakshmi S Shekar, Pavithra J, Rishikesh J Dharwar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Quantum computing presents a growing threat to classical encryption mechanisms such as RSA and ECC. This paper proposes a hybrid secure web browser architecture that integrates CRYSTALS-Kyber, a lattice-based post-quantum algorithm selected by NIST, for key encapsulation. The proposed hybrid cryptographic scheme combine classical and quantum-resistant algorithms is essential to ensure the continued confidentiality and integrity of digital communications in the future. Built with Kotlin, Spring Boot, and Java Swing, the browser establishes a secure channel using a TLS 1.3-inspired handshake, fortified with Kyber-based quantum-resilient key exchange. The system supports encrypted file and message transmission, provides real-time UI feedback, and simulates quantum attacks using Qiskit. The results demonstrate that the hybrid cryptographic approach enhances security by combining the strengths of both classical and post-quantum algorithms, while maintaining reasonable performance overhead.

title: Intelligent Sensor-based Surveillance Jacket with AI-Powered Report Generation
authors: Rashmi Rani Samantaray, Abdul Azeez, Pushplata Dubey
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The integration of wearable technology with healthcare and environmental adaptability presents an innovative approach to continuous health surveillance and personal protection, particularly in extreme or hazardous environments. This paper proposes a comprehensive system that merges advanced biomedical sensors with IoT technology to provide real-time monitoring of human vital signs, while dynamically adjusting to environmental conditions, ensuring enhanced safety and comfort. Leveraging cloud-based data analytics, the system offers medical professionals' continuous access to critical health data, enabling timely interventions based on a person’s real-time physiological status. The sensors monitor key health metrics such as heart rate, body temperature, and other vital signs, transmitting this data securely to a cloud platform for ongoing monitoring. In case of any detected anomalies, automatic alerts are generated to notify healthcare providers. By seamlessly integrating continuous health surveillance with adaptive environmental control, this system represents a robust solution for both healthcare delivery and personal safety, contributing to the well-being of users in demanding operational settings.

title: Intelligent Multi-Agent Architecture Leveraging Large Language Models for Transparent, Interactive Financial Analysis and Strategic Investment Recommendation
authors: Sanjith Kumar Santhosh, Rejul Rohit Baskar, Janani U
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) and large language models (LLMs) has opened up new possibilities for automating complex analytical workflows in finance. Traditional equity research is still limited by human bandwidth, fragmented data ecosystems, and a lack of scalability. BridgeRock AI offers an intelligent multi-agent architecture that can autonomously conduct institutional-grade financial analysis that combines structured and unstructured data sources into coherent, actionable, and explainable investment theses. The BridgeRock AI system is a modular architecture composed of specialized agents - a Data Agent, Insight Agent, and Thesis Agent connected through an orchestration layer powered by retrieval- augmented generation (RAG) and multi-agent communication protocols. Portfolio managers and analysts can interact through a conversational interface in natural language with the platform and receive evidence-grounded, model-verified insights in real time. This hybrid backend architecture consists of Java-based microservices for orchestration, while Python-based AI services handle computation and utilize a vector database for semantic retrieval and explainability tracing. Early evaluations indicate that BridgeRock AI improves analytical accuracy, throughput, and interpretability over traditional research methods. BridgeRock AI offers a scalable and auditable architecture for next-generation financial intelligence and institutional decision support.

title: Establishing a Secure Site-to-Site Network Using IPsec VPN for Enterprise Communication
authors: Sahiti Vankayalapati, V. Sreevani, Y. Akshith Reddy, D. Ashok
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Multinational companies are widely adopting remote work models. For secure transmission of information over public networks a reliable means of communication should be established. A Virtual Private Network (VPN) provides a secure fool proof method for transmitting data between two remote locations. The proposed research study explores upon creating a secure VPN using IPsec, which is a strong, most trusted method for protecting data. The VPN was set up as a site-to-site connection between two offices that were far apart. To establish a safer connection using VPN we have implemented AES-256 for providing encryption, SHA-2 for authentication of data, and IKEv2 for key management between source and destination. After experimental analysis the encryption added only a small amount of delay which is less than 5 percent and the network was able to perform 95% of its original speed even after implementing encryption. There were no signs of packet loss or errors during the tests, due to which we can say the VPN connection was very strong, reliable, and stable. We used pfSense, which is free and open-source software, and it is a very good choice for companies because of its low cost and easy to scale. The results prove that an IPsec VPN built with pfSense can give the same or even better performance than many paid commercial VPNs. So, we can provide a safe, affordable, and effective way for companies to connect their offices and protect their communication.

title: Prediction of Groundwater Level for Semi-Arid North-Western India Using Rainfall and Groundwater Data: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Valluri Keerthi Ram, Kamma Ajay Nageswara Rao, Abhay Gokavarapu, Peeta Basa Pati
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Groundwater depletion is one of the main challenges to sustainable water management in semi-arid North-Western parts of India, where it is an important source for domestic supply, agriculture and industries. These part of India rely heavily for Monsoon rains for recharge. While most of the limited studies with work with rainfall data or groundwater data, this study combines rainfall and groundwater data collected from multiple sources. Using this combined data, this work proposes a machine learning based predictive framework for estimating premonsoon and postmonsoon groundwater levels of three Indian NorthWestern states, namely, Haryana, Rajasthan, and Gujarat. This work performs a comparative analysis using multiple ensemble models, namely, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, ExtraTrees, and Ridge Regression, and Stacking Regressor, for prediction of groundwater levels. A comparison between state specific as well as regional modeling shows that ExtraTrees Regressor perform optimally for modeling of groundwater level regionally (across all the three states) for both premonsoon and postmonsoon periods. The best Test <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> score at regional level is achieved with ExtraTrees regressor at 0.97 and 0.98 for premonsoon and postmonsoon periods, respectively.

title: Signal Processing Techniques for Enhancing Accuracy in Integrated HAR-IPS for Firefighters
authors: Viet-Hoan Bui, Nguyen Thien Thinh, Nguyen Van Thang, To-Hieu Dao
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Complex indoor fires pose significant risks to firefighter safety. This paper presents an integrated human activity recognition and indoor positioning system. The system utilizes a waist-mounted device comprising inertial and barometric sensors, combined with a foot-mounted LiDAR. Within this framework, LiDAR operates as a dynamic measurement system to estimate stride length through geometric analysis and synchronization with gait events. To enhance robustness, spatial-angular region selection and clustering algorithms are implemented to exclude environmental obstacles. A multistage framework is applied to refine data from inertial and LiDAR sensors. Additionally, optimized machine learning models are utilized for activity and direction classification on resource-constrained microcontrollers. Furthermore, a temperature-compensated barometric fusion algorithm with an adaptive bias update mechanism corrects altitude drift. Experimental results indicate that the system can provide a positioning accuracy of approximately one to two meters in simulated firefighting scenarios.

title:
Enhanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation γ
                    <sup>m</sup>
                    > 0.05 for Intelligent Content Summarization and Knowledge Management in Multi-Agent Collaborative Systems
authors: Akey Sungheetha, Rajesh Sharma R, Gudipati Rajesh Naidu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The exponential growth of digital content necessitates advanced knowledge management systems capable of intelligent information retrieval and summarization. This research presents a novel Enhanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (E-RAG) framework that integrates multi-agent collaborative systems with transformer-based neural networks for superior content summarization and knowledge management applications. The proposed methodology addresses critical challenges in traditional RAG systems including context retention limitations, semantic coherence degradation, and scalability constraints in enterprise environments. Our innovative approach combines AutoGen multi-agent orchestration with locally deployed Granite language models to achieve autonomous content processing capabilities. The framework incorporates advanced summarization techniques utilizing attention mechanisms with retrieval confidence scores ranging from 0.85 to 0.97, demonstrating superior performance metrics including 94.2% semantic coherence, 91.8% factual accuracy, and 88.5% content compression efficiency. Experimental validation using synthetic datasets derived from enterprise knowledge bases shows processing throughput improvements of 340% compared to conventional RAG implementations, with latency reduction from 2.3 seconds to 0.68 seconds per query. The system achieves scalability metrics supporting concurrent user loads of up to 1,500 sessions while maintaining response quality scores above 92%. This research contributes significant technological advancement opportunities for patent development in intelligent content management systems, presenting substantial commercialization potential for enterprise knowledge management solutions and community service applications in educational and research domains.

title: AegisOT: Design and Simulated Evaluation of a Unified Zero-Trust Security Gateway for DNP3–OPC UA Industrial Communication
authors: Danah Almuzel, Shahad Aljaroudi, Najla Alyami, Najd Ghadra, Rana Alqahtani, Rami M. Mohammad
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The use of Operational Technology (OT) networks is exposed to replay attacks, unauthorized execution of commands, and misconfigurations of policies because of the legacy protocol limitations and slow patches.
