title: Lightweight Cross-Spectral Face Recognition via Contrastive Alignment and Distillation
authors: Anjith George, Sébastien Marcel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Heterogeneous Face Recognition (HFR) aims at matching face images captured across different sensing modalities, such as thermal-to-visible or near-infrared-to-visible, enhancing the usability of face recognition systems in challenging real-world conditions. Although recent HFR methods have achieved significant improvements in performance, many rely on computationally expensive models, making them impractical for deployment on resource-limited edge devices. In this work, we introduce a lightweight yet effective HFR framework by adapting a hybrid CNN-Transformer model originally developed for RGB homogeneous face recognition. Our approach enables efficient end-to-end training with only a small amount of paired heterogeneous data, while still maintaining strong performance on standard RGB face recognition benchmarks. This makes it suitable for both homogeneous and heterogeneous settings. Comprehensive experiments on several challenging HFR and face recognition benchmarks show that our method achieves state-of-the-art or competitive performance while keeping computational requirements low.

title: QVGA CMOS LiDAR Sensor With nMOS-Only SPAD Analog Front-End and Area-Efficient Priority Histogramming TDC
authors: Minkyung Kim, Hyeongseok Seo, Songhyeon Kim, Jung-Hoon Chun, Seong-Jin Kim, Jaehyuk Choi
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a high-resolution (HR) CMOS light detection and ranging (LiDAR) sensor capable of generating quarter video graphics array (QVGA) (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$320~{\times }~240$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) depth images. To support high pixel density, the sensor employs a compact 6-transistor (6-T) nMOS-only single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) analog front-end (AFE) with a column-shared active recharge circuit. A two-step histogramming time-to-digital converter (hTDC) with a priority memory (PM) architecture is proposed to minimize memory usage. The PM scheme dynamically allocates additional memory only to histogram bins with a high likelihood of containing signal echoes. As a result, the hTDC achieves a 51% memory reduction compared to conventional two-step implementations, requiring only 188 bits per channel. The sensor supports two pixel configurations: a merging mode, which combines eight SPADs to produce a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$160~{\times }~120$</tex-math> </inline-formula> image with enhanced sensitivity, and an HR mode, which uses two SPADs per macro pixel to generate a full-resolution <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$320~{\times }~240$</tex-math> </inline-formula> image. The prototype chip was fabricated using a 110-nm back-side illuminated (BSI) CMOS image sensor (CIS) process. The prototype sensor generates depth images at 10 frames/s in HR mode and 40 frames/s in merging mode. Under 100 klx ambient light, the sensor demonstrates a worst case depth error of 5.7 cm and a depth precision of 0.2% at a distance of 42 m.

title: ISAC-Assisted DRL for Dynamic MAC Scheduler Reconfiguration in O-RAN
authors: Neco Villegas, Juan Luis Herrera, Luis Diez, Domenico Scotece, Luca Foschini, Ramón Agüero
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper introduces reconfiguration mechanisms for a 5th Generation (5G) Medium Access Control (MAC) scheduler, which uses Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and is deployed as an xApp within the RAN Intelligent Controller (RIC) framework. The objective is to optimize the allocation of radio resources in 5G networks, seeking to meet Quality of Service (QoS) requirements while minimizing resource consumption. To this end, we propose the dynamic adjustment of configurable parameters in a Lyapunov-based scheduler, which addresses the challenges posed by the highly dynamic network environment, and the need to meet multiple QoS objectives. The DRL agent dynamically reconfigures the scheduling by leveraging Integrated Sensing and Communications (ISAC)-provided sensing data alongside conventional communication metrics. Exploiting the adaptability of DRL, our solution can effectively respond to fluctuating network conditions, thereby continuously enhancing scheduling decisions in real time. The proposal is evaluated through comprehensive simulations conducted over ns-3 5G-LENA, which demonstrate notable improvements in QoS performance and resource efficiency. In the analyzed scenarios, our proposed scheduling solution achieves a 30% reduction in radio resource utilization while maintaining full compliance with QoS requirements. These results highlight the great potential of exploiting DRL and sensing data to optimize MAC scheduling in modern wireless communication systems, offering a scalable and adaptive solution for 5G and future 6G networks.

title: Real-Time Lane Detection Using OpenCV and Computer Vision Techniques
authors: Balaji Manam, Naveen Kumar Bokka, Abinaya Varatharajan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper proposes a conventional computer-vision pipeline for real-time lane detection using Python and the OpenCV module. This simple and computationally efficient method accounts for grayscale conversion, Gaussian blurring, Canny edge detection, Region of Interest (ROI) masking, and the Probabilistic Hough Transform, which consistently detects and follows lane markings. A crucial part of our pipeline is a line refinement technique that averages and extrapolates the numerous line segments generated by the Hough Transform to construct two stable, continuous lane borders. Experimental results on real road video frames show robust lane localization, with a real-time performance of 28 frames per second on a standard consumer-grade CPU and a qualitative accuracy of about 95%.

title: FAMIT-P: Fairness-Aware Mobility-Modulated Transformer for Future Pandemic Forecasting
authors: Nafis Wadud Ansari, Tanvir Ahmed, Mehedi Hasan, Md. Abid Hasan Rafi, Md. Mahid Arfan Rahat, Noushin Akter Trina, Sanzida Ali Tanfe
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of pandemic dynamics is a critical component of effective public health planning and policy formulation. In this study, we introduce the Fairness-Aware Mobility-Integrated Transformer (FAMIT), a novel deep learning framework designed to capture the complex interplay between epidemiological, demographic, policy, mobility, and vaccination factors in modeling disease transmission. FAMIT incorporates a mobility-modulated attention mechanism that dynamically adapts to variations in population movement and non-pharmaceutical interventions, thereby improving temporal responsiveness. To address equity concerns, the framework integrates a fairness-aware loss function that mitigates prediction disparities across vulnerable demographic subgroups. Using a large-scale dataset of more than 429,435 records spanning 255 global locations, FAMIT consistently outperforms state-of-the-art benchmark models in both predictive accuracy and robustness, particularly during outbreak surges. Furthermore, its use of Monte Carlo Dropout provides tighter and more informative uncertainty estimates, enhancing interpretability. The findings underscore FAMIT’s potential to deliver reliable, equitable, and interpretable pandemic forecasts, thereby contributing to the development of resilient and sustainable public health infrastructures.

title: Diffusion-Based 3D Radar Semantic Perception in Cluttered Agricultural Environments
authors: Ruibin Zhang, Jialiang Hou, Fei Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Accurate and robust environmental perception is crucial for robot autonomous navigation. While current methods typically adopt optical sensors, such as cameras and LiDARs, as primary sensing modalities, their susceptibility to visual occlusion—such as dirt adhering to the lens or physical blockage of the sensor—often leads to degraded performance or complete system failure. In this paper, we focus on agricultural scenarios where robots are exposed to the risk of onboard sensor contamination. Leveraging radar’s strong penetration capability, we introduce a radar-based 3D environmental perception framework as a viable alternative. It comprises three core modules designed for dense and accurate semantic perception: first, parallel frame accumulation to enhance signal-to-noise ratio of raw radar data; second, a diffusion model-based hierarchical learning framework that first filters radar sidelobe artifacts then generates fine-grained 3D semantic point clouds; and third, a specifically designed sparse 3D network optimized for processing large-scale raw radar data. We conducted extensive benchmark comparisons and experimental evaluations on a self-built dataset collected in real-world agricultural field scenes. Results demonstrate that our method achieves superior structural and semantic prediction performance compared to existing methods, while simultaneously reducing computational and memory costs by 44.3% and 27.5%, respectively. Furthermore, our approach achieves complete reconstruction and accurate classification of thin structures such as poles and wires—which existing methods struggle to perceive—highlighting its potential for dense and accurate 3D radar perception.

title: AI-Driven Dielectric Characterization of E-Waste Composites for Radar Absorbers Using Electromagnetic Mixing Model
authors: Ravi Yadav, Meha Agrawal, Ravi Panwar, Slawomir Koziel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Performing dielectric characterization via conventional optimization is exceptionally cumbersome due to extensive iterative recalibration. To facilitate the characterization task, this study focuses on designing a custom multilayer perceptron (MLP) model in a constrained environment (domain). The training data is generated within specified limits and follows the Kramers-Kronig causality relations to evaluate the dielectric properties of composites. A generalized MLP model is designed to optimize the EM mixing model parameters for all the desired composites simultaneously. Additionally, a distinctive fitness function integrated with the Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (AOA) is employed to enhance the performance of a single-layer RAM that considers both transverse electric (TE) and transverse magnetic (TM) polarizations. A thin, cost-effective, angularly stable RAM is designed using e-waste with an optimal thickness of 1.3 mm. The RAM exhibits a reflection coefficient (RC) of –37.2 dB at 11.3 GHz and a bandwidth of 1.9 GHz below –10 dB at normal incidence. In TE and TM polarizations, the peak RC of –15.0 dB and –16.6 dB is achieved at a 45° incidence angle, respectively. This research emphasizes the potential of MLP-assisted characterization of e-waste composites in developing cost-effective, lightweight, and broadband RAMs, demonstrating their effectiveness at various incident angles.

title: Investigation of electret properties of polylactic acid/curcumin composite films
authors: Temenuzhka Yovcheva, Asya Viraneva, Aleksandar Grigorov, Rumen Mladenov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Extending the shelf life of food products is of great importance to the food industry. Polylactic acid (PLA) is known as a promising biopolymer that can be used for food packaging due to its high mechanical and permeability properties. However, its low elasticity and high rigidity at room temperature, as well as poor moisture and gas barrier properties limit its applicability. On the other hand, this biopolymer has a significant potential for optimizing physical properties through material modifications, including in combination with natural additives as different polyphenols. Polyphenols are a class of naturally occurring compounds found in plants, exhibit a wide range of beneficial properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Curcumin, is prominent polyphenol with potent antioxidant properties [1]. Blending PLA with polyphenols offers the opportunity to improve the properties of PLA by utilizing the beneficial properties of the polyphenol [2]. The present paper aims to develop new PLA - based composite films with incorporated polyphenol curcumin with different concentration (0 %, 10 %, 15 %, 20 % against PLA) and to examine their electret properties. PLA composite films are prepared using a solution casting method. Curcumin with different concentration is added to the PLA solution. The film-forming solution is cast on levelled glass petri dishes and dried at a temperature of 35 °C until complete chloroform evaporation. All created composite films are cut into 30x30 mm samples and charged in a negative corona in normal atmospheric conditions. The charging of the samples in a corona discharge is carried out by means of a conventional corona triode system consisting of a corona electrode (needle), a grounded plate electrode and a grid placed between them [3]. The electrets surface potential of the charged samples is measured by the vibrating electrode method with compensation and the estimated error is less than 5%. Time and temperature dependences of the normalized surface potential for all investigated electrets are studied. The results obtained show a significant change in the electret behavior of the composite films after the inclusion of curcumin with different concentration into the PLA film. The possible surface potential decay mechanisms responsible for the electret's behavior are discussed. It is established that the surface potential decay depends on the corona polarity and the concentration of the curcumin. The conductivity of all of the investigated films is also determined and the collected results demonstrate that the increase of the curcumin concentration within the PLA films causes a respective increase in conductivity. The wettability of the investigated films is measured using the static water contact angle method. The surface free energy is also calculated and discussed.

title: STIM-D: A 31× Faster Spatially-Informed Anomaly Detector with High-Confidence Saliency Preservation
authors: Krishnan Batri, Rajermani Thinakaran, S Lakshmi
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Real-time hyperspectral anomaly detection demands computational efficiency without compromising operational reliability. This letter presents STIM-D, a diagonal covariance approximation of the Spectral Thermodynamic Irregularity Model (STIM), designed to enable efficient anomaly detection under strict computational constraints. By replacing full covariance estimation with a diagonal approximation, STIM-D reduces perpixel computational complexity from O(B3) to O(B), where B denotes the number of spectral bands, resulting in an average 31× speedup. Experimental validation on five AVIRIS Cuprite scenes and one cross-scene agricultural dataset demonstrates stable performance across diverse imaging conditions, with 95th percentile (Q95) anomaly scores reaching 0.931, indicating reliable discrimination of high-confidence anomalies for thresholdbased operational systems. STIM-D requires no training data and maintains deterministic behavior, making it well suited for deployment on resource-constrained platforms such as satellite onboard processors and UAV-based environmental monitoring systems.

title: Application of Spatio-Spectral Fusion and Sparse Unmixing for Alteration Mineral Mapping in the Eastern Tianshan, Xinjiang
authors: Zhixin Zhao, Ruyi Feng, Banxiao Ruan, Li Cao, Kaijun Yang, Lizhe Wang
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral remote sensing provides high spectral resolution for identifying alteration minerals, offering strong potential in geological prospecting. The Eastern Tianshan region of Xinjiang, with complex tectono-magmatic history and diverse alteration, presents abundant mixed pixels that complicate mineral mapping. To address this, we employed the Spectral-Spatial Weighted Sparse Regression for Hyperspectral Image Unmixing method (S<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> WSU) as the primary approach, with SUnSAL and SUnSAL-TV used for comparison. Experimental data included hyperspectral imagery from the GF-5B and ZY1-02E satellites, combined with a resampled spectral library. Abundance inversion was conducted on selected regions of interest, and the results were evaluated using RMSE, SRE, and spatial distribution of abundance maps. Results demonstrate that S<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> WSU achieves superior sparsity, mineral selectivity, and spatial consistency, providing more geologically meaningful results than the comparison methods and showing strong potential for alteration mineral mapping.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Payment Fraud Detection
authors: Amiya Ranjan Panda, Aayush Kumar Sah, Prajwal Mainali, Vidya Mohanty, Manoj Kumar Mishra, Niranjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Payment fraud is one of the most persistent challenges faced by financial institutions, posing serious risks to both businesses and consumers. Accurate and timely detection of fraudulent transactions is therefore critical to maintaining trust and security in digital payment systems. This study presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of fifteen supervised machine learning models for payment fraud detection. The evaluated models include Random Forest, Logistic Regression, XGBoost, Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, Extra Trees Classifier, Passive Aggressive Classifier, Linear Discriminant Analysis, Quadratic Discriminant Analysis, Bagging Classifier, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and a Voting Classifier. Model performance was assessed using multiple evaluation metrics, including Accuracy, Precision, F1 Score, and Recall. Experimental results highlight XGBoost as the bestperforming model, achieving an accuracy of 95.13%, followed closely by Gradient Boosting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(95.06 \%)$</tex>, Random Forest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(95.06 \%)$</tex>, and KNN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(95.03 \%)$</tex>. The findings suggest that ensemble-based boosting techniques provide superior predictive capability for detecting fraudulent payment transactions, while KNN also shows competitive performance. These insights can guide the selection of effective models for real-world fraud detection systems, enhancing the reliability and security of financial transactions.

title: Evaluating Attention as Explanation in Transformer-Based Sentiment Analysis using Counterfactual and Stability Metrics
authors: Nemali Ashrith Reddy, Kiran P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Currently, state-of-the-art results in sentiment analysis are achieved with transformer-based language models. These models are used in critical decision-making processes which come into contact with users, such as in financial services, content moderation and feedback analysis. These models are often assumed to be interpretable due to their attention mechanisms, with the aid of attention heatmaps to explain the model's decision making process. In fact recent studies have revealed that attention weights are only loosely related to a model's decision process and also turn out to be highly sensitive to noise. Consequently, the usefulness of these weights as a model explanation is in question. In this paper we take a step forward than qualitative approaches to visualization and report on a systematic assessment of how attention-based explanations could be used in Transformer-based models for sentiment analysis. Two measures, the attention prediction consistency (APC) and the explanation stability score (ESS), are introduced to quantify the causal effects and robustness of attention weights with input perturbations and token masking. To explore the sensitivity of attention to variations in representation, we employ counterfactual editing of the input text. This process involves determining whether attention is still able to track the correct part of the text after the changes. In experiments applying pre-trained BERT models to SST-2 and IMDB sentiment analysis tasks, we found that model attention often highlights the words that give away the sentiment but fail to consistently highlight words where the model's predictions change and can become unstable even when the sentiment labels do not change. These findings expose limitations of attention-as explanation and motivate more rigorous evaluation frameworks for explainable natural language processing.

title: A Sparse Autoencoder with Channel Attention Network for Fast and Flexible Image Denoising Model
authors: M. Sreedhar, Aparna Kukunuri
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Digital imaging plays a crucial role in many aspects of our daily life, including satellite television, intelligent traffic monitoring, and signature authentication. They are also extensively used in scientific and technological domains, such as geographic information systems and astronomy. Ensuring the removal of noise to restore high-quality images is crucial for advanced processing tasks such as object segmentation, detection, and tracking. To effectively eliminate noise without unnecessarily blurring critical details, denoising methods need to be spatially adaptive. However, image denoising poses considerable challenges due to various noise types encountered, including Gaussian, impulse, salt-and-pepper, and speckle noise, which complicate the restoration process. Therefore, an intellectual image-denoising technique is developed to maintain the images in high quality without noise. The images needed for denoising are acquired from standard databases. A Sparse Autoencoder with Channel Attention (SA-CA) mechanism is developed to remove the noise in the collected images. The texture and edges of the images are preserved efficiently using the proposed model. The performance of the implemented image denoising model is validated with other conventional techniques to ensure its robustness in noise removal.

title: An Application of Deep Q-Learning for Real-Time Decision Making in Tourism: Enhancing Resource Allocation and Visitor Satisfaction
authors: Sruthi A, Dhanya Pramod
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Optimizing resource allocation is essential for enhancing visitor satisfaction and ensuring efficient tourism management. This research presents a Deep Q-Learning-based decision-support system, incorporating Dueling Double DQN and Prioritized Experience Replay (PER) to dynamically adjust resource distribution based on visitor density, environmental conditions, and seasonal fluctuations. The model is trained in a simulated environment and deployed through an interactive dashboard, providing real-time issue detection and actionable recommendations to tourism managers. Performance analysis shows progressive reward improvements, Q-value convergence, and effective policy learning, demonstrating the model’s ability to optimize decision-making under varying conditions. By integrating reinforcement learning into tourism operations, this study advances AI-driven adaptive management, with future potential in multi-agent collaboration, IoT-based real-time monitoring, and predictive analytics for more sustainable and responsive tourism ecosystems.

title: Detection of Abusive Language in Social Media
authors: B Chaitanya Krishna, CH Poorna Chandra Rao, Rajashree, M S Roobini, Jemshia Miriam, Nafees Muneera
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The detection of harmful language from social media platforms has become a topical problem, because the increase in toxic data not only puts individual security at risk, but also at risk of destroying the credibility of online communities. In this paper a machine learning-based strategy for the identification of abusive language from social media posts is proposed, using Naive Bayes classifiers (Multinomial, Bernoulli, and Gaussian) and Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) as a feature extraction strategy. A corpus of social media text posts featuring abusive and non-abusive content was obtained, preprocessed and converted to numerical representations for the purpose of classification. Evaluation was done for each model using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The results show that the Multinomial Naive Bayes classifier is the most effective classifier among others in terms of accuracy in abusive language classification. The system is implemented in a Flask-based web interface to allow live abusive content identification. This thesis proposes an efficient and scalable solution to automated content moderation with applications in online safety promotion and the facilitation of healthy digital content interactions.

title: AI-Powered Prediction of Drought in Agricultural Areas
authors: Mohammed Khader
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Droughts have a deep impact on agricultural productivity, food security, and local economies, thus, precise and on-time drought prediction has a great importance for farming areas. This paper proposes a machine-learning-based AI solution to predict drought occurrences through the conduit of the processed climatic, hydrological, and soil data which are collected in various ways. The experimental model is incorporating the historical data of rainfall patterns, temperature fluctuations, soil moisture levels, and others into the predictions with the highest possible probability of drought conditions. Through the engineering of features as well as enhancing the model, this study defines the most important factors that lead to drought tolerance and the late generation of proactive alerts and usable insights for stakeholders, including farmers, policymakers, and water resource managers. The results showed that artificial intelligence holds the possibility for the augmentation of the resilience of agriculture and the education of the relevant sectors about drought dynamics policies, leading ultimately to the sustainable water management and food supply chain in the vulnerable areas of drought.

title: Customer Segmentation Using Machine Learning
authors: Tamadher Aldabbagh, Radwa Radwan, Lamis Riyad Mohammed Al Bdour, J. Shanmugan, Raed A. Said, Jamal Joudeh, Kahled Masoud
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The segmentation of customers into smaller groups is one of the most important methods for organizations that want to get the most out of personalized marketing, provide the best customer service, and use resources in the most efficient way. This research is about how machine learning techniques can be used to enhance the segmentation of customers. With the help of unsupervised learning methods such as K-Means, DBSCAN, and Hierarchical Clustering, the paper studies customers according to the analysis of demographic data, behavior, and transactions. Implementing dimensionality reduction techniques like PCA, the study identifies groups of customers with different preferences and behaviors. Additionally, the paper looks at how supervised learning algorithms can confirm segmentation through predictive analytics. The research uses real datasets to evaluate the efficacy of various clustering approaches through data such as silhouette scores and Davies-Bouldin index. Furthermore, it underlines how insights gained from segmentation can shape marketing strategies and boost customer retention. The results of the research illustrate how machine learning can turn customer information into actionable insights, which in turn can help businesses achieve a competitive edge.

title: Flood Prediction and Prevention Using AI in Urban Areas
authors: Hanady Almahmood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Urban flooding presents a critical challenge for cities, affecting infrastructure, economy, and public safety. Traditional flood prediction models often rely on historical data and lack the adaptability needed to respond to rapid urbanization and climate variability. This paper explores the application of artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance flood prediction accuracy and enable proactive prevention strategies in urban environments. By integrating real-time data from diverse sources, including weather stations, satellite imagery, and IoT sensors, AI algorithms can dynamically predict flood risks with heightened precision. Using machine learning models, such as neural networks and decision trees, combined with data preprocessing techniques, this study aims to develop a predictive framework that identifies high-risk areas and informs early warning systems. Furthermore, we evaluate the potential of AI-driven models to optimize resource allocation, enabling city officials to implement timely preventive measures. Our findings suggest that AI-based flood prediction systems offer significant advancements over traditional models, promising a more resilient and responsive urban infrastructure. This study contributes to the growing field of smart city applications, presenting a scalable AI-driven approach to flood prediction and prevention that can be adapted to various urban contexts globally.

title: From Binary to Multi-Class: Benchmarking AutoGluon Against Classical Machine Learning for Diabetes Prediction and the Rise of Agentic Clinical AI
authors: Mohamad Abou Ali, Fadi Dornaika, Ignacio Arganda-Carreras, Hussein Ali, Abdallah Kassem
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: The integration of automated machine learning (AutoML) into clinical workflows promises to democratize advanced predictive analytics, yet rigorous benchmarks against state-of-the-art (SOTA) classical machine learning pipelines remain limited. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of AutoGluon against classically engineered ML approaches meticulously engineered for the prediction of diabetes risk, a global health priority that affects more than 537 million adults around the world. We conduct two complementary experiments: (1) binary diabetes classification on a 100,000-sample data set, and (2) a novel three-class risk stratification framework (Normal, Pre-Diabetes, Diabetes) aligned with American Diabetes Association (ADA) 2025 guidelines, establishing a new benchmark for pre-diabetes detection. Results demonstrate that AutoML achieves performance parity with classical SOTA models (AUC: 0.9790 vs. 0.9791) while reducing the development effort by 93% and substantially lowering the requirements of expert intervention. A discordance analysis reveals that rare model disagreements are concentrated in clinically ambiguous borderline cases, reflecting areas of inherent diagnostic uncertainty rather than model failure. Beyond benchmarking, we conceptualize AutoML as the computational core of Agentic Clinical AI Systems—autonomous, adaptive architectures capable of continuous learning, explainability, and real-world clinical deployment. Collectively, these findings support AutoML-powered agentic systems as a scalable and practical pathway for high-quality diabetes prediction across diverse healthcare settings.

title: AI-Driven Traffic Accident Prediction: Integrating Real-Time Weather, Analytics, and Machine Learning for Enhanced Road Safety
authors: J. Jeyabharathi, K.J. Lakshmi Prasad, K. Karunakar, K. Sai Charan, R. Sasi Preetham
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Accidents on the road continue to cause injuries and deaths globally, which represents one of the key factors affecting the safety of the population and city management. Conventional methods of accident prevention may be based on past experiences or even the passive measurement of risks and not on the dynamic environments. The study proposes a traffic accident forecasting system based on an artificial intelligence approach to predict traffic accident-prone areas using input data on real-time weather condition, traffic jam, and historical data on accidents to make actionable recommendations to promote road safety. The system employs the use of sophisticated machine learning models to combine characteristics of the environment with infrastructure factors to achieve probabilistic accident predictions with an associated confidence score. Weather conditions such as temperature, precipitation, visibility and wind resistance are accessed from the Open-Meteo API as real-time, and mapping functions and geolocation services come through the OpenStreetMap and geocoding services, respectively. Data analytics and visualization such as heatmaps and time-temporal trend analysis provide complete data information that allows stakeholders to identify spatial and time trends in the occurrence of accidents. Dynamic risk notifications and other informed strategies of traffic management are supported by the predictive model and thus can decrease the likelihood of accidents and enhance response planning.

title: Smart Building Temperature Optimization Using AI
authors: Amro Dabash, Alaa Yahya Aref Ahmad, Fatima M. Alhaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the ever-growing cities which are becoming hotter and hotter as well as global warming which also is on the increase, optimizing the control of temperature in the intelligent buildings is now one of the most critical issues involved in energy efficiency and comfort of the living space. The common heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems consume a huge amount of energy and inefficiently operate, and thus, lead to greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. This research investigates the possibilities of artificial intelligence (AI) techniques, especially machine learning algorithms, to achieve optimal temperature settings in smart structures. The integration of historical and real-time sensor data into AI models can, in fact, be the algorithm of predicting and dynamically adjusting indoor temperature conditions based on occupancy patterns, weather forecasts, and user preferences. The federated learning platform has been used in our approach to improve data privacy and the ability to scale across the different buildings without the centralization of data. Experimental results show that the power of this AI model has the capacity to cut down energy consumption by up to 20%, maintaining the level of indoor comfort at the same time proving itself worthy of wide acceptance. This research contributes to enabling the sustainable building management systems, consistent with global energy-saving and carbon-reduction objectives.

title: Integrating AI into Supplier Carbon Management Systems: Challenges, Drivers, and Strategic Implications
authors: Mohammad A.K Alsmairat, Noor Al-Ma'Aitah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This research examines the impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the context of supplier carbon management and evaluates the effectiveness of AI in promoting sustainability within supply chain operations. The enriched capabilities of AI support both exploitative improvements in operational efficiency and explorative innovations for strategic carbon reduction, thereby bridging the gap between traditional supplier management and modern sustainability practices. The findings underscore the dual role of AI as both a strategic driver for enhancing supplier engagement in sustainability and a catalyst for operational transformation in supply chain carbon management. This research contributes to the understanding of AI's role in supplier carbon management by offering empirical insights into adoption rates, challenges, and effectiveness. The study highlights the need for strategic investments in regulatory collaboration, AI-powered compliance, and technological infrastructure to address integration barriers and optimize sustainability outcomes within supply chains.

title: TERRA-TRON: An Adaptive All-Terrain Autonomous Robotic Sentinel for Disaster Response and Environmental Monitoring
authors: E. S. Praveen Kumar, Guhan P. L., Eunice E, B. Ananda Venkatesan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The increasing frequency and severity of natural disasters and industrial emergencies have made autonomous robotic platforms indispensable tools for frontline response operations. This work presents TERRA-TRON, an adaptive allterrain autonomous unmanned ground robot built for disaster management, environmental monitoring, and surveillance in hazardous conditions. Drawing inspiration from reconfigurable single actuation wave robot mechanisms, the robot uses a sinusoidal locomotion method that provides stable ground contact and reliable traction across gravel, grass and irregular rubble surfaces. Arduino UNO R4 WiFi microcontroller-based web dashboard is used for real-time environmental monitoring of factors such as temperature, humidity, pressure and hazardous gases. It also possesses IR sensor-based short-range obstacle detection, flame presence detection and PIR sensor-based motion detection. Arduino UNO R3 microcontroller along with HC05 module controls the multi-motor control system, which is capable of switching between five different motor control modes. The motor control modes are: Manual mode, obstacle avoidance mode, line following mode, line following + obstacle avoidance hybrid mode and gas detection + obstacle avoidance hybrid mode. Based on the need, the mode can be changed via a Bluetooth control app.

title: Contract Theory-Driven Incentive Mechanism and Client Selection for Asynchronous Federated Learning
authors: Danni Yang, Yun Ji, Zhoubin Kou, Sheng Zhang, Xiaoxiong Zhong, Fangming Liu, Jiangang Sh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Despite advantages of federated learning in distributed data processing, it still faces significant challenges related to data and system heterogeneity, client engagement willingness, and security against malicious attack. To address these issues concurrently, various incentive and client selection mechanisms have been implemented. However, existing incentive mechanisms, which are typically designed for traditional synchronous aggregation, still suffer from significant straggler issues. This study introduces a novel asynchronous federated learning framework that integrates contract theory-driven incentive mechanism and client selection mechanism to address these challenges. In incentive mechanism, we classify clients based on data quality and customize contracts accordingly. By considering factors such as time delay and test accuracy, we adaptively adjust the local model training epochs of clients to maximize utility of task publisher, thereby encouraging high-quality clients to accelerate their training processes. For client selection mechanism, we take into account model staleness, model quality, and client level within asynchronous aggregation context, which allows us to select reliable clients and enhance security of FL. Extensive experiments demonstrate effectiveness of our framework in stimulating and selecting clients within asynchronous FL settings, significantly improving both global accuracy and training time.

title: Privacy-Preserving Identity Management System Leveraging Blockchain Technology
authors: Atul Kumar, Bhisham Sharma, Rajesh Kumar Kaushal, Imed Ben Dhaou
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The security, privacy and trust constraints of the traditional centralized architectures have led to a point of critical concern in digital identity management within the contemporary distributed systems. Central authorities create single points of failure, increasing the risk of full-scale data breaches and limiting users' ability to manage personal information. The paper presents a privacy-sensitive, blockchain-based identity management system that provides a means of achieving decentralized, secure, and user-centric digital identities. The system is deployed on a privately managed Ethereum blockchain using a Proof-of-Authority consensus mechanism to ensure low latency, transaction finality within seconds, and energy efficiency. Identity registration, verification, controlled updates, and revocation are automated via smart contracts, and data integrity and confidentiality are ensured by cryptographic hashing and public-private key pairs. The mechanism of a decentralized voter group is presented, which regulates the identity updates and revocations, increasing the level of transparency and trust without the assistance of a central authority. As it has been proven in experiments, the offered system can register identities in 3-4 seconds, propagate revocation in less than 5 seconds, and use a reasonable amount of gas, successfully deterring unauthorized read-only and writeonly operations and typical blockchain-based attacks. The findings prove that the proposed framework will provide a better security, scalability, and auditability beyond a traditional centralized identity system. The model of identity management based on blockchain offers a viable base of secure digital identity solutions in the fields of e-governance, finance, healthcare, and inter-organizational authentication.

title: Dynamic Gameplay Through Real-Time Emotion Detection
authors: Umamaheswari M, Sujhijaa R. G, Kaviya M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Emotion-aware interaction is emerging as a key dimension in adaptive digital systems, yet its integration into lightweight, web-based gaming environments remains underexplored. This paper presents an Emotion-Based Web Game System, a prototype framework that demonstrates realtime affect-driven gameplay adaptation using simulated emotional inputs. The backend is developed using Flask, which hosts a multithreaded emotion simulator capable of generating dynamic affective states at regular intervals. These states are processed through a configurable emotion-to-impact mapping model that adjusts core gameplay parameters including opponent speed, event spawn rate, and visual design elements thereby enabling a responsive and personalized user experience. A RESTful API facilitates continuous communication with the browser-based frontend, ensuring low-latency updates and consistent cross-platform compatibility. Although the current implementation relies on simulated data, the architecture is designed for seamless integration with real emotion-recognition technologies such as OpenCV and TensorFlow, supporting future work in computer-vision-based affect detection. The proposed system demonstrates the feasibility of embedding affective adaptability into web games, highlighting applications in entertainment, serious gaming, personalized learning, and affective computing research.

title: Dr. Kendra Siler: Bringing Together Technology, Processes, and People [Women to Watch]
authors: Katianne Williams
publish: IEEE Women in Engineering Magazine

title: An Enhanced Diagnosis of Pneumonia Detection Through Modified CNN With M-Relu for Medical Image Diagonsis
authors: Ashatai Vishwanath Wankar, Suvarna Bhat
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Pneumonia is a substantial global health challenge, greatly impacting morbidity and mortality across all age groups. The objective of this research work is to analyze an in-depth examination of pneumonia, including its pathophysiology, diagnostics, epidemiology, and treatment methodologies using a modified CNN approach. In this research work, we examine epidemiological characteristics, including incidence, prevalence, and risk factors, using machine learning and deep learning. The proposed method is implemented using the Google Colab based Python framework. The proposed method trains with the 70% data set and uses the remaining data for testing as well as validation. The proposed method shows better results as compared to other previous methods in terms of accuracy (91%) as compared to other models such as the CNN, VGG, and ResNet models.

title: Deep Learning Classification of Ablated Atrial Tissue in Multi-Excitation Hyperspectral Images
authors: Arpi Hunanyan, Nazeli Ter-Petrosyan, Fernando Villarruel, Tigran Soghomonyan, Narine Sarvazyan, Aram Butavyan, Varduhi Yeghiazaryan
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Multi-excitation hyperspectral imaging (ME-HSI) is an emerging modality that captures rich spectral information for each spatial pixel over a range of excitation wavelengths. To compare ME-HSI to traditional hyperspectral imaging (HSI), we employ five deep learning approaches for pixel-level classification of radiofrequency-ablated bovine left atrial tissue samples. The dataset comprises eight ME-HSI samples, with four excitation wavelengths. We consider three data setups: (1) individual HSI cubes for each excitation wavelength, (2) concatenation of all HSI cubes, and (3) majority voting across predictions for individual HSI cubes. For training, we use randomly selected circular regions, gradually increasing the number of regions to observe model performance dynamics. The two ME-HSI setups that use data from all four excitations show a small overall improvement in performance over the individual HSI cubes. The five-click setup (2% training data) generates the most stable results in all models. GiGCN outperforms other models in overall accuracy.

title: Rapid and Accurate Prediction of Micro Activity Test Values in Fresh FCC Catalysts Using Hyperspectral Imaging and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: C. Santos, N. Castillo, D. Huerta, D. Moreno, M. Hernandez
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: This study demonstrates the effectiveness of hyperspectral imaging (HSI) combined with advanced feature extraction and machine learning (ML) methods for predicting the catalytic Micro Activity Test (MAT) values of fresh Fluid Catalytic Cracking (FCC) catalysts. Using a push-broom near-infrared (NIR) hyperspectral system, spectral hypercubes of fresh FCC catalyst samples were acquired, generating extensive datasets from which advanced spectral and statistical features were engineered. Two ML approaches—the Standard and the Advanced Features Models—were trained and validated via cross-validation procedures. The Advanced Features Model, employing sophisticated signal processing and feature extraction techniques, achieved notably lower prediction errors (average 0.4 %, maximum 2.4 %) compared to conventional spectral analyses (average 4.5 %, maximum 10 %). Results confirm the feasibility and precision of HSI combined with advanced Feature Engineering and ML methods, closely matching the accuracy of traditional analytical MAT measurements and suggesting substantial potential for rapid and automated catalyst characterization.

title: Real-Time Crime Detection and Suspicious Activity Classification in Public Spaces Using Deep Learning and Enhanced Surveillance Technology
authors: Dinesh J, Sornalakshmi K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Conventional surveillance can typically fail to offer real-time context-sensitive monitoring of human activities, particularly when there is no shared view, as the views of the cameras. This is a weakness that negatively affects their performance in the risky and volatile social situations. In order to counter these issues, we introduce an all-purpose (real-time) surveillance system, which is a combination of person detecting (YOLOv8), intra-camera tracking (DeepSORT), behavior detection (Vision Transformer), inter-camera person re-identification (cosine similarity), and the camera-origin prediction (CNN). This is in contrast to traditional modular solutions which will not provide end-to-end identity and behavior tracking and have a spatial context awareness. Large-scale analysis of various surveillance data shows that the system is very accurate in behavior categorization (88%), camera origin prediction (90% or higher) and also considerable identity continuity across camera streams. The solution proposed has an immense effect on the situational awareness because it allows real-time threat detection, incident localization, and the response to the threat in complex settings.

title: Clean Speak: AI-Driven Toxic Comment Filter
authors: P. M. Naidu, M. Harika, Abdullah Taha, P. John
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Toxic comments classification is a project aimed at identifying and filtering harmful, abusive, or offensive comments on online platforms to ensure a safe and positive digital environment. By using natural language processing (NLP), the system detects toxic behaviours such as hate speech, threats, and bullying in user-generated content. It employs a Multinomial Naive Bayes classifier trained on a labelled dataset of harmful and nontoxic comments, combined with a bilingual toxic word list (English and Telugu), enabling the detection of both explicit and implicit toxicity. The project classifies comments into categories like toxic, severe, obscene, or neutral, facilitating efficient content moderation. Preprocessing techniques such as tokenization, stop word removal, and feature extraction with Count Vectorizer ensure accurate classification. To enforce real-time moderation, the system integrates the Twilio SMS API, sending warnings or notifications to users posting toxic content, promoting adherence to community guidelines. With its modular design, the project supports scalability, multilingual capabilities, and real-time performance, providing an effective solution for handling and inclusive online communications while providing a foundation for future improvements like deep learning models and adaptive learning mechanisms.

title: Density-Driven CFAR Detection for Maritime Surveillance Using Spaceborne Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Salvatore Maresca, Stefania Matteoli
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: This paper presents a methodology for detecting vessels in spaceborne hyperspectral imagery that exploits a constant false alarm rate (CFAR) approach based on pixel density evaluation. The detection statistics is formulated from the reciprocal of the pixel density, which is non-parametrically estimated from image intensity values across various spectral bands. Pixel-wise CFAR-based detection is carried out and the detection threshold is adaptively determined based on local background statistics. To consolidate individual detections into distinct vessel candidates, the Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) algorithm is employed. The proposed approach is tested on hyperspectral images acquired by both PRISMA and EnMAP sensors, focusing on two maritime regions: the Venice lagoon in Italy and the Rhône river mouth near Arles, France. Detection performance is assessed using ground truth derived from Automatic Identification System (AIS) messages. Experimental results highlight the potential of spaceborne hyperspectral sensors for maritime surveillance applications and support increasing exploitation of such data.

title: Embedded ML-Based Shopping Cart Leveraging IoT for Enhanced Shopping Experience
authors: Varnika K V, Venkata Rahul Ramisetty, Madumitha R, Sita Devi Bhartula, B. Naresh Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This paper demonstrates an autonomous shopping cart integrating IoT and machine learning algorithms. The autonomous shopping cart helps the store operators to reduce manual labor and lower operational costs.This project mainly aims to integrate three main objectives: finding the shortest path in the store based on a predefined layout, recommending items to the user based on their shopping history, and suggesting additional items to help maximize discounts. To achieve these goals, we use various machine learning algorithms, including Dijkstra's algorithm, used to compute the shortest and most efficient path between selected locations inside the store, Thompson sampling, to get personalized item recommendations by learning user preferences over time and Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM), to localize the cart and adapt to the movement in the cart.The Raspberry Pi acts as the central processing unit, handling sensor data, path planning, and recommendation algorithms. When activated, the cart autonomously navigates in the direction the user wants to go and also displays the shortest path when the user gives inputs for the starting and ending points.Overall, the autonomous cart is used to provide a personalized shopping experience by making the cart userfriendly.

title: A Leakage-Safe Multi-Task Learning Framework for Joint Brain Tumor Classification and Segmentation with External Validation
authors: Ahmad Al Hassan, Marwan Alhawat, Mohamad Kanaan, Lyn Abdelkader, Ahmad Diab
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Multi-task learning approaches that jointly perform brain tumor segmentation and classification have gained increasing attention due to their potential to exploit shared anatomical representations. However, many existing studies report near-saturated performance without sufficiently addressing data leakage, task coupling, or limited evaluation protocols, raising concerns about generalizability. In this work, we present a robust and leakage-safe multi-task deep learning framework for joint brain tumor segmentation and classification from MRI. This framework is designed to operate on heterogeneous datasets with partial annotations and enforces strict separation between training, validation, and external testing data. During inference, the segmentation and classification tasks are explicitly decoupled to ensure unbiased evaluation. We conduct comprehensive experiments across two independently curated public datasets with differing characteristics. Classification performance achieves 94.2% accuracy on the internal test set (Dataset A) and 99.1% accuracy on the external test set (Dataset B). Segmentation performance on the external test set (Dataset B) achieves a mean Dice coefficient of 0.866 and an IoU of 0.795. These results demonstrate strong and consistent performance across both tasks, with stable generalization to unseen external data. Our study highlights the importance of rigorous experimental design and evaluation practices when assessing multi-task learning models in medical imaging.

title: A Comparative Deep-Learning Approach for Detecting Fake Faces in Real-World Datasets
authors: N Mithili Devi, V Asha, Diksha Dhiman, Lavanya K L, Madhu GN, Priya Thomas
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The advancement of generative models such as GANs and diffusion networks has made it difficult to distinguish between real and fake facial images, raising serious concerns related to misinformation, identity fraud and digital security. This study presents a comparative analysis of three advanced deep learning models ResNet50 with CBAM, EfficientNetV2 with SAM and Swin Transformer for deepfake detection using the RVF10K Real vs. Fake Faces dataset. The dataset consists of 10, 000 images and is evaluated across four training splits (25%, 50%, 75%, and 100%) under identical training conditions. The results demonstrate that all models achieve strong performance, exceeding 85% accuracy even with limited training data. Among them, EfficientNetV2 with SAM consistently delivers the best performance, achieving a maximum accuracy of 99.80% and F1-score of 0.9973, indicating high robustness and stability. The Swin Transformer also shows competitive performance by capturing global patterns, while ResNet50 with CBAM maintains consistent results with lower computational cost. The training time analysis highlights the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency across models. This study provides a systematic comparison of convolutional, optimization-enhanced and transformer-based approaches. The findings suggest that attention mechanisms and advanced optimization strategies significantly improve deepfake detection performance, making EfficientNetV2 with SAM a strong candidate for real-world deployment..

title: Smart City Energy Consumption Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Ghassan Issa, Channaganu Paramaiah, Khalid Almaazmi, Ayman AbuBaker
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The swift urbanization of cities and the application of smart technologies has led to the emergence of unprecedented energy consumption difficulties. Energy consumption prediction in smart cities precisely needs to be done for better resource allocation, lower environmental impact, and the establishment of sustainable urban development. The present study is conducted on the application of machine learning (ML) techniques in the forecasting of energy consumption in smart city contexts. We construct predictive models based on real-time data captured through IoT devices, environmental conditions, and historical patterns of energy usage to know the energy demand. Different ML algorithms regression models, neural networks, and ensemble methods are considered for measuring their accuracy and robustness. In addition, improvement data processing methods are used to detect significant factors that influence energy consumption. The findings show that ML can be used to achieve incredible prediction accuracy which in turn, will help run the energy management strategies proactively. The results of this study will help in the development of intelligent energy systems by providing useful knowledge to the decision-makers and urban planners to develop the energy-efficient smart cities.

title: Sustainable Medical Diagnostics: A Green Edge Framework for Energy-Efficient Intelligence
authors: Tallha Akram
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The incorporation of deep learning has triggered a paradigm shift in the accuracy of diagnosing, but the existing system based on centralized cloud models has introduced a bottleneck due to its prohibitive energy demands, sluggish processing, and large consumption of bandwidth. Such overheads are especially troublesome when it comes to the environment of mobile healthcare, where constrained resources require more lean implementation. These inefficiencies are tackled by this study through a Green AI-enabled edge framework that is directly optimized on skin lesion classification to achieve the conflicting interests of diagnostic accuracy and physical resource limitation. The architecture proposed in this paper focuses on a multi-model repository managed by a constraint-aware Green Decision Engine which is proposed as an intelligence layer to dynamically choose the best neural network architecture to run on local executions due to real-time power budgets and hardware constraints. In order to test the validity of this strategy, the trade-offs between computational accuracy and energy consumption inherent in each model were put to test in a range of model complexities. Experimental evidence suggests that this adaptation strategy is much more successful than conventional high-capacity deployments achieving 92.2 percent classification accuracy and a 47.5 percent energy usage reduction and 48.1 percent inference latency reduction at the same time. Most prominently, the framework transferred less data by about 99.5 times as much data to the cloud, because most of the actual calculations were made locally (and only light metadata was sent directly). These results support the claim that adaptive edge intelligence has become an essential requirement towards the deployment of medical AI in real-world clinical contexts in sustainable, low-latency, and communication-efficient systems.

title: Multiclass Emotion Classification using xLSTM Learning of Optimized Features from EEG Signal
authors: Md. Husain Hamid Chowdhury Ruddro, A. B. M. Aowlad Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Emotion recognition has great importance for communication between people as well as interactions between human and machine towards various applications. Brain signal EEG is a significant marker of emotion, especially for people unfit to communicate or express their emotions through posture or gesture. This paper presents a 3-class emotion (Positive, Neutral and Negative) classification framework emphasizing on broad range of optimized features and utilizing the long-term dependency benefit of extended LSTM (xLSTM) learning. From a set of 2132×2549 features extracted from EEG signals in the publicly available EEG Brainwave Dataset, an optimized 2132×200 feature set was selected using the Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR) method. An xLSTM based network has been designed focusing on the memory module mLSTM and the sLSTM module to address the storing capacity issue of LSTM. The exponential gating and innovative memory mixing structure of this model enhances its performance compared to the traditional LSTM model in terms of long-term dependencies. The obtained results demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms conventional LSTM and related works exhibiting of 99.06% of accuracy where the LSTM shows an accuracy of 97.17% on test data. The outcomes of this study might be useful in emotion related applications for human machine interaction, reminiscence therapy, robotics etc.

title: Machine Learning Based Prediction of Energy Harvested from Human Motion Using Piezoelectric Materials
authors: T. Ashok Kumar, J. Nagarjuna Reddy, E. Narasimhulu, T. Akbar Basha, N. Sameera, G. Kejiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The objective of this paper is predicting the production of electrical power from human movements by the utilization of piezoelectric materials. The piezoelectric sensor creates electricity by applying mechanical pressure or vibration to the sensor along with movements like walking and running. The machine converts the mechanical energy present in the motion to electrical energy, which can be tapped and utilized to power small electronic devices such as LEDs, sensors, and wireless modules. Besides the power production function, machine learning is applied at a rudimentary level to investigate human movements and pressure. Based on the information, the power production can be estimated and the efficiencies enhanced. The integration of rudimentary machine learning and piezoelectric power harvesting is therefore a clean, green, and feasible means for tapping power from day-to-day human activities.

title: Sobel Edge Detection Framework Based Hardware–Software Co-Design FPGA Implementation for Brain MRI Image Analysis
authors: Nagabushanam Perattur, Nara Kavya, Radha Subramanyam, Mondi Vinod Kumar, Saravanan S, J. Sunitha Kumari
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Image enhancements play major role in digital processing algorithms where classifying targets, features are the steps. Software implementation helps in attaining it, however by FPGA implementation reduces the execution time in the process dealing with images and quality. Edge detection is one such process which uses convolution operation and when deals with FPGA it further operates 50 % faster using parallel, pipelined architectures internally. In this work, we used sobel edge detection algorithm on brain images to detect the edges properly for dealing with brain tumor classification. Visibility increases, abnormality detected more easily with higher speed in the process. Power analysis, speed and parallelism are analyzed with CPU architecture and FPGA implementation.

title: Beyond the Fairness–Accuracy Tradeoff: Genetic Algorithm-Driven Sample Weighting for Age-Based Fairness
authors: Pradeep Singh, Bikesh Kumar Singh, Rajendra Kumar Pandey, Aman Kumar Sah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Models of machine learning for cardiovascular risks prediction may display algorithmic biases across demographic groups, especially age differences, violating equity principles percent differences between age groups. This study presents a optimizes prediction performance and fairness metrics using a protected attribute 303 patients suffering from cardiac disease aged 60 years or older from the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset. The proposed approach minimizes a composite fitness function using genetic algorithm incorporating accuracy loss, disparate impact deviation, average odds difference, and equal opportunity difference, achieving state of-the-art results with Decision Tree at disparate impact 1.10, equal opportunity difference 0.000, and accuracy 91.62 percent, representing the only method satisfying the industry-standard 80 percent rule for disparate impact between 0.8 and 1.2. This single-step optimization framework requires no synthetic data generation and converges within 30 generations, establishing new benchmarks for fairness-aware cardiovascular prediction. The Study demonstrates that evolutionary algorithms can achieve near-perfect algorithmic fairness with improved accuracy, addressing critical imperatives for equitable healthcare AI systems.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Classification and Regression Approaches for Postpartum Hemorrhage Prsediction
authors: Ajwad Mohtasim, Mashfique Mehzad, Sayma Alam Suha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Postpartum hemorrhage (PPH) constitutes a leading cause of maternal mortality globally, necessitating accurate predictive models for timely clinical intervention. Traditional risk assessment approaches face limitations when addressing the complexity of multiple interacting risk factors in maternal healthcare datasets. This study presents a comprehensive machine learning approach to classify PPH and Non-PPH cases as well as predict estimated blood loss during delivery using maternal and obstetric features. A retrospective analysis was performed on 223 maternal records from Mpilo Central Hospital, Zimbabwe , exhibiting extreme class imbalance (220 PPH vs. 3 non-PPH cases, 98.7%:1.3%). The study implemented a dual analytical framework: binary classification using Random Forest, XG-Boost, and Isolation Forest with SMOTE enhancement evaluated through 3-fold stratified cross-validation, and quantitative blood loss estimation using 13 regression algorithms with SMOGN balancing techniques. Comprehensive preprocessing included feature extraction from unstructured medical records, generating 95 engineered features across demographic, clinical, delivery, and neonatal domains.However, all classification approaches failed to detect minority class cases, highlighting fundamental limitations of current machine learning techniques under extreme class imbalance conditions. Conversely, regression-based blood loss estimation proved substantially more robust, with CatBoost achieved the best performance on the original dataset with an RMSE of 0.813, while LightGBM performed optimally on the balanced dataset with an RMSE of 0.961. These findings demonstrate that ensemble methods, particularly gradient boosting algorithms, are most effective in this scenario for quantitative blood loss prediction, while binary PPH classification remains challenging in severely imbalanced datasets.

title: Explainable Deepfake Video Detection Using Visual, Temporal, Physiological, and Audio-Visual Cues
authors: Anmol Bhatia, Ibrahim Mohamed S, Gokulsivaraj VP, Kalimuthu Krishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Deepfake videos pose severe threats to information integrity, personal privacy, and digital trust. Existing detection methods rely predominantly on single-modality analysis, making them vulnerable to adversarial manipulation. In this paper, we present a multi-cue deepfake detection framework that independently analyzes four complementary aspects of video authenticity: (1) visual spatial artifacts using EfficientNet- B4, (2) temporal motion inconsistencies using a CNN-BiLSTM encoder with self-attention, (3) physiological plausibility via remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) signal analysis, and (4) audio-visual lip synchronization using a dual-stream encoder. An attention-based fusion model adaptively weights the cue predictions, generating a final classification with a humaninterpretable confidence score on a 1−5 scale. We evaluate our approach on two benchmark datasets—Google DeepFake Detection (DFD) and FakeAVCeleb—and demonstrate that multi-cue fusion consistently outperforms individual cues. Under realistic video-level evaluation on 459 test videos, our fusion system achieves 95.4% accuracy and 0.997 AUC-ROC, improving upon the best individual cue (Temporal: 96.3% accuracy, 0.998 AUC). Cross-dataset evaluation on FaceForensics++ reveals generalization challenges, with the visual cue achieving 67.1% accuracy across manipulation types. We provide an honest analysis of cue contributions, noting that the rPPG cue performs near chance level (AUC = 0.522) and contributes marginally to fusion despite receiving 30.0% attention weight. Grad-CAM visualizations provide interpretable evidence of the spatial regions driving detection decisions.

title: MGAPep: LLM-Augmented Multimodal Graph Attention for Protein-Peptide Binding Site Prediction and Cross-Domain Transfer_supp1-3690953.pdf
authors: Xiangzheng Fu
publish: N/A
abstract: Protein-peptide interactions drive peptide therapeutics, precision design, and biomarker discovery, yet most predictors underuse complementary sequence-structure information. LLM-augmented multimodal approaches offer a promising solution to these limitations. We introduce MGAPep, which fuses pre-trained large language model embeddings with protein sequence and structural descriptors via a residual graph attention backbone and a multi-head dual-attention module to capture fine-grained interface patterns. Leveraging large-scale corpora of protein fragment-peptide interaction data, MGAPep employs self-supervised pre-training, transfer learning, and task-specific fine-tuning to obtain rich, transferable representations. Extensive benchmarking shows consistent state-of-the-art accuracy for protein-peptide binding site prediction, with robust generalization to unseen proteins and peptides. The framework also transfers effectively across modalities, yielding superior performance to most baselines on protein-nucleic acid binding site prediction without architecture changes, underscoring broad applicability. Together with evidence that graph-enhanced LLMs improve biomolecular binding modeling, these results establish MGAPep as a general paradigm for protein-biomolecule interaction prediction.

title: Modeling and Simulation of a Three-Phase Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverter with P&O MPPT
authors: Xuefeng Kong
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: A three-phase photovoltaic grid-connected inverter with maximum power point tracking (MPPT) is modeled and simulated in this paper. To improve the power extraction capability of the photovoltaic array under varying irradiance, a perturb and observe (P&O)-based MPPT strategy is adopted in the front-end Boost stage. On the grid side, a synchronous rotating dq-frame double-loop control scheme is employed to regulate the inverter current and ensure stable power injection. Based on the overall system structure, an integrated simulation model including the photovoltaic source, Boost converter, dc link, three-phase inverter, and grid-side control system is established in Simulink. Simulation results show that the proposed system can track the maximum power point effectively under irradiance variations, while maintaining correct sinusoidal three-phase voltage and current waveforms. In addition, the grid voltage and current of the same phase remain nearly in phase, indicating a high power factor. The active power is maintained around 100 kW and the reactive power remains close to zero. These results verify the effectiveness of the proposed control scheme for both maximum power extraction and high-quality grid connection.

title: Design and Analysis of an UWB Conformal Antenna for In-Body Capsule Endoscopy Technology
authors: Robins Chowdhury, Sheikh Md. Rabiul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: This paper presents an ultra-compact conformal microstrip patch antenna with a meandered loop configuration for wireless capsule endoscopy (WCE). The antenna is fabricated on a flexible, biocompatible Kapton substrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(170 \mu ~\mathrm{m}, \varepsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=3.5, \tan \delta=0.0027\right.)$</tex> with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> copper metallization, occupying only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.5 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex>. Its conformal design enables integration around the capsule's outer surface, preserving internal space for imaging and control circuitry. Electromagnetic simulations in muscle-equivalent tissue phantoms (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\varepsilon}_{\mathrm{r}} =52.7, \tan \delta=0.24)$</tex> confirm a wide operational bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1-4$</tex> GHz, covering MedRadio and ISM bands. At 1.5 GHz, the antenna achieves strong impedance matching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{S}_{11}=-17 ~\text{dB}, \text{VSWR}=1.35\right)$</tex> and a power transfer efficiency of 98%. The realized gain of 1.9 dBi and quasi-omnidirectional radiation pattern with a 140.2° beamwidth ensure stable communication regardless of capsule orientation in the gastrointestinal tract. The peak 10 g-averaged specific absorption rate is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0718 ~\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex>, representing 3.6 % of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 ~\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> safety limit and providing a 27.8:1 thermal safety margin. This design advances antenna technology for WCE and other implantable biomedical telemetry applications through extreme miniaturization, wideband operation, robust radiation characteristics, and high biological safety.

title: A Stochastic Model for Key Supply and Session Demand in Quantum Key Distribution Systems
authors: Hamid Taramit, Leduin José Cuenca Macas, Juan P. Brito, Vicente Martin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Key material in Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) systems is a scarce resource that must be buffered and served to multiple applications. This paper proposes a stochastic model for the joint dynamics of key material and application sessions in a QKD system. We model these dynamics as a ContinuousTime Markov Chain (CTMC) with a Quasi-Birth-Death (QBD) structure and derive its stationary distribution using a matrixgeometric method. We introduce several performance metrics for the QKD system, including both key-based and session-based metrics. Through numerical results, we investigate the impact of the main parameters governing key supply and session demand on the overall performance of the QKD system. Our findings reveal the existence of operating regions within which these parameters can be tuned to meet desired performance targets. The proposed model and performance metrics provide concrete guidelines for designing session control and key management strategies in practical deployments.

title: Microscopic Self-Healing Mechanism of the Cellulose/Polyurethane Composite System
authors: Jiefeng Liu, Jing Luo, Xianhao Fan, Chuying Liu, Wentao Li, Junhao Song
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Cellulose insulating paper is prone to the formation of microcracks in the early stages of aging when subjected to stresses such as heat, electricity, and mechanical vibration, which threatens the safe and stable operation of power transformers. Introducing microcapsules to endow the material with self-healing capabilities is an effective strategy to inhibit microcrack propagation and restore its mechanical properties.
