The proposed model directly processed raw nEMG signals to automatically extract volition signals (motor unit action potentials), eliminating the need for physician-dependent preprocessing. Results: The optimal segment length for volition detection was 0.060 s, and nEMGNet demonstrated the best overall performance. Model-extracted volition signals yielded higher segment-wise classification performance than raw nEMG signals, with area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) values of 0.806 for myopathy, 0.819 for neuropathy, and 0.819 for normal cases, compared with 0.761, 0.728, and 0.733, respectively. At the patient-wise level, AUROC improved by 20.10% for myopathy, 23.15% for neuropathy, and 23.15% for normal cases compared with physician-detected volition data. Conclusion: The proposed volition-detection model significantly enhances neuromuscular disease classification performance while enabling a fully automated and less labor-intensive diagnostic workflow.

title: Transformer-Based Modular Motion Generation Through Shoulder–Arm Decoupling for Upper Limb Rehabilitation
authors: Muhammad Fawad Khan, Naveed Ahmad Khan, Fahad Hussain, Prashant K. Jamwal, Shahid Hussain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: The shoulder girdle is one of the most complex components of the upper limb, and when coupled with the arm, modeling and prediction become even more challenging. To address this, we propose a modular motion-planning framework that explicitly decouples the shoulder girdle from the arm. This separation simplifies modeling, enhances interpretability, and improves adaptability for rehabilitation scenarios. By independently targeting scapular dynamics, the framework enables better generalization of motion prediction across subjects. For human-motion modeling, a Transformer-based deep learning architecture is employed to capture nonlinear dependencies between joint angles and scapular motion. The model accepts joint-specific features as input and predicts shoulder girdle configurations, which are then integrated with arm trajectories to reconstruct complete upper limb motion. In the exoskeleton-mapping stage, a machine learning framework translates predicted human motion into the configuration space of a 6- Degree of freedom (DOF) rehabilitation exoskeleton. This ensures that generated trajectories are physically realizable and clinically suitable. By isolating human-motion prediction from robot mapping, the framework remains modular, scalable, and resilient to subject-specific variability, making it ideal for personalized rehabilitation. The methodology was evaluated by comparing Transformer-based predictions with both experimental data, reinforcement learning models and long short-term memory across multiple rehabilitation tasks. Quantitative analyses included statistical measures (F1-Score, ANOVA, T-test) and kinematic error metrics (RMSE, DTW). Results demonstrated that the Transformer model achieved higher accuracy and better temporal alignment with experimental trajectories. Combining shoulder–arm separation with Transformer-based learning provides an effective and clinically relevant solution for generating human-like motion in upper limb rehabilitation.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Learning Rate Annealing and Domain-Decomposition Subnetworks for Solving 3D Maxwell’s Equations
authors: Haoran Qin, Tiancheng Zhang, Peili Ping, Huaguang Bao, Aiqaing Cheng, Zongguang Yu, Dazhi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: In this work, a novel approach based on physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) has been proposed for solving three-dimensional (3D) Maxwell's equations. The proposed method employs a modified fully-connected neural architecture integrated with transformer networks, which significantly enhances the capacity in minimizing approximation errors. A learning rate annealing strategy is adopted to dynamically adjust the penalty weights based on loss gradients, thereby balancing multiple objective terms and improving prediction accuracy. To handle multi-material domains, dedicated subnetworks are used along with enforced boundary conditions to ensure field continuity at interfaces. The accuracy and robustness of the proposed framework are validated through simulations of resonant cavities and waveguides, showing excellent agreement between predicted and analytical field solutions. The results show that the proposed approach reduces prediction errors compared to vanilla PINNs, while maintaining stable convergence behavior. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the method for solving 3D electromagnetic problems within Cartesian coordinates. The method uses linear dielectric materials and boundaries aligned with the coordinate axes. The framework provides a foundation for future extensions to arbitrary boundaries, multi-scale geometries, and nonlinear dielectric materials, thereby broadening its applicability in practical engineering design and analysis.

title: Flux-Controllable Design and Analysis of a Permanent Magnet Vernier Motor for Torque–Speed Performance Enhancement
authors: Deyang Fan, Linhao Qiu, Xiaoyong Zhu, Zixuan Xiang, Yixuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: To address the performance balance between high torque at low speeds and a wide flux-weakening speed range in permanent magnet vernier motors (PMVMs), this paper proposes a flux-controllable permanent magnet vernier (FC-PMV) motor. The contribution of this motor lies in the design of the air-gap flux density amplitude based on the flux-controllability module. Through the design of the flux-controllable module, the motor's torque output and speed regulation range have achieved comprehensive improvement. By trade-off optimization design of the flux-controllable module topology, the contradiction between improving torque and expanding the speed range is partially resolved. It is observed from the electromagnetic performance analysis that the FC-VPM motor exhibits a wide speed range and a high-torque capability at low speeds, which verifies the effectiveness of the motor design.

title: Towards Optimal User Cooperative Mobility for Mobile Ad hoc Networks with User-Specific Mobility Constraints
authors: Jihyeok Yang, Tutomu Murase
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper proposes a user mobility control method that explicitly considers heterogeneous user mobility constraints in ad hoc networks. In recent mobile communication systems, the shift towards higher frequencies has reduced coverage areas, making reliable communication increasingly difficult to maintain. Although ad hoc networks have attracted attention as a potential solution, simply forming them is insufficient to ensure adequate communication quality. To address this issue, several studies have proposed cooperative user mobility control methods, which improve communication performance by guiding users to geographically favorable positions to maximize throughput. However, these methods typically employ a spring-model-based heuristic, assuming that cooperative users can move to designated positions. In practice, users may have heterogeneous mobility constraints due to mobility costs, such as being unable to move or being able to move only within a limited distance. As a result, existing methods may fail to function effectively, leading to throughput degradation. To overcome this limitation, we propose a new spring model with an upper bound on movement distance and a heuristic method for finding user positions that maximize throughput while accounting for user mobility. The proposed method supports two operational modes: decentralized control, used when information on users’ mobility constraints is unavailable, and centralized control, used when such information is available. Numerical results show that the proposed method achieves greater throughput improvement over a previous method as the standard deviation of users’ mobility constraints increases, reaching about 40% under the evaluated scenarios. These results confirm that explicitly considering user mobility can further improve throughput.

title: Relative Range-Doppler Map for Multipath Ghost Suppression in FMCW Harmonic Radar
authors: Feng Zhao, Yuanzhe Li, Tao Yuan, Zhenhua Liang, Weidong Hu, Hongqi Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radar Systems
abstract: Harmonic radar is particularly valuable in clutter-rich environments owing to its excellent clutter rejection capability. However, in frequency-modulated continuous wave harmonic radar systems, multipath propagation caused by strong linear scatterers generates ghosts on the range-Doppler map (RDM), which significantly increases the false alarm rate. To address this issue, we first establish a signal propagation model that explicitly incorporates the intermodulation effect between multi-path signals. This model reveals that the true targets and ghosts can be categorized into four distinct types, exhibiting midpoint-symmetric relationships (MSRs) on the RDM. To leverage this property for ghost suppression, we propose a new range-Doppler domain termed the relative range-Doppler map, defined with reference to the specific range and velocity. The proposed signal model and ghost suppression algorithm are validated by simulation and experimental results. Compared with the comparison methods, the proposed algorithm demonstrates a significant advantage in ghost suppression effectiveness. Furthermore, this study can provide a feasible solution for multipath interference mitigation in other radar systems where targets show MSRs.

title: EEG Signal Analysis for Motor Imagery Tasks Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: AADHITYA S. V, S. Ishrath Sulthana, Karthikeyan. D, Sutharsan V, R. Lakshmi, Annamalai. G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This work examines the creation using a dryelectrode EEG setup developed for motor imagery signal classification, utilizing an array of deep learning techniques to control a wheelchair using brain-computer interface. While wet EEG devices are the industry standard for acquiring neural signals, they can pose discomfort and are not practical for longterm use. This study looks at the prospects of using dry EEG technology in which user comfort and practicality are critical aspects- the identified EEG signals corresponding to 4 motor imagery actions. As dry EEG signals can be weak, and prone to noise, the project investigated the development of discriminative features to improve classification accuracy for real-time wheelchair control. The primary classification method uses a CNN-based model and PCA is used to perform dimensionality and optimization of the feature extraction process. The framework demonstrated its ability to function as an effective BCI system because the model achieved 96.73 % accuracy with raw EEG signals and 98.86% accuracy with PCA-preprocessed signals.

title: HyGEO-MEC: A Transformer-GNN and Dueling DQN Framework for Energy-Aware Task Offloading in Edge Computing
authors: Bharani H, T.Manoranjitham
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Efficient energy utilization and real-time responsiveness are critical for the sustainable and scalable deployment of Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) systems. To address these challenges, this study proposes HyGEO-MEC, a novel deep learning–driven framework designed to minimize power consumption in dynamic MEC environments while satisfying latency constraints. The framework integrates three key components: a Transformer-enhanced Graph Neural Network (TE-GNN) for accurate spatio-temporal workload prediction, a BiLSTM-based module for energy profiling, and a Dueling Deep Q-Network (DQN) controller for intelligent task offloading and server activation decisions. Additionally, an energy-constrained task scheduler is implemented to ensure stable resource allocation under fluctuating network demands. Performance evaluation on real-world datasets, including the GCT and Alibaba cluster traces, demonstrates that HyGEO-MEC consistently outperforms baseline methods—ESR, ESP, GAA, and GAT—across multiple metrics. Notably, it achieves an average task delay of 0.18 seconds, a violation rate of 9%, and a high decision efficiency of 0.42 milliseconds. It also records the lowest workload prediction error (MSE = 0.033) and achieves an η(t) match rate of 84.27% with ESR, indicating strong alignment with near-optimal decisions. These results validate HyGEO-MEC as a robust, scalable, and intelligent energy optimization solution for next-generation MEC systems. Its ability to adapt seamlessly to varying workloads and network dynamics makes it well-suited for real-time mobile edge applications.

title: Reptile Meta Learning Based Beaconing and C2 Behaviour Detection in Network
authors: Graeson Joshua Elijah, Amrith Saras, Renu Mary Daniel, Diya Jothish
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: While covert Command-and-Control (C2)-communication is currently one of the toughest challenges in network security. Stealthy low-volume methods based on encryptions, which highly mimic benign traffic, are used in attacks. This article describes a full, to end, framework which mimics, records, interprets and categorizes C2 behavior by utilizing meta-learning. With a controlled virtual environment, a realistic reverse -shell attack was also performed, with a delivery made by the use of phishing in the transmission of a Meterpreter payload. The resultant packet stream level network traffic was converted into feature packets of flow at the feature vectors (flow temporal features and payload entropy features) and flow directionality and burstiness along with indicators of compromise (IoCs). It used these flow-level features to train a Reptile meta-learning model, thus, allowing it to quickly adapt to a variety of C2 patterns (or to a pattern that it has never seen before). The experimental analysis reveals that the accuracy (99.29-1) is high (99.29-1), its precision and F1-score are higher than the models which use conventional machine-learning algorithms significantly. In this way, the given system offers strong and scalable approach to the identification of the C2 activity in automatic mode using meta-learned behavioral features of network traffic.

title: Exploring the Effect of Leveraging Advances Technologies to Enhance Credit Scoring Process: (Case Study Based on Jordanian Commercial Banks)
authors: Abd Al-Salam Al-Hamad, Mohammad Alqsass, Abeer Al-Khoury, Ibrahim Ezmigna Azman Hashim, Jassim Algasawneh, Ghada Al-Rawashdeh, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The integration of AI and ML into the credit scoring process has enhanced the accuracy, speed, and efficiency of evaluating borrowers’ creditworthiness. This research explores the role of AI and ML in credit scoring within Jordanian commercial banks, focusing on the benefits these technologies offer to both financial institutions and their customers. The study highlights the disruptive potential of AI and ML in the evolution of credit scoring landscape, improving operational efficiency, and contributing to the overall stability of the banking sector. The sample, consisting of 13 commercial banks operating in Jordan, during year 2021. Appropriate descriptive statistical methods were employed to analyze the variables, and the hypotheses were tested using simple regression analysis. In this study, adoption of AI and ML was treated as an independent variable, with the credit scoring process as the dependent variable. The results demonstrate that credit scoring process has positively impacted by incorporation AI and ML. Ultimately, the findings demonstrate that AI and ML not only optimize the credit scoring process but also play a critical role in promoting financial accessibility and inclusion in Jordan’s banking system.

title: An LLM-Based Framework for Structured Knowledge Extraction and Summarization from Patent Documents
authors: Gopakumar G, Sreeja M U
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Intellectual property information in the competitive landscape is quite valuable for companies around the world. Patent analytics provide insights that support commercialization risk mitigation and facilitate the use of existing patent literature to solve commercially relevant technical problems. With the patent documents available in public domain such as Google patents or WIPO portals, the volume of intellectual property data available for companies is huge and the traditional manual methods followed by companies for analyzing company and competitor patents pose a challenge. There are opportunities to leverage modern day technologies such as large language models (LLMs) in the field of intellectual property and patent analytics to generate valuable insights about the company and its competitors. Generating concise summary of patents is one of the very first steps companies adopt as part of such analysis. The proposed work is performed as a first step that aims at accomplishing the patent summarization with problem-solutionapplication ontology using two large language models (LLMs) and comparing the results both qualitatively and quantitatively. The results also discusses the impact of hyperparameter tuning for patent summarization tasks.

title: Contour Optimization of DC-GIS Insulator Considering the Influence of Surface Charging Withstanding Transient Voltages
authors: Yu Gao, Pinhao Huang, Di Lu, Tao Han, Boxue Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Surface charging on insulator in DC-GIS is influenced by transient voltages like lightning impulse (LI) and polarity reversal (PR), which threatens the safe operation of the equipment. In this paper, a contour optimization method considering the influence of surface charging is proposed for insulator used in DC-GIS of ±320 kV. The insulation reliability under steady DC voltage is promoted, and the property to withstand the transient voltages is guaranteed as well. The normal and tangential components of electric field under steady DC voltage are simultaneously minimized, and the constraints on electric field under the transient voltages are incorporated. The results show that, under the steady DC voltage, the maximal value of normal electric field is reduced by 5.8%, and the tangential field is simultaneously reduced by 7.0% on the insulator with the optimal contour after optimization. The maximal values of electric fields under the LI and PR voltages are 8.82 and 3.49 kV/mm, respectively, meeting the insulation requirements. It is suggested that, the proposed method incorporating the property withstanding the LI and PR voltages yields a more reliable performance of the optimized insulator in DC-GIS.

title: Leaf Water Thickness Estimation from Spectral Reflectance
authors: Ketaki Vinay Jambhali, Bikram Koirala, Paul Scheunders
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Leaf Water Thickness, often described as equivalent water thickness, is an important trait in plant physiology and can be estimated non-destructively using the leaf's spectral reflectance. Leaf water thickness is defined as the depth of the water layer if all the water contained in a plant leaf were uniformly distributed over a given area. In this study, we propose a supervised machine learning approach based on polynomial regression, combined with our method, NRAL, to estimate leaf water thickness. The results are compared against those obtained using the PROSPECT model, a well-established physical model for simulating leaf optical properties. This methodology is evaluated on the LOPEX dataset, along with three additional hyperspectral datasets. In all cases, the proposed approach achieves a lower root mean squared error (RMSE) compared to the PROSPECT model. These findings highlight the potential of hybrid machine learning methods to outperform traditional models in accurately estimating leaf biophysical parameters.

title: Legal Text Generation Using Transformer Models for Contract Drafting
authors: Muayad Hassan Al-Tawalbeh, Mahmoud Mokhtar Abdelmoghies, Nawel Ben Sassi, Majd Waleed Almanasra, Shuq Hussein, Ahmed Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Contracts drafting is a thorough task that requires a high level of specialization and precision. The productive possibilities of NLP’s evolution of legal experts automation have attracted people's considerable interest, especially when it comes to transformer-based models such as GPT, BERT that generate some exceptional texts. This study investigates the applicability of transformers for the creation of top-notch legal paragraphs customized for contracts. By adapting these models to the specifics of the field, we want to determine whether they can effectively generate the appropriate, decent, and lawfully concluded sentence clauses. We confront the issues of ensuring legal compliance and dealing with regulatory language as well as the one of avoiding the generation of biased or otherwise inappropriate content. The study is based on metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, and expert legal review to measure the generated text's quality. The results of our experiments showcase that transformer-based models can bring about a reduction in drafting time while preserving legal accuracy. This study is aimed at contributing to the swiftly developing AI applications in law area by shedding light on the potential and future avenues for automated text generation’s use in law practice.

title: Stock Price Prediction: ANN or RNN - An Analytical Study
authors: Partha Sarathi Paul, Rajendrani Mukherjee
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Prediction of stock prices is a complex task for financial researchers and computer scientists. The intricate and ever changing dynamics of the market creates this complexity. Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) are popularly used for learning and forecasting from historical data of stocks. This paper compares the structure and data processing abilities of these two models for forecasting stock prices of the following day. It is found in the study that while Artificial Neural Network can effectively capture patterns in relatively stable markets, Recurrent Neural Networks can demonstrate superior performance in capturing sequential dependencies in volatile periods due to their inherent memory capability. The results obtained show Recurrent Neural Network is a more robust framework for time-series prediction dynamic financial context. This paper provides insight into the comparative superiority of Recurrent Neural Network model for stock forecasting and contributes to more informed selection of model in financial machine learning applications.

title: DECAF: Dual-Domain Evidential Cross-Attention Fusion Network for MRI-Based Cognitive Decline Staging
authors: Afshan Khan, Bushara A R, Bismi Mohammed Ashraf, Navya Raj, Muhammad Salah M. A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate and reliable staging of cognitive decline using MRI remains a major challenge due to limited accuracy, weak interpretability, and unreliable uncertainty estimation in existing diagnostic methods. To address these limitations, this research introduces DECAF (Dual-domain Evidential CrossAttention Fusion Network), a novel deep learning framework designed for trustworthy MRI-based dementia staging. DECAF integrates spatial and frequency-domain features from MRI scans to capture complementary information for precise classification. A pre-trained EfficientNet-B0 extracts rich spatial representations, while a lightweight CNN analyzes log-magnitude 2D Fourier transforms to model frequency-domain patterns. These features are fused through a Transformer-based cross-attention mechanism, enabling adaptive focus on the most informative feature interactions. Furthermore, supervised contrastive loss enhances class separation across similar dementia stages, while Evidential Deep Learning provides calibrated uncertainty estimation, strengthening clinical trust. Experimental results demonstrate DECAF's superior performance, achieving an accuracy of 0.9888 and a Cohen's Kappa of 0.9838, surpassing CNN (0.8725,0.8163) and Transformer (0.9588,0.9406) baselines. The confusion matrix reveals zero misclassifications in “Very Mild”, “Mild”, and “Moderate Dementia” stages, confirming its strong discriminative ability. Overall, DECAF establishes a state-of-the-art, interpretable, and reliable framework for automated dementia staging, advancing diagnostic precision and clinical reliability.

title: DL-HOL: Deep Learning Enhanced Hybrid Ordinal Learning for Healthcare Applications with Interval Labels
authors: Padma Chigulla, Punyala Ramadevi, Annamraju Thanuja, Pallapati Solmon, N. Srihitha Gunapriya, N.Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Predicting disease progression is a crucial task in healthcare, especially when the outcomes lie on an ordinal scale, such as stages of disease severity or rates of cognitive decline. However, many real-world clinical datasets suffer from label uncertainty, where only interval-based information is available rather than precise class assignments. This paper presents DL-HOL, a novel deep learning-based extension of the Hybrid Ordinal Learner framework, capable of integrating both precise and interval-labeled data for robust ordinal prediction. Unlike conventional approaches that rely heavily on manual feature engineering, DL-HOL directly processes raw neuroimaging scans alongside demographic and clinical variables, extracting highlevel representations automatically. Through extensive experiments on the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset, DL-HOL demonstrated clear improvements in overall accuracy, mean absolute error reduction, and class-wise stability compared to several existing ordinal learning baselines, including kernel-based HOL, PRIL, and VILMA. The results suggest DL-HOL provides a practical, scalable solution for individualized prediction of disease progression rates, with direct applications in early intervention strategies.

title: Comparative Analysis of Quantum and Multi-Model Deep Learning Approaches for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Sireesha Moturi, Chinnapareddy Anjali Reddy, Dogiparthi Dhanusha, Shaik Chikli Asifa, Chandra Mouli Kathi, Sasidhar Bola
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: In medical image analysis, skin lesion classification is a crucial problem since early detection can greatly lower health risks. The possibility of using quantum machine learning to replace traditional deep learning models has been studied recently.This study is positioned as a comparative benchmarking analysis to empirically evaluate the practical performance of quantum-based learning models against established deep learning architectures under identical experimental conditions.In this work, a renowned research technique was used to first create a Quantum Neural Network (QNN) and a Quantum Support Vector Classifier (QSVC). Many conventional deep learning models were then employed for comparison because of simulator limitations and poor performance. To provide a fair experimental setting, EFAM-Net, MobileNetV3, ResNet18, and EfficientNet-B0 were trained and assessed using the same dataset. Classical deep learning models performed better than quantum-based models, which had a modest classification accuracy of 62-65 %. ResNet18 and EFAM-Net had validation accuracy of 83.62 % and 79.89 %, respectively, while EfficientNet-B0 had the greatest validation accuracy of 90.21 %, closely followed by MobileNetV3 with 90.15 %. These findings suggest that conventional deep learning models are now more appropriate for real-world medical image categorisation since they provide more accuracy and training stability than quantum-based methods.

title: Hyperspectral Imaging System for the Broadband Characterization of Biological Tissues in the 500-5200 NM Range
authors: Matteo Savazzi, Emma Lacagnina, Alessandro Bossi, Paola Saccomandi
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) is gaining interest in the medical field for diagnostics and surgical guidance. While most studies focus on the visible and near-infrared (VNIR) range, spectral characterization of biological tissues in the short-wave (SWIR) and mid-wave infrared (MWIR) remains very limited. For the first time in the literature, we present a broadband HSI system for characterizing biological tissues across the 500-5200 nm band, using three hyperspectral cameras covering the VNIR (400-1000 nm), SWIR (1000-2500 nm), and MWIR (2700-5200 nm) ranges. Preliminary results are reported for porcine fat and spleen, using two specimens per tissue and two samples per specimen. The pipeline includes image registration for consistent pixel- and ROI-level analysis across all bands. The results confirm tissue contrast in the VNIR and indicate that the SWIR and MWIR ranges provide complementary potential for tissue discrimination. This study supports the development of a spectral reference database and future HSI applications in medical diagnostics.

title: Optimizing Fertilizer Use in Smart Farming Using AI
authors: Ahmad Azzazi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In modern agriculture, effective fertilizer management is essential for achieving sustainable crop yields, minimizing environmental impact, and decreasing the costs incurred by farmers. This research project examines the role of artificial intelligence (AI) in the accurate application of fertilizers through smart agricultural methods. AI systems by combining signals from sensors that analyze soil health, appliance forecasters, types of crops, and product history, will offer detailed advice on what and how much fertilizer should be applied at what point. The study articulated the participatory advantage of various machine learning methods such as the neural network, decision-surface-coupled adaptive predictive method, and the evolution of a resource efficiency assessment tool called simple calorimetry accompanied by optimization strategies, to build a decision aid that would mitigate the indicated environmental damages. By employing actual datasets from field trials, the improvements realized in resource productivity as well as product quality were considerable and at the same time nitrogen excess disposal and other forms of environmental harm were reduced. The present paper outlines the overall guiding framework for AI-based practices in fertilizer management, in addition to the challenges and opportunities in implementing such systems at a large scale in precision agriculture. The findings underline the feasibility of using AI in the agricultural technology ecosystem as both economic and ecological objectives are advancing.

title: Self-Adaptive HOA Driven Deep Optimization Framework for Robust Infant Pose Estimation
authors: Kalaimathi R, S. Latha, Samiappan Dhanalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate infant pose estimation is crucial for neonatal monitoring because it supports early detection of developmental and neurological issues in clinical practice. In this study, we present a Motion-Enhanced HRNet that utilizes a Self-Adaptive Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm (SA-HOA) for selecting informative feature channels in infant pose estimation. The framework starts by enhancing depth images with contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) combined with gradient-based motion cues to emphasize subtle limb movements. A HRNet backbone extracts multi-level features, and SA-HOA is then employed to identify the most relevant channels while reducing redundancy. Optimized features pertaining to infant joint keypoint prediction are fed into the HRNet regressor to extract the HRNet regressor outputs. Our technique exhibits significant improvements in MSE(13.39), MAE(1.12), RMSE(3.60), PCK(99.5), and KLE(1.71) as it outperforms baseline approaches on the BabyPose dataset. Improvement can be attributed to the optimized feature motion-aware preprocessing, relevance optimization, and pose estimation metaheuristic search, which results in the framework's enhanced computational efficiency. This work illustrates the growing importance of optimization driven deep learning over pose estimation computational efficacy in clinically accurate infant monitoring systems.

title: Predictive Analytics for Customer Churn in Retail
authors: Tamadher Aldabbagh, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the retail industry, the issue of customer churn poses a serious danger to business revenues in the short and long term. The method of predictive analytics, which is based on machine learning and data science, is a powerful tool for businesses to spot potential customer churners and create targeted retention plans. The main idea of this research is to design and apply predictive models for customer churn in retail. We look into customer data, such as purchase behaviors, demographic profiles, and engagement statistics, to create and evaluate machine learning approaches like logistic regression, decision trees, and gradient boosting. As our research shows, data preprocessing, feature engineering, and the comprehensibility of the model are all important in attaining the best prediction results. Our proposed approach is a clear example of how predictive analytics can empower retailers to address customer retention proactively, schedule marketing efforts more effectively, and improve customer satisfaction. Our analysis underscores the enormously innovative nature of data-led strategies as they can dramatically improve customer retention and, in turn, lead to business success in a tough retail market.

title: A Whale Optimization Assisted Deep Metaheuristic Framework for IoT-Enabled Pipeline Leakage Detection and Failure Prediction
authors: K. Arulmani, V N R Sai Krishna Kari, B. Arun Prasath, D. Mohana Priya, Narayanam R S Lakshmi Prasanthi, Sathiya Priya T
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Leakage in oil, gas, and water pipelines poses severe environmental, financial, and operational challenges, especially when traditional monitoring systems fail to detect early or small leaks due to noisy sensor data and limited modeling intelligence. To address these limitations, this study introduces a Whale Optimization Assisted Deep Metaheuristic Learning (WOA-DML) framework designed for smart, IoT-enabled pipeline leakage detection and predictive failure analytics. The proposed system integrates heterogeneous pressure, acoustic, vibration, and flow-rate sensors with a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM model to effectively capture spatial-temporal signal patterns related to pipeline anomalies. The Whale Optimization Algorithm is employed to optimize hyperparameters and feature-selection weights, enabling faster convergence, improved anomaly sensitivity, and stable learning under uncertain operating conditions. Experimental evaluation using benchmark datasets demonstrates that the WOA-DML framework achieves a superior leakage detection accuracy of 98.46 %, with corresponding precision, recall, and F 1 -score values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.92 \%, 98.63 \%$</tex>, and 98.27 %, respectively. Comparative analysis shows statistically significant improvements (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) over conventional CNN, LSTM, Hybrid CNN-LSTM, PSO-DL, and GA-DL models. The framework further reduces training time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23-31 \%$</tex> and converges within 17 iterations, highlighting its computational efficiency. Robustness testing under noise levels up to 30 % confirms that the model maintains accuracy above 94 %, demonstrating high resilience to sensor fluctuations and dynamic pipeline conditions. Overall, the statistical findings validate WOA-DML as a scalable, energy-efficient, and highly reliable solution for next-generation smart pipeline monitoring and proactive maintenance systems.

title: AMF-Net: A Multimodal Attention-Based Network for Rice Yield Prediction
authors: A. S. Radhamani, Laghumavarapu Chanikya, Gokaraju Surya Satya Narayana Raju, Venkata Sri Hari Haranath Pelluri, Byreddy Uday Pujith Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Precise prediction of rice yield is essential to world food security and climate-resilient agricultural planning; although it is rather difficult since meteorological and soil characteristics are highly non-linear and crop phenology is quite sensitive. Current Multimodal deep learning models are mainly based on static feature concatenation and do not readily adapt to the dynamic agronomic changes, which occur over the growing season and do not distinctly distinguish different sources of data. In this regard, this paper introduces a new architecture, Attentive Multimodal Fusion Network (AMF-Net), which is a dynamic form of integrating spatial Sentinel-2 multispectral images, phenology-constrained meteorological time-series, and a prior physicochemical soil characteristics. The framework uses modality specific encoders: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for spatial-spectral features, a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) encoder with seasonal weather dynamics, and a dense encoder with soil constraints. They are incorporated through a multi-head self-attention which selectively weighs cross-modal contributions at different stages of growth. Assessed in various agro-climatic areas of Andhra Pradesh, the experimental findings indicate that AMF-Net significantly outperforms single-modality and fixed-fusion baselines. The model proposed has a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.2024 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex> and a Coefficient of Determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.6765, demonstrating high predictive consistency within a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.5 \mathbf{t} / \mathbf{h a}$</tex> tolerance band. Also, the attention mechanism offers critical interpretability to agricultural decision-making. With a very small footprint of 191,009 trainable parameters, the AMF-Net provides a very scalable, robust, and resource-efficient basis of real-time precision agriculture systems.

title: Predictive Maintenance in Smart Homes Using ML
authors: Najlaa Abd Hamza, Wafaa M. Salih Abedi, Shatha Habeeb Jafer Al-Khalisy, Alaa Yahya Aref Ahmad, Hanady Almahmood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Predictive maintenance has come out as a core propriety in smart home management through allowing the timely objectification and fixing of probable system failures which in turn leads to a decrease in costs and a tolerance on system reliability. This research paper analyses how machine learning (ML) techniques will revolutionize the design of predictive maintenance systems in smart homes offering an efficient tailored maintenance service. The proposed plan which runs through real-time data collection from the Internet of Things (IoT) interconnected devices, sensors, and the like reveals significant patterns or anomalies that most likely precede equipment failure. Employing key ML algorithms namely supervised learning and anomaly detection models, the proposed system provides a highly precise prediction of the maintenance needs. The framework prioritizes scalability, energy efficiency, and data privacy, which are all the unique problems that heterogeneous smart home systems present. Experimental evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed system in minimizing downtime, optimizing maintenance schedules, and improving overall system performance. This research contributes to the development of smarter, self-sustaining homes that enhance user convenience and operational efficiency through intelligent maintenance solutions.

title: AI for Predictive Maintenance in Industrial Systems
authors: Zainab Al Ghurabli, Ahmed Ali Otoom
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Predictive maintenance or PdM has taken the lead in the control and productivity of industrial systems. Adaptations of artificial intelligence, particularly the machine learning (ML) and neural networks, which enabled real-time predictive analytics based on sensor data, have revolutionizing traditional methods of conducting maintenance. This paper studies the power of AI in predictive maintenance of industrial systems with a focus on its forecasting abilities of equipment failures, creation of optimal maintenance schedules, and downtime minimization. The paper expresses various AI methods such as supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning while explaining the strengths and difficulties of these approaches in practical industrial contexts. Moreover, we verify the Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, and the significance of big data analytics in the extraction of actionable intelligence, will be studied. This paper evaluates the advantages of AI-driven predictive maintenance such as reduction in cost, an increase in operational efficiency, and enhancement of safety. In conclusion, the paper points out the primary challenges of implementation and suggests potential expansions for research, emphasizing the incorporation of federated learning into privacy-preserving predictive maintenance in decentralized industrial systems.

title: Blight Disease Detection and Severity Estimation in Crop Leaves Using Deep Learning with Grad-CAM
authors: Karishma P, Akash Velan A, Vishal Ganapathy S, Malathi M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Overall, crop diseases cause sizeable yield losses all over the world and are a significant menace to agriculture productivity and food security. Standard illness identification techniques are based on manual inspection, which is inherently subjective, time-consuming, and insufficiently early warning. Although several deep learning methods guarantee a high level of plant disease classification accuracy, most of the current models and setups are black-box methods and do not provide severity assessments, limiting the potential of such precision agriculture in the fields. This article proposes an interpretable deep learning method, which integrates classification and severity assessment for crop leaf blight infection inspection. A light-weight ResNet11 model is used to categorize three types of crop diseases: late blight, early blight, and healthy and an infection rate module that obtained an infected pixel ratio is implemented, followed by an introduced Grad-CAM to visualize infection areas heating maps to improve the interpretation. This proposed approach is evaluated on the PlantVillage dataset and can provide not only classification and severity level but also quantitative explanations, and interpretable output with an accuracy of 99.02

title: X-DOA-SBOA: An Explainable Hybrid Optimized Transformer Framework for Trustworthy Brain Tumor Diagnosis from Multi-Modal MRI Scans
authors: M. Sai Prasad, P.Pramod Kumar, A. Manjula
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This research work proposes X-DOA-SBOA, a framework for reliable brain tumour diagnostics that makes use of explainable hybrid optimised transformers. Using a multi-modal Transformer-UNet backbone, the model incorporates the Dream Optimisation Algorithm (DOA) for adjusting hyperparameters globally and the Success-Based Optimisation Algorithm (SBOA) for optimised weight refinement. Furthermore, confidence maps and Grad-CAM are used to improve clinical transparency. Experiments conducted on the BraTS2020/BraTS2021 benchmark dataset show that the proposed framework outperforms prior representations like U-Net, Attention U-Net, then TransUNet. Improved boundary accuracy to 4.03 Hausdorff Distance and Dice scores of 0.942 (WT), 0.901 (TC), along with 0.854 (ET) are achieved by the proposed technique. Along with a 98.73% success rate, the area under the curve (AUC) for tumour classification is 0.994. These findings provide more evidence that explainable learning driven by optimising can be useful in clinic for accurate cancer detection.

title: SecureNet: A Next-Generation AI-Powered Cyber Defense System for Dynamic Attack Prevention
authors: T. Sasikala, J. Joshua Daniel Raj, P.Vishnu Raja, S.D.Prabu Ragavendiran, C L Vinaya, Harshitha V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: As the Metaverse is rapidly evolving, the security framework of the system will need to evolve to protect users against more cyberattacks. The growing rise in the size of Large Language Models (LLMs) has amplified risks of email phishing attacks, ransomware hits, data breaches, and state-sponsored attacks that have become more advanced and require smarter and adaptive responses. To meet this requirement, different secure services are provided by the use of AI-based, web-based cybersecurity ecosystem, called SecureNet that combines threat-monitoring, anomaly-detection, geolocation of attack that allows tracking the attack origin, quantum inspired encryption, and deceptionbased defense mechanisms. It is based on cyberpunk-themed dashboards, the system is built on Python-Flask server code that uses dynamic HTML, CSS, and JavaScript elements to represent real-time cyber-attack conditions. The fundamental modules that incorporate the live scoring of threats, IP geolocation tracking, trap files identification, emergency lockdown measures, and MirrorNet-based simulation of threats that are effective to test the incorporation of modern cyber defense systems. It has an architecture based on a modular clientserver model that allows continuous updates, simulated API-based interaction of data and responsive UI interaction. System testing to validate the stable operation of all modules, visualizing threats, mapping of geolocation accurately, and simulated lockdown performance, and smooth running of quantum key distribution logic are all confirmed. Combining the tricks of deception and AI-powered analytics, as well as accessible and intuitive visualization, SecureNet offers a scalable and easy-to-learn environment to learn about cyber-attack behavior and defensive response flows. This project exemplifies the potential to design lightweight technologies into extensive, impactful, and real-time cybersecurity simulation that may be applied to develop academic learning, conduct prototyping, and, subsequently, expand to fully autonomous cyber defense infrastructures.

title: Smart Vision for Safer Roads: Accident Detection using Image Intelligence
authors: G Raja Vikram, A. Harika, P.Rishi Priya, B. Kiran Reddy, A. Ravi Teja
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Traffic accidents are often inaccurately reported and are one of the main causes of fatalities and injuries at a global level. Traditional methods of accident management often rely on eyewitness reports or manual notifications, which can be limited by human delays and inaccuracies. The rapid development of artificial intelligence provides new opportunities for intelligent traffic and safety systems. New prospects have been created for the creation of intelligent traffic control and safety systems. This research proposes an IoT-based accident detection framework that uses deep learning approach for real-time identification of accidents. The system proposed employs a roadside camera to take traffic images continuously, which are processed by a deep learning model trained to identify accidents with high accuracy. When an accident is detected, a Raspberry Pi equipped with a GSM module immediately sends emergency messages to concerned authorities to facilitate quick response. The system was tested and trained in a wide range of accident situations to achieve high robustness across different traffic and environmental conditions. A working prototype, which utilized miniature vehicles in a controlled setup, achieved high reliability and performance. However, lighting inconsistency, weather conditions, and traffic patterns present challenges for the large-scale implementation. Potential improvements include the incorporation of GPS technology to facilitate accurate localization of accidents and the employment of cloud-based systems to facilitate real-time access to information. Overall, the system proposed presents an innovative, scalable, and cost-effective solution to road safety improvement and emergency response time reduction.

title: Brain Tumor Classification Using DenseNet121 and Vision Transformer
authors: G Komal Venkat Ganesh, G Ramya Sruthi, K Raja Sekhar, D Manoj Sudeep, Yepuganti Karuna, Saladi Saritha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Brain tumor classification through magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a crucial task in the diagnosis and treatment of brain tumors. However, the classification of brain tumors through MRI is a tedious task. To improve the efficiency of the classification task, we propose a hybrid deep learning model based on DenseNet-121 and the Vision Transformer model for brain tumor classification. The proposed model is able to learn features through the fusion of convolutional and transformer models. The model is evaluated on the publicly available Brain Tumor MRI Dataset, which contains four classes of brain tumors: glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and normal brain images. The experimental results show that the proposed model performs better than the baseline models in terms of classification accuracy on the same conditions. The classification accuracy of the proposed model is 99.01 Percent. The originality and contribution of the proposed model are the parallel fusion of the CNN and transformer models for brain tumor classification.

title: A Smart IoT-Based Robotic Surveillance System for Remote Monitoring
authors: Paarth Singh, Konnankii Saiisarran, Peethala Snehith, S. Umamaheswari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Current research on autonomous systems and mobile robots remains one of the most significant global research fields. The systems function as essential components which enable navigation during search-and-rescue missions. The applications face their biggest challenges in dynamic environments which feature sudden environmental changes that create operational difficulties for their systems. The use of computers enables fast detection of dynamic obstacles together with living things which results in reduced collision incidents and improved search efficiency. The conventional navigation systems rely on static heuristics which use offline data for their operations. The methods provide correct results, but they require extensive time, and their results demand human evaluation. The development of artificial intelligence AI together with machine learning ML creates a new method which enables automated systems to discover hidden patterns within environmental data. The study presents a complete model which enables robots to predict their movements and navigate based on current research in probabilistic robotics and feature extraction techniques. The research explores and clarifies hybrid path planning through its application of unique methods to autonomous mobile robots. The system design integrates an Arduino UNO with an ESP32 Cam-based system which operates through Dynamic Spanning Tree Coverage (D-STC) and Hybrid RRT-A* and Triangular Segmented Interpolation (TSI) algorithms. The model uses transfer learning through pre-trained models while it employs dynamic window methods to prevent collisions and optimized classifiers to identify the final entity. The proposed system achieves excellent results through its <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 8. 2 5 \%}$</tex> coverage efficiency and only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3. 0 6 \%}$</tex> overlap rate. The results exceed those of standard machine learning benchmarks. The paper goes on to present an innovative research framework which expands to new interdisciplinary research domains.

title: Explainable Monkeypox Classification Using Lightweight Attention-Based Separable CNN and Real-Time Web Application
authors: Md. Ismiel Hossen Abir, Abir Mahmud Shahariar, Sanjana Islam Kasfia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Monkeypox is a viral disease that causes skin lesions similar to other conditions, such as chickenpox. This similarity makes it difficult to diagnose accurately, especially in areas with limited medical resources. Traditional diagnostic methods are reliable but often slow and require a healthcare professional. In this study, we propose a lightweight attention-based separable Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model for binary classification of monkeypox skin lesions. The proposed architecture used depth-wise separable convolutions, channel attention mechanisms, and feature extraction blocks to improve performance. We compare our model’s performance against three transfer learning models, such as VGG16, DenseNet121, and MobileNetV2. The proposed model achieved 87.15% test accuracy, which is comparatively better performing according to the three transfer learning models. We further enhance interpretability using explainable AI techniques, including LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) and Saliency Maps to highlight critical regions influencing classification decisions. Additionally, we also develop a real-time web application for user-friendly interaction, enabling instant predictions with confidence scores. The study highlights the potential of lightweight and interpretable deep learning models with a real-time web application.

title: Stock Price Prediction using a Hybrid of TimeXer and FT-Transformer Models
authors: Denitta Daison, Blessy Paul P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Stock price prediction is a critical aspect of financial forecasting, providing valuable insights for traders and investors. TimeXer model is widely used Transformer based model for stock price prediction, particularly effective in modeling temporal dependencies through sparse attention mechanisms. However, despite its strengths, the TimeXer model has limitations, such as reduced capacity to capture nuanced feature interactions and dependency on temporal patterns alone. On the other hand, Conversely, the FT-Transformer is a newer architecture that uses feature-level attention to emphasize the important predictors. This model has shown promising accuracy in stock price prediction, it primarily focuses on feature importance and may fail to leverage long term sequential dependencies. This model has shown promising accuracy in stock price prediction, yet it primarily focuses on feature importance and may underutilize long term sequential dependencies FT-Transformer demonstrated accuracy, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>. However, FT-Transformer is less effective in modeling sequential dependencies and temporal patterns when used alone. This limitation motivated the development of a hybrid model that integrates the temporal sequence modeling capability of TimeXer with the feature interaction learning strength of FT-Transformer. In the hybrid framework, TimeXer learns how historical time points influence future stock prices, while FT-Transformer determines which features contribute most to prediction accuracy. Experimental results on AAPL, AMZN, GOOG historical stock data from Yahoo Finance from January 1, 2000 to March 13, 2025 showed that TimeXer alone achieved an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 5}$</tex>, while the hybrid model improved performance to an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.99, making it the most accurate approach in this study.

title: Bank Customer Churn Rate Prediction and Minimization Using Machine Learning
authors: Bollepalli Amrutha Varshini, Bolla Gayathri, V. Surya, K. Lalitha Devi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The customer churn turnover, which is the termination of a relationship with a bank by a customer, is a major challenge to financial institutions that may result in loss of revenue and decreased long-term stability. With the rise in the competition in the banking industry, customer retention has become as important as customer acquisition. The proposed paper will suggest a machine learningbased solution to customer churn prediction and reduction. Customer data is analysed to predict the churn with several machine learning models, like Random Forest, AdaBoost, XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and Naive Bayes. Handling missing values, eliminating duplicates, and normalization of data are some of the preprocessing processes in the methodology. Also, approaches like SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) are also used in combating imbalance in classes. The models are compared on performance measures like recall, accuracy and ROC-AUC, where AdaBoost is able to perform best in terms of recall, implying that it is very efficient in revealing at-risk customers. The system is a webbased application developed on the Flask framework that enables real-time churn prediction. This allows banks take proactive measures towards reducing churn by keeping high-risk customers.

title: Portable Water Risk Estimator
authors: Ferents Koni Jiavana K, Aamir Ayaaz A R, Dhayana V, Mohammed Shaheen K S, Bhavesshna K M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The need for rapid, user-friendly water quality assessment tools is increasingly important for active individuals and urban consumers. This paper presents the design and validation of the PWRE Development Board, a modular embedded prototype developed for a next-generation smart system capable of real-time risk estimation with hardware provisions for future UV disinfection. Built around the Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040), the board integrates TDS, turbidity, and temperature sensors with a compact user interface comprising an SSD1306 OLED display, status LEDs, tactile buttons, and haptic feedback. Flexible power options and JST-based modular connectors enable easy sensor upgrades and field deployment. The system supports diagnostic self-tests for each functional block, ensuring robust, reliable operation. Validation results show that the platform streamlines both component evaluation and firmware development, forming a versatile base for portable water safety products and future IoT enhancements.

title: Intelligent Human–Computer Interaction for Navigation Control Through Vision-Based Hand Gesture Recognition
authors: Venkata Sai Aditya Kondru, Shankar Balla, Dhavalkumar Thakar, Dheeraj Velaga, Sri Lekha Bandla
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper describes the importance of intelligent human-computer interaction in facilitating natural communication between human beings and the computer especially in navigation control as used in robotic and virtual worlds. The possibility of hand gestures being recognized visually has been a research topic of interest because of its non-contact feature, intuitive and non-hardware issue. It offers a systematic approach to the study, which combines adaptive hand segmentation, deep convolutional feature extractions, and temporal feature aggregations to predict dynamic hand gestures. Confidence-based filtering and attention-guided classification mechanism are used to boost discriminative learning and stable navigation choices respectively. Gesture-to-command mapping also employs temporal smoothing in order to allow continuous control. The performance evaluation proves to be better behavioral in comparison to four prevailing methods. The proposed system has the specificity of 0.95, Kappa coefficient of 0.90, Intersection over Union of 0.82 as well as Navigation Stability Index of 0.89 and achieves improvements of 4-12 percent as compared to deep static and handcrafted-feature methods. These findings suggest that the proposed framework is strong and sound in providing gesture-based navigation and can be applicable in the real-world intelligent interaction.

title: AI-Driven Portfolio Management Strategies
authors: Rasha Abousamra, Moustafa Kamal Moussa, Gamal Khalifa, Rahul Sharma, Kahled Masoud, Shatha Mustafa Hussain, Thair Kaddumi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Portfolio management is an essential part of financial planning and investment planning. That said, asset allocation optimization for maximum returns with minimum risks is the ultimate goal here. The introduction of AI into the industry has brought about a revolutionary shift in the industry that offers powerful and advanced tools such as predictive analytics, risk assessment, and decision-making capabilities. This research reflects on the processes of AI-based portfolio management strategy creation and implementation through the employment of, among others, the most notable machine learning models, and deep learning systems. Moreover, also natural language processing techniques. These instruments are successfully applied in the examination of huge unstructured financial data, as well as responding promptly to market trends. The paper thus examines the capacity of AI models of portfolio optimization by back-testing their results against conventional techniques. It also highlights the difficulties of financial applications such as algorithmic bias, data quality, and model interpretability. The study by means of case studies and simulations points out how AI can change the scenario of portfolio management and hence do away with the traditional way of data interpretation and interpretation by the investors. Thus, the IVF power of AI is presented by means of this research paper where the usage of AI will help the investors in making decisions that are driven by data that will eventually lead to better financial results.

title: Analysis of Malnutrition Prediction in Children using ML Techniques
authors: A.Amuthavalli, K.Nandhini
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Malnutrition remains one of the main reasons why young children die and get sick, significantly impacting their growth and development. To avoid long-term health effects, early detection of malnutrition is essential, and advanced technologies such as deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) provide promising solutions in predicting malnutrition risk. This research investigates the possibilities of ML and DL models in accurately predicting malnutrition in young children by analyzing a wide array of demographic, nutritional, and health-related factors. By employing advanced data analytics techniques, the research seeks to identify key risk factors and develop predictive models It can be used in environments with low resources. The study applies a range of Convolutional and recurrent neural networks are examples of deep learning designs; support vector machines, random forests, and decision trees, and makes use of several datasets, including medical history and dietary intake records. The efficacy of these models is assessed based on model robustness, forecast accuracy, and potential for real-world application. The findings indicate that these models offer significant promise for early diagnosis of malnutrition, enabling targeted interventions that can improve health outcomes for vulnerable populations.

title: Routing in Mobile Ad Hoc Networks using Hybrid DSR- OLSR Routing
authors: P. Vidhya Devi, B. L. Shivakumar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The difficulty of accomplishing effective routing in dynamic, self-organizing environments is faced by ad hoc networks that are mobile, or MANETs. This study presents a new method Hybrid DSR-OLSR to improve routing in MANETs, shows a chart that compares the security of the suggested hybrid performance. DSR excels in on-demand routing, while OLSR offers proactive routing. By integrating both protocols, our proposed hybrid solution aims to strike a balance between proactive and reactive strategies. This hybrid approach leverages the strengths of both DSR and OLSR to improve the network's adaptability and overall performance in MANETs, ensuring more reliable and efficient transmission of data. Results from simulations show how effective this innovative routing solution.

title: Multi-Modal Emotion Recognition on Physiological Signals Using Self-Supervised Deep Learning Models
authors: Mayank Sharma, Divyanshu Desai, Geetha Sri, Kumaran P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The Affective computing is the interdisciplinary field which bridges computer science, psychology, and cognitive science and aims to endow machines with the ability to recognize, interpret, and simulate human emotions. While the facial emotion recognition analysis and speech emotion recognition have achieved commercial success, they remain fundamentally limited by their reliance on external, voluntary displays of emotion which can be controlled and modulated accordingly to the situations. The Physiological signals offer an absolute, continuous, and involuntary window into the emotional state of the subject, as these are very difficult to manipulate or mask. However, the development of efficient physiological emotion recognition systems is hindered by three critical challenges: the scarcity of large-scale labeled datasets (the “small data” problem), high inter-subject variability, and the complexity of fusing heterogeneous data streams. This paper proposes an efficient, end-to-end Multi-Modal Emotion Recognition System (MMERS) that addresses these challenges through a novel combination of Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and Attention-based Deep Fusion. We introduce a Contrastive Predictive Coding (CPC) framework to pretrain the EEG encoders on the unlabeled data, which enables the model to learn generalized temporal features before supervised fine-tuning. In addition to CPC, we also developed a hybrid deep neural architecture. In the above model we integrated the lightweight 1D-Convolutional Neural Network (CNNs) with Bidirectional Recurrent units (Bi-GRUs), in addition to that we also integrated autonomic signal processing with a multi-head attention mechanism for decision-level fusion. The above architecture was validated using the following standard datasets DEAP, SEED-IV and AMIGOS. The above architecture improves feature effectiveness against the varying subjects, where the attention mechanism effectively weighs the contribution of different modalities.

title: AI Fitness Tracker with Posture Detection and Health Insights Chatbot
authors: Kevin J, Krishna Kaushik. N, Darshan S, Padmajothi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The paper presents a smart model for tracking fitness and wellness that uses real-time pose recognition and predictive health analysis. This system relies on a dynamic posture correction algorithm, which continuously extracts skeletal landmarks, unlike traditional fitness apps that follow fixed workout routines. Additionally, the model includes a predictive machine learning approach that assesses the risk of cardiovascular disease based on users' physiological data. To make the system more interactive, a conversational agent is integrated, powered by Natural Language Understanding. The system's effectiveness, as shown through real-time tracking performance, accuracy in posture classification, and reliability in predictions, is proven to be scalable and optimized for edge computing, making it a solution for preventive healthcare and personalized physical training.

title: Design and Commissioning of Control Algorithms for Optimal ECRH Power Injection in TCV’s Plasma
authors: F. Graziano, F. Braghin, D. Busi, S. Coda, C. Galperti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This work refers to the development, commissioning, and testing of a control algorithm to optimize the electron cyclotron resonance heating (ECRH) power injection in the Tokamak á configuration variable (TCV). The aim is to understand how the injected microwave polarization, set by corrugated mirrors in the waveguide, influences relevant plasma parameters, namely, electron temperature and stray radiation. To accomplish this task, real-time polarization control is required; therefore, a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controller is devised, based on an identified plant transfer function of the polarizing mirrors. The performance of the above-mentioned controller is first tested on the real plant without a plasma discharge and then during plasma operations. Sinusoidal trajectories are provided for the polarization angles, and the temperature-polarization and stray radiation-polarization correlations are studied. This development paves the way for future feedback control strategies based on real-time diagnostics to optimize power coupling and prevent component damage.

title: HybridTopo: Topology-Aware Supervision and Inference for Connectivity-Preserving Road Network Extraction from Aerial Imagery
authors: Ozer Ozdemir, Asli Kaya Karakutuk
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this study, we address road network extraction from high-resolution satellite imagery with explicit emphasis on structural connectivity in addition to pixel-wise segmentation accuracy. Although modern deep learning models can achieve strong IoU values, topological discontinuities may still degrade routing usability. To address this issue, we present HybridTopo, a topology-aware supervision and inference framework that integrates topology-sensitive loss design, auxiliary centerline learning, and inference-time refinement for road extraction. Within the Massachusetts Roads benchmark used in this study, our analysis indicates that explicit topology-aware supervision is more strongly associated with structural preservation than the architectural variations evaluated here. All quantitative results are reported as mean ± standard deviation over three independent runs unless otherwise stated. Under the full pipeline, network fragmentation is reduced from 37.4 to 10.3 connected components per tile, corresponding to a 73% relative reduction with respect to the baseline configuration. In addition, APLS-lite evaluation based on random sampling of 64 node pairs indicates improved path-length consistency in the predicted road graphs. These findings highlight the trade-off between pixel accuracy and topological discipline and show that hybrid structural supervision can improve functionally meaningful road extraction on this benchmark, while broader cross-dataset generalization remains future work.

title: BERT and Local Vision Language Models in Hate Speech Detection on Small Multimodal Datasets
authors: Nathanael P. Solagratia, G.S. Thejas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Social media platforms have made it easier than ever for people to express their opinions. However, society now appears increasingly polarized, particularly online, where it often seems that someone is offended by almost anything.With the rapid pace at which the internet evolves, moderating platforms has become an overwhelming challenge, particularly as new forms of hate speech and cyberbullying constantly emerge. Detecting offensive content in low-resource multimodal settings, presents significant challenges. This study presents a deep learning framework that combines Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) and EfficientNetV2L to classify offensive content in multimodal form, leveraging text and images, alongside local Vision Language Models (VLMs) for offensive content classification. With a late fusion strategy, features from both modalities are merged for a more comprehensive offensive content classification. This study addresses the detection of multimodal offensive content in low-resource settings, demonstrating that locally deployed open-weight VLMs can achieve competitive performance relative to proprietary, flagship models. Results indicate that the best-performing configuration reaches an accuracy of 0.744 on the Multi3Hate dataset, suggesting that open-weight VLMs represent a viable and computationally feasible alternative for multimodal hate speech classification.

title: Current Circulation Strategy for Active Thermal Control in Interleaved DC-DC Converters
authors: Abhishek Chanekar, Nachiketa Deshmukh, Abhinav Arya, Sandeep Anand
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Reliable interleaved dc-dc converters are desirable in numerous applications, including but not limited to, electric vehicle chargers, dc micro-grids, data centers, and industrial power supplies. In these power converters, cyclic variations in the temperature of power semiconductor devices (PSDs) occur due to varying load power and/or ambient conditions. These temperature swings cause degradation and eventual failure of PSDs, which affects system reliability. To enhance the expected lifetime of PSDs in interleaved dc-dc converters, this article proposes a current circulation strategy for active thermal control. The proposed thermal controller uses sinusoidal current circulation to thoughtfully change the power loss in PSDs and reduce the overall swings in their temperature. In addition to the reduction in temperature swings, the average thermal stress of the PSDs in each parallel connected converters is also equalized, despite the presence of practical non-uniformities in the design. Note that these changes in the thermal profile of the PSDs are done without altering the load power requirement with no extra circuitry required. The selection of parameters in the proposed thermal controller and their effect on the overall system performance are also delineated. Lastly, the effectiveness of the suggested approach for changing power loss in the PSDs is validated. A 1.5 kW solar PV to battery charger is developed to validate the proposed technique, in which the expected lifetime of the PSDs is increased by up to 37.8%.

title: Young Professionals and Microwave Students [From the Editor’s Desk]
