title: Plug-and-Play Machine Learning-Based Model Predictive Control
authors: Yuxiao Ji, Yao Shi, Ming Xiao, Zhe Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This work presents a plug-and-play (PnP) machine learning-based model predictive control (ML-MPC) framework for interconnected nonlinear systems. Assuming that each subsystem is input-to-state stable (ISS), the stability of the entire system is ensured via the small-gain theorem. Sufficient conditions are derived to guarantee stability when MPCs using ML models are integrated into or used to replace existing controllers. The effectiveness of the framework is demonstrated through a numerical example and a nonlinear chemical process network.

title: Analysis of VOCs for Prediction of Respiratory Disease Using TabR
authors: M. Latha, Hemavathy S, Aruthra B, Adarsh Annadurai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Respiratory diseases such as asthma, upper respiratory tract infections (URTI), and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) are challenging to diagnose at early stages due to overlapping symptoms and limitations of conventional diagnostic methods. Exhaled breath analysis by volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in breath reflect underlying respiratory conditions. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for multi-class respiratory disease classification using breath-derived VOC features and demographic data. The proposed approach employs a Lion-optimized Tabular Representation Learning (TabR) model to effectively model structured VOC data. Model validation is performed using standard evaluation metrics and stratified five-fold cross-validation. Experimental results demonstrate reliable classification performance and improved class-wise discrimination compared to an attention-based baseline. The study highlights the potential of modern tabular deep learning models for respiratory disease screening and could be used for point-of-care applications.

title: Analysis and Design of a Dual Three-Phase Nonuniform Teeth Permanent Magnet Generator for Wind Turbines
authors: Zhe Zhou, Shuai Xu, Yifei Gao, Jikai Si, Ge Qi, Rui Nie, Peixin Wang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Permanent magnet vernier generators are vital for direct-drive wind turbines but face key challenges in balancing light-wind startup, high-wind stability, and high-power density. To address these issues, this article presents three core innovations. First, a novel dual three-phase nonuniform teeth permanent magnet generator (DTNT-PMG) that enables four operating modes to adapt to both light and strong wind conditions is proposed. Second, two nonuniformity coefficients are defined to quantify the generator’s core performance: one evaluates the improvement in the back-EMF, and the other measures the range of multimode operation, providing an objective basis for design optimization. Third, an entropy-weighted surrogate modeling method for multiobjective optimization of the DTNT-PMG is developed: This method uses entropy weighting to determine the weights of surrogate models objectively and adopts the black-winged kite algorithm to search for the global optimal solution search. Experimental results show that the optimized DTNT-PMG reduces the cogging torque by 85.5% and the power fluctuation by 78.8%, with back-EMF total harmonic distortion of 1.71%. Compared with uniform-teeth PMGs, its average output power increases by 31.4%, verifying the effectiveness of the proposed innovations.

title: Covert Communications: A Generative Spectrum Map Paradigm
authors: Han Jiang, Jiacheng Wang, Junsheng Mu, Chengwen Xing, Henk Wymeersch, Nan Zhao, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: Spectrum map is emerging as an effective tool to characterize and visualize the radio environment for covert communications. However, current approaches for spectrum map acquisition still face significant challenges in terms of accuracy and adaptability in highly complex and rapidly changing environments. Generative artificial intelligence (GAI), with its powerful capability in data learning and generation, can provide a promising solution. In this article, we demonstrate a comprehensive study of GAI-based spectrum maps for covert communications. Specifically, we first classify spectrum maps, detail their construction methods from conventional to emerging approaches, and present a comparative analysis. Then, we review how GAI generates spectrum maps, illustrate their applications in covert communications, and highlight research gaps. To this end, we propose a novel hybrid framework with diffusion models to enable the rapid generation of fine-grained spectrum maps. We further present a case study to demonstrate its effectiveness in enhancing the covertness. Finally, three future directions for applying GAI-based spectrum maps to covert communications are outlined to further promote the research progress.

title: Implementing a Real-Time Intrusion Detection System for Quantum-Secure Email Using AI-Driven QKD
authors: V. Karthick, Shiiv R S, Yuven Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: While conventional cryptographic methods become increasingly vulnerable due to the rapid rise of quantum computing towards practical fault tolerant systems; this paper describes Enhanced BB84-4: a new Quantum Key Distribution protocol developed specifically for secure email communications. The Enhanced BB84-4 method includes features such as a Hyper-Entropic 7-Gate State Preparation method and an AI driven Intelligent Basis Selection method that provides security against efforts to attack these types of systems in the future. In addition to only using operations from two bases under the conventional method, Enhanced BB84-4 uses many different quantum gates for operation to create more uncertainty and enhance the security of information transmitted. To improve the efficiency of key generation, two synchronised Deep Reinforcement Learning agents are used by both communicating parties to predict and select the measurement basis; thus improving the performance of the entire transmission. The authors have prototyped a complete Enhanced BB84-4 system consisting of quantum simulation using IBM Qiskit; a back-end built in Python using Django; and a front-end email interface developed in React.js. In addition, the prototype features an intrusion detection module called “Red Alert;” which provides live visualisation of the Quantum Bit Error Rate to identify propagation of errors in real-time. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system has a significantly increased Key Generation Rate and reduced Keys' Error Rate; thus, providing increased reliability and robustness of the proposed Enhanced BB84-4 system than will be demonstrated by future research on currently available Quantum Key Distribution protocols.

authors: Julia Alexandra Sebastien, Joshua Zhu, Alice Chirico, Valentina Mancuso, Asher Marks, Gary Soffer, Andrea Stevenson Won

title: Multi-Height Magnetic Field Imaging for Defect Depth: A Biot–Savart Inverse Problem Approach
authors: Zhansen Shi, Lucas Lum, Fengkai Cai, Xuezhi Zhang, Chun Fei Siah, Bernice Zee, Jiann Min Chin, Yeow Kheng Lim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This paper presents a novel physics-based method for defect depth localization using multi-height magnetic field imaging (MFI) through a Biot-Savart inverse formulation. As three-dimensional (3D) semiconductor packaging and integrated circuit (IC) technologies become increasingly complex with advanced heterogeneous integration (HI), accurate localization of defect depth has become both more critical and more challenging. Conventional failure analysis techniques often struggle to resolve the vertical position of defects in deeply stacked IC packages. Recent advances in MFI using superconducting quantum interference device (SQUID) sensors enable highly sensitive current mapping with large sensor stand-off distances, making multi-height measurements practical. By correlating magnetic field profiles acquired at different scan heights, the proposed method enables quantitative defect depth estimation with a best demonstrated accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.2 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. The approach is first verified using a test structure and subsequently demonstrated with an actual case study on a 2.5 D HI device, showing improved depth accuracy compared to existing methods.

title: Interoperable Multi-Vendor, Multi-Domain, Multi-Layer, Multi-Service Quantum Key Distribution Network Field-Trial
authors: Konstantinos Tsimvrakidis, Alkinoos Papageorgopoulos, Florian Kutschera, Stephan Laschet, Sebastian Ramacher, Paul James, Luca Torresetti, Nikolas Makris, Persefoni Konteli, Jaime S. Buruaga, Rafael J. Vicente, Yorlandy Lobaina, Juan Pedro Brito, Petros Papapetropoulos, Ilias Papastamatiou, Hannes Hübel, Vicente Martin, George T. Kanellos
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: A multi-vendor, multi-domain Quantum Key Distribution network comprising 15 nodes across three independently managed domains was deployed and operated during QCI Days 2025 in Athens. The large-scale field trial integrated heterogenous QKD technologies, multi-layer Key Management Systems, and SDN-controlled infrastructures into a unified inter-domain architecture enabling cross-domain key exchange and coordination. The setup combined in-rack domain nodes extended to a 4-node field-deployed metropolitan QKD network segment, providing a realistic operational environment for validating end-to-end functionality. Multiple QKD-enabled services were successfully supported across the federated infrastructure, proving the technical feasibility and viability of scalable QKD deployments in inter-domain settings.

title: Clarification of Defect Generation and Annihilation Dynamics in MONOS Cells for Realization of Sustainable 3D Flash Memory with Cryogenic Operation and Recovery Annealing
authors: Viktoria Schlykow, Yuta Aiba, Yasushi Nakasaki, Hitomi Tanaka, Hajime Sano, Takanori Yamanaka, Keiichi Sawa, Masayasu Miyata, Harumi Seki, Masamichi Suzuki, Makoto Fujiwara, Masumi Saitoh, Tomoya Sanuki, Reika Ichihara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: Recently, two promising approaches for sustainable 3D Flash memory have been demonstrated: 7bit/cell by cryogenic operation and reuse by recovery annealing. In this paper, to further boost these approaches, we provide a systematic understanding of the degradation during program/erase cycling, including cryogenic operations, and its recovery by subsequent annealing in MONOS (Metal/Block-oxide/Nitride/Tunnel-oxide/Semiconductor), used as a gate stack in memory cells of 3D Flash memory. Direct observation of charge trapping/de-trapping linked to cycling-generated defects clarified the dynamics of hydrogen (H)-related defect generation in tunnel-oxide, which is significantly suppressed at cryogenic temperatures. Annihilation of cycling generated defects by annealing was also precisely detected. Moreover, by controlling the charge state of defects, we clarify that trapped electrons (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$e^{-}$</tex>) inhibit defect annihilation, likely due to Coulomb repulsion between a negatively charged defect and H, highlighting that defects should be neutral for an efficient recovery of cyclinginduced degradation. These insights advance sustainable memory technology, realized by extending the effective lifetime of 3D Flash memory.

title: A Special Protection Scheme for HVDC Station(2×500MW), Kushtia to Improve Frequency Resilience of Bangladesh Power System
authors: Md. Arifur Kabir, Md. Rashedul Alam, Abdul Hasib Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Disturbance in a power system may lead to a fall in system frequency and even lead to a blackout in an extreme condition. The HVDC station in Bheramara, Kushtia, Bangladesh consists of two poles of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 0 0 ~ M W}$</tex> imports <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0 0 ~ M W}$</tex> power from India, and injects it into Bangladesh power system (BPS). Tripping of any pole or both poles causes rapid frequency decline as BPS has very less number of machines with limited reserve to provide regulation service and primary response. However, there is no special protection scheme (SPS) to improve the BPS resiliency by arresting that frequency excursion. This paper presents an adaptive SPS that performs required amount of load shedding to hold the frequency decline within the threshold value following the HVDC pole tripping or sudden power cut and eventually prevents frequency instability. This developed SPS is scripted by python and then applied to BPS on Digsilent PowerFactory software. The effectiveness of the proposed scheme is investigated by simulating several trippings and sudden reduction of large power of HVDC station.

title: Real-time Twitter Trending Topics Detection and Sentiment Analysis Using Data Mining Technique
authors: Md Rayhan Hassan Mahin, Estaq Ahmed, Mousumi Hasan Mukti, Md Nasir Uddin Rana, Md Tanvir Islam, Norun Nabi, Sharmin Sultana Akhi, Mujiba Shaima
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: A social data revolution has been sparked by recent developments in scalable data management systems, trustworthy social web technologies, and data mining tools. This change has opened up a plethora of opportunities for improving data analysis techniques and social data science research in conjunction with the advancement of data mining tools. It is also in tandem with the evolution of data mining techniques. It also coincides with the development of data mining methods. Despite the abundance of data available, doing comprehensive analyses with social datasets poses significant methodological challenges that can obstruct productive research. In this research article, we introduce techniques for efficiently collecting trending topics in real time from the social web, with a focus on sentiment analysis of people using Twitter data. Furthermore, we propose several effective data cleaning methods specifically tailored for social media data to increase the accuracy of sentiment analysis. This allencompassing strategy tackles major obstacles to using social data for insightful study and analysis, expanding our understanding of the mechanisms at work in the digital social environment.

title: Exploring MuRIL for Emotion Detection in Bangla Text: A Transformer-Driven Methodology for Enhancing Multilingual Emotion Classification
authors: Sudipto Kumar Mitra, Tarifuzzaman Riyad, Md. Faysal Hossan, Palash Sen, Samiu Javed Islam, Sadia Tasnim Shara
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Detecting emotions in Bangla text presents a considerable challenge due to factors such as code-mixing, transliteration, and the limited availability of annotated datasets. This research presents a new MuRIL-based framework that utilizes MuRIL, a transformer model pretrained on Indic languages, to tackle these issues. Our system achieves a remarkable accuracy of 92%, surpassing models like SVM + TF-IDF, BanglaBERT, and XLM-R. It performs particularly well in recognizing emotions such as anger, joy, and fear, while effectively managing code-mixed and transliterated Bangla text. This research emphasizes MuRIL's capabilities as a strong instrument for multilingual emotion classification in low-resource languages like Bangla. Notable applications of this framework encompass social media sentiment analysis, monitoring mental health, analyzing customer feedback, and real-time emotion detection in customer service and humancomputer interaction systems.

title: From Blueprint to Reality: A Practitioner’s Method for SysML Hardware and Software Deployment Tracking
authors: Adam W. Weissman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: In the development of complex systems-of-systems, the gap between “as-envisioned” objective architectures, “asdesigned” physical architecture and the “as-deployed” reality frequently drives integration failure and configuration drift, particularly when relying on fragmented, manual tracking methods. This paper proposes a method to bridge this gap by extending SysML Block Definition Diagrams (BDDs) to model physical instances of hardware and software, rather than just abstract types. By employing specialized stereotypes (e.g., <<Deployment>>, <<HW_Instance>>, <<SW_Version>>) to capture critical metadata like serial numbers and patch levels, the model serves as a dynamic single source of truth. Examples illustrate how this approach enables automatic validation of deployed configurations against design baselines, facilitates rapid impact analysis, and provides stakeholders with an unambiguous visualization of the system’s deployment.

title: Memory-Efficient Quantization-Aware Optimization for FHE-Based Mammogram Classification
authors: Mohamed Khattab, Lydia Bouzar-Benlabiod
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Privacy-preserving medical imaging through Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) is critical for secure data processing but is severely constrained by the massive computational overhead of encrypted domain operations. This study evaluates a privacy-preserving medical diagnostic system for mammograms using an optimized, FHE-native Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a high-performance compilation environment. We demonstrate a critical precision-latency trade-off: while a highprecision configuration achieves $89.68 \%$ accuracy, it necessitates an inference latency of $\mathbf{3 4}$ minutes. Conversely, a 3-bit quantized model maintains a suitable accuracy for secondary screening of $86.04 \%$ while reducing execution time to just 74.14 seconds. These results characterize the feasibility of “FHE-first” architectural designs, providing a framework for high-throughput, secure oncological data evaluation. This study investigates the structural and precision-based optimizations required to balance evaluation reliability with the hardware constraints of modern FHE compilers.

title: Design of UAV Altitude Controller Based on RBF Neural Network
authors: Shaoran Liang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: This paper proposes a RBF neural network-based altitude controller for small unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to address the altitude control problem during autonomous route flight. Taking the sliding mode variable structure controller as the baseline controller, the introduction of the RBF neural network enables real-time prediction of external disturbances. The sliding mode controller is constructed using the output of an RBF network. Simulation results show that, under significant external disturbances, the proposed controller achieves faster convergence speed, reduces system overshoot, and attenuates the chattering phenomenon in the controller output.

title: Smart Automated Chicken Brooder: IoT-Based Environmental Control, Scheduled Feeding, and Water Management
authors: Adith Saha Dipro, Ashir Intesher, Mushfika Ikfat, Fahim Jahan Mahmood
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Effective poultry brooding management during the initial two weeks directly influences uniform growth and chick survival, which further contributes to meat yield and egg output. This work presents a low-cost IoT-based automatic brooder system capable of maintaining optimal temperature and humidity, delivering scheduled feeding, and automatic refilling of drinking water. The system integrates an Arduino microcontroller as the main processing unit, interfaced with ESP8266 module for Wi-Fi connectivity with the Blynk cloud, a DHT11 sensor for reading temperature and humidity, a DC pump activated by a water-level sensor and a servo-driven feeder scheduled by a DS3231 real-time clock (RTC) module. Field tests in a mesh cage prototype produced stable temperature regulation with automatic actuation, and reliable water-level monitoring and feed dispensing twice daily with telemetry and manual override available via Blynk IoT smartphone app. This prototype demonstrates a practical and affordable IoT-based solution for small-scale poultry farmers.

title: Workload Dependent NBTI Aging Analysis in 2 nm GAA RISC-V ALU
authors: Chinmay Dhole, Megha S, Mainak Saha, Laxmeesha Somappa, Souvik Mahapatra, Veeresh Deshpande
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: Reliability is becoming an important design constraint in 2 nm node and beyond Gate-All-Around (GAA) FETs. Guardbanding with conventional AC stress may not allow efficient performance utilization. A real workload-dependent degradation prediction allows for estimating a realistic aging scenario and enabling maximum performance optimization. This work demonstrates a workload-aware reliability estimation by using gate-level workload stress waveforms to quantify delay degradation in a 2 nm GAA RISC-V ALU. Workload-aging of a section of the slowest data paths in the ALU demonstrates a significantly higher delay under workload stress compared to conventional 50 % AC stress. Notably, the workload-aware analysis induced a critical path reordering, where the path initially ranked as the second-largest delay surpassed the original critical path post-aging. In contrast, the conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> AC stress model maintained the original path hierarchy, failing to capture this workload-induced shift in the timing bottleneck. The work highlights need for workload-aging reliability constraints in timing analysis and for optimum guardbanding.

title: New Characterization and Understanding of PBTI Recovery Post Soft Breakdown in FinFETs
authors: Anyi Zhu, Zuoyuan Dong, Zirui Wang, Haorang Wang, Hongbo Wang, Lang Zeng, Runsheng Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This paper addresses the critical phenomenon of incomplete recovery behavior of Positive Bias-Temperature Instability (PBTI) in FinFETs after gate dielectric Soft Breakdown (SBD). Although numerous Time-Dependent Dielectric Breakdown (TDDB) tests have been performed on such devices, prior studies lack in-depth characterization of PBTI after SBD. For the first time, various types of traps involved before and after SBD are distinguished, using advanced Discharge-based Multi-Pulse (DMP) technique. The results indicate that the primary root cause of incomplete PBTI recovery is the generation of interface traps after SBD. We quantify the increase in interface traps aligning with theoretical predictions, and establishing a critical link between SBD-induced interface traps and PBTI recovery degradation.

title: Low-Cost, Open Personal Health Wearable for Sports Performance and Rehabilitation
authors: Narasimha Sai Yamanoor, Srihari Yamanoor
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Participation and success in sports used to be an exclusive domain, with only a few nations of certain wealth and resource levels able to succeed and dominate international events, including quadrennial events such as Olympics, World Cups in various sports and others with interim frequencies. As economies develop, sports enthusiasts have emerged and found growing success in various sports endeavors. A fundamental requirement of competitiveness in the world of sports is the ability to measure and understand one’s health metrics to benchmark and set goals for improvement and readying oneself for participation and ultimately success. Citizens of developing nations continue to be stymied by the expense of measuring and managing metrics, as well as locating appropriate healthcare consultants. The current work presents an effort at tackling metrics at a minimal expense to the user. The configuration presented will have a variety of sensors useful for the determination of health including IMU, Temperature Sensor, Heart Rate & Pulse Oximetry Sensor, and Blood Glucose Sensor. The design is open and therefore can be appropriately modified or expand sensory metrics as desired. A costed BOM for the prototype is presented to guide the understanding and development of such open products on a scale. The open design also provides opportunities for success in regional entrepreneurship and manufacturing initiatives such as Make in India and may be adopted elsewhere worldwide.

title: Refining Brain Tumour Segmentation in MRI: A Synergistic Approach with Modified FCM and JAYA Algorithm
authors: Minal Kalpesh Deore, S Sakkaravarthi, Lynsha Helena Pratheeba HP, R.Indumathi, Kottaimalai Ramaraj, M Thilagaraj
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In the cerebral cortex, gliomas are a particularly common primary tumor. In the field of clinical imaging, segmentation of brain tumors and identification are extremely difficult. In the healthcare sector, magnetic resonance image (MRI) delineation is essential and significant since it can aid in prognosis and diagnosis, general expansion forecasts, tumor intensity measurements, and individual plan of action. Since cerebral tumors can vary widely in terms of their constructions, forms, frequency, location, and visual appeal—such as extent, contrast, and visual variation—segmenting them can be challenging. The ability to analyze medical imagery is essential for helping people recognize various illnesses. One important aspect that relies on the doctor's competence and understanding is the categorizing of tumors in the brain. Employing visual instruments, physicians or other specialists manually partition the brain by defining tumors on clinical pictures. Although this method is labor-intensive and sluggish, it might be precise. Moreover, manually segmenting images is difficult to measure due to the growing need for clinical imaging and the shortage of skilled physicians. To assist doctors, a smart algorithm for identifying and categorizing brain lesions is necessary. An intriguing avenue for cerebral tumor study is autonomous dissection of medical images that could made possible by new advances in Deep Learning (DL) for tasks like image classification. To improve segmentation of tumors from MR cerebral scans, the suggested method combines the JAYA optimization algorithm with Modified Fuzzy C-Means (MFCM) clustering. The computerized approach that was built helps the professionals identify, define, and detect abnormalities in clinical imaging earlier.

title: Parkinson’s Disease Prediction using Time-Aware Transformer Networks on Longitudinal EHR Data
authors: J. Jamuna, K. Kasturi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a disorder that is progressive and neurodegenerative, and is often not diagnosed until considerable overt motor signs are present, so there is little value in beginning early interventions. The study presents a new machine learning framework that uses longitudinal Electronic Health Record (EHR) data to predict Parkinson’s Disease earlier and in an interpretable manner. Specifically, a built a Time-Aware Transformer (TAT) model that accounts for specific temporal dependencies and irregularities in the sequences of patient visits, as well as provides clinical concept embeddings with constructs extracted from medical ontologies. To make the most of the data available, we included a self-supervised pretraining step that allows the model to learn important patterns using a large virtual unlabeled EHR dataset. The model was trained to predict the outcome of PD within a 12-month time period, with different baseline models, which were logistic regression, random forests, GRUs, and Med-BERT, evaluated the performance of the original model in various ways. In the end the TAT model scored 0.89 AUROC and 0.58 AUPRC against the baselines in accuracy, recall, and F1. The TAT model could highlight important features such as early tremor, early diagnosis, and dopamine prescriptions that predicted PD. Overall, our results suggest that there is a capability for time-aware deep learning models to improve the early detection of Parkinson's Disease, which may assist clinicians in making appropriate timely decisions and has the potential to guide the creation of AI-based neurodegenerative disease monitoring in future.

title: Hysteresis-Driven Thermal Behavior and Geometry Effects of Multi-Tube PCM Tanks: A 2D Enthalpy Model with Tank-Scale Correlations
authors: Noureddine Itani, Nazih Moubayed, Chantal Maatouk, Rabih Dib
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper presents AHMT-2D, a two-dimensional axisymmetric enthalpy-based finite-difference model for paraffin-based latent heat thermal energy storage in a multi-tube cylindrical tank. Direction-dependent logistic hysteresis functions distinguish melting and solidification and capture asymmetric phase-change behavior. The model resolves coupled heat transfer among the heat-transfer fluid, tube wall, and PCM while accounting for geometry-dependent PCM mass scaling and volume-weighted energy evaluation. Results show that the multi-tube configuration enhances thermal response, achieving complete melting in about 10.2 h and solidification in about 10.5 h, with an approximately 7 °C shift between melting and solidification midpoints. Reduced-order correlations are proposed for tank-averaged liquid fraction, enthalpy, effective heat capacity, and melting-solidification time asymmetry. The framework provides a compact basis for system-level modeling of PCM storage in solar-assisted absorption cooling and related renewable thermal applications.

title: Spectral–Spatial Physics-Constrained Deep Neural Networks for Underwater Image Restoration and Semantic Perception
authors: Malathi V, Bharath K, Virubash Neppolian, Srijan P, Divyalakshmi P, Manimegalai C T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Capturing usable images beneath the water surface is harder than it looks. Wavelength-selective absorption drains red light within a few meters; suspended particles spread luminous energy into a grey-white veil of forward scattering; and shipboard or diver-mounted lights produce gradients of saturation that change from frame to frame. Individually, these effects challenge existing restoration algorithms. Taken together, they break most of them. Equally troublesome is the tendency of the research community to treat restoration and semantic perception as separate problems, a division that costs accuracy in both objectives. We address this gap by introducing the Spectral–Spatial Physics-Constrained Deep Neural Network (SS-PCDNN), a single trainable system that encodes the Jaffe-McGlamery formation model as differentiable operations within the forward pass. Doing so lets the network infer scene-specific wavelength attenuation coefficients and ambient background radiance from the image itself, with no external depth sensor or water-quality probe. Our architecture centers on a dual-branch Spectral-Spatial Fusion Block (SSFB). One branch corrects color using a Spectral Channel Attention (SCA) module; the latter suppresses spatial haze via a Dilated Residual Dense Network. A content-adaptive Cross-Modal Attention Gate (CMAG) evaluates local image statistics and blends the two branches dynamically, depending on whether color distortion or haze dominates locally. We evaluated SS-PCDNN on three well-established benchmarks—UIEB, UIEBC, and RUIE-and found it reached a UIQM of 3.79 and UCIQE of 0.678 on UIEB, placing it ahead of all compared methods. Joint training with a segmentation head yielded 74.0% mean IoU on the SUIM eight-class benchmark, a result that neither pure restoration nor pure perception baselines can match simultaneously.

title: An Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Dynamic Career Path Optimization
authors: Chunduru Anilkumar, Kanithi Gunasagar, Akasapu Gayatri, Sai Ganesh Padhy, Susarapu Kavya, Sathishkumar V E
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The accelerated change in technical positions has shown the constrainment of stagnant manually populated career advice, such as obsolete advice, imaginary resources, and insufficient personalization. The present paper gives an introduction of CareerFlow AI, a Gemini-3 based Agentic Retrieval- Augmented Generation (RAG) system providing verified and market-aligned career roadmaps with interactive skill verification. The architecture coordinates two-stream retrieval by integrating Google Search Grounding, due to which real-time verification is provided, and a Qdrant vector database, which has <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 M}$</tex> embedding of semantic matching. In an experiment of 150 learners in four experimental conditions, CareerFlow AI had hallucination rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 \%}(\mathbf{3} / \mathbf{1, 5 0 0}$</tex> recommendation), which is lower than 11.8% of a usual RAG baseline, and 9.2% of an ungrounded LLM. It cut end-to-end response time by 40 percent as compared to online platform baselines, and the roadmap generation was under 30 seconds. The system achieved 88% accuracy in prediction of time-tocompetency, which is defined through scenario-based Readiness Lab tests, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$68 \% 7$</tex>-day return rate, which is 2.2 times higher than the work with a static guidance. Ablation experiments also measure the role of search grounding, dual-stream retrieval and interactive validation to the reduction of hallucinations, latency and engagement.

title: Osteovision: An AI-Enabled Deep Learning and Diagnostic Analytics System for Osteoporosis Detection
authors: S. Arun Inigo, Tamilselvi Rajendran, Parisa Beham Mohammed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Osteoporosis is a persistent skeletal disorder marked by diminished bone mineral density (BMD) and deterioration of bone microarchitecture, resulting in heightened fracture risk and related morbidity. Dual-Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DEXA) is the recognized reference standard for evaluating osteoporosis. However, its limited accessibility, high operational costs, and dependence on specialized equipment constrain largescale population screening, particularly in low-resource clinical environments. Optimal bone delineation and reduced imaging variability across modalities are achieved through a standardized image preparation pipeline that includes noise reduction, contrast enhancement, and illumination normalization. To provide a comprehensive skeletal evaluation, the model is trained and tested on a curated dataset of X-ray and DEXA images from various anatomical regions, including the spine, hip, and knee. A web-based clinical interface built with Next.js leverages the trained model, enabling rapid image processing and immediate diagnostic reporting. The experimental results show that the proposed framework performs well across multiple imaging modalities, achieving an overall classification accuracy of 90%. This shows that it could be a useful tool for early osteoporosis screening because it is effective, easy to use, and supported by clinical evidence.

title: Evaluation of Flaxseed Extract-Based Hydrogel for Antibacterial Activity
authors: Nafisa Nawal, Sadia Islam Shuchi, Prapti Thapa, Maruf Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Wound healing is a critical challenge globally, especially in preventing bacterial infections. This study develops hydrogels incorporated with flaxseed extract for antibacterial wound healing. Hydrogel samples were prepared by chemically crosslinking polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) and gelatin, followed by soaking in pure and diluted flaxseed extracts, labelled HG-100, HG-50, HG-25, and HG-12.5. The samples were evaluated for swelling behaviour, moisture retention, water vapour transmission rate, scanning electron microscopy (SEM), Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), tensile strength, and antibacterial properties. The extract-loaded hydrogels’ wound-healing ability aligns with previously reported studies. Statistical analysis revealed that varying concentrations affected the swelling behaviour, moisture retention, water vapour transmission, and antibacterial properties. No distinct pores were visible in the SEM images, which is likely due to limitations in sample preparation. All hydrogels displayed strong mechanical properties, confirming their elasticity and flexibility. The successful integration of the bioactive compound lignan inside the hydrogel was validated by FTIR. Compared to other hydrogels, the HG-100 formulation showed the most antibacterial behaviour against Staphylococcus aureus, likely due to its higher concentration of bioactive compounds. To summarize, the hydrogels provide an in vitro basis for wound dressing applications; however, additional in vivo evaluations, cytocompatibility assessments, and drug release studies are necessary to establish their biological significance.

title: ECG-Based Cardiac Abnormality Identification with Customized Hybrid Lightweight Architecture
authors: Md. Abid Hasan Rafi, Semanto Sharma, Md Morsalin Islam, Nahid Islam, Pankaj Bhowmik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases are the leading cause of global mortality, highlighting an urgent need for rapid and accurate diagnostic tools. Although electrocardiography (ECG) serves as a primary non-invasive cardiac assessment method, manual interpretation remains time-consuming and subjective. This study presents an automated deep-learning framework for classifying cardiac conditions from ECG images. We introduce a custom lightweight hybrid architecture that integrates a pre-trained MobileNetV2 backbone with a dedicated convolutional branch to capture both general and precise morphological features. Our pipeline incorporates two public datasets covering Myocardial Infarction (MI), History of MI, Abnormal Heartbeat, and Normal conditions, enhanced through advanced contrast adjustment and tailored augmentation. A two-phase training strategy with weighted loss effectively addresses class imbalance and data constraints. The model achieves exceptional performance, with near-perfect results (Accuracy: 0.9973, AUC: 0.9999) on a four-class dataset and strong generalization (Accuracy: 0.9510) on a smaller three-class dataset. Integrated Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) provides transparent visual explanations, confirming clinically relevant feature detection in ST-segments and QRS complexes. This work bridges high-performance automation with clinical trust, delivering a reliable and interpretable tool suitable for integration into diagnostic systems and point-of-care applications.

title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Framework for Beamforming Management in 6G-IoT Networks
authors: M. Venkata Subbarao, P. Aasritha Ramani, Y. Veera Sai Sindhu, V. Sravya Lakshmi Tulasi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Beamforming optimization in 6G-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) networks remains challenging because of rapid environmental variations and device mobility. These factors often lead to beam misalignment and increased latency, and inefficient resource utilization. Conventional beamforming techniques based on exhaustive beam training, classical signal processing, or deep reinforcement learning often incur high computational complexity, limiting their suitability for real-time IoT scenarios. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of machine learning (ML) techniques for intelligent beamforming optimization using a computer vision assisted 6G IoT dataset that integrates network parameters, environmental factors, device characteristics, vision-derived features, and beamforming performance metrics. To improve prediction accuracy and reduce computational overhead, Chi-square (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\chi}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>), Analysis of Variance (ANOVA), and ReliefF feature selection algorithms are employed to generate optimized feature subsets by capturing statistical relevance, discriminative power, and neighborhood-based feature importance. These subsets are used to train and evaluate multiple classifiers, including Decision Trees(DT), Support Vector Machines (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Neural Networks, and Ensemble models. Experimental results demonstrate that tree-based and ensemble classifiers, particularly Bagged Trees, consistently achieve high accuracy up to 99.5% on test data and 99.6% during validation even with reduced feature sets. The results confirm that featureoptimized ML frameworks enable accurate, low-complexity, and energy efficient beamforming decisions, making the proposed approach well suited for real-time and edge enabled 6G IoT beam management applications.

title: AI-Driven Framework for Zero-Day Vulnerability Detection and Prevention in Application and Cloud Environments
authors: Abdul Jaleel D, Krishna Sai J, Manikandan R, Dinesh K, Shankar M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Zero-day vulnerabilities are difficult to identify in modern software systems because exploit triggers are often novel, semantics-aware, and spread across both application logic and cloud execution contexts. This paper proposes an AI-driven autonomous discovery framework that prioritizes zero-day detection while preserving high quality forensic evidence at the moment a fault is surfaced. The framework integrates a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to guide semantic input exploration with an instrumented execution harness that supports hybrid monitoring and crash-resilient evidence logging. The design enables consistent analysis across local applications and cloud-hosted services, allowing vulnerability signals to be captured from heterogeneous runtime behaviors. In the implementation, the autonomous discovery engine executed 268,435,456 cycles and revealed 13 distinct logic- and memory-related failure modes, including high-severity deserialization driven faults and deep semantic gate violations.

title: Predicting Electromagnetic Emissions from Cable Configurations Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Zaynab Hamyeh, Moncef Kadi, Mahmoud Mehdi, Ali Alaeddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Radiated electromagnetic emission from cable harnesses poses a major challenge in the electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) design of modern electronic systems, particularly in automotive and hybrid electric vehicle applications. This paper considers a representative cable configuration used in hybrid vehicles to connect the battery to DC/DC converters, for which radiated electric field levels constitute a critical EMC concern. A hybrid data-driven framework based on deep neural networks (DNNs), combining spectrum-based and pointwise prediction models, is proposed for the efficient estimation of frequency-domain radiated electric fields. The framework is applied to predict the total, horizontal, and vertical components of the radiated electric field. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves high accuracy while reducing computation time compared to full-wave electromagnetic simulations.

title: VARENet: Variance-Aware Residual Enhancement Network for Microscopic Fungal Classification
authors: Mahamodul Hasan Mahadi, Souhardo Rahman, Md. Nasif Safwan, Md. Iftekharul Mobin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Superficial fungal infections are widespread and require reliable diagnosis for effective treatment, yet conventional microscopic examination is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to interobserver variation. Deep learning approaches have shown promise for fungal image analysis, but existing attention mechanisms often fail to capture channel variability, limiting their ability to discriminate subtle morphological patterns. To address this gap, we propose VARENet, a variance-aware residual enhancement attention module integrated with the EfficientNetV2S backbone for microscopic fungal classification. VARENet uses variance-based channel weighting and residual fusion to strengthen feature representation and improve stability. Experiments on a large collection of labeled microscopic images demonstrated that the proposed model achieved an overall accuracy of 92.98%, outperforming the baseline and conventional attention mechanisms. Grad-CAM++ visualizations indicate that VARENet focuses on diagnostically significant fungal structures, highlighting its interpretability. This study provides a reliable deep learning framework for fungal image classification with potential clinical utility.

title: Carbon-Aware Dynamic Workload Scheduling for Large Language Model Training with Real-Time Energy Optimization
authors: Judy Simon, Nellore Kapileswar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The intense development of large language models has significantly increased the amount of energy and carbon footprint of cloud-based training. This work presents a framework known as Carbon-Aware Dynamic Workload Scheduling (CADWS) that enables the reduction of the environmental footprint of the large-scale model training process through the coordination of the computational workloads with the times of reduced carbon intensity. The framework brings in live energy tracking, short-horizon carbon-intensity prediction and workload orchestration adaptation to dynamically control training run-time in maintaining the underlying model architectures. Empirical assessment in realistic conditions in a cloud environment proves that the proposed approach can reach an area under a curve of the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) of 0.93, which is 22 % points higher than a carbon-agnostic baseline. Furthermore, CADWS achieves the decrease of the overall carbon emissions up to 40 % and reduction of the total energy expenditure up to about 16 % and planning precision is reduced to about 97 %. These results indicate predictive and carbon-aware scheduling is a viable and feasible strategy towards attaining sustainable large language model training in modern cloud systems.

title: Fluid Antenna Aided Beam Split Mitigation in Wideband Terahertz Communications
authors: Parthiban. I, H. H. Sidhwa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) communication provides ultra-wide bandwidth and promote applications like augmented reality and virtual surgery. The THz communication system provides wider bandwidth at the expense of extensive path loss, restricted dispersion and hardware limitations. For proper alignment and focusing of THz signal to a far distance and to improve the signal strength, deployment of massive MIMO or Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces in THz communication system becomes the viable solutions. Beam split is a phenomenon that arises due to improper alignment of the transmitted beams across subcarriers. In this paper to accomplish robust beam alignment, a receiver architecture based on Fluid Antenna Systems is proposed. Unlike conventional fixed-position antennas that suffer from gain loss when the beam drifts, the proposed system dynamically adjusts the physical position of the antenna elements to track the frequency-dependent beam direction. Further to reduce the implementation complexity, a low-complex Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm is proposed and compared with Grid Search and Steepest Descent Algorithms. The performance metrics of the proposed system is compared with the conventional fixed antenna systems.

title: Discontinuous Integro-Differential Equations and Sliding Mode Control
authors: Andrey Polyakov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: The paper deals with analysis and design of sliding mode control systems modeled by finite-dimensional integro-differential equations. Filippov method and equivalent control approach are extended to a class of nonlinear discontinuous integro-differential equations and to a class of control systems modeled by infinite-dimensional differential equations in Banach spaces. Sliding mode control algorithms are designed for distributed input delay systems and for a heat control system.

title: An Almost Global Attitude Controller for Rigid Bodies Subject to Unknown Disturbances and Sensor Bias
authors: César Cruz-Hernández, Javier Pliego-Jiménez, César Cruz-Hernández
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper studies the attitude trajectory tracking problem of rigid bodies subject to unknown constant disturbances and angular velocity measurements corrupted by constant bias. We employ the exponential coordinates of rotation and the angular velocity to describe the state of the rigid body. Exploiting the structural properties of the attitude kinematics and dynamics of the rigid body, we propose a bias observer that compensates for biased measurements and a nonlinear control law with integral action that achieves attitude tracking for almost all initial conditions and compensates for constant disturbances. We design the control law based on a Barrier Lyapunov function to circumvent the exponential coordinates' singularity. Finally, numerical simulations and experimental tests illustrate the efficacy of our control law and bias observer.

title: Decentralized nonconvex optimization with linear convergence and quantization enabled privacy preservation
authors: Xiao-Yu Yue, Xiao-Kang Liu, Yan-Wu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Nonconvexity, communication compression, and privacy preservation are important and challenging problems in decentralized optimization. Existing decentralized nonconvex algorithms usually treat privacy and communication separately, such as introducing quantizers for communication compression and differential privacy for privacy preservation. However, they do not consider the role of quantization for privacy preservation, which causes a waste of privacy budget. To solve this problem, we propose a Quantization-based Privacy-preserving Alternating Direction Method of Multiplier (QP-ADMM), which ensures strict differential privacy and communication compression both by quantization. We show that QP-ADMM can obtain first-order stationary points at the sublinear convergence rate <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(1/T)$</tex-math></inline-formula> in expectation even in the nonconvex case, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$T$</tex-math></inline-formula> is the number of iterations, and obtain the optimal value at the linear convergence rate under the additional Polyak-Lojasiewicz condition. Comparative simulations show the superiority of the proposed algorithm over the existing ones.

title: mmHSense: Multi-Modal and Distributed mmWave ISAC Datasets for Human Sensing
authors: Nabeel Nisar Bhat, Maksim Karnaukh, Stein Vandenbroeke, Wouter Lemoine, Jakob Struye, Siddhartha Kumar, Mohammad Hossein Moghaddam, Jesus Omar Lacruz, Joerg Widmer, Rafael Berkvens, Jeroen Famaey
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article presents mmHSense, a set of six labeled mmWave datasets to support human sensing research within Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) systems. The datasets can be used to explore mmWave ISAC for various end applications such as gesture recognition, person identification, pose estimation, and localization. Moreover, the datasets can be used to develop and advance signal processing and deep learning research on mmWave ISAC. This article describes the testbed, experimental settings, and signal features for each dataset. The datasets cover both Wi-Fi and 5G signals. Furthermore, the utility of the datasets is demonstrated through validation on different downstream tasks such as pose estimation, gesture recognition, localization, and person identification. In addition, we demonstrate the use of parameter-efficient fine-tuning to adapt ISAC models to different tasks, significantly reducing computational complexity while maintaining performance on prior tasks.

title: Normalized Maximum Likelihood Code-Length on Riemannian Manifold Data Spaces
authors: Kota Fukuzawa, Atsushi Suzuki, Kenji Yamanishi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: In recent years, with the large-scale expansion of graph data, there has been an increased focus on Riemannian manifold data spaces other than Euclidean space. In particular, the development of hyperbolic spaces has been remarkable, and they have high expressive power for graph data with hierarchical structures. Normalized Maximum Likelihood (NML) is employed in regret minimization and model selection. However, existing formulations of NML have been developed primarily in Euclidean spaces and are inherently dependent on the choice of coordinate systems, making it non-trivial to extend NML to Riemannian manifolds. In this study, we define a new NML that reflects the geometric structure of Riemannian manifolds, called the Riemannian manifold NML (Rm-NML). This Rm-NML is invariant under coordinate transformations and coincides with the conventional NML under the natural parameterization in Euclidean space. We extend existing computational techniques for NML to the setting of Riemannian manifolds. Furthermore, we derive a method to simplify the computation of Rm-NML on Riemannian symmetric spaces, which encompass data spaces of growing interest such as hyperbolic spaces. To illustrate the practical application of our proposed method, we explicitly computed the Rm-NML for normal distributions on hyperbolic spaces.

title: A Neural Network Method for Land Surface Temperature and Emissivity Retrieval from Hyperspectral Thermal Infrared Data by Incorporating Prior Surface Knowledge
authors: Xiu-Juan Li, Li Ni, Yuan-Liang Cheng, Lei Lei, Xing-Xing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Land Surface Temperature (LST) and land surface emissivity (LSE) are the core parameters in thermal infrared (TIR) remote sensing research, which plays crucial roles in surface energy balance, climate change studies, vegetation monitoring, and agricultural management. With advances in remote sensing sensors, hyperspectral thermal infrared (HTIR) remote sensing has created new possibilities for LST and LSE retrieval. However, the rich spectral information provided by HTIR data remains underutilized. To address this, this study proposed a neural network method by incorporating prior surface knowledge for the simultaneous retrieval of LST, LSE, atmospheric transmittivity, upwelling radiance, and downwelling radiance. The method enhanced input features by incorporating prior surface knowledge with a split-window-like method and reduced the dimensionality of output space with principal component analysis (PCA), thereby improving the efficiency of the artificial neural network (ANN) retrieval process. Results demonstrated that an RMSE of 1.5 K for LST and 0.011 for LSE were achieved by the proposed method, while also improving the spectral shape rationality of LSE. Moreover, cross-validation further confirmed that the retrieval results aligned well with existing products. Overall, this study successfully improves the information efficiency of machine learning models, providing a promising technical framework for the land surface and atmospheric parameters estimation from HTIR remote sensing data.

title: First Demonstration of Airborne Quad-Pol SAR with Quasi-Orthogonal FM Coded Waveforms for Ambiguity Suppression
authors: Guodong Jin, Tao Zhou, Yunkai Deng, Daiyin Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The range and azimuth ambiguity suppression issues are a technical challenge for the synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems. The cross-polarized channels of quadrature-polarimetric (quad-pol) SARs are subject to more serious range/azimuth ambiguities compared with the single polarization SAR system since their ambiguities stem from the co-polarized echoes, and the echo signal received in cross-polarized channels is significantly weaker than that in co-polarized channels. To this end, advanced quasi-orthogonal frequency modulation(FM) codes are developed in this paper, and compared with the other quasi orthogonal waveforms (e.g., up-down chirp waveforms), they own two important characteristics: 1) low auto-correlation sidelobes, which can ensure a well range imaging performance; 2) low integral cross-correlation energy (ICCE), which can ensure the total ambiguity energy in a SAR image is effectively suppressed; 3) parameterized coding structure, which is beneficial for the generation of this FM coded waveform in real-time on the resource-constrained spaceborne SAR system. Moreover, well designed transmission schemes exploiting the proposed wave forms are presented and further applied in a real airborne quad pol SAR system with a bandwidth of 500 MHz and a carrier frequency of 35 GHz for the first time. The imaging results of the cross-polarized channel demonstrate that, compared with using the up-down chirp waveforms, the proposed method suppresses range ambiguity energy by over 4.0 dB for traditional quad-pol SAR and azimuth ambiguity energy by over 4.0 dB for hybrid quad-pol SAR. Besides, the proposed methods do not increase system and processing complexity; therefore, the improvement of 4.0 dB ambiguity suppression rate is very attractive for the SAR system, especially for the high-resolution and wide-swath (HRWS) spaceborne SAR systems.

title: Dynamic Energy-Saving Design for Double-Faced Active RIS-Assisted Communications With Imperfect CSI
authors: Yang Cao, Wenchi Cheng, Jingqing Wang, Wei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Although the emerging reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) paves a new way for next-generation wireless communications, it suffers from inherent flaws, i.e., double-fading attenuation effects and half-space coverage limitations. The state-of-the-art double-face active (DFA)-RIS architecture is proposed for significantly amplifying and transmitting incident signals in full-space. Despite the efficacy of DFA-RIS in mitigating the aforementioned flaws, its potential drawback is that the complex active hardware also incurs intolerable energy consumption. To overcome this drawback, in this paper we propose a novel dynamic energy-saving design for the DFA-RIS, called the sub-array based DFA-RIS architecture. This architecture divides the DFA-RIS into multiple sub-arrays, where the signal amplification function in each sub-array can be activated/deactivated dynamically and flexibly. Utilizing the above architecture, we develop the joint optimization scheme based on transmit beamforming, DFA-RIS configuration, and reflection amplifier (RA) operating pattern to maximize the energy efficiency (EE) of the DFA-RIS assisted multiuser multiple-input-single-output (MISO) system considering the imperfect channel state information (CSI) case. Then, the constrained stochastic majorization-minimization (CSMM) based AO algorithm address non-convex problems. Simulation results verified that our proposed sub-array based DFA-RIS architecture can benefit the EE of the system more than other RIS architectures.

title: VaN3Twin: the Multi-Technology V2X Digital Twin with Ray Tracing in the Loop
authors: Roberto Pegurri, Diego Gasco, Francesco Linsalata, Marco Rapelli, Eugenio Moro, Francesco Raviglione, Claudio Casetti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper presents VaN3Twin—the first open source, full-stack Network Digital Twin (NDT) framework for simulating the coexistence of multiple Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication technologies with accurate physical-layer modeling via ray tracing. VaN3Twin extends the ms-van3t simulator by integrating Sionna Ray Tracer (RT) in the loop, enabling high-fidelity representation of wireless propagation, including diverse Line of Sight (LoS) conditions with a focus on LoS blockage due to other vehicles’ meshes, Doppler effect, and site-dependent effects—e.g., scattering and diffraction. Unlike conventional simulation tools, the proposed framework supports realistic coexistence analysis across Dedicated Short Range Communication (DSRC) and Cellular-V2X (C-V2X) technologies operating over a shared spectrum. A dedicated interference tracking module captures cross-technology interference at the time-frequency resource block level and enhances Signal to Interference plus Noise Ratio (SINR) estimation by eliminating artifacts such as the bimodal behavior induced by separate LoS/NLoS propagation models. Compared to field measurements, VaN3Twin reduces application-layer disagreement by 50% in rural and over 70% in urban environments with respect to current state of the art simulation tools, demonstrating its value for scalable and accurate digital twin–based V2X coexistence simulation.

title: Wireless Communication for Low-Altitude Economy With UAV Swarm Enabled Two-Level Movable Antenna System
authors: Haiquan Lu, Yong Zeng, Shaodan Ma, Bin Li, Shi Jin, Rui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) is regarded as a key enabling platform for low-altitude economy, due to its advantages such as three-dimensional (3D) maneuverability, flexible deployment, and line-of-sight (LoS) air-to-air/ground communication links. In particular, the intrinsic high mobility renders UAV especially suitable for operating as a movable antenna (MA) from the sky. In this paper, by exploiting the flexible mobility of UAV swarm and antenna position adjustment of MA, we propose a novel UAV swarm enabled two-level MA system, where UAVs not only individually deploy a local MA array, but also form a larger-scale MA system with their individual MA arrays via swarm coordination. We formulate a general optimization problem to maximize the minimum achievable rate over all ground user equipments (UEs), by jointly optimizing the 3D UAV swarm placement positions, their individual MAs’ positions (or local positions), and receive beamforming for different UEs. To gain useful insights, we first consider the special case where each UAV has only one antenna, under different scenarios of one single UE, two UEs, and arbitrary number of UEs. In particular, for the two-UE case, we derive the optimal UAV swarm placement positions in closed-form that achieves inter-UE interference (IUI)-free communication when the uniform plane wave (UPW) model holds, where the UAV swarm forms a uniform sparse array (USA) satisfying minimum safe distance constraint. While for the general case with arbitrary number of UEs, we propose an efficient alternating optimization algorithm to solve the formulated non-convex optimization problem. Then, we extend the results to the case where each UAV is equipped with multiple antennas. Numerical results verify that the proposed low-altitude UAV swarm enabled MA system significantly outperforms various benchmark schemes, thanks to the exploitation of two-level mobility to create more favorable channel conditions for multi-UE communications.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Decoupling Capacitor Optimization in Post-Layout PDNs Using Transformer Architecture
authors: Haran Manoharan, Hanfeng Wang, Chulsoon Hwang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Power Integrity
abstract: In this paper, a novel transformer-based reinforcement learning framework is proposed for post-layout PDN (Power Distribution Network) decoupling capacitor (decap) optimization. The model is designed to be scalable and generalizable to any number of impedance observation ports, decap placement ports, and candidate capacitors. The proposed approach uses a sequential transformer decoder that learns to assign capacitors port-by-port, guided by impedance deviation and port priority features derived from the PDN's frequency-dependent impedance response. At each step, the model embeds the current system state, including the delta between observed and target impedance, and uses attention to select the most suitable decap from a candidate library. The model is trained via reinforcement learning, where the policy is optimized to minimize impedance violations and reduce capacitor usage. The architecture supports rapid adaptation to new decap libraries. Compared to traditional optimization techniques such as Genetic Algorithms (GA), which may require hours, the proposed model produces high-quality results in seconds, while maintaining adaptability across varying PDN configurations. The results demonstrate the model's robustness in handling real-world PDN scenarios with diverse port and component configurations.

title: A KAN-Based Decoupled Equalization Framework Towards Latency-Optimized 6G Optical Transport Systems
authors: Tianqi Zheng, Shen Hu, Kaihui Wang, Jiayi Li, Yifan Chen, Yuxiang Liu, Mingjie Zhu, Yujie Gong, Chengzhen Bian, Jianjun Yu
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: The demand for high throughput and low latency in 6G optical networks requires efficient equalization of complex impairments. Although neural network (NN) equalizers perform well, their computational complexity hinders real-time field programmable gate array (FPGA) deployment. This paper presents a novel equalization structure that decouples network updating from real-time inference by compressing layer computations into lookup table (LUT) mappings. Based on Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) theory, the design achieves effective nonlinear equalization with low overhead. Implemented on FPGA via parallel LUT mapping, it attains ultra-low latency while improving sensitivity. Experimental results on a terahertz-over-fiber testbed show a 1.2 dB sensitivity gain at the HD-FEC threshold with only 8.53 ns latency, demonstrating strong potential for high-speed, latency-sensitive optical systems.

title: Compatibility Study of the LiteBIRD SQUID Controller Unit With a Dual-Stage SQUID Amplification Chain
authors: Mario De Lucia, Paolo Dal Bo, Andrea Tartari, Jean-Francois Cliche, Giulia Conenna, Gabriele Coppi, Eugenia Di Giorgi, Matt Dobbs, Tommaso Lari, Maurizio Massa, Andrea Moggi, Joshua Montgomery, Donato Nicolò, Michele Pinchera, Graeme Smecher, Mario Zannoni, Giovanni Signorelli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The LiteBIRD satellite will map the polarisation of the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) with unprecedented sensitivity by means of antenna-coupled transition edge sensors (TESs). Its current design features single SQUID-Array Amplifiers (SAA) at a temperature of about 400 mK in order to read out up to 64 TESs in a frequency division multiplexing scheme. The front-end part of the electronics (the SQUID Controller Assembly -SCA) was designed to handle this configuration. Nevertheless, alternative configurations of the experiment are being studied that might require a dual-stage SQUID (DSS) amplification chain in order to meet thermal and noise requirements. In light of this possibility, we have successfully investigated the feasibility of controlling two individual SAAs in a DSS Amplifier (DSSA) configuration with two independent channels of the present SCA. The SQUID directly connected to the sensor, commonly referred to as ‘front-end’, behaves as an emitter-follower, or buffer, circuit and it decouples the impedance of the TES(s) from the rest of the readout chain whilst producing a moderate amplification. The second SQUID, ‘booster’, is responsible for a high-gain amplification of the signal and can be thermally decoupled from the front-end allowing it to dissipate a lot more power and thus exhibiting a higher gain. We characterised a breadboard model in which two SQUID amplifiers are cascaded but hosted on the same circuit board and thus not thermally decoupled. This was intended to perform functional tests and to assess the feasibility of controlling a DSSA without introducing major changes in the already developed LiteBIRD SCA. We present the typical I-V and I-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Phi$</tex-math></inline-formula> curves of the two SQUIDs along with the characterisation of the DSSA in terms of bandwidth, linearity and total harmonic distortion (THD).

title: SAGD: Boundary-Enhanced Segment Anything in 3D Gaussian via Gaussian Decomposition
authors: Xu Hu, Yuxi Wang, Lue Fan, Chuanchen Luo, Junsong Fan, Zhen Lei, Qing Li, Junran Peng, Zhaoxiang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting has emerged as an alternative 3D representation for novel view synthesis, benefiting from its high-quality rendering results and real-time rendering speed. However, the 3D Gaussians learned by 3D-GS have ambiguous structures without any geometry constraints. This inherent issue in 3D-GS leads to a rough boundary when segmenting individual objects. To remedy these problems, we propose SAGD, a conceptually simple yet effective boundary-enhanced segmentation pipeline for 3D-GS to improve segmentation accuracy while preserving segmentation speed. Specifically, we introduce a Gaussian Decomposition scheme, which ingeniously utilizes the special structure of 3D Gaussians, finds out, and then decomposes the boundary Gaussians. Moreover, to achieve fast interactive 3D segmentation, we introduce a novel training-free pipeline by lifting a 2D foundation model to 3D-GS. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves high-quality 3D segmentation without rough boundary issues, which can be easily applied to other scene editing tasks. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/XuHu0529/SAGS.

title: Guest Editorial Introduction to the Special Issue on Federated Learning and Digital Twins for Intelligent Transportation System
authors: Kuo-Hui Yeh, Yong Xiang, Yingjiu Li, Chien-Ming Chen, Muhammad Waqas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems

title: IT-Enabled Smart Workplaces and Employee Wellbeing: Pathways to Socially Responsible Management
authors: Prerana Baber, Mini Agrawal, Abhilasha Dixit, Ruturaj Baber, Ujjwal Kumar Pathak
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The study has uniquely contributed to the literature by offering a configurational perspective on employee wellbeing instead of linear models and analyzed configurational conditions that lead to a high level of employee wellbeing. This study investigated the combination of three variables i.e., smart workplace, job autonomy and organizational support, to predict employee wellbeing. fsQCA technique (Fuzzy-set qualitative comparative analysis) has been used to explore the different combinations research. Responses from 339 employees employed in an IT-enabled manufacturing environment have been collected. Findings revealed smart workplace management as a strong predictor of employee wellbeing. Whereas a combination of smart workplace and job autonomy or organizational support predicts equal employee wellbeing. A combination of all three variables predicts the highest level of employee wellbeing. The results highlight the importance of a systems thinking approach in the design of work environments where flexible configurations tailored to organizational context and employee needs can foster engagement and satisfaction.

title: Automated Classification of Brain Tumor Based on MRI Images Using Deep Learning
authors: Somaia Sarmin Shouma, Md. Rubel Miah, Shadhin Ahmed, Md. Ariful Islam, Ragib Raiyan, Md. Saymon Ahammad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: This study proposes a deep learning-based approach to classifying brain tumors using MRI images to enhance accuracy and stability for different kinds of tumors. Three new datasets from Mendeley Data are used: D1 (PMRAM: Bangladeshi Brain Cancer – MRI Dataset), D2 (Brain Tumor MRI Dataset with four types: Meningioma, Pituitary, Glioma, and No Tumor), and D3 (Brain Tumor Data). For training and testing purposes, D1 and D2 were combined to form a dataset of 13,664 MRI images (Meningioma: 3209, Glioma: 4173, Pituitary: 3450, No Tumor: 2832). D3 was used as an external validation set of 7023 MRI images (Meningioma: 1645, Glioma: 1621, Pituitary: 1757, No Tumor: 2000). A few of the state-of-the-art architectures—VGG19, InceptionV3, Xception, MobileNetV2, ResNet50V2, and DenseNet201—were compared against the proposed hybrid model, BrainNet. BrainNet achieved the highest model accuracy of 99% on the combined dataset and provided exceptionally well performance on the external validation set with an overall accuracy of 98.18%. It recorded the least error values among all models with MSE 0.0184, RMSE 0.1358, and MAE 0.0128, while other models achieved VGG19: 93.12%, InceptionV3: 97.16%, Xception: 97.41%, MobileNetV2: 97.87%, ResNet50V2: 98%, and DenseNet201: 98.22%. These results confirm the efficacy and accuracy of BrainNet in computerized brain tumor diagnosis and its feasibility in the clinical environment.

title: Synergistic Optimization of Solar Photovoltaic Generation and Energy Storage Deployment for Smart Grid Performance Enhancement
authors: Duy C. Huynh, Viet D. Q. Nguyen, Loc D. Ho, Phat T. Nguyen, Corina Barbalata, Matthew W. Dunnigan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources in smart grids requires effective planning strategies to reduce power losses and maintain voltage stability. This paper proposes a synergistic optimization framework for coordinated deployment of solar photovoltaic (PV) generation and battery energy storage systems (BESS) to enhance smart grid performance. The optimization problem is formulated to minimize total active power losses under operational and voltage constraints and is solved using an advanced artificial bee colony (ABC) algorithm with chaotic initialization. Numerical simulations on the IEEE 33-bus smart grid demonstrate that coordinated solar PV-BESS integration significantly outperforms solar PV-only deployment, achieving a maximum loss reduction of 65.84% while satisfying all voltage limits. Comparative results with GA, PSO, and the conventional ABC algorithm confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach for smart grid planning applications.

title: Eye Gaze Tracking System for Behavioural Analysis in Autism Support
authors: Kavitha R, Uma Barathi U, Thuriya M, Santhosh Raj V, Thamizhselvan I
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by impairments in social interaction and communicational abilities. Eye gaze atypical behaviour is one of the first behavioural features of autism. In this paper, a webcam based, non-invasive, eye gaze tracking system to support behavioural analysis during the autism support initiative is presented. The system suggested uses the computer vision methods to identify facial marks, monitor the eye movements and gaze patterns using Python and OpenCV. The duration of fixation, the distribution of gaze, and visual attention patterns are examined and presented as eye-gaze characteristics that can be used to assess the behaviour. Typical and atypical gaze behaviors are visualized using scatter plots and heatmaps. The suggested method is a facilitating tool to support early behaviour analysis of autism in a cost-efficient and accessible fashion without having to use specialized equipment in eye tracking.

title: Analysis and Mitigation of Pose Estimation Uncertainty on SE(3) for Magnetic Localization
authors: Pingyu Xiang, Hongye Zhang, Yue Wang, Rong Xiong, Haojian Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Robotics
abstract: Magnetic localization, owing to its immunity to line-of-sight occlusion and non-contact nature, is considered a promising technology for medical applications. While it is intuitive that localization performance degrades as the target moves farther from the sensor array, uncertainty analysis has long been overlooked, which is essential for quantifying localization quality. In this work, we present a pose estimation and uncertainty analysis framework on SE(3) for magnetic source localization using sensor arrays, which enables concise formulation of the problem and quantitative assessment of the results. The volume of the uncertainty ellipsoid is used to characterize localization confidence, while the surface shape in Cartesian space is used for visualization. This also provides insight into the effective workspace of the magnetic localization system prior to deployment. Guided by this analysis, we designed a movable magnetic sensor array to expand the limited sensing volume and mitigate localization uncertainty, thereby enhancing overall localization performance.
