title: Economic Optimal Dispatch of Integrated Energy System Considering Renewable Energy Output Level and Energy Storage Configuration
authors: Lei Chen, Yongqiang Zhu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: The study develops an optimal dispatch model for an electricity-heat-gas integrated energy system considering renewable energy output level and energy storage configuration. With the objective of minimizing the total operating cost of the system, the model comprehensively accounts for energy procurement cost, operation and maintenance cost, and penalties for wind and photovoltaic curtailment, thereby establishing an economic optimal dispatch framework for the integrated energy system. Four scenarios are designed for comparative analysis, namely, no energy storage, only electric storage, only heat storage, and combined electric and heat storage. The results show that, as renewable energy output level increases, the role of energy storage in reducing system operating cost and curtailment rate becomes more pronounced. Electric storage performs better than heat storage in both cost reduction and the improvement of renewable energy accommodation. Further analysis indicates that the effectiveness of heat storage is strongly constrained by electricity-to-heat conversion capability, and its performance is closely related to the capacity configuration of the electric boiler. The findings of this study can provide a useful reference for energy storage configuration and optimal dispatch of integrated energy systems under high renewable energy output level.

title: Research on the Construction of Rooftop Photovoltaic Systems in the Textile Industry
authors: Puyu Gu, Guiying Liu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Using the 3.9 MWp rooftop PV project at Zigong Hengxinchang Textile as a case study, this paper analyzes key electrical design aspects: inverter selection, string sizing, cable configuration, and grid connection. Optimizations improve efficiency and reduce losses. Estimated annual generation of 3.8 million kWh saves 1,397.45 tons of coal and cuts CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions by 1,660.7 tons, demonstrating economic and environmental benefits. The results serve as a reference for similar textile rooftop PV designs.

title: Smart Process Optimization in Apparel Manufacturing Through Value Stream Mapping and Integrated Root Cause Analysis
authors: Md. Saiful Islam Seam, Shezan Ahmed, Md. Foysal Uddin, Nasif Morshed, Altaf Hussain Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: In the apparel manufacturing industry, reducing lead time and minimizing waste are crucial for maintaining competitiveness and ensuring an efficient workflow. However, operational inefficiencies, such as excessive work-in-process (WIP) and inadequate process flow, affect productivity and market responsiveness in Bangladesh's garment sector facilities. This study focuses on identifying key inefficiencies in production systems and guiding improvement initiatives to achieve reduced lead times and streamlined operations compared to traditional methods. To identify the root causes of these inefficiencies, Fishbone Diagram, Fault Tree Analysis and 5-Why techniques were implemented. A case study was conducted in a major garment facility in Bangladesh using detailed time-study and observational data. Then, a detailed current state VSM was developed and revealed a high level of lead time, high WIP levels, manual handling, lack of standardization and poor process flow. Based on these insights, a future state map was developed incorporating lean interventions, including workstation realignment, WIP reduction, standardized task sequences, operator cross-training and implementation of 5S methodology. These changes led to a 33.8% reduction in total lead time, a 39.8% improvement in processing time and significant decreases in unnecessary motion and waiting times. Ultimately, this study emphasizes the importance of identifying systemic inefficiencies and applying targeted interventions to gradually but significantly enhance productivity and sustain operational performance in the apparel manufacturing industry.

title: Machine Learning and IoT Based Automated Remote Health Monitoring and Alert System
authors: Md. Nagib Mahfuz, Sabbir Ahmed, Md. Shahriar Hasan, Md. Hamidur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies has significantly transformed healthcare monitoring, enabling continuous, real-time, and remote patient observation. This paper presents the design and implementation of a low-cost, portable IoT-based patient health monitoring system. The system integrates multiple biomedical sensors to measure vital parameters such as ECG, SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, heart rate, body temperature, and fall detection into a portable, cost-effective, and scalable platform. The system employs ESP32 as the central controller and supports both local (OLED/TFT displays) and remote (cloud-based dashboards and Telegram alerts) visualization. Safety features such as toxic gas detection and smoke detection as well as ambient temperature and humidity are incorporated to enhance patient well-being, particularly for elderly and chronically ill individuals. Furthermore, a machine learning model deployed via Google Colab performs automated ECG classification, achieving a validation accuracy of 94.23%, enabling early detection of cardiac anomalies. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy across physiological parameter measurements (>97%) and fast alert delivery (<5 seconds), confirming the system's potential as a reliable solution for home-based and rural healthcare applications.

title: Multiscale Characterization for Physical Analysis of Time-Dependent Dielectric Breakdown in Ultrathin Dielectrics of FinFETs
authors: Zuoyuan Dong, Xiaomei Li, Zixuan Sun, Zirui Wang, Lan Li, Peng Xu, Yu Yao, Runsheng Wang, Xing Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: The time-dependent dielectric breakdown (TDDB) is a critical reliability concern in modern FinFET technology. This work presents a comprehensive multiscale characterization of the dielectric breakdown process in FinFETs from microscale to nanoscale. By combining focused ion beam (FIB), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS), and electron energy loss spectroscopy (EELS), we directly visualize the structural and compositional evolution from soft to hard breakdown. Our results reveal the progressive degradation of the gate dielectric on a single fin, culminating in a breakdown path. EELS analysis further identifies significant chemical changes, such as oxygen vacancies, localized at the breakdown site. The multiscale characterization physical analysis methods provide complementary structural information. This offers insights into the microscopic mechanisms of dielectric failure in three-dimensional (3D) semiconductor devices.

title: An IoT-Enabled Conveyor Belt System with LDR-Based Position Sensing and Speed Control for Industrial Automation
authors: M. Humayet Islam, S.M. Abdullah Al Muhib, Rejowan Arifin Nayeem, Sabbir Hossain Durjoy, Md. Emon Shikder, Sumaiya Khanam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: In this paper, a real-time object detection and position tracking belt conveyor system using LDR sensors was designed based on IoT at minimum cost. The system that has been implemented on a wooden frame with rope belt and Arduino Uno is a 12 V DC motor with speed control and LDR sensors with LED indication to precisely sense objects of varying shapes and size in different positions. In contrast to the traditional fixed-speed system, the proposed system can dynamically adjust its speed without influencing the detection performance. It's simple, modular, and flexible design is well-suited for factory simulations and applications like object counting, quality control, and automated sorting in small -to-medium-sized factories.

title: Design and Experimental Validation of a Low-Temperature-Resistant Lithium-Ion Battery Pack Using Fiber Carbon-Nanotube Film Laminated Composite Heaters
authors: Jinyang Li, Jirong Huang, Zonghong Xie
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: This study addresses the significant degradation in power performance of lithium-ion battery packs under low-temperature environments, which reduces the operational efficiency of electric devices such as electric aircraft and electric vehicles in cold conditions. A lithium-ion battery pack capable of stable operation in low-temperature environments was developed based on fiber carbon-nanotube film laminated composite heaters and a temperature control module. An experimental platform was established to conduct low-temperature heating experiments and constant-power discharge tests after heating for the power lithiumion battery pack. The results show that the designed battery pack achieved an average heating rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.98^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / {\min}$</tex>. After heating, the temperature difference within the battery pack was controlled within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, while the energy consumption accounted for 5.16% of the total battery energy. Compared with the unheated discharge condition, the initial current during 1000 W constant-power discharge after heating decreased by 2.9 A, while the discharge capacity increased by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>.

title: On the Specification and Analysis of Quantum Repeater Protocols
authors: Lorenzo La Corte, Anita Buckley, Pavel Chuprikov, Patrick Eugster
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: The distribution of high-quality entanglement over long distances remains a challenge due to losses in quantum channels. As a solution, quantum repeater protocols generate entanglement over short segments and progressively extend its range using entanglement swapping, while using distillation to compensate for entanglement degradation. Numerous studies analyze repeater protocols through tailored tools that are tied to specific protocol types and network assumptions. In this work, we propose a formal language to specify quantum repeater protocols in a unified manner, modeling the concurrent distribution of entanglement across multiple pairs of network nodes. We consider delays arising from failures and contention for resources, and account for the effects of noise and decoherence on the quality of entanglement. Furthermore, we provide a prototype tool based on our language to precisely track the rate and fidelity of the distributed entangled pairs, supporting heterogeneous hardware configurations and constraints on available quantum memories. Finally, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach by expressing common protocols from the literature and evaluating their performance.

title: Optimized Design of a Compact High Suppression Microstrip Low-Pass Filter Using Defected Ground Structure for 5G Communication
authors: Mehdi Hasan Rafi, Md Aminul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Low-pass filters (LPFs) are essential in wireless communication systems for suppressing unwanted high-frequency components and preserving signal integrity. This paper proposes a generalized Chebyshev microstrip LPF integrated with a defected ground structure (DGS) to achieve compact size, low insertion loss, and wide stopband rejection for 5G applications. The design was initially modeled as an LC resonator network and converted into microstrip form using transmission-line transformations, with simulations performed on a Roger RT/Duroid 5880 substrate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{r}=3.55$</tex>, thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\sim \mathbf{0. 3 8 1 m m}, \tan \delta=0.0009$</tex>). Simulation results indicate a cutoff frequency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 ~ G H z}$</tex>, an insertion loss of 0.65 dB, and a suppression level of about 28 dB extending from 3.4 GHz to beyond 20 GHz. The performance of the proposed filter with DGS is significantly improved compared to the configuration without DGS, particularly in terms of spurious resonance suppression and stopband enhancement. The filter also demonstrates stable surface impedance characteristics, acceptable efficiencies, and low dielectric and metallic losses. These outcomes confirm that the compact generalized Chebyshev LPF with DGS provides superior stopband suppression and efficient high-frequency performance, making it suitable for nextgeneration communication systems and emerging wearable robotic applications.

title: Origin of Pinched Hysteresis Loop: Unified Model for Wake-Up and Imprint in Sub-5 nm Ferroelectric HZO
authors: Zhenhong Liu, Zuocheng Cai, Mitsuru Takenaka, Shinichi Takagi, Kasidit Toprasertpong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This study systematically investigates the origin of the wake-up effect in ferroelectric <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Hf}_{0.5} \text{Zr}_{0.5} \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> (HZO) films, particularly found prominent in sub- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5\ n m}$</tex> thickness. We examine and verify a model explaining the pinched loops, wake-up, and imprint behavior in ultra-thin HZO: (1) a pinched loop in pristine samples results from imprint with opposite directions of the net-zero polarization state; (2) this imprint-induced pinched loop is stronger in thinner films due to more trap sites for charge trapping; (3) wake-up can be considered as the recovery of imprint. We experimentally confirm this model by reproducing the pristine condition through properly initializing films to a net-zero-polarization state. We can observe the re-splitting of switching peaks by imprint at the net-zero polarization state with the more prominent re-splitting behavior in thinner HZO films, reproducing the pinched loop in sub-5nm films.

title: Aging Characterization on Band Gap Reference Circuit
authors: Gumer Rodriguez-Martinez, Srijita Patra, Ricardo Ascazubi, Rahul Sharma, Shiv Shankar Gupta, Sachin L, Balkaran Gill
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: Band gap reference (BGR) circuits are crucial for maintaining a stable reference voltage under power supply and/or temperature fluctuation. However, limited studies are available on how aging will affect the performance of BGRs. In this study, we present an accelerated aging evaluation on a sub 1 V BGR circuit designed and fabricated in Intel 4 process technology, and validated on the Quality and Reliability On-Chip (QROC) platform. The BGR reference output voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{ref }}$</tex>) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 3 6 ~ m V}$</tex> was measured while sweeping the power supply voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{cc}}$</tex>) from 1 V to 1.4 V across temperatures from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to 120° C. The variation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{ref }}$</tex> was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 6 \text{mV}$</tex>, which ensured the robustness of the BGR circuit. The accelerated aging stress conditions were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{cc}}=1.45 \mathrm{V}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T}=120^{\circ} \mathbf{C}$</tex> for 8 days, corresponding to an estimated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-year equivalent aging. After stress, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{V}_{\text{ref }}$</tex> degraded by approximately 1%, confirming BGR's robustness and precision, and highlighting the importance of aging evaluation for integrated-circuit (IC) performance and reliability.

title: Intelligent Mango Leaf Disease Detection and Classification Using Transfer Learning and Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Al Arian Ahmad, Md.Rafiul Islam, Rayhan Rabby
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Mango is a vital fruit crop, both economically and nutritionally. Its productivity plays an important role in food security and the economy of many developing countries. However, mango trees are susceptible to a variety of leaf diseases that can severely affect both yield and quality. The most marked among these diseases are Anthracnose, Bacterial Canker, Cutting weevil, Die Back, Gall midge, Powdery Mildew and Sooty mold. Early and accurate detection of these diseases is crucial for farmers and recent advancements in modern technology now enable automated identification. A deep learning-based disease detection system has been developed using a dataset of 12,729 mango leaf images categorized into eight disease classes. The performance of several transfer learning models—MobileNetV2, VGG16, EfficientNetB0 and ResNet50 has been analyzed. Furthermore, the ResNet50 model has been enhanced with Grad-CAM-based explainable AI to improve both interpretability and performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the ResNet50 + Explainable AI model achieves the highest accuracy of 99.36%, outperforming the other models. This explainable model not only accurately identifies diseases but also highlights the specific leaf regions that influenced its predictions through Grad-CAM heatmaps, thereby increasing user trust. The proposed method is efficient, cost-effective, suitable for real-time field deployment and offers a practical disease monitoring solution for farmers.

title: A Multi-Vehicle Autonomous Driving Simulation System Based on DORA Middleware
authors: Yipei Yang, Ziheng Yuan, Ming Cen
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: System-level simulation is indispensable for developing autonomous driving vehicles, however existing tools provide little native support for the Dataflow-Oriented Robotics Architecture (DORA) middleware, which severely limits the efficient design and validation of cooperative multi-vehicle systems. To improve this situation, an integrated simulation framework built explicitly on DORA is presented. The framework is partitioned into two layers: a high-fidelity simulation platform and a cluster of domain controllers. The platform combines traffic scenario simulation and vehicle dynamics simulation with a novel multi-vehicle Dora-bridge. Acting as the sole gateway, the bridge publishes ground-truth states, sensor data and cooperative awareness messages as DORA topics, receives control commands which are issued as DORA topics by each domain controller, and forwards them to the vehicle dynamics simulation software. Each domain controller hosts a full autonomous driving pipeline decomposed into reusable DORA nodes, with one controller is mapped to one vehicle, enabling true distributed testing. Experiments show that the proposed approach shortens development cycles, improves timing accuracy and achieves real-time performance, providing a reliable environment for rapid prototyping and verification of DORA-based cooperative driving algorithms.

title: CoV-ME: Memory Editing for Self-Consistent Reasoning in Large Language Models
authors: Mir Tahmid Hossain, Mahsa Sanaei Nourani, Nasrin Sultana
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Large Language Models often generate fluent but flawed reasoning chains, with undetected errors in logic, arithmetic, or assumptions. While methods like chain-of-thought prompting and self-refinement improve outputs, they do not correct internal representations. We propose CoV-ME (Chain of Verification with Memory Editing), a framework that enables LLMs to detect and correct flawed reasoning within their hidden states. CoV-ME combines a verifier that identifies inconsistencies in a reasoning chain with a differentiable memory editor that modifies intermediate transformer activations to reflect corrected beliefs. The editor is trained via self-supervised learning using synthetic reasoning errors, allowing it to reconstruct coherent hidden states during inference. We evaluate on GSM8K, StrategyQA, HotpotQA, and AQuA, showing that CoV-ME achieves state-of-the-art accuracy, outperforming strong baselines by +3.9 to +6.5 points. Gains are largest on StrategyQA (+6.3 over PAL), where reasoning steps are implicit and must be inferred, a key strength of our verification-guided editing mechanism. Ablation studies confirm the critical role of the memory editor, while visualizations show targeted corrections to erroneous steps. By changing hidden states based on consistency checks, we have presented solid evidence for self-correcting and more warranted reasoning.

title: Automated BPMN Model Generation from Textual Process Descriptions: A Multi-Stage LLM-Driven Approach
authors: Ion Matei, Maksym Zhenirovskyy, Praveen Kumar Menaka Sekar, Hon Yung Wong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Automatically reconstructing BPMN models from unstructured natural-language descriptions remains challenging due to heterogeneous modeling conventions, multilingual sources, and the lack of reliable ground truth. We present a scalable, multi-stage LLM-driven pipeline that automates both groundtruth construction and model reconstruction. Multilingual BPMN XML files are translated into English, validated using executionoriented compliance checks in SpiffWorkflow, and iteratively repaired through targeted LLM-guided corrections to produce a consistent ground-truth corpus. From these validated models, process descriptions are generated and used to reconstruct executable BPMN 2.0 XML diagrams without manual curation. We introduce a multi-dimensional similarity framework combining structural metrics, type-distribution alignment, and embeddingbased semantic measures. In an empirical study of 750 public BPMN diagrams, the pipeline generated 387 validated groundtruth models and achieved average reconstruction similarity above 0.75, including approximately 50 near-perfect reconstructions differing only in minor naming variations. The results demonstrate that LLMs can generate structurally compliant and semantically meaningful BPMN diagrams at scale.

title: Threat Modeling of Infineon AURIX for Automotive ECUs: A Systems Engineering Approach
authors: Narges Rahimi, Mitra Mirhassani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The increasing connectivity of modern road vehicles has elevated the critical importance of cybersecurity within embedded automotive systems. Although much research has focused on vehicle-level cyber risks, limited attention has been given to micro-level threat exposure within embedded architectures such as Electronic Control Units (ECUs). ECUs constitute the computational backbone of vehicle control and communication networks, and their microcontroller platforms present highly attractive attack surfaces for adversaries. This study focuses on the Infineon AURIX TC3xx microcontroller family, a platform extensively deployed in safety-critical automotive domains that demand high reliability, security, and functional safety compliance. A structured threat modeling methodology is applied by integrating systems engineering principles with the STRIDE framework. The AURIX architecture is decomposed into process components, external entities, and data stores, followed by the construction of Data Flow Diagrams (DFDs) to represent internal connectivity and communication paths. Based on these relationships, threat scenarios are systematically generated, analyzed, and refined using the Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool (TMT), one of the commonly used tools for threat modeling. The resulting threat model establishes a foundational reference for identifying cybersecurity threats associated with AURIX-based ECUs. The outcomes support early-stage design decisions, facilitate securityaware system engineering, and contribute to the development of robust, standards-aligned threat analysis practices for embedded automotive systems.

title: Understanding Gate Breakdown Mechanisms in RF GaN MIS-HEMTs with thin gate SiN
authors: Anna Navolotskaia, Hao Yu, Ying-Chung Kuo, Yi Yang, Cheng-Ying Huang, Wei-Tung Lin, Yiliang Chong, Barry O'Sullivan, Aarti Rathi, Amratansh Gupta, AliReza Alian, Uthayasankaran Peralagu, Bertrand Parvais, Nadine Collaert, Tian-Li Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: We investigate forward-biased gate breakdown mechanisms in RF GaN MIS-HEMTs employing thin in-situ MOCVD SiN gate dielectrics with thicknesses of 10, 3.5, and 1 nm. A refined ramped-voltage-stress (RVS) methodology is proposed, in which incremental gate-bias steps are combined with intermediate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{G}}-\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{G}}$</tex> checks to detect SiN degradation, overcoming the limitations of conventional constant-current breakdown criteria for ultra-thin dielectrics. We show that, depending on the dominant forward gate-leakage mechanism, SiN breakdown occurs at different equivalent electric fields. Time-dependent dielectric breakdown (TDDB) under constant-voltage stress (CVS) is further evaluated for the 10 nm (M10) and 3.5 nm (M3.5) stacks, enabling lifetime extrapolation and assessment against GaN power-amplifier (PA) operational requirements. The 10 nm SiN stack satisfies a 10-year PA reliability target, while thinner dielectrics fall short. Finally, by comparing forward and reverse CVS through combined measurement and device simulation, we demonstrate that high electric field alone is insufficient to trigger breakdown; instead, SiN failure is governed by the combined impact of electric field and injected charge. These results provide a comprehensive framework for gate-stack design and reliability qualification of thin-dielectric GaN MIS-HEMTs for RF PA applications.

title: Topology-Dependent Enhancement of Entanglement Extraction in Repeater Graph States
authors: Poramat Chianvichai, Poramet Pathumsoot, Naphan Benchasattabuse, Michal Hajdušek, Rodney Van Meter, Sujin Suwanna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Quantum repeaters are essential for establishing long-distance quantum communication to overcome the exponential decay of entanglement due to photon loss. Traditional repeater architectures rely on physical quantum memory, which introduces decoherence and poses significant practical implementation challenges. The repeater graph state (RGS) architecture offers a promising memory-less alternative that is inherently resilient to photon losses. A key challenge in implementing RGS lies in the requirement for highly efficient graph state generators and complex qubit measurement. In this work, we aim to investigate the strategies for extracting the maximum number of Bell pairs from the RGS structure via its qubit connection to resolve the well-known bottleneck problem of RGS in which only a single Bell pair can be extracted from a complete bipartite graph state. From simulations, we observe that the maximum number of extracted Bell pairs depends on its connection topology, where the Bell-pair yield tends to be maximal at low to moderate edge densities. As the number of network hops increases, the RGS must be equipped with higher inner-qubit connectivity to maintain a sufficient yield of extractable Bell pairs. Thus, the expected resource requirement shifts toward the use of RGSs with higher innerqubit connectivity.

title: Dynamic on-State Breakdown of GaN-on-Si HEMT
authors: Hao Yu, A. Gupta, Y.-C. Kuo, Y. Chong, T.-L. Wu, S. Yadav, R. ElKashlan, S. Banerjee, H. Hahn, C. Mauder, A. Rathi, W.-M. Wu, B. Kazemi Esfeh, E. Bury, B. Vermeersch, B. O'Sullivan, A. Alian, M. Asad, U. Peralagu, B. Parvais, N. Collaert
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: We apply ramp-drain-voltage-(. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{d}}$</tex>) -stress (RVS) method to characterize breakdown loci of GaN-on-Si HEMTs under high-power on state conditions. We observe that GaN-onSi HEMTs have distinct breakdown dependencies in the fully-ON state (when conductive current <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 400 \text{mA} / \text{mm}$</tex>) and semi-ON state (when conductive current <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<400 \text{mA} / \text{mm}$</tex>). In the fully-ON state, the breakdown loci are independent of HEMT top barrier options (InAIN, AIGaN, or AIN) while in the semi-ON state, the breakdown loci have clear top barrier dependences. We systematically screen key parameters behind the fully-ON breakdown voltages loci. We report significant dependences of fully-ON breakdown on device width, gate-to-drain spacing Lgd and RVS sweep rate. The dependence of fully-ON breakdown on device width results from self-heating effects. The dependence of fully-ON breakdown on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{L}_{\text{gd}}$</tex> and RVS sweep rate suggests correlation between device failure and dynamic trapping effects in the gate-to-drain access region.

title: Fluorine Plasma-Induced Modulation of Electrical Performance and Reliability in IGZTO TFTs
authors: Kai Chen, Rui Zhang, Yunlong Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: Amorphous oxide semiconductor thin-film transistors (TFTs) are promising for back-end-of-line integration, yet their electrical performance and reliability are often limited by oxygen vacancies and hydrogen-related defects. Fluorine plasma treatment is widely used to passivate these defects. However, excessive plasma exposure may introduce physical damage and degrade device stability. In this work, the effects of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CF}_{4}$</tex> plasma treatment time and chamber pressure on indium–gallium–zinc–tin–oxide (IGZTO) TFTs are systematically investigated. Controlled fluorine incorporation effectively suppresses oxygenrelated defects and modulates the band structure, resulting in pronounced threshold voltage shifts and mobility variation. Prolonged treatment induces subthreshold swing degradation and aggravated bias-stress instability due to plasma-induced damage. By increasing the chamber pressure, ion energy is reduced while maintaining sufficient fluorine incorporation, enabling concurrent improvement in electrical performance and reliability. These results clarify the trade-off between fluorine passivation and plasma damage, providing practical guidelines for optimizing fluorine plasma processes in oxide TFT technology.

title: Ground-Plane Effect on Random Telegraph Noise in Mesa-Isolated SOI MOSFETs for 3D Sequential CIS
authors: A. Machmach, C. Theodorou, F. Balestra, S. Place, O. Gauthier, S. Joblot, F. Ponthenier, J. Lacord
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This paper investigates wafer-level statistics of Random Telegraph Noise (RTN) in source followers (SFs) mesa-isolated multi-gate SOI MOSFETs for 3D-sequential CMOS image sensors (CIS). At a constant drain current, raising the ground-plane (GP) bias increases RTN detectability and amplitude by modulating the carrier distribution: the drain-side pinch-off, the charge centroid, and the edge channel conduction. These effects push more events above the RTN detection threshold and amplify drain current fluctuations from active oxide traps. The results were supported by DC electrical characterization, noise measurements, time domain RTN analysis, and 3D TCAD simulations.

title: Symbolic Quantum State Representation and its Simulation
authors: Simon Sekavčnik, Janis Nötzel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We introduce a symbolic operator framework for simulating quantum photonic systems that works directly with the canonical commutation relations and the Weyl algebra. Unlike existing Fock-space or Gaussian simulators, our method treats temporal wave packets and polarization modes in a continuous setting and does not rely on discretization or Hilbert-space truncation. Device operations are expressed as algebraic rewrite rules acting on creation and annihilation operators, allowing exact evolution of finite-photon states through linear optical networks. As an illustration, we reproduce Hong-Ou-Mandel interference for Gaussian pulses with controlled temporal and spectral mismatch.

title: System Architecture Optimization Using SysML v2: Language Extension and Implementation
authors: Jasper H. Bussemaker, Francesco Torrigiani, Olexiy Kupriyanov, Elias Allegaert, Luca Boggero, Björn Nagel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: System Architecture Optimization (SAO) enables the exploration of large architecture design spaces by applying numerical optimization algorithms to the generation and evaluation of system architectures. This work presents the integration of SAO with Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE), by interfacing with SysML v2: the newly-released second version of the Systems Modeling Language (SysML). A SysML v2 language extension library is presented which enables representing all concepts of the Architecture Design Space Graph (ADSG), a graphbased method for modeling architecture design spaces. Then, the implementation of this language extension and link to SysML v2 is presented for ADORE, a tool for editing the ADSG. Finally, the connection is demonstrated by optimizing the architecture of a Guidance, Navigation and Control (GNC) system of which the design space consists of 79 million architectures, and which is optimized for mass and reliability.

title: Now that your System has been Reproduced, What does this Mean for the Users?
authors: Angelo Geninatti Cossatin, Timo Breuer, Noemi Mauro, Maria Maistro
publish: ACM Transactions on Information Systems
abstract: None

title: Enabling Active Sensing with Zero-Power Components
authors: Mingqi Xie, Qinyu Wang, Meng Jin, Fengyuan Zhu, Jiaxin Ding, Xinbing Wang, Chenghu Zhou
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM/IEEE International Conference on Embedded Artificial Intelligence and Sensing Systems
abstract: None

title:
Optimization of SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    and HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    based BIOFET by Varying the Channel Length in Order to Improve Impedance Characteristics
authors: S. Gomathi, D. Sungeetha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of channel length variations on the impedance characteristics of SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based and HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based bio sensitive field-effect transistors (BIOFETs). The objective is to analyze how channel length influences the overall impedance and performance of these materials in BIOFET applications. SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> were utilized as dielectric materials, with channel lengths ranging from 845 nm to 905 nm for SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs and from 193 nm to 253nm for HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs. Simulations were conducted while maintaining a constant gate oxide thickness and substrate properties to evaluate impedance variations. The results indicate that HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs exhibit lower impedance due to their higher dielectric constant, leading to improved charge transport and reduced resistance. Conversely, SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs demonstrated consistently higher impedance, making them less efficient for high-speed sensing applications. These findings highlight the importance of selecting suitable dielectric materials to optimize BIOFET performance, with HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emerging as a superior choice for minimizing impedance in biosensing technologies.

title: Predictive Analysis of Drug Toxicity
authors: Samar Thiab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The issue of drug toxicity continues to pose a major problem in the drug discovery as well as development processes, leading to a series of unfavorable reactions that in turn bring barriers to the safety and effectiveness of pharmaceutical treatments. In this research, the application of predictive analytics and machine learning approaches as tools for a more precise and reliable examination of drug toxicity has been particularly scrutinized. Through the analysis of various types of information sources such as biochemical, molecular, and clinical trial information, we have utilized the latest algorithms such as ensemble learning, deep learning, combined with features selection techniques which are intended for the identification of the main factors of toxicity. The examination of the results included the disciplines of molecular modeling, pharmacogenomics, and bioinformatics through the application of a complete framework for the toxicity screening in the first steps of drug discovery. The findings show marked improvements in predictive accuracy and a decrease in both false-positive and false-negative rates when compared to traditional approaches. The present study emphasizes the power of AI-based predictive analytics to shorten the time of drug development, reduce risks, and ensure patient safety.

title: Supply Chain Disruption Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Cheng Ling Tan, Haitham M. Alzoubi, Ramakrishna Yanamandra, Samer Barakat, Fanar Shwedeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The growth in the size and intricacy of worldwide supply chains has made them more vulnerable. The effect of unpredictable events such as natural disasters, geopolitical tensions, and market fluctuations has increased. In this research, we focus on the use of machine learning (ML) tools as a means of forecasting supply chain disruptions, thus enabling early identification of risks and better decision-making. Collection data that typically include a historical record of supply chain performance, external environmental factors, and economic indicators together with other information, were combined for the purposes of the study to obtain accurate predictive models that will be able to identify potential disruptions. The predictive capability of various ML methods such as random forests, gradient boosting, and neural networks is compared. The research goes further to examine some engineering concepts and the incorporation of real-time data streams in the model to enhance its stability. The objective of the study is to provide practical ideas for managers of the supply chain to implement risk-reducing measures, work better, and become structurally stronger. The results shed light on the ability of machine learning to guide the development of highly secure and flexible supply chains.

title: Building Smart Cities: How Technology is Shaping the Future of Urban Development: (UAE Context)
authors: Soumaya Askri, Mohamed Dawood Shamout, Zakariya Chabani, Salima Hamouchi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: As urban centers worldwide expand at an unprecedented rate, the demand for sustainable, efficient, and expertly managed urban spaces has become critical. In the UAE, where rapid urbanization drives significant change, the smart city concept emerges as a strategic solution to meet citizens’ evolving needs. Advanced information and communication technologies not only enhance resource management and urban governance but also necessitate robust management frameworks—encompassing strategic planning, project management, stakeholder coordination, and risk management—to ensure seamless integration and scalability of these innovations. This article explores a range of transformative technologies—including deep learning (DL), machine learning (ML), Internet of Things (IoT), mobile computing, big data, blockchain, 6G, Wi-Fi 7, Industry 5.0, connected cars, robotics, HVAC technology, and cybersecurity—and delves into the management challenges that accompany their deployment in urban environments. By examining the complexities of integrating these technologies within a single ecosystem, the study highlights the importance of effective operations management, data governance, and collaborative leadership in overcoming issues related to infrastructure robustness, data privacy, and security. Furthermore, the review identifies key future dimensions of smart city development with a focus on the UAE’s ambitious goals for smart living. It underscores that proactive urban planning, enhanced energy designs, and advanced medical technologies must be supported by strategic management practices to achieve sustainable and resilient urban spaces. Ultimately, the success of smart cities hinges on the collaboration between public institutions, private enterprises, and citizens, all guided by innovative management solutions that balance growth with environmental sustainability.

title: Optimizing Heart Stroke Prediction Using Ensemble Learning: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Anchal Kumawat, Biswaranjan Acharya, Smita Nirkhi, Subhashree Choudhury
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Heart diseases remain a significant global health concern, underscoring the need for accurate predictive models to enable early diagnosis and intervention. This study presents an ensemble learning framework based on the Random Forest (RF) algorithm to predict the risk of heart stroke using a sensitive medical dataset characterized by inherent outliers. The proposed model demonstrates superior performance compared to conventional machine learning techniques, achieving an accuracy of 99.25%. Comprehensive evaluation metrics further validate the robustness and reliability of the RF-based approach in handling complex clinical data. The findings highlight the potential of ensemble learning methods to enhance stroke risk prediction, thereby supporting early clinical intervention and improving patient outcomes. This research offers valuable insights for informed clinical decision-making and effective cardiovascular disease management.

title: AI-Based Wearable Posture and Muscle Fatigue Monitor
authors: Rajasekar P, Nagul J, Naveen Kumar V, Neeladhasan M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The major cause of musculoskeletal disorders is the improper body posture and extended muscle strain especially in people who do desk jobs. Traditional wearable devices can generally sense posture or muscle motion separately and do not normally provide feedback in an effective timely fashion. The proposed paper describes an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based wearable posture correction system with an integrated muscle fatigue detection algorithm used as a continuous monitoring and real-time intervention. The proposed system is a hybrid of posture sensors based on MPU6050 MEMS inertial measurement unit (IMU) and muscle sensors based on electromyography (EMG) sensor. A Random Forest machine learning classifier is used to process multimodal sensor data to detect improper posture, muscle fatigue conditions. When unfavorable posture is detected, an embedded vibration motor will give immediate haptic feedback, causing correction to be possible immediately, without the latency caused by the cloud. The wearable gadget has a small rechargeable Li-ion battery and is designed in manner that it utilizes low power consumption to enable all- day use. Experimental testing done on actual users shows a posture and fatigue classification accuracy of over 95 percent with an average alert response time of under one second. The proposed system will provide a small, privacy-conscious, and efficient system to correct the posture and prevent injuries.

title: Customer Behavior Analysis on Purchase Intention with the Mediating Role of Personalized Recommendations
authors: Ghassan Issa Ibrahim Alomari, Muhammad Alshurideh, Khaleel Ibrahim Al-Daoud, Beenish Shameem, Basma Waleed Kashmoola, Lama Alsmadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: A quantitative analysis examines how individualized suggestion influence customer actions and shopping preferences in Jordanian retail operations. The increasing adoption of personalized marketing approaches has not resolved the unclear relationship between customer behavior and purchase intention when using personalized recommendations in Jordanian retail environments. Data from 288 retail industry respondents underwent analysis through SmartPLS in order to evaluate the proposed hypotheses. The research indicates that personalized recommendations generate positive effects on purchase intention and serve as a major decision-making bridge between customer actions and purchasing choices. The study demonstrates why personalization has become fundamental for marketers who want to influence consumer choices. This research presents substantial commercial value to Jordanian retail stores which demonstrates that dedicated recommendation engines boost consumer participation and boost purchase likelihood thus leading to greater sales performance. Retailers should use these insights to develop marketing strategies that match their recommendations to specific customer preferences and behaviors which results in better business outcomes.

title: Touchless Interaction : AI Driven Virtual Mouse and Air Writing
authors: V P Leena, P Anusha, S Neethu, B Neha, P P Prathyush, K P Sayandh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: By introducing a touchless interface, the Virtual Mouse and Air Writing system helps consumers to engage with computers solely via hand motions. The system records live video from a webcam to track finger positions, utilizing computer vision and Media Pipe for hand landmark detection. Smooth navigation without physical contact is made possible by the arrangement of certain Mouse movement functions such as scrolling, moving, and left and right clicks. Furthermore, the air writing function captures the movement of the fingertip to create characters or symbols in midair, which are subsequently shown on the screen. By doing away with the need for traditional input devices, this innovation enhances accessibility and hygiene, particularly in public kiosks, healthcare settings, and smart classrooms. A more intuitive and engaging user experience is produced by combining gesture control and air writing, which advances This project makes it possible for users to interact with their devices without making physical contact by integrating cutting-edge machine learning and sensor technologies. It was created with Python, OpenCV, Media Pipe, and TensorFlow. It functions effectively in real time and provides a scalable and reliable framework for a variety of uses, including educational technology, virtual environments, and accessibility tools. Free human-computer interaction is provided by the suggested project.

title: Glauco Vision: Early Detection System of Glaucoma using Vision Transformer
authors: Shamili P, Arivazhagan M, Boaz Aaron Anand
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Glaucoma is a type of eye disease that slowly damages the optic nerve and is one of the main causes of blindness that can't be reversed. Detecting it early is hard because the changes in the eye's structure are not very obvious, and relying on human doctors for diagnosis has its limits. This paper introduces Glauco Vision, a tool that automatically detects glaucoma using a type of artificial intelligence called Vision Transformer, or ViT. Unlike traditional neural networks that focus only on small parts of an image, this new model uses a special way of looking at the whole image to understand the bigger picture. A web-based tool built with Flask makes it easy to use in clinics. The system performs very well, with an accuracy of 96.40%, and it also has high precision, recall, and F1-score values, which are 95.44%. These results show that Vision Transformer models are a great choice for quickly and effectively screening for glaucoma, especially in places where there are not enough healthcare resources.

title: Cybersecurity Risk Assessment Using AI-Based Predictive Models
authors: Al Rafy, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain, Mostafizur Rahman, Mostafijur Rahman, Nisher Ahmed, Mashfiquer Rahman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Over the years, traditional cyber risk approaches have become of less or no value to most dynamic organizations as a result of increased complexity and frequency of cyber threats. This paper develops Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based predictive models for automated cyber risk assessment systems and their detection, prediction, and mitigation capabilities across various network environments. Through supervised and unsupervised machine learning, the study incorporates model precision evaluation, threat intelligence, and risk quantification automation. Model effectiveness was validated through comparative performance testing on datasets. AI-driven models significantly improve detection rates while decreasing false positives and automating responses to dynamic threat scenarios. The paper also addresses engineering challenges in the deployment of such systems in enterprise security infrastructures.

title: Intelligent Medical Report Analysis System: A Serverless Cloud Architecture for Automated Healthcare Diagnostics
authors: Adithiyaa D, Lakshhmi Narayanan T, Gokulakkrishna Saravanan, Deepa Nivethika S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The explosion of digital medical records has created an urgent need for tools that do not just store data, but actually understand it. This paper presents a serverless ecosystem that is built on AWS and combined with Google's Gemini AI that is designed to automate the analysis of medical reports. We have structured the framework of the system to include features like secure uploads through presigned URL's, structured metadata handling in DynamoDB, text extraction from PDF files using PyPDF2, AI-driven assessment via the Gemini API, and timely notifications delivered through Amazon SNS. Performance evaluation shows an overall processing time of roughly 85 seconds, with Lambda 1 taking an average time of 0.5 seconds, Lambda 2 about 1 second, and the AI-enabled Lambda 3 taking 83 seconds. The tests that were performed on 10 reports confirms that Gemini reliably detected all clinically relevant deviations and produced clear, meaningful summaries, validating the system's accuracy, and clinical usefulness.

title: Applied Deep Learning for Automated Gastrointestinal Disease Detection: Model Implementation and Assessment
authors: Sourav Das Polok, Salil Batra, Gouri Shankar Chakraborty, Makul Mahajan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases encompass a broad spectrum of conditions that demand accurate and timely detection to facilitate effective treatment. Relying solely on symptom-based diagnosis often leads to delays, highlighting the need for automated and intelligent diagnostic tools. With the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in healthcare, various deep learning models have been developed for disease identification using endoscopic images. This study evaluates and compares the performance of six deep learning architectures— EfficientNetV2S, MobileNetV3Large, ResNet50V2, VGG19, ViT-B16, and InceptionResNetV2—on the Kvasir v2 dataset. After preprocessing and applying transfer learning techniques, InceptionResNetV2 emerged as the most effective model, achieving a test accuracy of 95% along with high precision, recall, and F1 score. These results affirm the potential of InceptionResNetV2 in delivering accurate and reliable GI disease classification, offering substantial support for clinical decision-making and advancing AI-driven diagnostic systems.

title: Semantic-Guided Diffusion for Robust Multi-Object Tracking with Temporal Enhancement
authors: Yuhao Li, Haotian Zheng, Jinqiu Sun, Yu Zhu, Yanning Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Diffusion-based motion prediction methods have demonstrated strong capabilities in modeling nonlinear motion for multi-object tracking (MOT). However, in complex scenarios involving target interactions or occlusions, these methods still suffer from frequent identity switches and inaccurate trajectory predictions. This is primarily due to insufficient joint modeling of appearance and motion cues, as well as limited adaptability to diverse motion patterns. To address these challenges, we propose a semantic-guided diffusion-based method, termed SGDMOT, which jointly models target motion dynamics and identity consistency. Specifically, we leverage historical trajectories to query image-level global features, incorporating appearance and contextual information. These are fused with motion information via an attention mechanism, guiding the diffusion process to generate semantically consistent trajectories. Furthermore, we introduce a learnable multi-scale temporal modulation module that dynamically adjusts the encoding of diffusion time steps based on historical motion states. This enhances the model’s ability to adapt to motion variations across different temporal granularities, improving temporal modeling during the generation phase. Extensive experiments on the DanceTrack, MOT17, and MOT20 benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Notably, on the DanceTrack test set, SGDMOT achieves an absolute gain of 2.3% in Higher Order Tracking Accuracy (HOTA) compared to a baseline diffusion model relying solely on motion features. Our code and pretrained models will be publicly released.

title: Fault Tolerant Transformer Based Hybrid Multilevel Inverter for Uninterruptable Power Supply
authors: Kannabiran Arumugam, R. Jayapragash, Sudha V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: A high reliable Transformer based Multilevel inverter enhances the efficiency and life time of the system. Recent research studies focus on the development of new fault-tolerant multilevel inverter (FTMLI) topologies. However, these topologies unable to maintain rated voltage and power with a minimum switch count during switch fault conditions by preserving its rated voltage and power. This work presents a new transformer based multilevel inverter (TBMLI) to generate 27 level output voltage with minimum switch count. The proposed configuration can handle open circuit(oc)/short circuit (sc) faults, by preserving the rated voltage and power during faulty conditions. Level shifted pulse width modulation(LSPWM) is used to generate the switching signals. A redundant leg is used to tolerate the fault occurred in any one of the switch or legs in TBMLI. The proposed configuration provides uninterrupted operation during faulty conditions which increases the reliability of the circuit and reliability analysis is presented in this work. The proposed circuit is simulated in MATLAB/Simulink and the simulation results are validated with hardware results.

title: Ultra-Wide Voltage Range Bidirectional EV Charger with Topology Morphing Control
authors: Sachin Chauhan, Hans Anniste, Andrei Blinov, Andrii Chub, Dmitri Vinnikov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Various fast-charging standards, such as megawatt charging systems (MCS) and CHAdeMO 3.0, have specified charging voltages above 1kV. Also, the growing number of battery pack voltages for fast-charging solutions has driven demand for ultra-wide-voltage-range converters. To meet requirements determined by charging trends, an active-clamped dual-current-fed (ACDCF) series resonant topology has been proposed and designed for wide-voltage-range EV charging/discharging operations. This solution features a combination of an active-clamped current-fed configuration and topology morphing control (TMC), enabling an output voltage range of 150V to 1000V, while keeping the voltage stress within limits and allowing the selection of relatively low-voltage switches with low on-state resistance. The TMC also offers a more cost-effective alternative to the reconfigurable converter that uses multiple expensive breakers to achieve wide voltage ranges while maintaining soft-switching over the entire operating range of the proposed topology. The operational study, converter design, and control are briefly outlined, along with the experimental validation of a 3.3kW proof-of-concept prototype. Moreover, the performance of the proposed topology has been tested for 400V and 800V battery systems, yielding promising time/energy-weighted average efficiencies (T/EWAE) of around 95.7% and 96.5%, respectively. Although this design is primarily intended for EV charging, it can also serve as a DC interface for a different range of energy sources and storage technologies.

title: Modeling Consumer Adoption of Energy-Efficient Appliances Using Artificial Neural Networks and Clustering: Insights for Sustainable Energy Systems
authors: Sowmiya R, M. Velavan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Energy-efficient appliances play a vital role in demand-side energy management, yet their adoption remains limited despite energy labeling and awareness initiatives. This study proposes a machine learning-based framework to analyze consumer adoption behavior using Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and K-means clustering. The ANN model captures complex nonlinear relationships among behavioral, economic, and social factors to predict adoption likelihood. In addition, clustering analysis segments consumers into distinct behavioral groups. The results identify three segments—highly engaged adopters, resistant consumers, and transitional users—revealing significant heterogeneity in adoption patterns. The study provides valuable insights for designing targeted policies and strategies to promote sustainable energy consumption.

title: STRIDE-SA: Streaming, Transfer-Invariant, and Distilled Sentiment Analysis for Real-Time Social Media
authors: Akash Balaji, Bobby George, Medida Venkata Sai Vinay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: To offer STRIDE-SA, a single framework for looking at how people feel on social media that works with streaming, transfer-invariant, and distilled data. The method fills in three gaps that have been present for a while: (i) cross-platform robustness, (ii) trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency, and (iii) real-time scalability. STRIDE-SA contains a RoBERTa encoder, lightweight adapters, and a lexicon-gated fusion that only adds sentiment priors when they make sense in the context. It also contains a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BLSTM) head to pick up on small changes over time, adversarial + MMD alignment for domain invariance, and a stack that is ready to be used with knowledge distillation, confidence-based early exits, and online calibration with drift monitoring. Sentiment140 is the major benchmark for the teacher, and they receive strong results offline (Macro-F1 0.892, AUPRC 0.951). At P95 latency 38 ms, which is substantially faster than RoBERTa-base (95 ms), the student with early departures preserves most of the accuracy (Macro-F1 0.878, AUPRC 0.940). Sequential mitigations, such as class-prior reweighting, temperature scaling, and adapter fine-tuning, raise Macro-F1 from 0.762 to 0.835 and lower shift indicators (PSI 0.32 to 0.12) with very little extra labor. Ablations demonstrate that lexical gating, distillation, early departures, and adapters are all beneficial. STRIDE-SA is a full, mathematically sound system that is correct, quick, and ready for use in the real world. It shows how to do sentiment analysis in real time outside of Twitter.

title: TACA-ProtoNet for Task-Adaptive Cross Attention in Few-Shot Learning
authors: Rohith Ganesh Kanchi, Azhagesh S, S D Madhumitha, Berin Shalu S, Vergin Raja Sarobin M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Few-shot learning (FSL) aims to recognize novel categories with only a handful of labeled examples by transferring knowledge from base classes. Prototypical Networks provide a simple and effective approach by computing class prototypes as mean embeddings and classifying queries via nearest-neighbor matching. However, static prototype representations fail to capture task-specific discriminative cues, limiting generalization to novel classes. This paper presents TACA-ProtoNet (Task-Adaptive Cross Attention Prototypical Network), a framework that refines query representations by attending to class prototypes before distance computation. The central component is a multi-head Cross-Attention Refinement (CAR) module that enables queries to selectively aggregate prototype information, producing task-adaptive embeddings that emphasize class-discriminative features. A ResNet-12 backbone is used under standard few-shot protocols, evaluated on both the fine-grained CUB-200-2011 and general-domain miniImageNet benchmarks. Results show that TACA-ProtoNet achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 2. 4 7} \% \pm \text{0. 5 8} \%$</tex> accuracy on CUB-200 5-way 5-shot and 67.85% on miniImageNet 5-way 1-shot classification, outperforming the vanilla ProtoNet by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+4.23 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+5.46 \%$</tex> respectively across 1 -shot and 5 -shot regimes. Ablation results further confirm the advantage of cross-attention refinement over self-attention and no-attention baselines.

title: Zero-Shot Real-Time Pedestrian Re-Identification Services Via Edge-to-Edge Cooperation
authors: Guo Li, Jiandian Zeng, Zihao Peng, Ke Chen, Tian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: Real-time person Re-Identification (ReID) services in video surveillance remain challenging due to real-time requirements and limited generalization of supervised models. Traditional supervised methods typically require extensive labeled training data and are confined to closed identities, struggling to generalize to unseen individuals or new environments. To overcome these limitations, recent work leverages large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for zero-shot ReID, utilizing their rich visual-textual representations to identify pedestrians without prior training. However, directly deploying such VLMs in the cloud inherently introduces high latency, further hindering real-time performance. To tackle the above challenges, we propose VL-ZSReID, a novel edge-collaborative inference framework that deploys VLMs on resource-constrained edge devices to enable zero-shot ReID in complex multi-camera campus surveillance environments. The framework partitions the ReID task into a Tracking Service and a VLM Service, which are coordinated by a Cache-assisted Heuristic Edge Scheduling Strategy (CHESS) to reduce latency and improve throughput across heterogeneous edge devices. To enable practical deployment of VLMs on resource-constrained edge devices, VL-ZSReID adopts several system-level design choices. It incorporates a Tracking ID Discriminator (TID) as an intelligent caching module, an Attribute Caption Generation (ACG) mechanism that produces standardized feature descriptions, and a Temporal-Camera Feature Generator (TCFG) that leverages spatiotemporal context to improve accuracy. Implemented and evaluated in a real campus deployment, VL-ZSReID achieves 19.98%–195.47% higher throughput and 38.00%–82.27% lower latency than baseline methods, while improving Rank-1 and mAP by 2.44%–44.68% and 4.11%–60.89%, respectively.

title: Large-scale and High-density Hydrogen Sensor Based on OFDR and Weak Fiber Bragg Grating Array
authors: Xing Liu, Qiujuan Li, Wanru Tang, Xinyue Yu, Weiming He, Yaxi Yan, Dexin Ba, Yongkang Dong
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: A large-scale and high-density hydrogen sensor is proposed by integrating a Pt/WO₃ coated identical weak fiber Bragg grating (WFBG) array with optical frequency domain reflectometry (OFDR). Pt/WO₃ generates local temperature change upon hydrogen exposure, inducing a spectral shift of WFBG with hydrogen concentration changes. Leveraging OFDR's high spatial resolution, it achieves simultaneous spatial localization and spectral demodulation of identical WFBGs without crosstalk, overcoming scalability limits of conventional hydrogen sensors. Experimental results show that the hydrogen sensing scale, density, sensitivity and response time are 5,000 points, 20 mm, 0.365 nm/% and 10 s, respectively. And the sensor has good repeatability, selectivity and in-situ temperature compensation capability. The proposed large-scale and high-density hydrogen sensor provides a scalable solution for leakage monitoring of hydrogen storage facilities and pipeline transportation.

title: Reliability Investigation and Efficiency Analysis for a Family of High-Gain DC-DC Converters
authors: Vemparala Seshagiri Rao, Mahmoud F. Elmorshedy, M. S. Bhaskar, Dhafer Almakhles, S. Kumaravel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Most conventional high-gain dc–dc converter topologies prioritize voltage gain enhancement. However, for practical applications such as photovoltaic and fuel-cell systems, improving converter efficiency rather than increasing voltage gain beyond a certain threshold is of paramount importance. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of five newly developed high-gain dc–dc converters (HGDC) architectures. The introduced configurations substantially reduce current stress on both passive and active components, particularly those positioned near the input side, thereby improving overall efficiency. A detailed comparative study against published HGDCs is conducted. The results demonstrate that reduced current stress directly contributes to higher efficiency in the introduced Topologies-1 and -4, outperforming both the remaining introduced converters and conventional solutions. Furthermore, a small-signal model for Topologie-1 is developed, and the necessary transfer functions and frequency response are obtained with a Bode plot, and a PI controller is designed. Experimental validation for Topologie-1 and Topologie-4 demonstrates successful step-up of 48 V to 650 V at 0.5 kW and 50 kHz switching frequency. Finally, conducted the component-level reliability investigation to assess the failure rate of components.

title: Finite-Time Distributed Optimization of Nonlinear Multi-agent Systems: A Small-gain Theorem
authors: Zhanxiu Wang, Zhenghong Jin, Hua Li, Fengjun Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: In this paper, the finite-time distributed optimization problem is investigated for nonlinear multi-agent systems under the quasi-strongly connected and weight-balanced graph. The main assignment is to drive all agents to an optimal solution of a specified global objective function, which is viewed as the referenced output, in finite-time, using the partial actual-output-related knowledge of the local information function. In order to achieve this assignment, finite-time adaptive distributed optimal coordinators and finite-time tracking controllers are designed separately. Exploiting the actual outputs of agents, the adaptive distributed optimal coordinators generate reference outputs that converge to the optimal solutions of the global objective function in finite-time, while the finite-time tracking schemes are developed to drive all output signals of each agent to track the reference outputs also in finite-time. Thus the entire closed-loop system can be perceived as an interconnected system involving the two design modules mentioned above and nonlinear multi-agent systems. The timescale separation approach, finite-time input-to-state stability (FTISS) and small-gain approach are applied to deal with the module of tracking controllers, distributed optimal coordinators and the entire interconnected closed-loop system, respectively. Finally, the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed algorithm are demonstrated by a numerical simulation.

title: P-dits for the Frequency Assignment Problem with Transmitter Deactivation in Dense Environments
authors: Christian Duffee, Jordan Athas, Igor Kadota, Randall Berry, Pedram Khalili Amiri
publish: IEEE Journal on Exploratory Solid-State Computational Devices and Circuits
abstract: The frequency assignment problem is a non-deterministic polynomial-time hard optimization problem concerning the assignment of frequency channels to wireless transmitters. Typically, the aim is to minimize interference between transmitters while maintaining a high level of service. In this work, the use of probabilistic d-dimensional bits, or p-dits, for this task is considered. These are stochastic elements which oscillate between discrete states that lie in a multi-dimensional space. Here, each dimension corresponds to an allowed wireless channel. Additionally, a special 0 state exists which corresponds to a disabled, and consequently completely non-interfering transmitter. Each transmitter maps to a single p-dit that undergoes repeatable stochastic updates. The p-dit influences the probability distributions of its neighbors, biasing them towards non-interfering states. To provide a realistic and varied dataset, geospatial data for a medium-sized city was used. A simple greedy, Tabu-style, DSatur-style, a traditional p-bit based probabilistic Ising machine solver, and the proposed p-dit based probabilistic Ising machine solver, were tasked with finding an assignment which penalized both deactivated transmitters and interference on a two, three, and four wireless channel problem. Overall, for the same non-trivially short runtime budgets, the probabilistic Ising machine solver found the highest quality solutions of all the approaches explored.

title: Dimension Decomposition Transformer for Enhanced Underwater Image Restoration
authors: Thallapally Harika, Tirumalasetti Supraja, Kankanala Srinivas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Underwater images often suffer from low contrast, color distortion and degradation in detail due to light absorption, scattering and the complex underwater environment. Existing deep learning-based methods mainly rely on convolutional neural networks (CNN), which have limited capabilities in multiscale feature extraction and global context modeling. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a dimensionality decomposition transformer-based (DDT) network for underwater image enhancement (UIE). The framework integrates dimension decomposition locally enhanced transformer (DLT) and self-attention locally enhanced transformer (SLT) layers to effectively capture both high- and low-resolution features. A local enhancement block (LEB) is used to restore fine details and textures. The network is trained in a supervised manner using paired underwater and reference images and optimized with a combination of L1 loss, perceptual loss, structural similarity index measure (SSIM) loss and total variation loss. A discriminator (D) guides the generator (G) through adversarial learning to enhance the perceptual quality. Experimental evaluation on benchmark underwater datasets shows that the proposed method outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches in both quantitative metrics and visual quality, producing perceptually accurate and visually appealing images suitable for underwater perception and visual engineering applications.

title: Acoustic Bird Species Classification Using Modified CNN and Attention Bi-LSTM
authors: Uma Maheswari Pandyan, R. Sundarajapandian, Mohamed Mansoor Roomi Sindha, S. Gandhimathi alias Usha, Zaakir Ahmed M, Manish Rahul P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Classification of bird species plays an important role in biodiversity monitoring, habitat health, etc. Earlier studies depend heavily on experts, making the process time-consuming and laborious. The proposed work aims to provide the classification system that uses bird acoustic information with modified convolutional neural network (CNN) and attention Bi-directional Long Short Term (Bi-LSTM) framework. Due to the shortage of local bird species acoustics, novel dataset with 20 different species was created. The proposed acoustic dataset comprises of 3,365 samples. The classification pipeline consists of a feature extraction phase, modified convolutional neural network, attention infused Bi-directional Long-Short Term framework. The Salient acoustic features were captured using Mel-frequency Cepstral Coefficients(MFCC) which were based on mel spectrograms from the input sound signal. Then the modified CNN extract the initial features from the MFCC and then Bi-directional layers of the long-short term memory requirements based on a recurrence network to learn all temporal dependencies. Experiment results show that the trained model can accurately classify bird species with the accuracy of 83.4% using the sample of the bird species acoustic details, and it is enabled by feature representations based on the MFCC. The experimental analysis also highlighted the potential of bioacoustics informatics of using a modified attention based deep-learning model to support ecological surveillance and avian conservation devices.

title: 3D Polyp Morphology Estimation from 2D Endoscopy using Deep Segmentation and MiDaS-Based Monocular Depth Reconstruction
authors: Palanivel Rajan S, Harshavarthan KS, Khader Sheriff I, Loganaath S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Accurate 3D characterization of colorectal polyps, capturing their height, diameter, and fine depth variations, plays an essential role in dysplasia risk assessment and clinical decision-making. However, most clinical endoscopy systems provide only 2D RGB images, making true morphology difficult to interpret. In this work, we describe a simple framework that recovers an approximate 3D geometry from a single endoscopy frame without extra hardware. The pipeline combines U-Net based polyp segmentation, MiDaS DPT-Hybrid for dense monocular depth estimation, and pinhole-camera back-projection to generate a detailed 3D point cloud. Unlike stereo or SL systems requiring specialized hardware, our method enables shape recovery using standard monocular endoscopy. We introduce a convex-hull-based normalization step that maps MiDaS relative depth values to an approximate metric scale, enabling computation of key morphological descriptors, including polyp height, centroid depth, diameter, height-diameter ratio, and estimated camera-to-polyp distance. Evaluation of ten Kvasir-SEG samples demonstrates stable geometric trends, with a mean height of 7.50 mm (range: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2.96 - 14.29 mm}$</tex>) and a centroid distance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{11.16 mm}$</tex>, confirming that relative monocular depth can be reliably transformed into interpretable anatomical measurements.
