title: mmOVD$^+$: Detecting Fully Occluded Vehicles With Millimeter-Wave Signals
authors: Chenming He, Chengzhen Meng, Xiaoran Fan, Dequan Wang, Lu Zhang, Yubo Yan, Yanyong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Reliable detection of nearby vehicles is critical for autonomous driving. Problems thus arise when a vehicle is occluded and becomes undetectable, which may lead to accidents. In this paper, we develop mmOVD+, a millimeter-wave radar-based system that enables fully occluded vehicle detection by leveraging ground-reflected signals that propagate beneath the blocking vehicle's chassis. To eliminate ghost points caused by frequent multi-path reflections, we devise a set of neighborhood behavior-based point features and integrate them into GhostNet, a customized deep network that enables accurate point classification through combined feature learning. In addition, we propose a cumulative clustering algorithm to effectively aggregate the unstable ground-reflected radar points over consecutive frames to derive the bounding boxes of the vehicles. We have evaluated mmOVD+ in both controlled and real-world environments. In an underground garage and two campus roads, we conducted controlled experiments in 70 scenes with 9 vehicles, including a minibus, a motorcycle, and an electric moped. Our system accurately detects occluded vehicles, with a 92.7% F1 score for occluded vehicle detection and a 100% success rate for occlusion event detection. More importantly, we drove 324 km on crowded roads at a speed up to 70 km/h and show we could achieve a 93.8% occlusion detection success rate and a 2.4% false alarm rate with only 10% of the training data.

title: An Offline-First Intelligent Legal Chatbot for Indian Law Using Deep Learning and NLP
authors: Mallisetty Divya Sree, K. M. Rayudu, Nidamanuri Srinu, D. Bujji Babu, G. Sujatha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Navigating the complexities of the Indian legal system presents a significant challenge for the general public, largely due to convoluted legal terminology and limited access to reliable legal counsel. This paper introduces an intelligent, offline- first legal chatbot designed to demystify Indian law, specifically the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC). The proposed system leverages Natural Language Processing (NLP) for understanding user queries and a deep learning model Long Short- Term Memory (LSTM) for robust intent classification. Unlike conventional online legal assistants that rely on web scraping and keyword matching, our system operates entirely offline, retrieving precise and contextually relevant legal sections from a structured MySQL database. The chatbot supports both text and voice-based inputs, enhancing accessibility for a diverse user base, including those in low- connectivity regions. Experimental results demonstrate that the LSTM-based approach achieves high accuracy in classifying legal intents, outperforming traditional machine learning baselines. This work contributes a scalable and user-friendly solution that promotes legal literacy and provides preliminary legal assistance without requiring an internet connection, thereby democratizing access to legal information.

title: Element-Level Access Control on Encrypted SQL Tables Using CP-ABE: Optimized on Reusable Sub-policy
authors: Juhao Hu, Jing Wu, Chengnian Long
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the rapid growth of cloud computing, securely outsourcing sensitive data has become crucial for protecting user privacy. SQL, renowned for its expressiveness and flexibility, remains the predominant query language for big data analytics. However, existing access control mechanisms for encrypted SQL databases primarily operate at coarse granularity levels, such as databases, tables, or columns, failing to meet nuanced user access requirements at the individual data-element level. To address this challenge, we propose EGuardSQL, a novel architecture that enables fine-grained, element-level access control over encrypted SQL tables. Our approach leverages Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption (CP-ABE) to define hybrid access policies that integrate both row-level and column-level permissions, facilitating precise and flexible control. To mitigate the inherent computational overhead associated with CP-ABE, particularly when dealing with complex policy structures, we introduce an optimized reusable sub-policy mechanism that effectively reduces redundant cryptographic computations during encryption and decryption processes. We formally prove the selective IND-CPA security of the EGuardSQL scheme under the decisional $q$-parallel Bilinear Diffie-Hellman Exponent (BDHE) assumption and demonstrate its resistance to collusion attacks. Comprehensive performance evaluations illustrate that EGuardSQL achieves significant improvements in computational and communication efficiency compared to conventional CP-ABE approaches, while maintaining low storage overhead and robust security guarantees.

title: IEEE ITSS Technical Committee on Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence in Transportation [ITS Technical Committees]
authors: Renran Tian, Jing Chen, Yisheng Lv
publish: IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: AVIR: Audio-Visual Information Refinement for Robust Multimodal Deepfake Detection
authors: Xiaolong Liu, Yang Yu, Xiaolong Li, Guangzhe Zhao, Yao Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Recent advances in Deepfake generation have enabled forged audio to be synthesized alongside manipulated videos, significantly enhancing realism and posing new challenges for Deepfake detection. However, due to the inherent cross-modal gap between audio and visual information, existing multimodal methods often fail to effectively exploit complementary information across modalities. Moreover, both unimodal and multimodal representations commonly contain redundant and noisy information, which weakens discriminative cues and degrades detection performance. To address these issues, we propose Audio-Visual Information Refinement (AVIR), a robust multimodal Deepfake detection framework that explicitly reduces redundancy and noise. AVIR integrates superpixel modeling and the information bottleneck (IB) principle to learn compact and task-relevant representations. Specifically, a Superpixels-based Defects Mining Transformer (SDMT) is designed to cluster local forgery artifacts into semantically coherent regions, thereby reducing redundant and noisy visual information. To further refine both unimodal and multimodal features, an Artifacts-Emphasis Information Bottleneck (AEIB) module is proposed, which learns minimal sufficient representations by maximizing mutual information with detection labels while constraining irrelevant information from the input. In addition, AEIB is applied to multimodal representations to effectively mitigate the cross-modal gap and enhance discriminative fusion. Extensive experiments on multiple public benchmarks demonstrate that AVIR outperforms state-of-the-art unimodal and multimodal Deepfake detection methods in cross-dataset generalization scenarios.

title: Extrapolation-based practical observers
authors: Laura Menini, Corrado Possieri, Antonio Tornambè
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: A novel practical observer that mitigates the classical trade-off between transient peaking and steady-state estimation error is presented. The proposed extrapolation-based observer consists of multiple practical observers, whose gains are suitably chosen, and fuses their outputs using the Richardson extrapolation formula. The effectiveness of this observer is analytically studied and is validated using a chaotic system from the literature.

title: Physics-Informed Voltage Data Imputation for Robust Machine Learning-Based Battery State of Charge Estimation
authors: Showrov Rahman, Kaushallya Adhikari
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society
abstract: Machine learning (ML) estimators used for battery state of charge (SOC) prediction are vulnerable to missing measurement data and therefore require data imputation techniques to replace unavailable observations. However, most existing data imputation methods for SOC estimation rely primarily on statistical approaches rather than incorporating battery physics and dynamic behavior. Consequently, the estimation accuracy of such estimators degrades significantly during long periods of consecutive missing data. This study addresses this research gap by proposing a physics-informed (PI) data imputation method that reconstructs missing voltage measurements using a battery equivalent circuit model. The proposed PI data imputation technique is integrated with a long short-term memory (LSTM) architecture to develop the ML estimator. To evaluate the estimation performance, the developed model is compared with two other LSTM estimators that use conventional voltage imputation methods: last observation carried forward (LOCF) and extrapolation. The estimation errors of the three estimators are evaluated using experimental data obtained from a photovoltaic battery energy storage system (PV-BESS) equipped with lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries. Furthermore, the robustness of the estimators is analyzed by quantifying the confidence intervals using Bayesian approximation and Monte Carlo forward passes, providing a comprehensive assessment of estimation performance. The estimators are evaluated under different missing data ratios of 25%, 50%, and 75% in order to systematically compare their performance. The results demonstrate that the proposed PI-based estimator consistently achieves superior performance across all scenarios, and the performance gap between the PI-based approach and conventional imputation methods (LOCF and extrapolation) increases as the duration of missing data becomes longer.

title: 2026 AESS Distinguished Lecturers Program
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine

title: An Ultra-Compact Doherty Power Amplifier Based on Reciprocal Three-Coil-Coupled-Network
authors: Lei Zhang, Jianhua Zhu, Qian Zhang, Xiaoxian Liu, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This brief presents an ultra-compact Doherty PA based on the three-coil-coupling-network (TCCN). The TCCN supports load modulation while satisfying impedance matching, and imposes minimal constraints on the output parasitic capacitance of the transistors. Moreover, a detailed parametric analysis is conducted, clarifying the relationship between the traditional 90° equivalent lumped matching network and the specific parameters of the TCCN. A Ka-band Doherty PA prototype is fabricated in 65nm CMOS process to validate the effectiveness of this method, with a total core area of 0.074 mm². At 27GHz, the proposed DPA achieves a peak power output of 21.9 dBm, a peak power-added efficiency of 27.8%, a power-added efficiency at 6dB power back-off of 18.76%, with a maximum power density of 2.09 W/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: WiSNeT2027 CFP
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Microwave Magazine

title: Power Quality Detection Based on AT-CoSaMP
authors: Lang Wu, Zeying Zhi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: This paper proposes AT-CoSaMP (Adaptive Threshold Compressive Sampling Matching Pursuit), an improved version of CoSaMP incorporating an adaptive threshold selection strategy to enhance reconstruction accuracy in power quality signal detection. Unlike conventional CoSaMP, AT-CoSaMP dynamically adjusts the threshold during iteration to refine the support set, improving identification accuracy when signal sparsity is unknown or variable. To address the non-sparsity of time-domain signals, Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) is applied to enable frequency-domain sparsity, where both algorithms perform denoising and reconstruction. The denoised signal is then transformed back to the time domain via Inverse FFT (IFFT). Simulation results demonstrate that under identical noise conditions, AT-CoSaMP achieves superior reconstruction accuracy compared to CoSaMP.

title: Dual-Subspace Robust Predictive Control of Marine Propulsion Six-Phase PMSM Based on Composite Virtual Vectors
authors: Xiangjun Wang, Yancheng Liu, Haohao Guo, Fengkui Zhang, Shi You, Qiaofen Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: This paper presents a model-free robust predictive control strategy for a shipboard six-phase permanent magnet synchronous motor (six-phase PMSM) propulsion system, addressing challenges such as parameter uncertainties, load disturbances, and nonlinear harmonic currents under long-term heavy-load operations. The proposed approach utilizes a dual-subspace composite virtual vector set to simultaneously achieve precise current tracking in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha-\beta$</tex> subspace and harmonic suppression in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x-y$</tex> subspace, effectively reducing current distortion and energy losses in heavy-load conditions. By integrating nonlinear disturbance estimation with an ultra-local modeling framework, a model-free current controller is developed to enhance robustness against parameter variations. Furthermore, an independent model-free speed loop is incorporated to improve system adaptability in plug-and-play and variable-load scenarios. Simulation results under typical ship propeller load conditions demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed method in harmonic suppression, robustness, and dynamic response compared to conventional approaches.

title: Explainable Sentiment Analysis of E-Commerce Product Reviews in Bangla Using Attention-Based BiLSTM
authors: M. Humayet Islam, Rejowan Arifin Nayeem, Neha Khan, Sumiya Rahman, Faria Afsana Rimu, Abdullah Raian
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: In the age of digitization, customer reviews on ecommerce websites contain valuable insights on consumer satisfaction, product quality, and effectiveness of services. Sentiment analysis, a subfield of natural language processing (NLP), enables automatic classification of reviews to determine public opinion. In this work, a sentiment analysis system is presented for e-commerce product reviews in Bangla using both traditional machine learning models and deep learning approaches. A manually labeled dataset of 1,000 reviews and an augmented dataset of 5,000 reviews were used for training and evaluation. Word embeddings were applied for feature representation, and Explainable AI (XAI) with attention scores was used to identify key tokens influencing predictions. Among all models, BiLSTM with Attention achieved the best performance, with 94.00% accuracy on the original dataset and 98.80% on the augmented dataset. This study highlights the effectiveness of combining augmentation, deep learning, and attention mechanisms in enhancing automatic review analysis for e-commerce platforms.

title: Overshoot-Free Decoupling Control Strategy for Steer-by-Wire Systems based on Asymmetric Cost Function and Dual Neural Networks
authors: Yiting Huang, Zheng Wu, Bole Su, Yuan Yao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Four wheel independent steering (4WIS) could improve the stability and active safety of electric vehicles. Its steer-by-wire system needs permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) with high position tracking accuracy and fast dynamic response. However, a conventional proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controller faces a trade-off between speed and overshoot in high-dynamic scenarios, further causing trajectory deviation and degraded performance, especially considering the tire-road nonlinear disturbances. To address these challenges, an overshoot-free decoupling control strategy for PMSMs in 4WIS systems is proposed based on a modified dual-neural network (DNN) PID, which employs a DNN cascade structure and innovatively designs an asymmetric cost function for offline training. Besides, the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is involved to solve extremum trap problem caused by asymmetric constraints in the solution space. Simulations show that PSO effectively escapes local optima under step loads, which is different from conventional gradient-free methods. Furthermore, the proposed strategy significantly improves the dynamic response speed while strictly maintaining the zero-overshoot constraint, enhancing the handling stability of the 4WIS system.

title: Product Reliability of Commercial Vertical SiC Power MOSFETs Achieved by Optimized Screening
authors: Ayan K. Biswas, Daniel J. Lichtenwalner, Callie Woods, Shadi Sabri, Sei-Hyung Ryu, Brett Hull, Adam Barkley, Donald A. Gajewski, Elif Balkas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This paper discusses gate oxide reliability in SiC vertical power MOSFETs under gate and drain bias stresses. Excellent intrinsic and extrinsic reliability has been achieved via optimized processing and screening methodologies. Time-dependent dielectric breakdown (TDDB) and accelerated reverse bias (ARB) tests demonstrate that the reliability of the gate oxide in SiC MOSFETs is comparable to that of silicon devices.

title: A Multi-Die Self-Consistent Framework for Thermal Stability Analysis in Nanosheet 3D SiPs
authors: Yukai Chen, Moritz Brunion, Matthew Walker, Dwaipayan Biswas, Julien Ryckaert, James Myers
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: In nanosheet-era 2.5D/3D chiplet-based systems, strong temperature-dependent power introduces pronounced power-temperature feedback, rendering conventional fixed-power thermal analysis insufficient to assess thermal feasibility. In vertically integrated architectures, such temperature-induced power feedback can eliminate the existence of a stable steady-state thermal operating point, leading to thermal instability and runaway behavior that remain invisible in one-pass steady-state simulations. This work presents a multi-die self-consistent thermal analysis framework that explicitly couples temperature-dependent leakage with system-level thermal solving across heterogeneous 3D Systems-in-Package (SiPs), enabling direct evaluation of thermal stability rather than peak temperature alone. The framework is applied to a representative HPC-class SiPs integrating an N7 FinFET I/O chiplet and an A10 nanosheet compute chiplet with multiple 3D integrations, including face-to-back, face-to-face, and face-to-face with backside power delivery network (BSPDN). The results show that configurations deemed thermally feasible under fixed-power analysis can become thermally unstable once temperature-induced power feedback is considered, while enhanced package-level cooling restores convergence and exposes clear stability boundaries. These results highlight the necessity of self-consistent thermal stability analysis for nanosheet-era 3D integration and provide a practical basis for reliability-aware early-stage system-technology co-optimization.

title: Radiation Reliability of BEOL Compatible Ga-Doped Indium Oxide MOSFETs for eDRAM Applications
authors: Sharadindu Gopal Kirtania, Chengyang Zhang, Stuart E. Wodzro, Faaiq G. Waqar, Hyun Jae Lee, Dyutimoy Chakraborty, Jason Dean Yeager, Douglas E. Wolfe, Asif Khan, Shimeng Yu, Suman Datta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This work addresses a key bottleneck in radiationhardened computing: achieving high-density embedded memory in monolithically stacked (M3D) systems without relying on areainefficient SRAM or radiation-sensitive scaled Si access devices. We evaluate back-end-of-the-line (BEOL) compatible Ga-doped indium oxide (IGO) MOSFETs as access transistors for vertically integrated 1T-1C embedded DRAM (eDRAM) under both ionizing and displacement-damage radiation. Devices are subjected to<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</sup> Co gamma total-ionizing-dose (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex>-TID) up to 10 MRad(air) and neutron fluence up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{1 5}} \mathbf{n} / \mathbf{c m}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>. IGO MOSFETs exhibit exceptional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex>-TID tolerance: transfer characteristics, threshold voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{V}_{\mathbf{T h}}$</tex>), and effective mobility remain statistically invariant up to 10 MRad, and sub-gap density-of-states (DOS) extraction from multi-frequency CV confirms negligible ionization-induced defect generation. In contrast, neutron irradiation produces pronounced displacement damage, manifested as positive <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{th}}$</tex> shifts, mobility degradation, and increased mid-gap and band-tail defect densities. Bias-temperature-instability (BTI) measurements show radiationaccelerated parameter drift; enhanced PBTI is consistent with activation of deep traps in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{HfO}_{2}$</tex> gate dielectric, while increased NBTI is consistent with radiation-assisted hydrogen diffusion. Calibrated TCAD reproduces these trends, linking <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\gamma}$</tex> TID primarily to modest interface-state increases and neutrons to broader/deeper defect spectra. System-level analysis indicates IGO-based 1T-1C eDRAM achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{5 0 0} \boldsymbol{\times}$</tex> higher retention than silicon baselines and only a 2% retention penalty at 10 MRad (vs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{4 2 \%}$</tex> for Si FinFETs), positioning BEOL IGO as a scalable access-device platform for dense, radiation-hardened monolithic eDRAM.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Mango Leaf Disease Classification: Integrating InceptionV3 and Vision Transformer
authors: Md. Faysal Hossan, Rayan Alam, Tarifuzzaman Riyad, Tahira Taposhi, Zarin Tasnim Prionty, Hasibul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Early and correct identification of plant diseases is highly vital for sustainable agriculture, especially in highvalue crops such as mangoes. This research presents a Hybrid InceptionV3 plus Vision Transformer (ViT) model to automate mango leaf disease classification with high accuracy. Capitalizing on a large dataset of high-resolution images of the Rajbari and Pabna districts in Bangladesh, the model categorizes mango leaves into six disease classes: Anthracnose, Dried, Gall Midge, Healthy, Powdery Mildew, and Senescent. The hybrid model outperforms standalone models like MobileNetV2, EfficientNetV2B0, and InceptionV3 through the combination of local feature extraction abilities of InceptionV3 with global context sensitivity of ViT, rendering a whopping 98.89% accuracy. The model's effectiveness is also proven by ROC curve analysis with AUC values getting near 1.0000 for all but one class. The approach shows the power of merging CNN and transformer models, providing a robust solution to machine learning-based detection of mango leaf disease, which could have a significant effect on crop management and help advance precision agriculture.

title: A 1-kV HBM/250-V CDM Low-Leakage Back-Drive Protection I/O ESD Network Design in Nanosheet/Gate-All-Around Technology
authors: I-Cheng Lin, Ping-Chang Lin, Chun-Cheng Chen, Wen-Hsiang Huang, Jie-Ting Chen, Bo-Shih Huang, Che-Yuan Jao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: A low-leakage back-drive-protection I/O (BDPIO) ESD network in leading-edge nanosheet/gate-all-around (GAA) technology is presented. The I/O leakage issue in the process with transistors of only thin-oxide devices has been successfully solved by ESD design of this work. The internal ESD bus (EBUS) active clamp is replaced with a properly sized two-stage diode string, by which pin leakage is reduced to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<5 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 5}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{C}$</tex> and comparable to conventional FinFET designs using thick-oxide devices. Component-level ESD robustness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-\text{kV}$</tex> HBM and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$250-\mathrm{V}$</tex> CDM was achieved and further validated by TLP/vfTLP characterization results.

title: Entanglement Throughput Measurements and Capacity Estimates for Aerial-Inground Fiber
authors: Nageswara S. V. Rao, Muneer Alshowkan, Aneesh Ramaswamy, Joseph C. Chapman, Nicholas A. Peters, Hsuan-Hao Lu, Joe Lukens, Saikat Guha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: The throughput of entangled qubit pairs per second (eqps) is a basic performance metric of quantum networks that provide the entanglement distribution capability. Over fiber connections, it is measured using specialized instruments, including photonic entanglement sources and single-photon detectors. Extensive theory has been developed to estimate the capacity of a generic quantum channel, which in turn is applied to estimate the maximum achievable eqps over a fiber connection. However, there is a gap in relating these two performance metrics, in part due to their disparate nature, namely, mathematical formulae of the channel capacity and specialized instrumentation for eqps measurements. We describe eqps measurements collected over testbed connections composed of aerial and inground fiber of lengths up to 45 km. We estimate the normalized capacity using the transmissivity parameter derived from single photon detector measurements. These estimates are then converted to capacity bounds on the connection's eqps using the source eqps rate derived from co-located detector measurements. The results indicate a consistency between eqps measurements and their capacity estimates, and provide insights into relating the parameters of analytic capacity estimates to physical measurements.

title: Quantum Advantage Without Real-Time Classical Communication in Networks with Tripartite Entangled Sources
authors: Leonardo Oleynik, Seid Koudia, Symeon Chatzinotas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: The distribution of multipartite entanglement is a key requirement for scalable quantum networks. While LOCC networks are theoretically universal, their reliance on quantum memories limits practical deployment. LOSR networks, which avoid real-time classical communication, offer a more feasible alternative but are fundamentally constrained when limited to bipartite sources. We conjecture that these limitations can be overcome with higher-dimensional entangled resources. Focusing on a square LOSR network with tripartite sources, we demonstrate a fidelity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 / 2$</tex> with GHZ states, surpassing the bound established in the literature. This result provides a concrete example of quantum advantage in LOSR networks and opens new directions for entanglement distribution under realistic constraints.

title: Design and Implementation of a Dual-Mode Floor Mopping Robot
authors: Mohammad Al Hosan Kanon, Ilias Ahmad, Dipto Archishman Saha, Md. Al Amin, Nahian Tasnim, Puspita Mobarak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Maintaining clean floors is essential for a hygienic environment. However, relying solely on manual mopping is labor-intensive and time-consuming. This paper presents a dualmode floor mopping robot, which is capable of both automatic and manual operation. Four DC motors are used for the robot's movement, and two separate motors rotate mop pads for floor cleaning purposes. In automatic mode, the robot uses two ultrasonic sensors on the sides and a front sensor mounted on a servo to detect obstacles or walls. For obstacle bypassing or wall avoidance with systematic navigation, it uses a gyrosensor to make accurate 90° turns based on a position-dependent decision algorithm. In manual mode, the robot is controlled via a Bluetooth module using a Bluetooth-enabled Android app, which allows the user to clean specific spots when needed. During mopping, a timed water pump reduces water waste, and a waterlevel sensor with an alert system notifies the user when a refill is needed. Tests in a 10×16ft room showed a 100% success rate in obstacle and wall detection. Additionally, the robot was able to complete four to five cleaning cycles on a single battery charge. Overall, the system provides an efficient and flexible solution for indoor floor-cleaning applications.

title: Cryogenic Mismatch and Variability in 22nm FDSOI MOSFETs for Digital Circuits
authors: Yihong Qing, Jinghao Zhao, Xue Fan, Valentijn De Smedt, Jeffrey Prinzie
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This work evaluates mismatch and variability in 22nm fully depleted silicon-on-insulator (FDSOI) MOSFETs at cryogenic temperatures (CT). By measuring 3200 devices in total (LVTNFETs and LVTPFETs), mismatch data for the threshold voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{t h}$</tex>), the subthreshold swing (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S S$</tex>), and the current ratio (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>) were extracted using Pelgrom's scaling law, showing a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 5 0 \%}$</tex> increase in the Pelgrom scaling factors at 4.5 K compared to 300 K. The drain-current variability increases at CT and is accurately predicted by the Croon model based on extracted parameters. At the circuit level, the static noise margin (SNM) for inverter-based SRAMs decreases at 4.5 K, degrading the inverter stability. The variability of SNM under normal supply voltage increases by 2 times at 4.5 K compared to that at 300 K. The overall inverter stability is worse, which potentially lead to functional failure of the inverter at CT. This work provides empirical data and validated models for advanced FDSOI-based digital circuits at CT.

title: Comprehensive Analysis of Hot Carrier Injection Reliability in FinFET LDMOS Devices with Various Trench Structures and Doping Configurations
authors: Sangku Park, Kwanyeol You, Jinwoo Kim, Wooyeol Maeng, Jaehyun Yoo, Taiki Uemura, Myoungsoo Yeo, Shinyoung Chuong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: In this study, hot carrier injection (HCI) degradation test and simulation were conducted for fin-based laterally diffused MOS (LDMOS) devices with varying process conditions. Trench structure, channel length, and doping profile significantly influence HCI degradation. In particular, the interface doping profile in the drift region induces HCI degradation trends that differ from those observed in CMOS devices. Since these device process characteristics have a critical impact on both reliability and performance, careful device design that considers both performance and reliability under various process conditions is essential to provide optimal high-performance computing capabilities. Finally, an on/off HCI degradation model was provided to predict fin LDMOS HCI degradation under circuit operating conditions.

title: Integrating Deep Reinforcement Learning with a Hybrid GNN Model to Optimize Neighborhood Selection for Link Prediction
authors: Salman Monir, Sagor Hossain, Md. Rafiquzzaman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Link prediction in knowledge graphs is a critical task that has numerous applications such as knowledge base completion, recommender system and question answering. One major challenge in link prediction using graph neural networks (GNNs) is selecting the appropriate neighborhood around each node. Existing models tend to have fixed scopes of aggregation, which results in inadequate capture of contexts and sub-optimality. We present a novel link prediction framework, which incorporates reinforcement learning (RL) into a hybrid model of Relational Graph Convolutional Networks (R-GCN) and Graph Attention Networks (GAT). The RL agent dynamically selects the size of the neighborhood around the node, and the hybrid model leverages relation-aware message passing and an attention mechanism to assign weights to the neighbors in order to prioritize the neighbors. We test the suggested model on various real-world data sets and also compare the performance of the model to other already existing state-of-the-art models, such as GCN, GAT, GraphSAGE and R-GCN. The findings show that our approach outperformed the baselines with regard to AUC, Precision, Recall, and F1 Score, confirming the validity of using adaptive neighborhood selection to enhance the accuracy of link prediction.

title: ES-YOLO: An Edge-Guided and Scale-Aware Model for Infrared Substation Equipment Detection
authors: Zheng Zhu, Jingcheng Wang, Qiong Wu, Junlu Xia, Rui Xia, Bo Zhang, Bo Xu, Wei Yang, Junjie Qian
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Infrared object detection plays a critical role in overheating fault diagnosis and operational safety assurance for substation equipment. However, substation infrared imagery often exhibits cluttered backgrounds, blurred target boundaries, and substantial scale variation, which degrade detection and localization accuracy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes ES-YOLO, an oriented detector for substation infrared equipment in thermal imagery. Built on the YOLO11-OBB framework, the proposed method introduces an Edge Fusion Attention (EFA) module, which integrates structural edge cues into intermediate feature learning to strengthen contour-aware representation, alleviate background interference, and improve the characterization of weak-boundary targets. In addition, a Scale-Aware Refinement (SAR) module is designed to enhance multi-scale feature representation through progressive receptivefield expansion and dynamic scale weighting. Experiments on a real-world substation operation-and-maintenance infrared dataset show that ES-YOLO improves mAP@50 and mAP@50:95 by 2.8 and 3.4 percentage points, respectively, over the YOLO11 baseline, while achieving 118.2 FPS. These results indicate that the proposed method provides an effective and efficient solution for practical substation infrared equipment detection.

title: Design Principles for Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor Structures for Vertical GaN Power Devices (Invited)
authors: T. Narita, K. Ito, H. Iguchi, D. Kikuta, S. Iwasaki, K. Kataoka, M. Kanechika, E. Kano, N. Ikarashi, K. Tomita, M. Horita, J. Suda, T. Kachi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: High channel mobility of a metal-oxide-semiconductor (MOS) channel is the greatest advantage of a GaN power device. To attain high performance with reliable operation, traps and charges at the oxide/nitride interface should be controlled. Interface traps at the oxide/nitride junction reduce free electrons as well as the channel mobility. Electron injection into oxide traps under a positive gate bias causes a shift of the threshold voltage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(V_{\text{th}})$</tex>, referred to as positive bias instability. Electron capture at interface and oxide traps can be controlled by a thin crystalline AIN interfacial layer (AIN-IL) deposited by plasma-enhanced atomic layer deposition. The quality and thickness of AIN-ILs are important design parameters that affect the channel mobility and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{th.}}$</tex> In addition, their roles differ between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$c$</tex>-plane and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex>-plane channels. Hole traps near the valence band of GaN cause negative bias instability and can be controlled by an AIN-IL and Mg doping in the body layer. The design principles for a vertical GaN MOS field-effect transistor that exhibits a good balance between low channel resistance and high reliability are discussed.

title: Transient Short-Channel Effects Induced by Si $\delta$ Doping in Buffer-Free $\mathrm{L}_{\mathrm{G}}=0.15 \mu \mathrm{m}$ AlGaN/GaN HEMTs
authors: F. De Pieri, C. De Santi, I. Rossetto, A. Carlotto, S. Roy Chowdhury, M. Saro, F. Rampazzo, H. Stieglauer, J. Grünenpütt, D. Sommer, Jr-Tai Chen, M. Meneghini, G. Meneghesso, E. Zanoni
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: The impact of deep-levels on the control and charge confinement of the channel has been studied in RF buffer-free AlGaN/GaN HEMTs on SiC. The adoption of Si <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\delta$</tex>-doping to compensate 2DHG, under high lateral electric field, leads to temporary degradation of the OFF-state and subthreshold characteristics. The degradation of the parameters is affected by the Si concentration and process condition, finding that thermal annealing could drastically improve the device behavior.

title: Research on Loss Assessment and Risk Identification of Natural Disaster Power Outages Based on Multi-dimensional User Portraits
authors: Wei Liu, Shenglong Zhu, Jian Ge, Chao Zhen, Zhengkai Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: To address challenges in disaster-induced outage assessment-coarse classification, static loss functions, and lagging risk identification-this paper proposes a framework integrating multi-dimensional user portraits with machine learning. We construct a “Disaster-Grid-User” portrait system and employ Feature-Weighted Adaptive Clustering (FWAC) to refine user segmentation. A library of dynamic, multi-variable loss models replaces static equations for diverse user types and scenarios, while <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \text{km} \times 5 \text{km}$</tex> grid technology enables a four-level risk early warning mechanism. Validated on 34 disasters in Anhui Province, the framework reduces MAPE for industrial and commercial users to 9.7% and 11.9%, outperforming Logistic Regression and SVR. By precisely identifying high-risk zones, this study offers robust tools for enhancing grid resilience, optimizing resource allocation, and guiding rapid recovery.

title: Interpretable and Explainable AI for Predicting Systems Thinking Skills
authors: Saroj Raut, Fazla Rabbi, Niamat Ullah Ibne Hossain, Raed Jaradat, Alex Gorod, Randy Buchanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Rapid technological advancements in recent years have led to escalating complexity in the modern systems environment. As a result, many contemporary challenges may exceed engineers’ ability to operate effectively within today’s highly complex systems landscape, where systems thinking skills are particularly important. This study aimed at applying Interpretable Machine Learning (ML) algorithms to assess individual systems thinking (ST) ability in light of seven essential ST dimensions, including Level of - Complexity (CMP), Independence (IND), Interaction (INT), Change (CNG), Uncertainty (UNCR), Flexibility (FLEX), and Systems Worldview (SYSW). Consequently, several ML algorithms, including Logistic Regression (LR), AdaBoost, Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Random Forest (RF), and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) were trained and evaluated to classify participants’ ST levels across these dimensions. In addition, all the models underwent hyperparameter tuning to improve predicting capabilities, with an optimal model selected based on comparison analysis that can better forecast an individual’s ST skills. Further, a set of explainable ML analysis (XAI) tools, such as Brier Score, Youden Index, LIME, SHAP force, and summary plots, has been generated to offer further insights into the underlying ML models. The comparative analysis indicates that LR outperformed other models with respect to all four performance metrics (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1 score). The study’s model can assist industries in adopting systems thinking principles for both hiring candidates and workforce development. Moreover, integration of XAI enhances the validity of the analysis and enables more intelligent decision-making.

title: A Compact Frequency Reconfigurable Hybrid Monopole-Patch Antenna for Wideband Applications
authors: Lujain F Al-Hunity, Raneem A. Al-Mashni, Yanal S. Faouri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper introduces a compact frequency- reconfigurable wideband antenna for ultrawideband (UWB) adaptive and spectrum-aware applications. The antenna inherently operates as a wideband (WB) radiator, where frequency reconfiguration is realized by shifting the wideband operating region across the UWB spectrum, enabling full UWB coverage. The proposed antenna was fabricated on a low-cost FR-4 substrate with compact dimensions of 20 mm × 60 mm × 1.6 mm. Frequency tuning is implemented through two PIN diodes, which alter the surface current paths and the effective electrical length of the radiator. Stable impedance matching and reliable radiation performance were preserved across the entire reconfiguration range. Owing to its compact size, simple structure, and continuous wideband frequency reconfigurability, the proposed antenna can be considered a suitable candidate for cognitive radio systems and reconfigurable wireless communication platforms that require efficient and flexible spectrum utilization.

title: Time-Synchronized Wearable Sensor Fusion for Real-Time Multi-Muscle Fatigue and Gait Asymmetry Analysis
authors: Manvi Manjunath, Tarun Alur Gowri Shankar, Nikhil Shiraguppi, Shagun Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The proposed wearable system is a system that combines surface Electromyography (sEMG) and Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) to give real-time gait analysis. This system is constructed using ESP32 microcontroller and transmits synchronized data wirelessly to a Python server. Random forest and SVM machine learning algorithms are used to classify gait abnormalities providing a cost-effective and portable solution to clinicians and patients. This paper demonstrates that wearable technology has the potential to transform gait diagnostics.

title: Real-Time Touchless Human Computer Interaction Using Hand Gesture–Based Virtual Keyboard and Mouse
authors: P. Priyanka, Jaddu Hemalatha, Saikamal Suro, Akkalapotu Surya Pavithra, Tedlapu Sanjuna Sharmista
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Recent developments in computer vision and machine learning has significantly interact with computers in so many natural ways. This study describes the devel- opment of low-cost virtual keyboard and mouse and it works through hand gestures by using OpenCV, MediaPipe and PyAuto Gui for real-time hand landmark tracking. By eliminating the need for traditional input devices, it provides completely touch-free interaction, which is mainly used for people having mobility impairments and it is also beneficial for the environments where hygiene is important. The main feature of the system is its support for multilingual typing in English, Hindi, Telugu,Tamil, and Kannada, helping accessiblility in different regions. Experimental evaluation shows that the system achieves a gesture recognition accuracy of 87 % with an average response time of about 0.4 seconds. The system runs by using a standard webcam and making it practical for everyday use. This work supports to develop an user- friendly human- computer interaction technologies.

title: BlockSafe: A Hybrid Architecture Combining AI-Driven Security and Blockchain Integrity
authors: Georges El Hajal, Gaby Abou Haidar, Roy Abi Zeid Daou
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Due to the rapid increase in digital services, relying on passwords as a primary method of authenticating the identity is increasing. This shift has resulted in the continual increase in data breaches, credential thefts, and phishing attacks on users by the digital-service providers they use. Traditional password-management systems depend upon centralized architecture for their operation, which creates a single point of failure and exposes the system to exploitation by Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks. Current implementations of blockchain technology do offer a decentralized approach, with secure and immutable credentials; however, such implementations tend to be costly from both a hardware and an operational standpoint. BlockSafe is a lightweight, artificial intelligence-based password management system that combines machine-learning-driven security analytics with simple blockchain architecture. The application leverages the Gemini Large Language Model (LLM) to perform intelligent, real-time password strength classification and provide contextual security feedback. In addition, BlockSafe utilizes client-side asymmetric encryption through MetaMask and Ethereum-based smart contracts to ensure the immutability and verifiable integrity of credential data.

title: Evaluating Context-Aware Scoring and Supervised Classification Methods for Toxicity Detection in Social Media Comments
authors: Nayan Adhikari, Sayan Duari, Debdut Parali, Soumyadeep Maity, Siddhartha Chatterjee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The heavy usage of social media platforms has led to a massive increase in toxic, abusive, and offensive comments. Detecting such content is particularly difficult in multilingual and code-mixed settings such as Hinglish, where comments often contain informal language, slang, emojis, and expressive punctuation. Traditional supervised machine learning models, including logistic regression, support vector machines, and Naive Bayes classifiers, are widely used for toxicity detection; however, these approaches depend primarily on statistical patterns and may struggle with ambiguity, leading to higher false-positive rates. This paper presents a comparative study between traditional supervised machine learning models and a context-aware rulebased approach called the Score-based Toxicity Classification Algorithm (STC-Algo). STC-Algo assigns severity weights to abusive terms and incorporates contextual modifiers to compute an interpretable toxicity score using predefined thresholds. Our experiment shows that STC-Algo gives 82.9 % accuracy in predicting the toxicity classes.

title: Deep Autoencoder Features with Metaheuristic Optimized ML Models for Brain Tumor Detection
authors: T. Shailaja, M. Shalini, A.Srinivasa Reddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This research work proposes a hybrid intelligent framework that combines deep autoencoder-based feature representation with metaheuristic-optimized machine learning classifiers to achieve precise brain tumor detection. A deep autoencoder is used to learn compact and distinct latent representations from MRI scans. Then, a metaheuristic optimization algorithm is used to adjust the hyperparameters of machine learning classifiers to make them work better at classifying. The suggested framework does a good job of combining deep representation learning with optimized decision modelling. Experimental assessment on benchmark brain MRI datasets reveals enhanced performance regarding accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and F1-score in comparison to traditional deep learning and machine learning methodologies.

title: Operational Efficiency Clustering Analysis of Wind Farms in Peru Using Multidimensional Performance Metrics
authors: Elmer Arellanos-Tafur
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This research presents a comprehensive analysis of wind energy generation efficiency clustering in Peru using multidimensional performance metrics. The research analyzes operational data from eight major wind energy companies spanning from May 2023 to April 2025, encompassing a total generation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6,876.3 \text{GWh}$</tex>. The methodology employs clustering algorithms based on four key performance indicators: total generation capacity, capacity factor, operational variability, and efficiency index. Results reveal three distinct operational clusters: high-efficiency operators (Parque Eólico Tres Hermanas and Parque Eólico Marcona) with capacity factors exceeding 55%; medium-efficiency operators (Enel Generación Peru, Energía Eólica, and Energía Renovable del Sur) with capacity factors between 25% and 32%; and high-volume operators (Engie, GR Taruca, and GR Paino) with lower efficiency but higher absolute generation. The clustering analysis demonstrates significant operational disparities, with variability coefficients ranging from 43.3% to 109.8%, while seasonal patterns show peak generation during autumn months (March-May) with average monthly outputs of 270 GWh. The research findings provide valuable insights for optimizing wind farm operations, strategic planning, and policy development in Peru's renewable energy sector, contributing to the understanding of regional wind energy performance patterns and establishing benchmarks for future wind farm developments in similar geographical and climatic conditions.
